dialogueLines.text: There's one more thing. This was not my first such vision. Long ago... I saw one of Varatha's other bearers. It was you. Your Infernal Arms... they must be weapons of the Fates' design. They know already who shall brandish them. Now they ask a favor. Seek Varatha. Speak the words. Go.

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: Achilles...! Something awakened in Varatha when I spoke the words. It revealed to me a form I'd never seen. Wielded by the warrior called Guan Yu. Handsomely dressed! And just as you described. That's no Greek name, is it...

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Achilles_01
dialogueLines.text: No, I don't think so, lad. I don't know who this Guan Yu is, or where he's from. Or even when he's from. But the Fates must have a plan for him, and you. Anyway... I think my part in this is ended. Varatha's served you well, no? I trusted it in life; I trust it now. Fear is for the weak.

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: Hey, tell me something, sir. Any rumors swirling about the House regarding the outcome of a certain interaction I had with my father recently? Involving a great deal of violence? Because I'd like for everyone to know: I won.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Achilles_01
dialogueLines.text: I wouldn't know of what you mean, there, lad. For there are certain subjects so forbidden here right now that not even rumors can take hold. But, just between us for the time: Congratulations. Truly.

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: Thank you, sir. I didn't know whether I had it in me. As for the other bit, I'll keep it quiet, then, for now. I probably shouldn't be tying my sense of self-worth to praise from friends, acquaintances, and strangers, anyway.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Achilles_01
dialogueLines.text: I saw your father storm out of the House in his regalia. So he must have confronted you himself, at last. I... see that must have not gone well. You were so close.

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: Well, at least I know who will be waiting for me the next time I climb all the way to the Temple of Styx and manage my way out.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Achilles_01
dialogueLines.text: Don't lose heart, lad. I did not train your father, only you. He is a god; but he is just a god. If you'll forgive my bit of treason there.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Achilles_01
dialogueLines.text: What news this time, lad? Did you find your mother, once again?

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: No, I failed, sir. No thanks to Father! Urgh, what am I going to do?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Achilles_01
dialogueLines.text: Just take it easy, now. You got there once, there isn't any question in my mind that you can do it once again. Your father's acting as though nothing's changed, besides. So, don't lose hope. You're capable of this.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Achilles_01
dialogueLines.text: I understand you've made it all the way to Asphodel of late, haven't you, lad?

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: I guess word travels faster than I do. Why do you ask, Achilles?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Achilles_01
dialogueLines.text: ...No reason in particular. Just that it brings me joy to hear of your progress.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Achilles_01
dialogueLines.text: I haven't seen you in a while, lad. You made it to Elysium last time, didn't you.

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: I did, Achilles. It's crawling with great warriors still looking for a fight. It's not exactly what I was expecting.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Achilles_01
dialogueLines.text: Your father's marshalled the most willing ones to deal with you. Doubtless there's much at stake in it for them, if but the pride of having done the task. Although I think that pride will always be their weakness.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Achilles_01
dialogueLines.text: I couldn't help but notice, lad, that Cerberus has been away for quite some time. Turns out your father's put him back to work above. I truly hope you shall not have to come to blows.

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: Don't worry, sir. I've run into Cerberus up top, all right. Even if he's technically Father's pet, he'd never bare his fangs at me. No matter how much Father might wish otherwise.

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: Wanted to say, the Lady Aphrodite asked that I relay a message to you, sir. She said that she admires you. Mentioned a certain war.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Achilles_01
dialogueLines.text: Admires me, does she? Sometimes, lad, I think that Aphrodite is the mightiest of all the gods. Her power is what spurs us all to action. Whether to love, or war. Or sometimes both the two. I'm grateful for the message.

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: Hey, Achilles! I heard from Lady Artemis out there. It seems you have a bit of a fan in her. She wanted me to tell you you're the best! She's not mistaken, by the way.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Achilles_01
dialogueLines.text: The goddess of the hunt herself said that? That's very flattering to hear. Good thing she doesn't know I'm all washed up, by now. Her memory is of my glory days.

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: Achilles, I... sometimes I fear that Father, if he learns that you've been aiding me, that he will... not be generous with you.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Achilles_01
dialogueLines.text: Don't worry about me, lad. Your father's still getting caught up with work that I created for him in my mortal days. Besides... there's nothing he can take from me. We have a mutual arrangement, he and I.

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: Achilles, I'm concerned that Father's growing angrier with you. He thinks you've taken sides against him in all this. I don't want anything to happen.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Achilles_01
dialogueLines.text: He's right, lad. I have taken sides. But, as for you not wanting anything to happen... while I know what you meant, I don't want you to say such things again. Do you understand? You're doing this because something here needs to happen. Hold back your fear.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Achilles_01
dialogueLines.text: ...You asked I train him, Master. Prepare him for life's labors. You ordered it! Now you admonish me?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hades_01
dialogueLines.text: Be careful of your tone and temper, Shade. You are in my employ. You could be wandering the pastures of Elysium again instead. Or lesser planes, if I so choose.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Achilles_01
dialogueLines.text: ...What would you have me do?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hades_01
dialogueLines.text: I shall be giving it some thought. Until then, you just watch that hall. And watch yourself, as well.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hades_01
dialogueLines.text: ...I understand the terms of your employment have been altered in my name, Shade. Be grateful that you lack the standing for such changes to require my personal approval. Though you remain in my employ, correct?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Achilles_01
dialogueLines.text: As far as I'm aware, yes, Master. I'm sworn eternally here to this House, except... it seems I'm now at liberty to see Elysium, from time to time. While off duty, of course.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hades_01
dialogueLines.text: How lenient they make these pacts, today. You are off duty now, is that not so? Go, then, take advantage of the added benefit.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_FurySister_01
dialogueLines.text: ...What's that you have there, Shade? That looks like contraband to me.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Achilles_01
dialogueLines.text: It is, Mistress. I'm glad you're here, as maybe you can help me quickly to dispose of it.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_FurySister_01
dialogueLines.text: Let us immediately do so, then. Before concerns arise around the presence of this stuff within the House.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_FurySister_01
dialogueLines.text: ...He must be somewhere in Elysium, you know. Where else could he have gone?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Achilles_01
dialogueLines.text: I realize that, Mistress. It's just... I think we've other matters to attend to, such as... emptying the contents of this contraband.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_FurySister_01
dialogueLines.text: I see. That's fine. But you will need to face your fears someday, in all of this. You act as if you can't afford to get involved, but it's a little late for that.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_FurySister_01
dialogueLines.text: ...You found him after all? Because of Zag? That's... how very fortunate for you, Shade.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Achilles_01
dialogueLines.text: Mistress, for certain it is more than I deserve. Although, perhaps it's for the best that a forgotten shade ought to be put out with the rest of them. I shall not cause you so much trouble, then.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_FurySister_01
dialogueLines.text: Well, if your journeys take you from this House, then... you are nothing but a Shade, and so... there will be others, but... take care, OK?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Persephone_Home_01
dialogueLines.text: ...Honored to have met you, great Achilles, our royal guard! Good to know whom to turn to, should anything run afoul around here. So it was you who instructed my son in the martial ways?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Achilles_01
dialogueLines.text: Indeed, Your Grace. He was a talented student. If only you could have seen his progress. Surely you would have been very proud.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Persephone_Home_01
dialogueLines.text: I am proud! You know, you were already quite the name back on the surface when I lived upon Olympus. I should think your legend's spread much farther still, since then!

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Achilles_01
dialogueLines.text: Oh, I doubt that... although, thank you, Your Grace.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Dusa_01
dialogueLines.text: Um, Zagreus? I wanted to talk some more, if you're available? I mean, if you're free. I mean! If you have time to right now. Is that OK?

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: Of course, Dusa! Where should we chat? The lounge, or maybe somewhere more private...?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Dusa_01
dialogueLines.text: Somewhere more private, please, if that's OK? Thank you.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Dusa_01
dialogueLines.text: ...Um, Zagreus... so, ever since you told me you had feelings for me, it's been, like... my whole mind is on fire. I haven't been able to think straight, or get anything done. It's all been so much!

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: I'm sorry, Dusa, I... put a lot on you, and... maybe I shouldn't have, since I'm the Prince and everything, as you point out, and... sometimes, I forget myself. I'd never pressure you, it's just... I thought you had these feelings for me, too.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Dusa_01
dialogueLines.text: I do have feelings for you, silly! That's what I've been trying to tell you, it's just... it's just...

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: It's just what...? Whatever it is, it's OK. You can be yourself with me, Dusa, I promise.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Dusa_01
dialogueLines.text: I'm glad you put it that way. I... I'm not looking for an intimate relationship. You're the kindest soul in this whole House. And I have learned so much from you! But, when I really think about my feelings... I'm just, not attracted to you, in that way. I'm sorry. You're a really nice guy!

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: Oh, hahaha, look, there's nothing to apologize for, not at all. In fact... all this is a relief. I've learned a lot from you, too, and I cherish our friendship for what it is. Do you... do you think that we could still be friends? Or, is it better that you didn't see me anymore...?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Dusa_01
dialogueLines.text: Don't say that! Though, I was going to ask you the same thing. I'd really like it if you'd be my friend! Though, my career comes first, so... I don't want you doing any special favors, promise me? I need to see how far I can get on my own.

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: I respect that so much, and yes, I promise, Dusa. So, well, then... friends? For all eternity?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Dusa_01
dialogueLines.text: Yes. Friends. For all eternity. Now... I had better go. See you, OK?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Dusa_01
dialogueLines.text: Hey, Zagreus, I... wanted to talk to you again, I... what I mean is, I just, hope I didn't hurt your feelings, because, if I did, you'd let me know, wouldn't you?

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: Dusa, please! Remember friends for all eternity? That's a long time, so, there's no need for second guessing. I'm glad we had a chance to talk.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Dusa_01
dialogueLines.text: Oh, well, good! That's a relief to hear, but... one last thing, so I can clear my conscience of it, I... I've saved all the Ambrosia that you gave me, and I figured since... I didn't realize, you know, the way you felt? I can be a little dense! I have it here. And I would like to give it back. Is that OK?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Dusa_01
dialogueLines.text: Take back the Ambrosia? (She won't mind.)

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: I... look, if you don't want the Ambrosia anymore, and receiving it made you feel uncomfortable in any way, I'll take it back. It was my gift to you, to do with as you please. Are you sure you don't want it?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Dusa_01
dialogueLines.text: Yes, I'm sure, and thank you for your understanding, Prince. It really was quite sweet of you, but it's important that I learn to stock the lounge myself. So, here you go, and I will see you soon, OK?

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: Hey, now, there's no need to do anything rash like that! That Ambrosia was my gift to you, and it was unconditional. Just try the stuff and see! They say the purer the act of giving, the better it is. Won't you keep it? If not for yourself, then for the lounge?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Dusa_01
dialogueLines.text: Ah, all right, I... had to ask, so, thanks again. Just wanted to make sure! I hope we can enjoy it at the lounge sometime!! I'd better go put it away. Not drink it! Store it for safe keeping, now, OK?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Dusa_01
dialogueLines.text: Hey, um, Zagreus? I wanted to say, that was very sweet of you, letting me hang onto all that Ambrosia you gave me before? It's in safe keeping now! Except one bottle... that kind of slipped, and broke. Though that did mean I got to try the stuff!

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: Oh, you got to try it, after all? What did you think, all it's cracked up to be?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Dusa_01
dialogueLines.text: It was amazing. I've never had anything like it! It's so good to have a forked tongue, meant I could taste it twice at the same time! So, um... if one of the other bottles slipped and broke... you wouldn't mind, would you?

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: Dusa, I can't shake this feeling, so I wanted to apologize to you, because... I think when we first met, I just... I don't think I took it the right way. I never had a fan before! I wanted to live up to your idea of me.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Dusa_01
dialogueLines.text: Oh, Prince, Zagreus, don't worry about that! Even I didn't know what I was going through. When I first started working here, I was just so impressed with you, I mean... you were the only one who would stand up to your father! I thought about you all the time!

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: But you didn't even know me, yet. We didn't get to talking for what seemed like ages. You can be difficult to find! Since I can't fly up to the rafters like you can.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Dusa_01
dialogueLines.text: Well I'm so glad you kept trying to talk to me! If you hadn't... I'd probably be up in the rafters still, building up this idea of you that's not even as good as the real thing!

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: Erm, Bouldy...? I'm Zagreus, good to make your acquaintance. I know that friends are difficult to come by here in Tartarus, and so, I'm grateful that you seem to be a trusty one.

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: What's the latest, Bouldy? Had any good tumbles down that hill of late?

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: You know, I think I'm growing more accustomed to relating to a giant stone.

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: Don't know why I keep doing this, Bouldy, yet here we are.

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: You take good care of Sisyphus, there, won't you, Bouldy?

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: Sometimes a conversation with a friend is its own reward, isn't that right, Bouldy?

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: You're looking as stately as ever, there, Bouldy.

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: A tribute to the greatest boulder of all time.

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: To our most famous boulder!

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: May you roll smoothly for eternity.

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: Thank you for keeping my friend company.

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: This is for you, Bouldy.

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: Bouldy, please accept this humble offering.

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: Bouldy, lend your favor to me, won't you?

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: An offering to the finest boulder in all of Tartarus.

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: Cheers, Bouldy! Thank you for brightening my day or night.

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: Bouldy, ever get the feeling that we've had this conversation in the past?

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: Let's be sure to do this again sometime, Bouldy my friend.

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: Any advice for me on this occasion, Bouldy?

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: Bouldy, you can speak up if there's something on your mind, you know.

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: You're always here for me, Bouldy! Cheers.

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: How's everything going lately, there, Bouldy?

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: Looks like you and Sisyphus are doing just fine, Bouldy.

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: Good seeing you as ever, there, Bouldy.

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: You're a good friend, Bouldy. I'm glad you're here.

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: You'd tell me if anything was on your mind, right, Bouldy?

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: Hey Bouldy, what's your favorite color?

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: Hey Bouldy, what's your favorite form of sustenance?

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: Hey Bouldy, what do you like to do in your spare time?

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: Hey Bouldy, how's everything of late?

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: Bouldy, while on one hand I know that making offerings to you won't really help get me out of here, on the other hand... maybe it will?

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: Bouldy, how do you handle being trapped in Tartarus for all eternity, doomed to be pushed up and down a hill repeatedly for no good reason?

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: So, erm, there something you like to do for entertainment out here, Bouldy? Or just, I don't know, take it all in, I guess?

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: Enjoying staring at that ball I got you, boy? No fun if it doesn't fight back, huh...

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Cerberus_01
dialogueLines.text: The hell-forged multi-headed monster feels no urgency to waste his precious time on simple play-things, much preferring to conserve his vast energy reserves in case any trespassers need to be eaten suddenly.

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: You know, after dying in some wretched manner, giving pets to my good boy sure helps me get back on my feet. Thanks for being here for me.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Cerberus_01
dialogueLines.text: The burning embers in the eyes of monstrous Cerberus, notorious hound of hell, reveal that he, too, is amenable to the pets of which the Underworld Prince speaks, and in fact is deeply grateful for the quantity of them.

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: I hope I haven't been inconveniencing you too much with all the visits to the Temple lately, boy. Say, how do you get back? Charon giving you a lift?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Cerberus_01
dialogueLines.text: Thus asks the Prince his question of the guardian of the gates of hell, knowing full well the multi-headed monster keeps a wide berth of the river-boatman, as do all with any sense.

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: I found her, boy! My mother! Persephone, you remember her, don't you? She was pleased to hear about you. But I couldn't stay for long, and I have to find her again, so... guess I'll see you at the Temple, then?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Cerberus_01
dialogueLines.text: The ghoulish features of the much-reviled hound of hell all soften instantly upon the mention of a certain name; briefly, then, the monster is at peace.

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: Hey, boy. I hope all the construction and renovation around here hasn't been too inconvenient for you, lately. But hey, the lounge is just about ready for you to utterly destroy again!

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Cerberus_01
dialogueLines.text: Prince Zagreus implants in the impressionable, vicious watchdog of the Underworld a new appetite for more destruction, to be sated in another age.

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: All right, boy, I got your favorite treat. But you have to promise to let me pass if I hand it over, OK?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Cerberus_Field_01
dialogueLines.text: Terrifying Cerberus accepts the fetid sack, and scans the darkened hall for where best to consume the stomach-turning contents.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Cerberus_Field_01
dialogueLines.text: Cerberus again accepts the sack.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Cerberus_Field_01
dialogueLines.text: Cerberus again accepts the sack.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Cerberus_Field_01
dialogueLines.text: Gigantic Cerberus accepts the Prince's foul offering.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Cerberus_Field_01
dialogueLines.text: Gigantic Cerberus accepts the Prince's foul offering.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Cerberus_Field_01
dialogueLines.text: The fearsome Cerberus accepts the Satyr sack.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Cerberus_Field_01
dialogueLines.text: The Prince persuades the Underworld's ferocious guardian to move aside.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Cerberus_Field_01
dialogueLines.text: The Prince compels the multi-headed hound to let him pass.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Cerberus_Field_01
dialogueLines.text: Thus has the Prince again persuaded fearsome Cerberus to get out of his way.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Cerberus_Field_01
dialogueLines.text: Joyfully the hound of hell accepts another Satyr sack.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Cerberus_Field_01
dialogueLines.text: The monster's solitary weakness, Satyr sacks, prove terribly effective, once again.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Cerberus_Field_01
dialogueLines.text: Fang-bearing Cerberus directs all three of his attentions toward the Satyr sack.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Cerberus_Field_01
dialogueLines.text: The guardian of hell itself decides the time has come for a short break and snack.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Cerberus_Field_01
dialogueLines.text: Prince Zagreus once more offers to Cerberus one of his favored sacks.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Cerberus_Field_01
dialogueLines.text: Cerberus moves for no one, but for Satyr sacks, he can make an exception.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Cerberus_Field_01
dialogueLines.text: Vigilant Cerberus decides to look the other way, this once.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Cerberus_Field_01
dialogueLines.text: Each violent countenance of Cerberus agrees the Satyr sack must be destroyed.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Cerberus_Field_01
dialogueLines.text: After deliberating briefly, Cerberus concludes that, yes, he shall accept the sack.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Cerberus_01
dialogueLines.text: Cerberus looks mournfully toward the Prince.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Cerberus_01
dialogueLines.text: The Prince and Cerberus exchange a look.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Cerberus_01
dialogueLines.text: The watch-dog Cerberus observes the Prince.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Cerberus_01
dialogueLines.text: Cerberus cannot be bothered at this time.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Cerberus_01
dialogueLines.text: Cerberus is comfortably at rest, observing all.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Cerberus_01
dialogueLines.text: Cerberus lies as ever at the foot of his Lord Master's throne.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Cerberus_01
dialogueLines.text: Cerberus makes no attempt to move.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Cerberus_01
dialogueLines.text: Cerberus is busily considering upon what else to gnaw.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Cerberus_01
dialogueLines.text: Cerberus goes about his business: gnawing, in this case.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Cerberus_01
dialogueLines.text: Cerberus is preoccupied with scratching and biting at himself.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Cerberus_01
dialogueLines.text: Cerberus is uninterested in conversing at this time.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Cerberus_01
dialogueLines.text: The hound of hell rests peacefully upon his favored spot.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Cerberus_01
dialogueLines.text: All three of Cerberus the watch-dog's terrifying countenances greet the Prince.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Cerberus_01
dialogueLines.text: The vicious three-faced guard-hound of the gates of death barks out a brief hello.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Cerberus_01
dialogueLines.text: The ever-faithful Cerberus remains resting steadfastly in his spot.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Cerberus_01
dialogueLines.text: Notoriously vicious Cerberus is watching quietly his Master's court.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Cerberus_01
dialogueLines.text: One of the frightful maws of Cerberus busily grooms the other two.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Cerberus_01
dialogueLines.text: The triple-headed monster Cerberus is dutifully sitting there as ever.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Cerberus_01
dialogueLines.text: The many-headed Cerberus is busy thinking of his next savory meal.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Cerberus_01
dialogueLines.text: Cerberus acknowledges the Prince with an affection tempered well with time.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Cerberus_01
dialogueLines.text: The hellish watch-dog snarls at the listless Shades crowding about the court.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Cerberus_01
dialogueLines.text: The much-feared Cerberus licks endlessly at something on the floor.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Cerberus_01
dialogueLines.text: The hound of hell barely regards the Prince, having been sated for the time.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Cerberus_01
dialogueLines.text: The vicious triple-headed Cerberus shows some restraint, for now.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Cerberus_01
dialogueLines.text: Cerberus is too busy living in the lap of luxury to pay much heed.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Cerberus_01
dialogueLines.text: The monster Cerberus sits vigilant atop his bedroll.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Cerberus_01
dialogueLines.text: Each savage maw of Cerberus salivates in anticipation of the next time to feed.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Cerberus_01
dialogueLines.text: Terrifying Cerberus is busy gathering his strength for when next he shall bar the Prince's path.

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: Oof, you showed me back there, boy. With Father on the surface? I know you were just playing, but... go easier on me, next time, will you?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Cerberus_01
dialogueLines.text: The unrepentant monster Cerberus is thoroughly incapable of showing neither restraint nor remorse.

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: Look what I got for you, boy...!

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Cerberus_01
dialogueLines.text: As one of Cerberus' heads receives the Prince's gift, another muzzles something back into his hand.

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: There's a good boy... thought you'd like that!

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Cerberus_01
dialogueLines.text: Cerberus is overjoyed to have received such a delicious treat.

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: Only the very best treats for my best friend, right, boy?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Cerberus_01
dialogueLines.text: Cerberus yawlps in merriment upon receiving such a generous offering.

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: It is my humble duty to oblige my favorite boy with all the treats as I am able to procure.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Cerberus_01
dialogueLines.text: Knowing the watch-dog's never-ending appetite for treats, the Prince obliges him.

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: You like this stuff, don't you, boy? Well, good, because I like sneaking it over to you!

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Cerberus_01
dialogueLines.text: The delectable taste of Nectar is enough to briefly sate even the ferocious appetite of the hound of hell.

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: This Nectar not fancy enough for you, boy, is that it? You've developed quite the delicate palate at your distinguished age!

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Cerberus_01
dialogueLines.text: Though having little appetite remaining for the lightness of Nectar's flavor, the multi-headed monster of the Underworld nevertheless accepts.

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: That's right, boy... Ambrosia, just for you. You're going to love it. Pretty much every creature, living or dead, does!

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Cerberus_01
dialogueLines.text: The six eyes of the hideous hound of hell go wide at once from having received the exquisite gift of rare Olympian Ambrosia. The creature is rather beside himself.

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: Hits the spot, doesn't it, boy? I know you're caught up in the middle of all this and having to weigh your loyalties more than you'd like. I'm sorry we're putting you through this.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Cerberus_01
dialogueLines.text: The Underworld Prince's next attempt to bribe the monstrous triple-headed hellhound into further liking him proves once again successful, as the kingly gift of heavenly Ambrosia is beyond compare.

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: Cerberus, not to get all sentimental on you, but... oh, who am I kidding, look... you've been with me my entire life! I know you're here at home partly because of me, aren't you?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Cerberus_01
dialogueLines.text: The bond between a hound and its master is as hard as adamant. Cerberus, the fearsome monster born of hell, and guardian of its gates, answers firstly to the Prince's father. But it turns out, the Prince may be the next best thing.

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: You're the best boy in the whole world. Even if heads Beta and Gamma don't much care for pets, do they? I've got more than enough lined up for you, though. Count on it.

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: You really ripped the lounge to pieces, boy? I hope you didn't get yourself in too much trouble. I'm sorry to have worried you like that.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Cerberus_01
dialogueLines.text: The guilt-wracked monster Cerberus admits to committing the crime, expressing some remorse for having implicated the incendiary Prince.

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: Hey, did you see, boy? The old lounge is good as new again, you ought to check it out! Just, don't annihilate it like you did last time.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Cerberus_01
dialogueLines.text: Each savage countenance of Cerberus lights up to hear the news, doubtless beginning to assemble stratagems for ravaging the lounging area once more.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Cerberus_01
dialogueLines.text: Infernal watch-dog Cerberus regards the Underworld Prince with mixed emotions, from purest joy to deepest melancholy.

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: You watch over things for me, won't you, boy? You know I'd take you if I could.

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: How you holding up, boy, eating well lately? All three mouths full and happy?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Cerberus_01
dialogueLines.text: Each terrifying maw of the infernal watch-dog Cerberus responds affirmatively, one after the next, as if to reassure the ever-doubting Prince.

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: Take it easy, boy. I'll probably be back here in no time.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Cerberus_01
dialogueLines.text: The watch-dog Cerberus merely sulks upon his supple bedroll.

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: You keep an eye on Father for me, boy. Don't let him sour up everyone's mood.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Cerberus_01
dialogueLines.text: Cerberus promises to do exactly that, in his own way.

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: How's everything been going lately, boy? What's the good word?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Cerberus_01
dialogueLines.text: The Underworld Prince's casual inquiry serves but to frustrate the infernal hound, as none of his three heads possess the gift and curse of speech.

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: Well, look at you, boy! Never seen you quite so perky, relatively speaking of course, big tough guy like yourself. You must have missed Mother just as much as she missed you. Well, you've got plenty of time for catching up on things, now!

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Cerberus_01
dialogueLines.text: The monstrous triple-headed beast of Hades would perhaps be overcome with pure emotion by the joyous circumstances, were he only capable of this.

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: Bet you're happy to see Mother home again, huh, boy? Only wish you could have told me about her before.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Cerberus_01
dialogueLines.text: The hell-born monster Cerberus contentedly ignores the Prince's callous, inconsiderate remark.

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: We're all very proud of you, boy! You were so well-behaved at the feast. I was worried sick one uncle or the other was going to set you off at some point, but you were Patience Incarnate. Thanks for helping show our extended family a good time!

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Cerberus_01
dialogueLines.text: How infernal Cerberus, the hound of hell, managed to maintain civilized behavior throughout the duration of the recent feast remains a mystery to all who attended.

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: Hey, boy, just me again. Here to report it's pretty dangerous out there even without you on guard duty all the time.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Cerberus_01
dialogueLines.text: The venerable hound of hell barely acknowledges the softly-spoken Prince's platitude, knowing full well the Underworld is not as vigilantly guarded as in ages past.

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: Guess what, boy? Made it all the way to Asphodel last time! I fully understand how come you never liked it there. Now to trek on back, I guess. Take care!

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Cerberus_01
dialogueLines.text: Despite the Underworld Prince's optimism, his old companion Cerberus, the multi-headed hound, maintains a somewhat sulky attitude for now.

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: Hey good to see you back here, boy. I'm glad you're back on guard duty! Even if it means having to find a way past you. I know you're just doing your job.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Cerberus_01
dialogueLines.text: The fear-inducing hellhound eyes the Underworld Prince uneasily, his six ferocious eyes each glistening within the dim light of the House.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Cerberus_Field_01
dialogueLines.text: The multi-headed Cerberus stands vigilant before the Underworld's entryway; the tales of what happens to those foolish enough to attempt to pass are far too graphic to describe.

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: So it's really come to this, boy? After everything we've been through. Well then, if this is how it has to be, there's nothing left to say. Except for: On your guard!!

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: Hahaha, no, but seriously, boy. I know why you're here. There has to be a way to make you look the other way. What if I help you with the Satyr problem, would you let me go? Please. I have to find Persephone... my mother. You know what this means to me.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Cerberus_Field_01
dialogueLines.text: After a moment's hesitation and deliberation, the voracious hound of hell barks once, affirmatively, toward the Prince, who sets off in search of something that can sate the monster's appetites.

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: Charon mate? As a longtime customer, I just wanted to take a moment to thank you again for your invaluable service as a part of my repeated attempts at getting out of here. What would I have done without your wares all these many times? Besides dying a lot faster.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Charon_01
dialogueLines.text: Hrrnngghh, grhhh, grhaaaaaaaahhhh...

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: Hi Charon! I finally met Hermes, god of swiftness, travel, bringing souls of the departed to your boat. Calls you his professional associate. You and he, you've been working together for a while, haven't you?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Charon_01
dialogueLines.text: Hrrrrrrnnnnghhhhhhhh...

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: Charon, I always thought Olympians never consorted with chthonic gods such as yourself. But it sounds like Hermes is a close colleague of yours. You wouldn't... happen to know why he's helping me, would you?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Charon_01
dialogueLines.text: Grrrhhhoooaaaaaahhhh...!

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: Charon, Hermes told me that the other Olympians are unaware he's lending his support to me. I'm curious to know what's going on. Thinking of asking Nyx. Figured I'd check with you first.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Charon_01
dialogueLines.text: Mmnnnrraaauuugggghhh!!

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: Hey, Charon mate, wanted to thank you once again for the fine hourglass you gave to me before! It's been very handy with those Wells of yours.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Charon_01
dialogueLines.text: Hheeehhhhhhh....

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: Charon? Unrelated to anything whatsoever, I just wanted to say... I'm very grateful to have your support. You've always done your own thing... I never get to see you at the House. But I know you care! An awful lot. So, cheers.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Charon_01
dialogueLines.text: ...Khhrraaa hhaaaaahhhh...

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: Charon mate, I know you meet an awful lot of dead, and it probably gets very difficult to remember them all, but... I don't suppose the name Skelly means anything to you, does it?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Charon_01
dialogueLines.text: ...Ssss... kkkhhh... rrrrnngghhh... raauuuggghhh!!

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Charon_01
dialogueLines.text: Haaahhhhh....

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Charon_01
dialogueLines.text: Mmmrrrrnnn....

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Charon_01
dialogueLines.text: Hrrnmmmm....

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Charon_01
dialogueLines.text: Khhhrrrrr....

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Charon_01
dialogueLines.text: Heeehhhhhhh....

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Charon_01
dialogueLines.text: Hhrrrrnneehhhh....

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Charon_01
dialogueLines.text: Urrrrrrgggghhh...

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Charon_01
dialogueLines.text: Hrrrnnnhh....

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Charon_01
dialogueLines.text: Nnnnrrrrrhhh....

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Charon_01
dialogueLines.text: Hnnnggghhhh....

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Charon_01
dialogueLines.text: Hrreehhh hohhhhhh....

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Charon_01
dialogueLines.text: Khhrraaa hhaaaaahhhh....

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Charon_01
dialogueLines.text: Nrrrrggghhh....

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Charon_01
dialogueLines.text: Hrrnnaaauugghhh....

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Charon_01
dialogueLines.text: Grrruuuooaahhh....

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Charon_01
dialogueLines.text: Mmmrrnnngghh....

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Charon_01
dialogueLines.text: Rrraaauuuggghhh....

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Charon_01
dialogueLines.text: Hhrrrnn, haahhhhh....

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Charon_01
dialogueLines.text: Urrrnngggghhh....

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Charon_01
dialogueLines.text: Khhrrrnnnnnnggghh....

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Charon_01
dialogueLines.text: Heeehhh, hohhhhh....

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Charon_01
dialogueLines.text: Gguhhhhhh....

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: Why good to see you, Charon mate! Just minding my own business, taking in the sights, and, hey what's that you got, some sort of wares for sale? Then, I'll just have a look around.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Charon_01
dialogueLines.text: Gguhhhhhh....

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: Why good to see you, Charon mate! Just minding my own business, taking in the sights, and, sure is awfully convenient to be running into you like this! I'd all this coin I wanted to get rid of.

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: Charon, you've been such a reassuring sight, I thought I'd offer you a token of my gratitude.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Charon_01
dialogueLines.text: Hrrrhhrooooh, hnnnn....

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: Wait, you don't have to give me something in return, it was a gift! Though, you're insisting, aren't you.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Charon_01
dialogueLines.text: Hohhhh....

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: Glad you like it, mate. Thank you for helping me out with your supply. Even if you fleece me with those prices!

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Charon_01
dialogueLines.text: Hrrrnnnn...?

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: You enjoy that, mate! And, when it comes time to reconsider your price structure, hope you think of me!

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Charon_01
dialogueLines.text: Hrrrn, hhmmm hmmmm....

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: Enjoy it, Charon mate! Don't go thinking I'm just being kind to get you to lower your prices for me, that would be unconscionable!

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Charon_01
dialogueLines.text: Hrrooooohhh....

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: You know, I thought you'd say that, Charon mate, but I insist you take it and enjoy, all right?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Charon_01
dialogueLines.text: Hhhrrrrhh... hhhrrrnnnn... hrrrrrhhh...

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: I'm pleased to have you as a friend as well, Charon. The pleasure's mine.

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: Charon, I realize you have a very busy schedule and certainly needn't keep going out of your way for me like this, so here. Just a small token of my appreciation for you. If you ever want to talk, know that I'm here.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Charon_01
dialogueLines.text: Hrrrnnnnnnnnn...! Thhhrrrrrrrrnnnnnn... kkrrrrrrrrhhh...!

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: Well, look at you, Charon mate, you're looking well! Something's different about you, can't quite put my finger on it. Have you done something with your hair?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Charon_01
dialogueLines.text: Urrraaauuhggghh...

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: Erm, Charon, mate...? I don't suppose you've given any further thought to my proposal to, just, you know... ferry me up to the surface? Seeing as it's not out of your way.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Charon_01
dialogueLines.text: Rhhuuaaauuggghhh....

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: Charon mate, now hypothetically, if I provided you with, say, a thousand coins, would you be willing to give me a ride in your beautiful boat? Upriver, I should say, specifically.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Charon_01
dialogueLines.text: Nrrnnhhh, hnnnn, hrrnn, hrrrrrnnn....

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: Charon, mate? Wanted to thank you very, very much for bringing Mother and me home. Your boat's quite comfortable, you know! Though, I suppose you won't be making a habit of giving me a ride, will you?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Charon_01
dialogueLines.text: Hrrrhhhaaaaauuugghhh...!

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: Charon, as I know you serve the realm, I just wanted to say I look forward to continuing to work with you in a somewhat more official capacity. I'll still be breaking out of here like always, but... well, you've probably heard.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Charon_01
dialogueLines.text: Nnnrrraaaauggghhhh....

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: Hope you enjoyed the feast with the Olympians, Charon! We didn't have much of a chance to chat, though it looked like you and Hermes were having a good time.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Charon_01
dialogueLines.text: Krrhh, hrraaaahhh...!

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: No hard feelings about that whole battle-to-the-death thing last time, right, Charon? Especially since you absolutely demolished me. I was just messing with you, you know that, right?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Charon_01
dialogueLines.text: Nrraaauuuggghhh...!

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: Hey Charon, you're not angry with me after we had it out last time, are you, I mean... I figured since you gave me one of your Loyalty Cards, must mean everything's fine, but... I'm... just making sure.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Charon_01
dialogueLines.text: Krrr, krraauuuggghhhh...!

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: Hey, Charon. If there happens to be something really horrible awaiting me beyond that door there, then... give me a sign, won't you?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Charon_01
dialogueLines.text: Hhhaaaaaaahhh....

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: Care to provide me any insight as to just what sort of hideous monstrosity awaits me over on the next infernal island, Charon mate?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Charon_01
dialogueLines.text: Hheeehhhhhhh....

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: Got to say, you are a sight for sore eyes, there, Charon! So, uh... looks like you're selling, is that it? You come to help me out?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Charon_01
dialogueLines.text: Uunngggghhhh....

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: Hey good to see you out here, mate! And looks as though you've got some fine wares up for sale, don't you?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Charon_01
dialogueLines.text: Urrrnngghhhhh....

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Charon_01
dialogueLines.text: Haaahhhhhhhhh....

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: Fine day to you as well, there, mate! Now let's have a look at your supply, shall we?

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: You must have a lot of stops around these parts, don't you, Charon mate. Most of the dead are here in Asphodel, correct?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Charon_01
dialogueLines.text: Nnnnrrrrrhhh....

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: Hey, Charon, I'm in Asphodel now, aren't I? I'd always heard this was some flower-covered meadow type place, not such a... terrifying hellscape.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Charon_01
dialogueLines.text: Hhhhaaaaaaahhh....

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: You know Achilles reasonably well, don't you, Dusa? Seems like he's always stuck there in that hall. At least you get to fly around. Sometimes I think he wishes he was someplace else.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Dusa_01
dialogueLines.text: There has to be a way to help him, Prince! He's been so loyal to this House, and he has to be your truest friend of everybody here. But he's the pity-party type. He needs someone to get him out of this!

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: I hope it's not been too much, having to care for Cerberus, is it, Dusa? He isn't quite as fearsome as he seems. At least, that goes for two heads out of three!

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Dusa_01
dialogueLines.text: Oh, Cerberus is a good boy, he's fine! One of the highlights of the job, for sure. We really hit it off after he finally realized I'm not a ball! So, I've been feeding him, grooming him, changing his water dish...

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: ...Cleaning up after him...?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Dusa_01
dialogueLines.text: Most definitely, yes. If you'll excuse me, by the way? He's got to go. I mean! I've got to go! I mean!! You know what I mean, bye!

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Dusa_01
dialogueLines.text: Oh hey, you brought some fish, could smell it all the way from down the hall, that's great!! The Head Chef can take it off your hands, should be right in the back of the lounge like always! Thanks for helping us out, Prince!

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: Erm, Ms. Dusa, I ran into hostile gorgons that resembled you out there in Asphodel. Resembled only in the vaguest sense, I mean, they lacked your charm or social graces, anyway. I just, hope you are not related, since I sort of... you know.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Dusa_01
dialogueLines.text: Oh, hahaha, those old things? They're relatives just in the loosest sense, like... say you had some nasty, almost mindless siblings born entirely from your own blood, something like that? Do you have any idea how hard it was to get a steady job because of them?

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: So I have your permission to continue hacking down such gorgons with impunity?

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: I had another question about your relationship to other gorgons, Dusa. So... I notice you're not much for hissing, snakelike noises when you speak, and so on? Is that some sort of special gorgon language or something?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Dusa_01
dialogueLines.text: No, um, not exactly, Prince! It's what it sounds like, hrrrsssss!! Just, scary hissing, really, takes some practice not to sound like that with a forked tongue! I really wanted to fit in. Hey, how about you? Always wondered how come you have a surface accent. Weren't you born here?

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: Oh, wait, me? I never gave it any thought. I... guess it's from my father. He insisted that he teach me how to speak, himself. Maybe it's in the blood.

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: Hey, Dusa, this is kind of personal, and you don't have to answer, but I have to know. So... did you... use to have a body, and all that? Because, I've heard of gorgons, well... that you had wings, and such?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Dusa_01
dialogueLines.text: Um, Prince, so, look. That's something very private that you're asking, and... I know you don't mean any harm, but it just isn't something I discuss with anyone, OK? I'm who I am. Besides! As you can see, I don't need wings to fly!

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: Ms. Dusa, I, erm, wanted to apologize for when I pried about your past. I just wanted to get to know you better, but... I was insensitive, and I'm sorry.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Dusa_01
dialogueLines.text: Oh, Your Highness, Zagreus, come on! Don't even worry about it! I'd already forgotten, really no big deal. It's just, the past me, it's as though... it wasn't even me. This is me, now! I'm glad I'm here.

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: You know, it must be pretty handy, being able to turn somebody into stone. Is that something you can just do whenever you please, like... I don't know, sticking out your tongue or something?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Dusa_01
dialogueLines.text: Not quite as easy as all that, but not so hard! You have to get yourself pretty worked up, and picture what you're seeing like a... well, um, like a picture, really, and then, fwoop! Stone statue. It's funny, I used to think everybody could do it!

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: Dusa, in my father's chambers, he... he has a bedside portrait of somebody. Is... is she someone with whom you are familiar?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Dusa_01
dialogueLines.text: Oh, Prince, I'm not permitted in your father's chambers, are you kidding me? I'd ask you what it's like in there, but... I don't think I'm supposed to know!

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: Do you have to work with Hypnos pretty closely as part of your job, Dusa? Feels like I've been running into him a lot lately.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Dusa_01
dialogueLines.text: Mister Hypnos, I have trouble getting through to him, I think? He gives me plenty of advice, it's just, it isn't really why I talk to him. I... only talk to him when I don't have a lot of other choice, I guess.

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: Dusa, wherever did you get that Fidi Companion you gave me? You know how much that thing is worth? To me at least, I don't know who collects those anymore, but I sure do!

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Dusa_01
dialogueLines.text: Oh! I'm so glad you like her, Prince! I could think of no one better to take care of her, and best of all... she'll remind you of me! But as for where I got her, you're not going to believe it... can you guess?

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: What, wait, you don't mean... no, not from Nyx, herself? Did you, wait, Dusa... you didn't, how to put this, borrow her from Nyx, did you?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Dusa_01
dialogueLines.text: Haha, no! Happen to value my job, here, among other aspects of my well-being, but... after the lounge assignment, Nyx herself commended me, and gave me Fidi as a nice reward! Although, she probably knew I'd just end up giving her to you!

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: Have to tell you, Dusa, your job seems really stressful, here. It's a big House! You have to tend to the entire thing?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Dusa_01
dialogueLines.text: Oh! Well, most of it isn't so bad at all, but... ever since Cerberus tore up the lounge, I... guess it's been a little hectic, getting everything back to normal like before!

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: I'm not sure it was ever normal around here, but... I'm sorry I created work for you. Cerberus tore the place apart because of me. Though, maybe I can help you patch things up?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Dusa_01
dialogueLines.text: Don't say that, Prince! Cerberus knows better, and besides, you have your job to do, don't you? And I have mine! And I will do my very best at it, just wait and see!

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: Hey Dusa, something I was wondering, and it's just curiosity, mind you, but... is there somebody else...? I mean, somebody special, whom you're close with, or would like to be. Romantically, I mean. If it's all right to ask.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Dusa_01
dialogueLines.text: Oh, you can ask me anything! But, um, somebody else romantically, not really, no? It's not really my thing? It doesn't mean I don't have feelings for you, though! You know that, right? I wouldn't drop what I'm doing and throw myself at danger for just anyone, you know!

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: I definitely know. There's seven kinds of love, or so, isn't that right? It isn't something that's taught extensively down here, though, so... there's still a lot for me to learn, I guess.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Dusa_01
dialogueLines.text: Wow, I never thought of it as love until you said it there just now, Prince, but you're right! I love you, Zagreus! Hah, haha, that feels good to come right out and say, you know? OK, well, bye!

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Dusa_01
dialogueLines.text: Hey, Prince, is-is it really true? About Miss Meg and you, I mean, that you're, you know, um, I? You know!! Because, if so, then I want you to know, I'm just so happy for the two of you! That's wonderful, you two!! I'm so glad!

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: Erm, thank you, Dusa, I know you and Miss Meg are close, so, I am grateful that we have your blessings. Though, we're kind of... not out in the open with it, necessarily, so if you please, just... keep it quiet for us, would you?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Dusa_01
dialogueLines.text: Oh, you better believe it, I will tell nobody whatsoever, no! I'll just be very, very happy to myself! All right, then! Bye!

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Dusa_01
dialogueLines.text: Oh, Prince, I heard the news about Master Achilles, that's so great! I'm going to miss him being here as often as he used to, but I'm just so happy for him, and for you!

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: Me too, Dusa. The happy-for-him part, at any rate! He's a good friend, and he deserved much better than to be stuck here.

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: Dusa, there is something you should know about me. Me and Nyx, that is. She's not my real mother...! My real mother's called Persephone. She's out there, somewhere. On the surface. That's why I left. Wait, just a moment, are you laughing?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Dusa_01
dialogueLines.text: Haha, oh, uh, no, it's just, uhh... I'm really, really, really sorry, I can't help it, I, um, didn't want to interrupt, because, I kind of knew all that, already? Um, meaning, I had heard about it, everybody talks, you know, from time to time? But I just... really hope you find her soon!

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: Dusa, there is something you should know about me. Me and Nyx, that is. She's not my real mother...! My real mother's called Persephone. She's out there, somewhere. On the surface. That's why I left. Wait, just a moment, are you laughing?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Dusa_01
dialogueLines.text: Haha, oh, uh, no, it's just, uhh... I'm really, really, really sorry, I can't help it, I, um, didn't want to interrupt, because, I kind of knew all that, already? Um, meaning, I had heard about it, everybody talks, you know, from time to time? But I just... really hope you find her soon!

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: I trust you not to mention this to everybody in the House, but... I have. Found her, that is! I'm... trying to figure out what happens next. Can't really talk about it yet, so... bear with me?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Dusa_01
dialogueLines.text: Whooa, that is exciting, what's she really like? Is she tall? Does she have your eyes? Where does she live? What does she do? Does she have a job? What color is her hair? Is she, you know, is she with anybody?

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: Ungh, Dusa... it's a little much out there sometimes, I have to say. Different stresses than in here, but still.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Dusa_01
dialogueLines.text: Oh don't you lose hope now, Prince Zagreus! There isn't anything you couldn't handle, I just know! You're every bit as tough as your father, except, you're nice! And you never back down from anything. You can do this!

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: ...I don't know that anyone can do this. No escape from the Underworld and all that. Though, thanks for cheering me up.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Dusa_01
dialogueLines.text: I'm not just saying it, I mean it, Prince! Look at how far you've come! Practically everybody in this House believes in you! Even the Wretched Broker and the Head Chef! Oh, I've asked them! Everybody thinks you're going to succeed!

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: Can I ask you a couple of purely figurative questions here, Dusa? First, what have I gotten myself into...? And second, what am I going to do...?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Dusa_01
dialogueLines.text: Believe me, I know the feeling, being in over my head! Though, if I can find my way out of my messes, then I know, I know, you definitely can! I don't know what you ought to do, but I am always here to listen.

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: Dusa, hey, I wanted to congratulate you! Saw you were chosen as the featured servant of the House, getting your portrait there, right in the lounge for all to see! That's quite an honor!

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Dusa_01
dialogueLines.text: Oh, haha, it's really nothing, Prince, something that anybody could have done really, as long as they were fired up to do their very, very best, all the time, with a positive attitude, and without worrying too much about the overwhelming amount of work that still needs to be done!

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Dusa_01
dialogueLines.text: Hey, Prince, I saw that Orpheus, he got some time away, did you do that? He's visiting his muse, that's what he said! That's so exciting, I mean, they've been separated the whole time they've been dead!

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: They have a lot of catching up to do, for sure. Though I think they're going to be all right.

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: Say, Dusa, in your work around the House, you wouldn't happen to have run into a Skelly character, out in the courtyard, there, have you?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Dusa_01
dialogueLines.text: Oh! Not really, um, because, my work is in the House itself, and, well, the courtyard, technically, it's kind of right outside, so, I'm supposed to stay inside the walls. But, I think I know who you mean! I've seen him, standing there just past your chamber doors. Why, is he OK?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Dusa_01
dialogueLines.text: Hey, I saw you and Thanatos recently, Prince! You two, if you don't mind me saying, you are quite the pair, I mean, you ought to see yourselves! You're, whew...! Makes me a little woozy just remembering it now! Well, up I go, then, bye!

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: Dusa, I don't mean to upset you but, I'm worried you're going to burn yourself out if you keep pushing yourself past your limits... are you sure you don't need a break?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Dusa_01
dialogueLines.text: I, I'm sure, Prince. This is my chance! I'm custodian of the House of Hades! I need to keep doing my best right now. Thanks so much for the Nectar! Bye!

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: So, what even happened when you lost your job, Dusa? Did you have someplace else to stay? Was everything all right?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Dusa_01
dialogueLines.text: You know, it's funny, after I was fired... at first, I just couldn't believe it, I... felt lost! But, after a few days or nights, it's like, it opened up my eyes, you know? Suddenly, I had all this time to myself, and... what I realized was, I was... so, so tired. I think I really needed the break.

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: I'm surprised you wanted to come back to work after something like that. Wouldn't you be totally fed up? Burned out on the whole thing?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Dusa_01
dialogueLines.text: I love this work too much to get burned out, Prince. But that's me. I know there's more to being alive or being dead than just your work, but... my work happens to be important to me. And besides... if I don't push myself from time to time, how can I ever know my limits, right?

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: But if you go too far beyond your limits, things can get bad. Believe me. I mean, you almost lost the job you care so much about.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Dusa_01
dialogueLines.text: You're right, I... need to be more careful, not to push myself too hard, too often. Hey, could I ask a favor? Would you tell me if I go too far with work again?

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: How's work lately, Dusa? You seem to have a handle on your new responsibilities, or so I hear from Nyx.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Dusa_01
dialogueLines.text: Oh yes, it's going pretty well, so far, I think! I have some new accounting duties, though I've parceled everything, and got a budget for a little extra help. It's funny, I-I should be busier than ever, but... I have more time to spare, lately, and things are good, I guess?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Dusa_01
dialogueLines.text: I, I, I, oh no, I, just forgot something, I, I have to go!

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Dusa_01
dialogueLines.text: Oh, um, Your Highness, I was just, making the rounds again!!

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Dusa_01
dialogueLines.text: Um, everything is spick and span like always, Your Highness!!

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Dusa_01
dialogueLines.text: Y-you'd tell me if I did anything wrong, wouldn't you, Prince?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Dusa_01
dialogueLines.text: ...dusted the halls, dusted the library, dusted the sconces, dusted the doorknobs, dusted the chairs...

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Dusa_01
dialogueLines.text: ...let's see, I cleaned the mantle, cleaned the rafters, cleaned the skulls, cleaned the bar-room...

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Dusa_01
dialogueLines.text: Oh, um, hi! Hello, Your Highness, nice to see you! Thanks!

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Dusa_01
dialogueLines.text: I'll make sure everything is taken care of!

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Dusa_01
dialogueLines.text: Oh, hahaha, thanks for talking to me, Prince, I have to go!

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Dusa_01
dialogueLines.text: Hi Prince, I fed Cerberus already, all three heads!

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Dusa_01
dialogueLines.text: Wha, hah-haha, oh, you startled me, Your Highness...!

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Dusa_01
dialogueLines.text: I'm just keeping busy busy busy here like always, Prince!

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Dusa_01
dialogueLines.text: I, I, I, I'm sorry that you died again out there, Your Highness...!

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Dusa_01
dialogueLines.text: Oh, um, hi! Is... everything all right, there, Prince?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Dusa_01
dialogueLines.text: Nothing gets dirty around here while I am on the job, Your Highness...!

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Dusa_01
dialogueLines.text: Don't worry, Prince! I'll keep an eye on things!

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Dusa_01
dialogueLines.text: You can count on me, Your Highness!

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Dusa_01
dialogueLines.text: Anything I can do for you right now, or, maybe later...?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Dusa_01
dialogueLines.text: ...let's see, I did the dishes, fed the heads, cleaned the cupboards, cleared the pool...

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Dusa_01
dialogueLines.text: Oh, Prince, um, I'm supposed to be at work, so, I can't talk, OK?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Dusa_01
dialogueLines.text: Excuse me, sorry, I just have to file this report, it's very late!

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Dusa_01
dialogueLines.text: I, I, I have everything here well under control, Highness!!

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Dusa_01
dialogueLines.text: What, is something wrong, Prince? I'll look into it, right now, up there!!

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Dusa_01
dialogueLines.text: Am I in trouble for something again, or, no...?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Dusa_01
dialogueLines.text: I'm always at 110%, hah, hahah!

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Dusa_01
dialogueLines.text: Um, I, I, I, I'm sorry, I, I better get back to my cleaning...!

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Dusa_01
dialogueLines.text: Oh, wow! I'm so sorry, Prince, I'm just behind already on my daily chores!

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Dusa_01
dialogueLines.text: Ah! Haha, got me again, Prince, sneaking up on me like that!

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Dusa_01
dialogueLines.text: You'll get them next time, Prince, I'm sure of it! I think?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Dusa_01
dialogueLines.text: Just making sure the lounge is squeaky clean!

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Dusa_01
dialogueLines.text: This is going to be the best lounge in all the Underworld!

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Dusa_01
dialogueLines.text: You let me know if you see any dust around here, won't you, Prince?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Dusa_01
dialogueLines.text: You rest assured I'm not even remotely tired, Prince!

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Dusa_01
dialogueLines.text: Please count on me for all your household needs!

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Dusa_01
dialogueLines.text: I'll make sure Cerberus has everything he needs while you're out!

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Dusa_01
dialogueLines.text: Don't tell me I'm in trouble, am I, Prince?!

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Dusa_01
dialogueLines.text: Just thought I'd mention, I, I really love this job!!

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Dusa_01
dialogueLines.text: ...stocked the store room, checked the locks, cleaned the tiles, watered the trees...

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Dusa_01
dialogueLines.text: ...checked the contracts, cleared the ledgers, set the tables, washed the windows...

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Dusa_01
dialogueLines.text: ...sorted the files, gathered the feedback, updated the schedule, sent the report...

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Dusa_01
dialogueLines.text: Now, here you go, Cerbie! And, um, don't eat me, please?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Dusa_01
dialogueLines.text: Um, here you go, boy! Triple order, just the way you like it!

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Dusa_01
dialogueLines.text: ...Just, please be careful where you go after you eat all this, this time, OK, Cerbie?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Dusa_01
dialogueLines.text: Oh, come on, Cerbie, hurry up and eat! This stuff can't be that bad! I mean... can it?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_FurySister_01
dialogueLines.text: Not now, Zag. Private meeting.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Dusa_01
dialogueLines.text: Don't work yourself too hard, OK, Highness?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Dusa_01
dialogueLines.text: Please do remember to take breaks, OK?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_FurySister_01
dialogueLines.text: We'll have to chat some other time, here, Zag.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Dusa_01
dialogueLines.text: You get rest from time to time, for me, OK?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Dusa_01
dialogueLines.text: I hope you get to see Miss Meg out there next time!

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Dusa_01
dialogueLines.text: I hope you'll get to see Thanatos again soon!

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_FurySister_01
dialogueLines.text: Can't you see we're in the middle of a conversation, Zagreus?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_FurySister_01
dialogueLines.text: Excuse us, Zagreus, we're in the middle of a meeting here.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_FurySister_01
dialogueLines.text: This particular discussion doesn't concern you, Zag.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_FurySister_01
dialogueLines.text: ...Sometimes, though, Dusa, he's not the best at taking hints...

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: Erm, Ms. Dusa, I wanted to, uh, cordially invite you to an outing in the lounge? Remember when you asked me about a drink?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Dusa_01
dialogueLines.text: Aaah! Oh, oh, oh, really, what you mean, right now, you're... I... I guess I'm overdue to take a break...

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Dusa_01
dialogueLines.text: ...And, well, since I don't exactly fit the mold of your traditional gorgon, I mean, I have no body for example, I had trouble finding work! That is until Lord Hades took me in, and then, I met you, and, well, I just love this job!!

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: Oh! I can definitely relate at least to some of that.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Dusa_01
dialogueLines.text: <Sigh> I, I, I, thanks, Your Highness. Prince. Prince Zagreus!

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Dusa_01
dialogueLines.text: Um, P-Prince, the way you look so sad sometimes, when you come back in here, out of the Pool of Styx? Is everything OK? Because if there was something I could do...

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: Oh, it's nothing for you to be concerned about, Dusa. I'm just having a harder time fighting my blasted way out of this place than I anticipated. If you'll excuse my language.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Dusa_01
dialogueLines.text: Oh, I... this is silly, but, I'd no idea anybody could beat you, I mean, the Prince? Greater than the mighty Achilles, more cunning than his father, faster than Hermes! But... you're just struggling, like me?

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: I am. And that stuff you heard about me isn't true.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Dusa_01
dialogueLines.text: Oh! The Prince himself, you, want to talk to me...? Me?! Oh, wow, oh wow!!

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: I, uh, just wanted to say, the lounge looks wonderful, as good as new. Better! Hope it's not been too much trouble patching up.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Dusa_01
dialogueLines.text: I, I, I, aah, I have to clean the rafters, I...!

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Dusa_01
dialogueLines.text: Oh! The Prince himself, you, want to talk to me...? Me?! Oh, wow, oh wow!!

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: I, uh, just wanted to say, don't bother picking up my room today. I'm headed out for a while.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Dusa_01
dialogueLines.text: I, I, I, aah, I have to clean the rafters, I...!

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: Erm, Ms. Dusa? I wanted you to have this. It's nothing really, just... for your hard work.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Dusa_01
dialogueLines.text: P-P-Prince, I'm really grateful for this, really, and I thought, I, kind of got something for you...?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Dusa_01
dialogueLines.text: Wh-whaaaa, b-but, there must be some mistake, I, Prince, you're giving this to me...?

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: I want you to know we all appreciate you, Dusa. Don't work yourself too hard on my account, OK?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Dusa_01
dialogueLines.text: Ohh, oh, oh, oh my, Your Highness, are you... are you really sure you want me to have this?

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: I really am, Dusa. I hope you enjoy it.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Dusa_01
dialogueLines.text: B-b-but, Prince, I, don't know what to say, I... are you joking around with me, maybe...?

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: Oh, I am deadly serious, Dusa, honest. It's for you.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Dusa_01
dialogueLines.text: Oh no, no no no, Your Highness, are you, really giving this to me?

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: Yes, yes I am, Ms. Dusa, and I hope you enjoy it. Be seeing you, OK?

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: Dusa, you must work harder than anyone in this House, keeping everything so neat and orderly. I don't think it should be thankless work, so, thank you! And I'd like for you to have this.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Dusa_01
dialogueLines.text: Oh, Prince, wow, thank you! Thanks so much!! You've been so generous, I... I think I'm speechless! No, can't think of anything to say right now!

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: Oh, you said it all! Don't mean to put you on the spot or anything. I'm just glad we've had a chance to get to know each other.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Dusa_01
dialogueLines.text: I... I'm glad we've had a chance to know each other, too. Thanks for the Nectar! I can tell it's really, really good! Um, OK, bye!

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: Hey Dusa? Here's some more of this really, really good Nectar for you! Please keep up the wonderful work, and more importantly, keep being your wonderful self.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Dusa_01
dialogueLines.text: I, I, I, I'm really very flattered, Prince, but, I've just been so busy, lately, that I haven't even had a chance to try the last bottle you gave me, so... you want this back, maybe for someone else?

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: Please, it's yours! Besides, you must have some time off coming up when you can enjoy it, don't you?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Dusa_01
dialogueLines.text: Oh! I mean, maybe I do? I think maybe I do, except, nobody's told me anything about that, and besides, there's so much work to do around the House still, I just can't leave everything like this!!

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: Hey, Dusa? Congratulations on completing your assignment with the lounge. Look at this place! It's better than ever. All thanks to your effort and dedication. Here, this is for you. Something special for the occasion.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Dusa_01
dialogueLines.text: Whoa, I, this, this is Ambrosia, Prince! But, this is for gods...! This isn't for... someone like me? But, wait, wait wait, no, here, I'm going to do it, I am going to do it, here, take this!! I got it, just for you, so, take it, please! Farewell!!

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: Ambrosia isn't only for the gods, you know, Dusa. It's for anyone we really care about. So, here. Please, enjoy this, or... just add it to the lounge's stash. Whatever suits you.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Dusa_01
dialogueLines.text: Oh, I, but, you... really mean that, huh...? But, you're the Prince himself, and I'm just... look, we both know I am just a floating monster head. And a custodian. I just don't understand. Why keep on being nice to me, like this? I thought you'd never even talk to me...

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: You're so much more than what you said. I wish you could see that. If only everybody in this House had your spirit.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Dusa_01
dialogueLines.text: Ah. If only everybody in this House had yours, Prince Zagreus. You've been so kind to me, and I'm glad we've had a chance to get to know each other more. It's really nice, sometimes... knowing somebody really cares about me here?

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: It should be more than sometimes that you feel that way, as far as I'm concerned. I just... I've never met anybody like you before. My father always told me not to smile quite as much, but... you're like me.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Dusa_01
