dialogueLines.text: I want nothing to do with them, correct. My hasty nephew assists Charon out of necessity, alone. Not even Thanatos is able to keep up with the rate at which mortals expire and must then cross our borders.

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: Want me to pitch in? Just give me your blessing and I'll head straight for the surface.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hades_01
dialogueLines.text: Hahaha, no. I think not, boy. You are born of this realm. The surface is a wind-swept waste. You don't know how good you have it here.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hades_01
dialogueLines.text: Ah, verify something for me, here, boy. According to the latest reports... you attempted unsuccessfully to shoplift from Charon, himself? And were consequently slain? Utterly ignoble end, even for you.

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: Erm, I was borrowing, OK? Not shoplifting. Why didn't anybody ever tell me Charon was such a monster? I thought the boating mercantile thing made for a peaceful life.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hades_01
dialogueLines.text: You incur the wrath of ancient chthonic gods, and then are surprised to be destroyed by them? Have you learned nothing, after all this time?

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: Greetings, Father. Ran into Lady Demeter out there, on my last outing. Or should I say, my grandmother? What's she to you, then, ex-mother-in-law? Source of abject terror? Hard keeping track. Our family's rather complicated.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hades_01
dialogueLines.text: Do not invoke her name inside my House, you hear me, boy? You do not know at all what she is like. Though I can see why you two hit it off. She hated her father. Though understandably, as he was worse even than mine. Even than yours.

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: Father, I met an oak nymph called Eurydice. You know her name, I think. Is it really true what happened between her and Orpheus? They say you almost let her out, so why not me?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hades_01
dialogueLines.text: Pah! Orpheus is a talented musician, and a fool. I knew he would never get his beloved wife out of here. There's no escape for her, or anyone, including you.

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: So you tricked him. Or maybe offered him a deal in bad faith. No matter. What's it going to take to waive the separation order between them?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hades_01
dialogueLines.text: That's non-negotiable, boy. Waiving a separation order! That's a veritable fortune in dues alone. Off the table. Now, go mind your own affairs, and leave the waiving and the ordering to me.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hades_01
dialogueLines.text: Back to your senses, then, boy? The Pact of Punishment is not something one ought to trifle with unknowingly, in case you've yet to notice.

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: Father... the Pact... you were so blasted strong. I had no idea! All those other times we fought... I can't believe that you were holding back. You were desperate to stop me, you could have!

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hades_01
dialogueLines.text: We are bound by the Fates to this realm. It is ours, and we belong to it, in turn. And... I did not hold back against you. Rather... I had forgotten my old strength. Some of it since returned to me.

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: Must have been all our vicious battles to the death? Or maybe it's from Mother being back in your life?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hades_01
dialogueLines.text: Pointless to even ask. What motivates us is a complicated thing. But if you long to face me with the power I once held during my own youth... you have available to you the means.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hades_01
dialogueLines.text: Urgh... come, get your gloating well out of the way, so I can get back to this parchmentwork. It piled up, in my prolonged absence!

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: Not here to gloat, Father. Well fought back there. I can't believe I got you.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hades_01
dialogueLines.text: ...Nor I. Next time, the Fates may not decide to favor you like that. If there shall be a next time.

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: Megaera and her sisters ganged up on me that time. I didn't think anyone could talk her into working with Alecto and Tisiphone again. Or even be in the same chamber.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hades_01
dialogueLines.text: I can be quite persuasive under certain circumstances, boy. Though my advice to you, not that you'll take it, is not to discuss the matter openly. Megaera is a professional; but she has made her feelings on the matter clear.

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: Who's that sniveling shade to your immediate left, there, Father? Already found someone new to do the busywork. And here I thought I was utterly indispensable.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hades_01
dialogueLines.text: That shade, boy, is my newly designated contractor; one whose responsibility is to work on this House. Though, who shall pay for everything my subjects here demand, I do not know.

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: You're trying to guilt me into funding the exorbitant furnishings you desire.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hades_01
dialogueLines.text: Perhaps once we are finished with repairs for all the carnage you have sown throughout my realm, then we might finally have time for all the needed renovations that have piled up.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hades_01
dialogueLines.text: You have been keeping our contractor rather busy, I must say. Commissioning these frivolous projects around the House with the wealth you wrest from my domain. What are you hoping to achieve?

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: Oh, you know, just giving back to the community. Figure if I'm to keep getting dragged back here it might as well be somewhat livable.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hades_01
dialogueLines.text: Livable! When last I checked, making the Underworld livable was not among our top priorities. Just remember, in your brazenness, whose House this is.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hades_01
dialogueLines.text: You have been keeping our contractor rather busy, I must say. Commissioning these frivolous projects around the House with the wealth you wrest from my domain. What are you hoping to achieve?

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: Oh, you know, just giving back to the community. Figure if I'm to keep getting dragged back here it might as well be somewhat livable.

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: There's quite a lot of renovating to be done, isn't there, Father. Your House Contractor there has shown a wide variety of pending jobs.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hades_01
dialogueLines.text: I've ordered Nyx to supervise that list. Entirely her problem, boy. And likely yours. How does it feel, knowing that your rampages create the need for so much maintenance?

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: Feels pretty good, now that you mention it. Keeps everybody occupied, no?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hades_01
dialogueLines.text: Be certain that you take appropriate precautions with the Sigil of the Dead that you now bear. Should some misfortune come to it through negligence, it cannot be replaced.

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: I can't imagine what I'd do without your blessing, Father! But, thank you again. I don't often inherit Fate-appointed honors, and will take good care of it. Though... what if the Olympians catch on? Wouldn't do for everybody knowing that you're aiding me even a little, right?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hades_01
dialogueLines.text: Correct. It would not do, at all. Fortunately, the power of the Unseen can remain well-hidden as it pleases. What is it your instructor always says?

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: ...Fear is for the weak?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hades_01
dialogueLines.text: So, you had a run-in with the Minotaur out in Elysium, did you? Perhaps the only soul within this realm more bull-headed than you.

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: How long have you been waiting to use that line, Father? Wouldn't put it past you to have sent the Bull of Minos to Elysium entirely so we could have this conversation now.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hades_01
dialogueLines.text: You give me too much credit, boy. The Minotaur resides where he resides because of an entirely different arrangement.

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: He's working for you, isn't he? You think he can stop me.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hades_01
dialogueLines.text: Most in Elysium jump at the opportunity to do my bidding. Not that you know what that is like. Now go, see how you fare against the bull.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hades_01
dialogueLines.text: She is gone, boy. She is gone. You are never going to locate her. I thought you would have come to that conclusion for yourself, by now. But apparently it must be said.

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: You mean Mother. How would you even know? You said reaching the surface was impossible as well, and I'm in striking distance.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hades_01
dialogueLines.text: This is my final warning to you, boy... to cease this mindless quest. Or else, the consequences shall be worse than what you have experienced thus far.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hades_01
dialogueLines.text: You altered one of my own pacts, without so much as seeking my consent... undoing terms that were agreed upon. Do you intend to undermine everything in this House, boy? Or only me?

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: Only you, Father. Good thing I've inherited some of your Fate-given authority, being your son and all. Otherwise some of your most loyal subjects would be bound by exploitive terms for eternity.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hades_01
dialogueLines.text: Terms that they chose, themselves! Terms to the benefit of this House. What if Achilles were to leave his post for the green pastures of Elysium, what then?

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: You could find a replacement? But it's a pointless question anyway, because he's here! He isn't leaving. He might even do better at his job, now that he has increased ability to step away from it.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hades_01
dialogueLines.text: A naive hope, at best. The more you step away from your responsibilities, the less you shall want anything to do with them.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hades_01
dialogueLines.text: I know what you did. Stay out of Nyx's affairs next time, boy. She is needed here! Not wallowing in the primordial dark.

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: You're just angry because some of us still want to have anything to do with their parents.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hades_01
dialogueLines.text: Parents, pah! You know nothing of what parents are capable of. Conspire all you like against my wishes, I care not. Get out.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hades_01
dialogueLines.text: So help me, boy, I cannot fathom why you would have bought back the authority to enter the administrative chamber once again.

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: I knew you thought I'd never do it under any circumstances, Father. That seemed reason enough. Though, fear not, I've no plans to reapply for a position there.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hades_01
dialogueLines.text: That does alleviate a deeply rooted fear of mine, in fact. The scene you caused in your brief time there yet remains a widely talked-about event among the shades who toil for me still.

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: You know, Father! I must say ransacking your domain beats having to toil endlessly in the administrative chamber. Far less painful, really.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hades_01
dialogueLines.text: Hah! Is that your way of asking me for your old job back, boy? You were a scourge upon every administrative duty to which you were assigned.

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: Yes, please, Father, give me back my old, wretched job, won't you?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hades_01
dialogueLines.text: Privileges are earned not begged for, boy. Unless you plan to reimburse this House for all the income that we lost because of you, then you shall not so much as set foot in the administrative chamber, again.

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: How much is the reimbursement fee, anyway?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hades_01
dialogueLines.text: Whichever exorbitant amount the House Contractor has on record, now leave me be!

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hades_01
dialogueLines.text: One thing, before you depart again, boy. Until the Queen's intentions with Olympus come to pass... not a word to anyone about her presence, here. Do you understand?

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: I do. But she won't have to hide forever.

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: Father... all this stuff with the Olympians... will you just tell me what's going on, for once? How did Mother come to join you here? What's your problem with Uncle Zeus?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hades_01
dialogueLines.text: I thought she already told you...! Urgh. Come with me. Now.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hades_01
dialogueLines.text: ...Swear to me that you shall never repeat what you are about to hear. Swear it!

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: All right, all right, I swear. I swear, in your own name, I'll keep what you're about to say between us.... So?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hades_01
dialogueLines.text: So... you had it mostly right, before. After I inherited the Underworld... your Uncle Zeus felt sympathy for me, he says. Your mother, he knew she was unhappy on Olympus. And... he knew I was drawn to her. So what did he do? He brought her here. Gave her to me as a gift!

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: You weren't expecting a gift like that. But she didn't want to return to Olympus, and you liked her, so you didn't send her away. And you're angry with Zeus for putting you in that position? Cut ties with him?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hades_01
dialogueLines.text: Yes!! The blasted fool, with all his flights of fancy! He does whatever he wants! He could have achieved a similar outcome in a limitless number of reasonable ways... rather than resort to what was tantamount to kidnapping! Even if she wanted to leave!

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: And now if Demeter and the others found all this out, you and Mother... even Zeus would be in a bad spot, wouldn't he? He must have a temper on him to match yours.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hades_01
dialogueLines.text: You know nothing of tempers if mine is your frame of reference, Zagreus. Now, enough of this! You know the rest. Remember your oath.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hades_01
dialogueLines.text: I understand you have attempted to persuade our court musician to resume his craft. That is among the relatively useful tasks to which you have applied yourself of late.

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: I can't be completely sure but, what you said just now I think contained some of the component pieces of a compliment?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hades_01
dialogueLines.text: I'm certain that your boasts and twists of words shall soon inspire him to greater acts of compositional superiority, one of these days or nights.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hades_01
dialogueLines.text: I had a feeling that our court musician would eventually come back to his senses and his talents, boy. Here, yet again, my intuitions were correct. Though, you appear a bit perturbed. Don't like the song? It's about you!

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: It says I'm really the bastard son of Zeus, but also indelibly connected to the god of wine, and how your parents tore me to pieces, giving rise to mortals, but that I'm always going to be stuck here.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hades_01
dialogueLines.text: You fill Orpheus' mind with nonsense, he produces nonsense. Nonsense I presume shall spread from this place far and wide, so everyone might know that I have nothing to do with you.

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: I'm not worried. Orpheus plays for a very small audience.

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: You call my chambers a mess, look at your desk, Father! Whatever happened to your court musician, huh, where's his sentencing parchmentwork? Stuck in Tartarus, all because he stopped singing for you on command?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hades_01
dialogueLines.text: My desk is far more organized than you, boy! And if you're so concerned about our court musician, why don't you commute his sentence, yourself? Seeing as I haven't gotten around to it yet.

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: Sure, happy to oblige. I'll just ask the House Contractor since you're too busy.

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: Question for you, Father. The name Kore happen to mean anything to you, by any chance?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hades_01
dialogueLines.text: What, where did you...?! Ahh, the cold witch brought it up, did she? Never repeat that name again, not here, nor anywhere, you hear me, boy? Know only this: The one called by that name despised it even more than I.

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: So let's see. Demeter's daughter went missing. She wound up down here. I... happened. Mother left. Demeter, and everyone else on Olympus apparently, has no idea. That about right? Tell me what happened.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hades_01
dialogueLines.text: I'll tell you nothing! Keep meddling in matters that you cannot understand, and you'll incur the wrath of Olympus on all that you hold dear. And, know that it is not for my sake that I'm warning you.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hades_01
dialogueLines.text: What is it now? I see that sneering look upon your countenance, which must mean you have yet another one of your insipid questions, haven't you.

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: Oh, this one's not insipid in the slightest. Let me get this straight. My mother's Persephone. Her mother's Demeter, but her father... evidently was some farm-boy? My red blood comes from him? And my propensity to die I guess.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hades_01
dialogueLines.text: You blame your ancestors for your own weakness? You should be thankful you were born with immortality! Though, your meddlesome curiosity and constant need for affirmation must certainly be from your mortal side.

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: Then, Mother, did... she grow older with age? Is she just going to expire as the mortals do? With demigods I hear it can go either way.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hades_01
dialogueLines.text: I said never to speak of her, blast you! You said, yourself, she is the daughter of the goddess of seasons! Use your fool head, for once.

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: Father... how are you doing without Mother around? Will she be all right, on Olympus?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hades_01
dialogueLines.text: Clearly she is more than capable of handling herself. And we here have been well accustomed to not having her about.

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: So everything's back to normal, then? That sounds lovely.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hades_01
dialogueLines.text: Nothing is normal, anymore. Besides! She shall return. Knowing that she shall return, is that not sufficient for you? Be patient, for once.

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: It's good to see Mother's back. Not that I was too concerned, but... I was definitely a little concerned.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hades_01
dialogueLines.text: I shared in that particular concern, and yes... it is good the Queen has once again returned. And she shall have to depart again before we know it, so cherish your time with her, boy!

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hades_01
dialogueLines.text: It is woefully infrequent that I've cause for this, but I do have to thank you, boy. You've culled the Satyrs and the vermin bringing ruin to our Temple, there! Blasted creatures multiply at an alarming rate.

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: They're not doing your bidding, yet they're every bit as bothersome as all your wretched servants I've met. I'm surprised you aren't getting along.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hades_01
dialogueLines.text: They are a plague upon this realm, although we have them well contained, for now. And once they are rightly dead, they tend to swear to me their fealty.

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: Always wanted to ask you something, Father. Whose skull is that there on your shoulder? If it's not too personal.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hades_01
dialogueLines.text: I did not chronicle all Typhon's wretched offspring, boy. No one did. Some of them sit upon our shoulders now, lest we forget that things could easily have turned out rather differently, and much, much worse.

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: So these are just the remains of the spawn of the most hideous monstrosity of all time, which you and your brothers slew, got it. I guess putting the dead in their place is all part of the job. I can hardly imagine what it was like before you and the Olympians took charge.

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: What's the giant pact posted out in the courtyard, Father? You offering me a job?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hades_01
dialogueLines.text: Perish the thought. It's so that all your toiling can be more painful, nothing more.

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: Why would I want my toiling to be more painful than it already is?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hades_01
dialogueLines.text: Who knows, boy, or better yet, who cares? To make the mortals up there worship you for all your labors? Go on, then, go get yourself dispatched some more, so I can make well certain that my realm can properly contain you.

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: Saw your new Pact of Punishment out in the courtyard, Father. Impressed that you could find a parchment large enough to fit all the unpleasant stipulations. So you're taking even harsher measures to contain me?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hades_01
dialogueLines.text: Whether you consent to the terms of the Pact is entirely up to you, boy. But, in all your comings and goings, it is important that we have full knowledge of which property is being carried from the premises.

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: Father, have you ever considered that Orpheus might be much better as a court musician if you permitted him to see his muse again?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hades_01
dialogueLines.text: I have considered many possibilities, boy. And I trust Orpheus considered many possibilities before he signed away his rights to see his so-called muse when once he invaded my realm!

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: So you're forcing him to stay here for eternity just because he signed a contract with you?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hades_01
dialogueLines.text: Contracts with me are binding! Until such time as they dig themselves out of the archives and tear themselves up! And I don't see that happening anytime soon.

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: Father, let's say, hypothetically, that I could pay the dues to waive the separation order between Orpheus and Eurydice. Would you honor the new terms, or spitefully cheat me out of a fair deal?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hades_01
dialogueLines.text: I honor the terms of my commitments, boy. The rules we put in place are all that keep this Underworld in any semblance of a shape! But, what you ask is utterly absurd. The costs of processing a separation order are well beyond your means.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hades_01
dialogueLines.text: You voided the contract with our court musician. Used your own authority against me.

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: So what if I did? Just gives him a bit of break from time to time. He's still your court musician, isn't he?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hades_01
dialogueLines.text: He is. And he knows better than to undermine me, I think. Out with you, then! I'll deal with your insubordination some other time.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hades_01
dialogueLines.text: You voided the contract with our court musician. Used your own authority against me.

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: So what if I did? Just gives him a bit of break from time to time. He's still your court musician, isn't he?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hades_01
dialogueLines.text: He is. And he knows better than to undermine me, I think. Though, I suppose the Queen would take your side that he has paid his dues in full, by now...?

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: Father, let me ask you something work-related, here. I know the shade called Sisyphus once wronged both you and Thanatos, and has since paid a heavy price. But don't the Fury Sisters now have better things to do than torture him?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hades_01
dialogueLines.text: You come to me to question how I allocate my resources? Preposterous. I care nothing for that wretched shade. That he is tortured for eternity is but a technicality resulting from his arrogance. The terms of his incarceration are long lost!

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: So then if I could somehow find those terms, then theoretically, I could grant him a pardon...

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hades_01
dialogueLines.text: If you could somehow do your job, and enact miracles, then yes! You could do anything, then, I suppose. Including waste your time on those of his undeserving ilk.

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: Father, of all the skeletons and such in your direct employ... you wouldn't happen to have any here around the House, would you? Out in my courtyard, for example?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hades_01
dialogueLines.text: Skeletons! I'm sure they'll welcome you when next you visit Asphodel. You may not realize it, boy, but we have a certain standard of decency we uphold around here!

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hades_01
dialogueLines.text: Indulge me for a moment, boy. Why bother to construct a sundial, there? Knowing full well it was my wish to have one installed. To demonstrate your awe-inspiring authority? Or to deny me the small pleasure of doing it myself?

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: Maybe a little of each? Or maybe you ought not second-guess my every motive and decision, constantly, searching for some sinister agenda that has yet to cross my mind?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hades_01
dialogueLines.text: Pah! I would have chosen an entirely different marble texture for it, anyway.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hades_01
dialogueLines.text: How goes your heated rivalry against the greatest king of Athens lately, boy? Your battles with him and the Minotaur must be the talk of the Underworld of late.

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: Admit it, Father. Theseus was the only hero you were able to recruit to stand against me. All the while he gets to boast about being the champion. What, was Heracles too busy for the task?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hades_01
dialogueLines.text: Speak not to me of Heracles. You know these heroes by their deeds, not by their character. Heracles is fortunate he's in my brother's favor. Else he would be in Tartarus where he belongs. The mortal concept of what constitutes a hero is absurd.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hades_01
dialogueLines.text: Whatever you did to my blasted seat is of no consequence to me. From my vantage-point, I can't even see it! A foolish prank and waste of valuable resources, nothing more.

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: It's more for everybody else than you, Father. Think of it as a tribute to Cerberus! Besides, you really should think about changing up the look of this place from time to time. Keep things fresh!

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hades_01
dialogueLines.text: The limitless work keeps things fresh enough as it is. Though if making aimless changes in this House is how you choose to spend your limitless time, I'll have no part of it.

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: You know, Father, while I'm sure you don't appreciate all the distractions as of late, your ceaseless toil has had some noticeable effect. Is it my imagination, or are the ongoing Underworld renovations almost complete?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hades_01
dialogueLines.text: There you go, getting ahead of yourself, again. The final stages of renovation are some of the most vital! Second only to the initial planning. There's a great deal of important work ahead!

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: I wasn't saying it wasn't important, I was just saying maybe there's finally an end to it in sight. What are you going to do with all your spare time, then?

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: You ever think of giving Thanatos a break, Father? He seems to have more than his fair share of work of late.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hades_01
dialogueLines.text: Blame the blasted mortals for perishing so easily, or blame Lord Ares, prior to accusing me. With their surface wars, not only do they kill each other readily, but also often subsequently perish from the consequences of it all!

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: What wars, what's going on up there? Why don't the other Olympians do something?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hades_01
dialogueLines.text: Pah! How should I know? The mortals are equipped to settle their petty differences by any number of means. We have to make room for them all sooner or later, regardless.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hades_01
dialogueLines.text: How are you finding my old trusty spear, then, hm? Or perhaps you have a preference for my brothers' and my sisters' former arms. Kept under lock and key, and yet, they're now in your possession, it would seem.

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: The weapons in the courtyard. Of course you know about them, by now. I stole them. Why not just take them from me? I'd be stuck.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hades_01
dialogueLines.text: The thought has long since crossed my mind. However, I'm not fool enough to tamper with those cursed things. They go where they please. They cannot be contained. Like you. Now, why don't you go entertain each other more?

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: So tell me, Father. Just how much did it hurt your soul knowing my face is up there on the Featured Houseservant Board, for all in the lounge to see?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hades_01
dialogueLines.text: Has it occurred to you that I, alone, have final say about whose countenance is shown up there? Although, others who shall not be named did put in a good word for you, repeatedly.

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: Blood and darkness... all my life I thought that board was a joke. But seeing my face up there... it felt good. Thanks, Father. Maybe it won't be the last time.

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: Father... look, Lord Uncle Zeus, I've been in contact with him, and he wishes to make peace. He asked for me to tell you. To tell you he forgives you for... whatever happened.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hades_01
dialogueLines.text: My brother Zeus... forgives me? Zeus forgives me?! How dare that blasted little brat! His beard may be luxurious but he's as much a child as are you. Forgives me, bah!

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: What did you do...? What happened between you?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hades_01
dialogueLines.text: None of your business! Go on now and frolic with that Lord Uncle of yours, the both of them! And don't you ever speak of him to me again, you hear me?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hades_01
dialogueLines.text: You are late, boy! Have you any idea what time it is?

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: No. Wait, what? I vanquished you in single combat and finally found Mother, and that's all you have to say to me?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hades_01
dialogueLines.text: Hah, haha! Nonsense. Don't you think I would recall such an event?

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: You can't be serious. You're going to pretend as though it never happened? She wants answers, same as I do. She demands you let me pass! Let me go to her again.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hades_01
dialogueLines.text: Silence! This discussion is over. I hardly think this is the time or place to indulge your overwrought imagination.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hades_01
dialogueLines.text: I told you, boy. I told you! There's no escape from here. Learned that the hard way, then?

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: You could have told me more important things. Mother left because she never even knew I was alive. Thanks to you! She's got a lot of questions, herself, as you can probably imagine. I'm headed back to her. You going to cooperate?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hades_01
dialogueLines.text: Hahaha, nonsense! Listen to me: If you have any sense remaining in that head of yours, I caution you not to discuss this here and now.

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: Let me go. Let me find her. She demands it!

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: Quite a mess you've created, I must say, Father... after being of no help whatsoever when it comes to disentangling it all. Though, this isn't the place to talk, right?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hades_01
dialogueLines.text: No! It most certainly is not. Though, I take it it shall not be long before another of our private meetings, then.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hades_01
dialogueLines.text: Those Chthonic Companion things you have from Nyx, or whoever they ended up with... I know they have been benefiting you.

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: Yeah, well... I'm to attempt to escape this place by any means necessary, right? I figure if I can call on Olympus for aid, maybe some of my friends can help me in a pinch, as well.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hades_01
dialogueLines.text: Not what I mean. Some of your friends have been reluctant to engage against me during our scuffles on the surface. Know that I have authorized them to proceed, if necessary.

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: Wait, really? You're saying it's OK for your own loyal subjects to side against you up there? You won't take it out on them?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hades_01
dialogueLines.text: I consider it an expansion of their responsibilities. Now, go.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hades_01
dialogueLines.text: Whatever you may wish to say, I do not wish to hear it.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hades_01
dialogueLines.text: You're interrupting me during my time at work.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hades_01
dialogueLines.text: I think I've heard more than enough from you lately.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hades_01
dialogueLines.text: You're willfully wasting my time, aren't you.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hades_01
dialogueLines.text: Go, get out. Don't let me stop you.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hades_01
dialogueLines.text: Shouldn't you be getting skewered by the dead?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hades_01
dialogueLines.text: Go to your room, or anywhere but here.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hades_01
dialogueLines.text: Blood and darkness, what is it with you?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hades_01
dialogueLines.text: Have you come up with more impertinence to spout at me?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hades_01
dialogueLines.text: I'll not be bothered with your disrespect.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hades_01
dialogueLines.text: Has it occurred to you that I'm preoccupied?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hades_01
dialogueLines.text: I am uninterested in conversing with you, boy.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hades_01
dialogueLines.text: Your presence is no longer welcome here.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hades_01
dialogueLines.text: How possibly could I have given the impression that I wished to talk?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hades_01
dialogueLines.text: You realize just how much property of mine you have defaced?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hades_01
dialogueLines.text: Fail all you like, you're never getting out of here.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hades_01
dialogueLines.text: You'll learn your place, the hard way, evidently.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hades_01
dialogueLines.text: To suffer such indignity, from my own kin.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hades_01
dialogueLines.text: Such foolishness as yours I scarce can comprehend.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hades_01
dialogueLines.text: We've nothing left now to discuss between us.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hades_01
dialogueLines.text: Whatever you may wish to say, I do not wish to hear it.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hades_01
dialogueLines.text: Have I mentioned that you're never getting out of here?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hades_01
dialogueLines.text: You're here to stay, boy. All of us are.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hades_01
dialogueLines.text: I'm busy now, as you can plainly see.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hades_01
dialogueLines.text: Stupid boy. I told you nobody gets out of here, whether alive or dead. Though, how was your wanton ransacking of my domain?

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: Greetings, Father. My ransacking was a delight, thank you for asking. So, I'll just be on my way, again.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hades_01
dialogueLines.text: Be on your way, indeed. What do I care? You shall never reach the surface. Go, see for yourself.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hades_01
dialogueLines.text: What is it, now? I've a mountain of infernal parchmentwork.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hades_01
dialogueLines.text: Out of my sight, now, go.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hades_01
dialogueLines.text: To what do I owe the pleasure, boy? For I'm unaccustomed to being approached by you here in my House.

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: Oh, you know. Just thought I'd check in, see what you were up to. Parchmentwork going all right, I'm sure?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hades_01
dialogueLines.text: Oh yes, the parchmentwork is going splendidly. Would you care for some? There is no shortage of it, you see.

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: Hey, uh, Father. I thought, maybe, you know, here.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hades_01
dialogueLines.text: Where did you get this...? You think you can butter me up with such trifles? Give me that, and then get out of here.

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: Hey, uh, Father. I thought, maybe, you know, here.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hades_01
dialogueLines.text: You owe me nothing, certainly not this. And I've nothing to give you in exchange. But fine, I'll honor our custom. Give it here.

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: Erm, Father... I know we can't exactly change the past, but we can try to move forward. Look, I know I'm not even supposed to have this stuff, but everyone likes it, won't you try it, at least? Here, take it, either way.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hades_01
dialogueLines.text: Hrm. I... can dispose of this properly. Speaking of disposal... I've no further need for this, here, so... rid me of it, why don't you? On your way out.

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: More Nectar for the old disposal service, Father, if you don't mind. Come on, you must have tried the stuff, at least in the days before you were here! Didn't you ever enjoy yourself, at any point?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hades_01
dialogueLines.text: I don't require beverages in order to enjoy myself, boy. Unlike so many of your kin upon their mountain. Yet this blasted substance somehow finds its way to my domain. Then, fine! You wish for the customs here to be more akin to Olympus, why not?

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: Have another bottle, why don't you, Father? Not like it's going to change anything between us, right? All the Nectar in the Underworld can't change what's already happened. Or change yourself.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hades_01
dialogueLines.text: Hrm. You're probably correct. You must think it is my age that makes me obstinate. Unable to change my nature. But I have always been inflexible. You think I'm proud of it? You call me 'Father' like it's an insult. I deserve the title only as a technicality, I know.

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: You could start being a better father at any point. There's no one stopping you but you.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hades_01
dialogueLines.text: Yes, well... unfortunately, I am a significant impediment. You should be well aware of that. You were able to stop me. I have thus far been unable to do so...

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hades_01
dialogueLines.text: Enough of this, boy! Even mortals know better. I don't want your Nectar, or Ambrosia, or whichever offerings. You owe me no such thing, and I shall not accept it any longer. In fact, here... you shall take all of it back!

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: Why? It's just Nectar, Father, lighten up. I get that you're surly about everything, but please at least try toward decency with me! If not for me, then for Mother.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hades_01
dialogueLines.text: How dare you lecture me? I mean... urgh... look. As you told me yourself... there's no changing what already transpired. And our memories are warnings. When you have lived as long as I have... you come to understand your weaknesses.

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: What are you getting at? You're just going to give up, stick with what's comfortable for you instead of making any real effort at making peace with your son?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hades_01
dialogueLines.text: Rngh. Listen to me. My attempts at making peace are going to be rather subtle for your tastes. I cannot change the past. And there is only so much I can do about the future. I tell you this because... I shall not lie to you again. That much, I swear.

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: Father, what happened to the lounge? The only place for anyone to have a little fun around here, and you seal it shut.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hades_01
dialogueLines.text: Oh, I had little choice about that, boy. You see, after you fled the first time, Cerberus, why he was very much upset. Utterly tore the place to shreds. It's now under repair. All thanks to you.

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: You're really blaming me for not minding the dog while I was out, and while you all were here?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hades_01
dialogueLines.text: I lay the blame entirely upon you, yes. Who else? It's fortunate that Cerberus has calmed himself since then! Come to his senses, I should say. May you yet come to yours.

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: Guess what, Father? The lounge is all tidied up. Your House Contractor there is quite efficient once you muster up the necessary funds. You should try it sometime!

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hades_01
dialogueLines.text: Are you expecting words of thanks because you paid the contractor to fix a bit of devastation that you caused? I fear it shall take rather more than that.

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: What if I replaced every furnishing in this blasted place, would you treat me any better then?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hades_01
dialogueLines.text: I suppose, boy, that there's only one way to find out. Though I suggest you save yourself the trouble.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hades_01
dialogueLines.text: I see you've decided to come home. Changed your mind yet, or looking for more pain and suffering?

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: More pain and suffering, for sure. After all, you taught me to appreciate the finer things.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hades_01
dialogueLines.text: You keep telling yourself that, boy. But, mark you my words, sooner or later, this life that you have chosen for yourself shall teach you I was right.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hades_01
dialogueLines.text: Well that was quick. What news, boy, ready to give up, or no?

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: You'd like that, wouldn't you? Lucky I've got other family who believe in me.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hades_01
dialogueLines.text: Be careful whom you give your trust. My brothers frolic on Olympus while we work. While I work! You're just like them. No wonder they like you.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hades_01
dialogueLines.text: So, how did it go? How did you die ignobly this time, pray tell? Cerberus and I can't wait to hear the tale.

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: In that case, I'll spare you the details.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hades_01
dialogueLines.text: Fine. Your failure is quite easily imagined. How often it recurs! Almost as though my realm is built to keep you here. You, and every single soul that's ever lived. But, by all means, continue struggling.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hades_01
dialogueLines.text: Welcome home, boy. You're late for dinner... or, breakfast, or whatever blasted meal's next.

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: That's all right, Father. I'm not feeling very hungry at the moment.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hades_01
dialogueLines.text: ...I've asked repeatedly for the construction of a sundial, but every time, they tell me no, can you believe it? Your impertinence with me inspires all.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hades_01
dialogueLines.text: Welcome home, boy. You're late for dinner... or, breakfast, or whatever blasted meal's next.

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: That's all right, Father. I'm not feeling very hungry at the moment.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hades_01
dialogueLines.text: ...I've asked repeatedly for the construction of a sundial, but every time, they tell me no, can you believe it?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hades_01
dialogueLines.text: Blood and darkness, but this place is falling apart... nobody works an honest day down here.

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: Father, everybody's dead. Give them a break.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hades_01
dialogueLines.text: What would you even know of honest work? At least the wretched dead yet honor me as Lord. I cannot say the same for you.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hades_01
dialogueLines.text: Have you yet satisfied your foolish urges? Perhaps a lashing from the Furies' whips would set you straight.

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: I had it out with them already, thanks.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hades_01
dialogueLines.text: Such nonsense. First you defy me openly, and now you lie. On my next meeting with the Furies, we'll have a word about all this, you rest assured.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hades_01
dialogueLines.text: Why, I can scarce believe my eyes, is that you, boy?! Cerberus, look who it is, he's back!

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Cerberus_01
dialogueLines.text: Cerberus looks mournfully toward the Prince.

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: No need to drag the dog into this, Father.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hades_01
dialogueLines.text: Anything you say to me, you say in front of Cerberus! You might even learn a thing or two from him of listening, and of obedience!

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hades_01
dialogueLines.text: He's back already, Cerberus! Just as I indicated.

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: I'm standing right here, Father.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hades_01
dialogueLines.text: I'm sorry he disturbed your rest again, Cerberus, you've done a good job. The realm is well defended still, to the high standard that you set.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hades_01
dialogueLines.text: What did I tell you, boy? Ever wasteful of your time, and mine. How fares my Underworld, hm?

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: I don't know how you can stand it.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hades_01
dialogueLines.text: Well, at least some things are running smoothly still. Next you go to die again, do give the tortured souls out there my warm regards.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hades_01
dialogueLines.text: Ah, for shame, boy. I thought perhaps you'd finally made it out.

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: Really...? I mean, oh, wait, you're not serious. That famous sense of humor shining through.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hades_01
dialogueLines.text: I jest, of course, you'll never make it out; as I keep telling you to no avail. But you've gone farther than expected. So, who knows....

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hades_01
dialogueLines.text: ...You've been gone a while...

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: I'm going to find Mother. No matter how long it takes me.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hades_01
dialogueLines.text: For the last time, you're not to speak about that woman in my House. You want to know of her so much, you go find her, indeed.

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: Greetings, Father. So! I can just... head on out there again whenever I want? Don't need to stick to any rigid schedule?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hades_01
dialogueLines.text: Your unpredictability is one of your assets. Our defenses must always be on guard. So, yes: Go about your business as you best see fit!

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: And the Olympians are going to keep helping me, thinking I'm trying to get away from you, to them. We can't keep this up with them forever, you know.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hades_01
dialogueLines.text: Tell me something, boy... Zagreus... why did you do all this? Return the Queen. Go to such lengths. At a certain point... you must have learned the way I felt about her. Yet still you persisted. If I were you... I'd not have done me any favors.

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: I didn't often stop to give it much thought. I just knew I needed to find her... and then as soon as I did, I knew I needed her in my life. In other words, doing you any favors was never a priority, Father. Just ended up that way, I guess.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hades_01
dialogueLines.text: ...I see. I appreciate your candor. Nevertheless... I thank you. I do not know how you accomplished what you did, but know that I am grateful for the outcome. Even if I fail to act like it, much of the time.

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: Say, Father... back when you drew lots with your brothers, and wound up with the Underworld, rather than the heavens or the seas... which would you have claimed, if the choice was yours?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hades_01
dialogueLines.text: I do not deal in hypotheticals, boy. Each realm has its host of unenviable problems. You have never experienced the cold and sopping sea, or the blinding heights of Olympus. I have. And I assure you, you're not missing much.

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: Didn't you try to leave the Underworld, yourself, at some point? You once gave me that impression. I always thought it was because you hated it here.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hades_01
dialogueLines.text: I hate not having any choice. Enjoy the freedoms that you have. As you grow long in years, you gain more burdens and responsibilities, until they bind you.

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: Father, all these shades... aren't they going to let on that Mother's back? What if word spreads... and the Olympians find out?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hades_01
dialogueLines.text: You are so well-accustomed to defying me that you forget there are no others, either living or dead, who would so dare. None in this House shall speak of this until we are prepared to make any announcements, on our own terms.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hades_01
dialogueLines.text: Blasted parchmentwork, it never goes away.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hades_01
dialogueLines.text: Your recent efforts have proved useful to this place.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hades_01
dialogueLines.text: Go instill some fear in those wretches out there, would you?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hades_01
dialogueLines.text: He's back again, Cerberus! I'll try not to keep him for as long, next time.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hades_01
dialogueLines.text: Doubtless I shall get you for that one next time.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hades_01
dialogueLines.text: Another satyr outbreak to look out for next you venture there...

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hades_01
dialogueLines.text: Be certain to give your regards to your mother as well!

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hades_01
dialogueLines.text: Repairs for all the carnage that you sowed are already underway.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hades_01
dialogueLines.text: What else can we do to contain you, I wonder...

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hades_01
dialogueLines.text: I have some catching up to do, as you can see.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hades_01
dialogueLines.text: Ah, we ought to reconvene the court now, I suppose.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hades_01
dialogueLines.text: As you can see, I must attend to all this parchmentwork.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hades_01
dialogueLines.text: I must say, you made a hollow mockery of Theseus that time.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hades_01
dialogueLines.text: Our security efforts in Asphodel are evidently lacking.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hades_01
dialogueLines.text: Go rest yourself if need be. Though the Underworld is ready when you are.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hades_01
dialogueLines.text: Against my better judgment, I went easier on you that time.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hades_01
dialogueLines.text: Thus starts and ends our brief exchange of pleasantries.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hades_01
dialogueLines.text: Continue to perform your duties as you have.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hades_01
dialogueLines.text: Cerberus there was anticipating your return.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hades_01
dialogueLines.text: Your mother expected this most recent result of yours.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hades_01
dialogueLines.text: Do I appear as though I've time for conversation, boy?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hades_01
dialogueLines.text: Let us discuss your work performance at some other time.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hades_01
dialogueLines.text: Your infamy throughout Elysium has only grown.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hades_01
dialogueLines.text: I must admit... that was a job well done back there.

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: So, now that the festivities are over with, it's back to work like this, the same as ever?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hades_01
dialogueLines.text: Do you have any idea what happens when you announce the Underworld has a Queen, boy? Urgh, damn it! Zagreus, I mean. You become inundated with well-wishes from every blasted shade that wants something.

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: You're responding to well-wishes? Aw. Don't you think some of your subjects are sincerely happy for you?

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: I never stopped to think about why pomegranates grew here in the Underworld. Mother's doing from before, then? And all that stuff she told the Olympians, about how they bind you to this place... it's all nonsense?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hades_01
dialogueLines.text: You know full well that the pomegranates in my realm hold a certain power beyond the tartness of their taste, boy. The Queen... she has a way with plants, you see. A bit of her own mother's gift, applied more sensibly.

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: You kept them growing all this time... flourishing, even, because of her.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hades_01
dialogueLines.text: Tell me, in brief, boy. Our own upon Olympus... are they continuing to aid you as expected?

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: Still showing up and lending their support. I think some of them are a little disappointed I'm not planning to come join them, though... I think they understand now that they owe the Underworld a debt. We all have to do our part, looking after the mortals. Whether alive or dead.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hades_01
dialogueLines.text: Indeed. I half-expected they would disappoint. Abandon you to toil on your own. At least thus far they've honored their commitment, then.

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: So, what happened, when Mother first arrived, if I may ask? Some of your residents seem to remember the Queen from before. Yet the Olympians just never found out?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hades_01
dialogueLines.text: They had no jurisdiction here. No knowledge of our goings-on. Well, Hermes did, perhaps, but in a very limited capacity. As far as anybody knew beyond this realm... our Queen was Nyx. Ask nothing more of this.

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: So, are you maintaining better contact now with Uncle Zeus, Uncle Poseidon, and the rest? Oh, and... is it all right for me to speak about them here?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hades_01
dialogueLines.text: Urgh... I'm certain the superficial messages we have exchanged shall taper off, with time. We have always been apart, irrespective of the distance separating us. I know Zeus is content with your mother's version of events. Thus we are at peace, for now.

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: Wait, what do you mean, for now?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hades_01
dialogueLines.text: I mean only that our family is prone to causing problems for itself. Never content to leave things well enough alone. But if we have little to do with them... their quarrels, all their nonsense on Olympus... it should be of no consequence.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hades_01
dialogueLines.text: The latest reports indicate that mortals are fully twice as fearful of ending up in Tartarus as in our last evaluation period, thanks to the destruction you have caused. You are an unseen power in the Underworld. Commendable.

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: Commendable, me? An unseen power, huh. Surprised you're giving me the credit, Father.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hades_01
dialogueLines.text: I give credit where it's due. But the mortals? They know nothing of what transpires here. Only rumblings we permit to escape our realm... rumblings that reinforce their deepest fears about what shall transpire after their demise.

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: So the more havoc I wreak in the Underworld, the more mortals hear about all the unpleasantness, and... it makes them fearful of this place? If more of them lead upstanding lives, it'll keep Tartarus from overflowing, I guess.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hades_01
dialogueLines.text: I know that Nyx has been abetting you. You wish to take advantage of her pity? I care not. Her powers have their limits, anyway.

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: How can you... Nyx is the Night Itself, and can do as she pleases, isn't that correct, Father? You think I asked she get involved in all this business?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hades_01
dialogueLines.text: You, boy, have already dragged this entire household, not to mention my fool relatives, into this mess, which you've created. Which you dare call business.

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: So, tell me something, Father, if you please. Since we're more candid lately in regard to matters of my birthright here, and all... did you and Mother... how to put this? Any brothers or sisters I should know about?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hades_01
dialogueLines.text: I thought I told you never to invoke her here! Be thankful that you have no blasted kin. You've such weak blood, and such a temperament... why, then, would you think I'd risk another, such as you?

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: Sure, that's fair enough, it's just not all such things occur as planned, as I am told. From tales of your brothers and so forth.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hades_01
dialogueLines.text: Oh, blast my brothers, and blast you, as well! All those who know me know I have no heir. You could not rise to the occasion if you tried! And, there is no one else. You've only me. And I have only you.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hades_01
dialogueLines.text: Regarding your responsibilities: Our Resources Director is available to you. In my private quarters. Normally, it would be I, alone, transferring excess resources back into our realm.

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: The fabled Resources Director! So, now I get to give away the hard-earned resources I've painstakingly gathered over time?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hades_01
dialogueLines.text: You get to do precisely that. It is not necessary that you do. But if you wish to rise above your current station, you shall do it.

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: What's the point in rising above my current station? I thought our work was to be done in secrecy.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hades_01
dialogueLines.text: It is. There is no point but pride. And pride is dangerous. Do what you will. I care not, as long as everything gets adequately done.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hades_01
dialogueLines.text: Regarding your responsibilities: Our Resources Director is available to you. In the administrative chamber. Normally, it would be I, alone, transferring excess resources back into our realm.

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: The fabled Resources Director! So, now I get to give away the hard-earned resources I've painstakingly gathered over time?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hades_01
dialogueLines.text: You get to do precisely that. It is not necessary that you do. But if you wish to rise above your current station, you shall do it.

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: What's the point in rising above my current station? I thought our work was to be done in secrecy.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hades_01
dialogueLines.text: It is. There is no point but pride. And pride is dangerous. Do what you will. I care not, as long as everything gets adequately done.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hades_01
dialogueLines.text: So you were able to dispatch my Hydra, boy. Right down to the last hateful head. It's an accomplishment I felt was certainly beyond your talent set. Though, here you are again, nevertheless.

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: You cheated me. There wasn't anywhere that I could go! I could have gone much farther. All the way to Mother!

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hades_01
dialogueLines.text: You shall do no such thing. Besides, if you were any help to me, you'd know vast portions of my realm are presently still under renovation. You won't be getting farther than you have.

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: I can wait. You can't just keep this place on lockdown for eternity. The mortals up there wouldn't stand for it.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hades_01
dialogueLines.text: They have no patience, yes. The same as you. Then wait, with all the rest of them. It matters not to me. For I have other work to do.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hades_01
dialogueLines.text: Now you begone from here, get yourself killed some more for all I care.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hades_01
dialogueLines.text: Well, boy. Word has reached me that you were able to dethrone the Champion of Elysium as well as his bull-headed companion. Tell me, how did you accomplish such a thing?

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: First, you tell me when I can get out of there. The path guarded by Theseus was sealed! Is there even a surface? Is the outside world all a lie?!

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hades_01
dialogueLines.text: Ah, now you are asking the big questions! Comforting to see such growth of character. Well if there is an outside world, you shall not be seeing it for quite some time. You know why, don't you?

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: Ongoing Underworld renovations?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hades_01
dialogueLines.text: Ongoing Underworld renovations. Now go and let King Theseus drive that sharp spear of his into yourself some more, until such time. The mortals soon shall force me to reopen, and then what?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hades_01
dialogueLines.text: Now you begone from here, get yourself killed some more for all I care.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hades_01
dialogueLines.text: You are late, boy! Have you any idea what time it is?

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: No. Wait, what? I just vanquished you in single combat, and that's all you have to say to me?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hades_01
dialogueLines.text: Vanquished me? Hah, hahaha, nonsense. Don't you think I would recall such an event?

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: You can't be serious. You're going to pretend as though it never happened?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hades_01
dialogueLines.text: Everything is as it always was. And so shall it be, until the Fates determine otherwise. Or until our ongoing Underworld renovations are finally complete. Whichever comes first.

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: Something the matter, there, Father? You seem a little quieter than usual. Dare I say even a little somber and remorseful, for some reason?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hades_01
dialogueLines.text: Silence, boy! And better yet, why don't you get out of my sight? I've more important things to do now than indulge your boastfulness. We'll have to catch up at some other place and time.

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: So you're really not going to answer my question about Mother from the other time, until the Underworld renovations are complete?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hades_01
dialogueLines.text: I know not what you're going on about, there, boy. Although, my focus is entirely upon our renovations, yes. Perhaps someday or night when this place is in proper working order, I shall have more time for your insipid questions.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hades_01
dialogueLines.text: Megaera tells me that you surpassed her while out there. How utterly embarrassing for her, to say the least.

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: I suppose you're going to punish her in some cruel fashion, then?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hades_01
dialogueLines.text: Oh, I've already seen to that, of course. You think she volunteered to deal with you? Nobody did. That's how ignoble a task it is.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hades_01
dialogueLines.text: Tell me something, boy, did you enjoy the searing heat of Asphodel? You must have thought that you'd be more resilient to it.

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: I'm open to a change of climate. Even if it gets a bit uncomfortable.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hades_01
dialogueLines.text: You know nothing of discomfort, yet. Making it out of Tartarus is nothing in comparison to what lies ahead for you.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hades_01
dialogueLines.text: I understand that you created quite a stir within Elysium. An all-out brawl within eternal paradise!

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: You call that paradise? It's just a bunch of shades bored out of their former skulls, itching for a chance to feel alive again.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hades_01
dialogueLines.text: What would you know of it! The greatest mortals have their every need fulfilled within that place. I dread to hear what they shall say of all of this.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hades_01
dialogueLines.text: Tell me something, boy, because my own set of ideas on the subject is beginning to run dry: What, exactly, is it going to take for you to understand that you're not leaving here?

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: Perhaps you ought to have me bound next to Prometheus, and have the dreaded Eagle peck my liver for eternity?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hades_01
dialogueLines.text: You would have me resort to such barbarity? Blast you, boy. Punishment is not the path to rehabilitation; it is for its own sake. I'd have expected all your trips through Tartarus to have taught you as much.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hades_01
dialogueLines.text: I'm truly sorry that he's troubled you again, Cerberus. I know how much you value resting here, and yet, I have no better means to bar the entry to our realm than you.

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: I thought you'd figured out by now that guilting me into abandoning my quest won't work. You're the one who should feel guilty, forcing Cerberus to block my path.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hades_01
dialogueLines.text: What would you know of it? Cerberus cannot be forced to do anything. He simply understands he has responsibilities.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hades_01
dialogueLines.text: You've taken more advantage of the Pact of Punishment than I would have expected, boy. I almost wish to commend you for doing such a thing.

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: Almost? I've been rampaging through the worst your realm has to offer. Causing you untold inconvenience and likely a fortune in repair costs. I thought you'd be upset.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hades_01
dialogueLines.text: Upset? Heavens, no. Through your repeated bumbling, you have revealed a number of shortcomings in our operations, which my underlings are busy working to address.

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: You know you sound especially despicable when you say underlings, don't you?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hades_01
dialogueLines.text: Don't let me keep you any longer, boy, go forth! Try to escape again. You're helping make the Underworld a better place.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hades_01
dialogueLines.text: Explain to me something: How did you do it? I mean the Pact. For all of the conditions it imposed... you still surpassed me. How?

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: Plenty of practice, regular exercise, a positive attitude, the blessings of assorted family members you don't like, and your discarded Titan-slaying weapons I suppose. Nothing special. Why do you ask?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hades_01
dialogueLines.text: To think that you would squander such a talent on frivolous pursuit. You do not even realize what you have achieved, do you.

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: Wait, wait wait wait wait, care to repeat that first part that you said back there? The talent part?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hades_01
dialogueLines.text: Would that you had a talent for listening. Begone for now.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hades_01
dialogueLines.text: Well? What is the latest explanation for your tardiness? Lost track of time admiring the courtyard view? Perhaps you were recuperating from your martial training. Achilles is not prone to holding back.

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: I... I'm sorry, Father, I just overslept. I must have overslept. How am I even supposed to know exactly when to be here, I mean, every moment every day and night, here, it's all the same!

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hades_01
dialogueLines.text: It's not at all the same! The hue of Ixion's green light takes on an obviously lighter hue when it is time! You're an embarrassment not just to me, here, boy, but to this entire operation. Now get back to work!

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hades_01
dialogueLines.text: Not only were you late, again... but then you also failed to perform even the simplest tasks that I had asked, repeatedly, that you complete. How many chances do you need at this? I am too lenient with you.

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: Too lenient? Instead of lambasting me nonstop, have you considered, maybe, training me some more to do this fascinating work to your satisfaction? You'll forgive me if I didn't memorize every laborious detail the last time you performed your monologue about how all this works.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hades_01
dialogueLines.text: Excuse me?! If I am not mistaken, you're deflecting your incompetence toward me? As though the Goddess of Wisdom herself has come to aid you in your time of need! I could replace you here and now with any number of simple shades, hungry to work here!

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: Then why don't you?! I don't want any of this. I'm not the one who drew the short lot with your brothers and got stuck here forever!! Why am I having to do this senseless parchment-pushing for you?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hades_01
dialogueLines.text: Why are you stuck? You're not. You're fired! I was wrong to think you were prepared to take on any responsibility at all. And, don't you ever invoke your uncles here again. Or I'll have you cast into the lowest pits of Tartarus. Now, get out of my administration, and my sight!

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hypnos_01
dialogueLines.text: Hey, did you see? Somebody got me this incredible new sleeper-seat right here! Though, I would never lie down while on the job! I'm perfectly all right sleeping while standing up!

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hypnos_01
dialogueLines.text: Hey, you keep dying! You happen to run into my brother anywhere out there, by any chance? Haven't seen him in ages, what's with the long assignment?

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: Something about a war. Father sent Thanatos to take stock, but you're right, it's been a while now that you mention it. I guess a lot's occurred down here since then.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hypnos_01
dialogueLines.text: I'll say! The lounge re-opened, let's see, what else... Anyway! You see my brother out there you tell him we've got everything here under control!

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hypnos_01
dialogueLines.text: Hey, did you see? My brother, he's come back! He's here! Somewhere, I think! Good old Thanatos. He's so busy all the time, he... never talks to me! Kind of like your dad with you, I guess!

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hypnos_01
dialogueLines.text: Whaa! Oh, hah, you just startled me there, is all, I was just... um... I...

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: Hey, uh, everything all right, there, Hypnos?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hypnos_01
dialogueLines.text: Oh, absolutely! Everything definitely is. It's just... it's Thanatos. He said some stuff, and... now I'm not so sure I'm doing the best job I can, you know?

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: I'm sure he wasn't trying to hurt your feelings. Wait, what am I saying, he's always been harsh with you, hasn't he?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hypnos_01
dialogueLines.text: Hey, what if he doesn't like me anymore? He's got other brothers!

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hypnos_01
dialogueLines.text: Oh hey! Um, this might be a little weird, but I, uh... thanks! I mean, you talked to Thanatos about me, right? At first I thought he was acting funny. But he has definitely been a lot nicer to me, lately!

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: Come on, Hypnos, I didn't have anything to do with that. You seem like you've been working extra hard, and taking some of the feedback you've been getting to heart. I think Thanatos really appreciates that sort of thing. Who doesn't?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hypnos_01
dialogueLines.text: I don't know! But I really appreciate that you went out of your way, I mean... I just stand here and greet and register the dead! But you? You're a real friend!

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hypnos_01
dialogueLines.text: Hey, there you are! So is it true?! You know, about you and Thanatos? Because he told me, and I just said, whooooaaa, but then, everything made sense?

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: I'm sure whatever it is he said is completely true, yes, Hypnos.

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: Hey, Hypnos? This is for all the well-meaning advice you've given me. A little something from a certain double-bladed-axe-wielding, bull-headed, oversized monster of a man who you admire?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hypnos_01
dialogueLines.text: What's this, it's kind of hard to read the hand-writing, it says, no way... To Hypnos, my biggest fan. Stay vigilant. Asterius. There's... even a little picture of a bull. You... I... wow... wow!

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hypnos_01
dialogueLines.text: Welcome to the House of Hades! Glory in Death!

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hypnos_01
dialogueLines.text: Welcome to the House of Hades, where death is our life!

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hypnos_01
dialogueLines.text: Welcome to the House of Hades, where death is our business!

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hypnos_01
dialogueLines.text: Welcome to the House of Hades, and thanks for dying!

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hypnos_01
dialogueLines.text: Just keeping watch over the Pool of Styx, there, same as always!

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hypnos_01
dialogueLines.text: They won't catch me sleeping on the job again!

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hypnos_01
dialogueLines.text: Oh, hey, I didn't see you come in, um... welcome back!

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hypnos_01
dialogueLines.text: Whoa, I guess you must have died out there again, huh.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hypnos_01
dialogueLines.text: Oh, whoa, you're back already, that's too bad!

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hypnos_01
dialogueLines.text: Oh, hi, um, you're welcome! Just, go right ahead, I guess!

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hypnos_01
dialogueLines.text: Um, I was just resting my eyes... you won't tell anybody, right?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hypnos_01
dialogueLines.text: Well, you know what they say: There is no escape!

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hypnos_01
dialogueLines.text: Hey, you look like you could use some sleep!

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hypnos_01
dialogueLines.text: Oh, it's you, just, come on in, you're on the list!

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hypnos_01
dialogueLines.text: You're back, huh? That was pretty soon I guess!

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hypnos_01
dialogueLines.text: You must be pretty used to dying like this, huh?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hypnos_01
dialogueLines.text: The Fates are pretty mean to keep on doing this to you!

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hypnos_01
dialogueLines.text: Thanks for dying, nice to see you here again!

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hypnos_01
dialogueLines.text: Go right ahead, I'll get you all signed in!

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hypnos_01
dialogueLines.text: Good thing you're basically immortal, huh?
