dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hypnos_01
dialogueLines.text: Guess you're here to stay, hah-ha!

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hypnos_01
dialogueLines.text: There's always next time, isn't there?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hypnos_01
dialogueLines.text: Hoh, says here one of the Wretched Thugs got you, too bad! They're slow but they hit pretty hard I guess. Have you tried moving out of the way?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hypnos_01
dialogueLines.text: According to my list here, why, you must've just got clobbered all the way back home by one of those Wretched Thugs again! The old moving-out-the-way trick didn't work, huh...?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hypnos_01
dialogueLines.text: Looks like one of the Wretched Witches shot you dead last time, sorry to hear that, huh? Have you tried shooting them first?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hypnos_01
dialogueLines.text: You must have had a run-in with a Spreader, huh! They're much more talented than the low-grade witches down in Tartarus, so your dad lets them stay warm up in Asphodel, I guess!

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hypnos_01
dialogueLines.text: Oof, a Splitter zapped you, didn't she! There's lots of witches in your dad's place, huh? Witchcraft must be pretty dangerous for all those mortals up there. Pretty dangerous for you, too!

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hypnos_01
dialogueLines.text: Aw, looks like you died from one of those nasty booby traps left by a Wretched Pest, little guys who look like urns? Have you tried avoiding them maybe?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hypnos_01
dialogueLines.text: Ooh wow, so Megaera herself got you last time, that must have been intense! She's fast, and strong, and beautiful, and... um... anyway, I hope the Fates are nicer to you next time!

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hypnos_01
dialogueLines.text: Hah! Megaera the Fury got you again! She is unstoppable, isn't she? One time, I tried to stop her on the way to have a chat with your dad, and she didn't even turn her head! <Sigh...>

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hypnos_01
dialogueLines.text: You got whipped by Megaera the Fury! Eh?! I always wanted to say that. Whipped, you know? Referring both to how badly you lost, and to that glowy purple whip she's always threatening me with whenever I try to talk to her?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hypnos_01
dialogueLines.text: So you got skewered by a Spike Trap out there, huh? Well, that's too bad! Have you tried not standing there, like, when the spikes come out?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hypnos_01
dialogueLines.text: Ooh looks like one of those Dart Traps killed you last time, bet that must have hurt! Have you tried getting behind something?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hypnos_01
dialogueLines.text: Ow looks like you got blasted to bits by an explosive urn last time! We keep some really nasty shades all bottled up in those, but... you so much as touch them, BOOM! Hah!

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hypnos_01
dialogueLines.text: Whoaaa, you just got killed by one of those Bloodless Inferno Bomber skeleton type guys, nice! I always wondered what would happen if you just kind of stood around while all their bombs went off!

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hypnos_01
dialogueLines.text: Owie, looks like the Bone Hydra of Asphodel just stomped you that time, huh? I hear each of its heads has its own nasty tricks. That must be tough!

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hypnos_01
dialogueLines.text: Bone Hydra got you again, huh? Real sorry about that. Though, you know what? I want you to get back out there, you know, all through Tartarus, and Asphodel, again, and knock some sense into it for me, OK? OK!

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hypnos_01
dialogueLines.text: Wait let me guess, don't tell me... you got slaughtered by the Bone Hydra, again! How many times was that? But you will get 'em next time, I just know it! Maybe the time after that. Eventually, no doubt about it!

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hypnos_01
dialogueLines.text: Daw, one of those Wretched Louts just killed you dead that time, those mean old slappy guys? Maybe try killing them beforehand, I don't know!

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hypnos_01
dialogueLines.text: Ooh, says here a Wretched Lout slapped you to death again, that's a tough break! Have you tried talking it out, or something, I... I don't know?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hypnos_01
dialogueLines.text: Hey you got turned to ashes by those floating Brimstone crystally type things! Next time one's turning you to charcoal with those beams of theirs, how about you step on over to the side?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hypnos_01
dialogueLines.text: Ooh, ow, so you got burned down to a crisp out there, standing in magma, right? I know you've got your burning feet and all, but standing in magma, why, I'm not so sure that's a good idea!

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hypnos_01
dialogueLines.text: Huh, so you walked straight into a burning magma spout, is that what got you this time? Bet if you waited long enough it'd cool off, instead of killing you!

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hypnos_01
dialogueLines.text: Hooh, looks like one of those little Numbskulls took a big old bite right out of you back there, I guess they got you, huh? Maybe if you weren't quite so tasty, they'd leave you alone! And so would I!

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hypnos_01
dialogueLines.text: Did you just get chomped by one of those little Numbskulls, again?! Oh, that is definitely rough. I guess you must have done something to make the Fates real mad!

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hypnos_01
dialogueLines.text: Oh, hohoh, you got blown up by a Slam-Dancer, I can see it on your face! It also says it on my list! Got everybody's cause of death, right here. Some real morbid stuff from time to time! So anyway, take care!

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hypnos_01
dialogueLines.text: Why you look like someone who just got beaten silly by a dirty skeleton! Beaten well beyond the brink of death, I ought to say! And, sure enough, according to my list, you did! Those Bloodless sure are tough!

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hypnos_01
dialogueLines.text: My list here says that you got petrified to death that time by some sort of nasty floating gorgon head? No offense to Dusa! Sounds like a pretty painful way to go, though hey! At least you got to be a statue for a little while! I like statues.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hypnos_01
dialogueLines.text: Pff, can I just say ouch? Because according to the list here, you got flattened like a pancake by a Skull-Crusher, out in Asphodel somewhere! Keep that up and you're going to get even shorter, haha!

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hypnos_01
dialogueLines.text: Oh, I recognize that look! That is the look of someone who just got here after having met a most unfortunate demise, courtesy of the notorious Bloodless Wave-Makers!! How do they make those waves, and why do they hurt so, so much? Plenty of time to think it over, now!

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hypnos_01
dialogueLines.text: Looks like you really took the heat from one of those Bloodless Burn-Flingers back there, didn't you? I know how tempting it can be to try and catch those fiery explosive bombs they throw, but... if I might offer you a bit of personal advice? You really shouldn't do that.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hypnos_01
dialogueLines.text: Oh, wow, Alecto's got it out for you...? Normally she doesn't kill anyone, she likes keeping them alive... for quite a while, really. Hurts them still, but doesn't kill them off! I guess she really doesn't like you, huh!

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hypnos_01
dialogueLines.text: Says here that you got stuck like a piece of meat on a spit by an Exalted Longspear, wow, I'll bet that really hurt! Too bad you don't have a thick, leathery hide or something that could help protect you in that type of situation. Oh well!

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hypnos_01
dialogueLines.text: Looks like you just got pounded down by an Exalted Greatshield, huh? Did you try attacking while their guard was down? Or, did they just not let down their guard at any point... Oh...

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hypnos_01
dialogueLines.text: Let me guess, you got caught out by one of those Exalted Strongbows, did you there? Next time, if you don't stand around like you're doing here right now, maybe you won't make such an easy target!

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hypnos_01
dialogueLines.text: My understanding is you had a little run-in with a Nemean Chariot, just kind of plowed right into you, there, didn't it? You got to watch where you're going crossing the streets of Elysium next time!

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hypnos_01
dialogueLines.text: Those Archer Statues in Elysium have got to be a real sight, no doubt about it. But, you can't just stand there and admire them, or else they'll shoot you dead like they did just now!

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hypnos_01
dialogueLines.text: Sounds to me like you got stuck in more ways than one gawking at a Phalanx Statue in Elysium back there! I got to hand it to your dad. He made sure those look just as good as they stab any intruders like yourself!

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hypnos_01
dialogueLines.text: Says here that you got flash-fried to a crisp within a Phoenix Urn's destructive blast back there, I mean, you really ought to stay away from those, if you ask me!

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hypnos_01
dialogueLines.text: Woow, I can't believe you took on the Minotaur, no way! No wonder you're back here! Everyone knows that he's unstoppable. Especially me! Hey, next you run into him, could you... get his autograph, for me?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hypnos_01
dialogueLines.text: The Minotaur killed you again, that must have been exciting, huh? So, any chance you got that autograph for me, before you got destroyed?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hypnos_01
dialogueLines.text: Slain by the Minotaur once more, now he's a real beast, there, isn't he! But, tell me something, any progress on that autograph request of mine?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hypnos_01
dialogueLines.text: Oh, I just knew that you'd be back, soon as I heard you faced the Champion of Elysium, himself! You know, I've heard that, among mortals, it's an honor to be killed by somebody like Theseus! Keep it up, and maybe you'll be famous, too!

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hypnos_01
dialogueLines.text: Look, I have to say, I just don't think you have a real chance against both Theseus and the Minotaur, together! But, you might just have a chance to get the Minotaur's autograph for me, if maybe you ask nice or something?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hypnos_01
dialogueLines.text: Good, you're back! So, prior to when Theseus killed you again back there, did you, by any chance, happen to ask his big bull buddy for an autograph for me, this time?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hypnos_01
dialogueLines.text: Looks like somebody just found out that some types of butterflies are incredibly dangerous! Especially the ones that come swarming out of those Soul Catchers! If only you could just... not have a soul, or something? Then they'd bother somebody else!

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hypnos_01
dialogueLines.text: So, one of those Flame Wheels drove right into you, and burst into a fiery explosion, killing both of you, is that what happened there? Well if it makes you feel any better, technically, I think this means you got your revenge!

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hypnos_01
dialogueLines.text: You had a run-in with those nasty Satyrs all the way up in the Temple of Styx, huh. I can't even imagine the sheer agony, having their lethal poison coursing through my body!

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hypnos_01
dialogueLines.text: You know, I've only heard the tales of the gigantic, vicious, flesh-eating vermin up there near the surface. Now I guess we both know that those tales are definitely true!

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hypnos_01
dialogueLines.text: Eugh, it says here you got chewed up by an angry swarm of Crawlers somewhere just a stone's throw from the exit to your dad's domain? Talk about adding insult to injury, huh?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hypnos_01
dialogueLines.text: Oh, I heard the pests of Tartarus had bigger, meaner counterparts up near the surface, but... I guess you just found out the hard way, huh? When you tripped one of their explosive things?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hypnos_01
dialogueLines.text: I know that look, why, that's the look of someone just incinerated by a Snakestone, am I right? They spit a whole bunch of those white-hot, flesh-burning beams right at the same time, there, don't they?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hypnos_01
dialogueLines.text: Hey, you're back! Wait, wait, what happened? My list says you got killed by a Redacted, what's that even mean? I've never seen anything like this!

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hypnos_01
dialogueLines.text: Redacteds got you again, huh. Here's the thing, I keep asking around about them and no one seems to know what I'm talking about! Could you maybe describe them for me, please?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hypnos_01
dialogueLines.text: Huh, that's funny. Nothing on my list at all this time, except... I could have sworn I saw you walk right out of the Pool back there. I'll have to have this thing looked at.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hypnos_01
dialogueLines.text: The list is telling me you caught one of those gigantic Temple saw blades square in the chest, huh? Eugh... At least you're back in one piece!

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hypnos_01
dialogueLines.text: What's the matter, there, you got a splitting headache from those giant falling axes in the Temple of Styx? Well I have just the cure, which is: Don't get hit by them!

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hypnos_01
dialogueLines.text: So you met a violent end aboard the infamous Barge of Death sailing the river of flame! Surrounded by skeletons bleeding you dry, Voidstones making all of them invincible...! Have you thought about taking a different barge?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hypnos_01
dialogueLines.text: So, you just plain ran out of time and died, all according to the Pact of Punishment, there, huh? Now that is what I call a deadline, hahahaha!

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hypnos_01
dialogueLines.text: Going toe-to-toe with one of those Bloodless Bone-Rakers didn't go so well, huh? Next time they're ripping you to shreds, remember: That's your signal to be someplace else, instead!

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hypnos_01
dialogueLines.text: Wow, you just barged into the Witches Circle out in Asphodel, and they just filled you full of all those stinging purple orbs and killed you, huh? You can't just show up expecting to join their club! You have to study witchcraft for years!

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hypnos_01
dialogueLines.text: Hey, my brother Charon killed you, am I seeing this right? He's never been particularly nice to me! Whacked me with his oar a couple times, but it never got that bad. Must be some misunderstanding, have you talked to him?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hypnos_01
dialogueLines.text: You had it out with Charon again, huh? And turned out like it did before! The thing you have to understand is he's got lots of upper body strength. You seen the definition in his arms? Maybe try some rowing for yourself!

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hypnos_01
dialogueLines.text: Wait, what, you're back already? Hey, my list says that you barely made it out of here before you died! Were you even trying? Because if you were, why, maybe you need to try a whole lot harder than that next time!

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hypnos_01
dialogueLines.text: Why, says here you just blew yourself right up that time, with that weird infernal shooty thing you stole? Remember, next time, shoot the other guys!

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hypnos_01
dialogueLines.text: Ah, looks like you ran smack into the Doomstone out in Tartarus, thing gives me the creeps! Next time, try standing perfectly still. That way, it'll all be over that much faster!

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hypnos_01
dialogueLines.text: Mmm, you look like someone who just got turned to stone then crushed to a bloody pulp by the Megagorgon of Asphodel, am I right? Well, look, I'm sorry to hear that, and before you ask, I won't tell Dusa, don't you worry about that!

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hypnos_01
dialogueLines.text: Whoa, wait wait wait, this just can't be right, it says, it says... hahahahah, you got beaten by a tiny little rodent? Oh, haha, sorry I just, I, you have my sympathies.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hypnos_01
dialogueLines.text: Well somebody just walked into a cloud of noxious gas, there, huh! Must have lost all your vital functions pretty quickly after that! Next time, you'll know it's seriously deadly stuff, because it killed you! And because it's green!

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hypnos_01
dialogueLines.text: So, let me guess, one minute you were minding your own business out in Asphodel, and then the next, one of those Dracons pops up right in front of you and pfeh! Just kills you dead with noxious acid breath. Ah, well! It happens, right?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hypnos_01
dialogueLines.text: Whoa, what happened to you, you just... died? You just plain died, like... nothing stabbed you, bludgeoned you, poisoned you, burned you, crushed you, blew you to bits, turned you to stone, but... you're back here! Must have been those Natural Causes mortals talk about!

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hypnos_01
dialogueLines.text: Natural Causes got you again, huh? That's a real shame, I mean, imagine one day you're just going about your business, and then ack!! You're dead! Although I guess you don't have to imagine it like I do!

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hypnos_01
dialogueLines.text: Darned Natural Causes got you once again, there, didn't they? Well, you show them next time! Would you do that for me, huh?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hypnos_01
dialogueLines.text: So, you lost a duel to the death with an Exalted Brightsword, did you? They're pretty much the best of the best, so don't feel bad! It had to happen! But if it's any consolation, it'll probably happen again!

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hypnos_01
dialogueLines.text: If you got killed by Tisiphone the Fury, why, then it must mean you've been accused of murder! A most heinous crime, as I'm to understand! Well, I hope you clear your name. Or whatever those mortals do about that stuff!

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hypnos_01
dialogueLines.text: Hey, you're the latest victim of a Wretched Sneak, that's something, right? They like to get you when you least expect it, so next time, why not keep spinning around and around? Then you'll catch them trying to sneak up!

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hypnos_01
dialogueLines.text: Oh, you got throttled by a Wringer, must have grabbed and crushed you like a grape! You know what grapes are, right? They're like big, squishy pomegranate seeds.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hypnos_01
dialogueLines.text: Welcome to the House of Hades, where... wait, I know you! Guess that means you died out there, huh? Well don't be sad, though, pretty much everybody dies sometime!

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: Surprise, Hypnos! I got you this.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hypnos_01
dialogueLines.text: Whoa whoa whoa you can't bring that in here! Hah, just kidding, thanks! But wait, I'll trade you, look!

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hypnos_01
dialogueLines.text: Oh! Wow! Thanks! This is real nice of you, hah! I sure am glad you died out there just now!

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: I'm glad I died, as well, Hypnos! I'm glad I died, as well.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hypnos_01
dialogueLines.text: Ah, neat! Huh, thanks!! It's not so bad that you keep coming back like this, is it? At least for me it isn't!

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: I'm getting used to it. Though I don't think I'd feel particularly welcome anymore if not for you, Hypnos.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hypnos_01
dialogueLines.text: Whoaaa you must've really gotten pretty far out there if you could get me one of these, so, wow! And thanks for stopping by!

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: You lighten up my numerous untimely deaths, Hypnos.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hypnos_01
dialogueLines.text: Hah, well this is like a dream come true! I mean, how come you're always being so darn nice to me? But anyway, um, thanks!

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: Sure thing, Hypnos! Knock yourself out.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hypnos_01
dialogueLines.text: Oh you are just the best, I bet you died on purpose just to get this back to me, didn't you! Didn't you?

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: I have a secret, Hypnos. I've died on purpose every single time, just to see you.

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: Hypnos, wanted to say you have really helped the thought of my coming back here time after time, painful death by death, not be inherently abhorrent. Your greetings really help! So I wanted you to have this.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hypnos_01
dialogueLines.text: But, but, but, wait, this, it's Ambrosia! You are not supposed to have this here! We could both get in a lot of trouble, and I mean a lot, if they catch us in the middle of this totally forbidden exchange!

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: I notice that you haven't yet returned the bottle to me, which I'm going to take as an indication that you're going to hang onto it.

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: One more forbidden exchange, Hypnos. Sometimes when I'm out there and about to die, I catch myself thinking... well, at least Hypnos will share one of his deep insights about why it is I utterly failed. So here! For all your generous advice.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hypnos_01
dialogueLines.text: Aww, I... feel like I am going to cry, I mean, all my advice is free! You really didn't have to do this. But I sure am glad you did! Even if it is highly illegal! I... I... I...!

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: You what, Hypnos? You what?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hypnos_01
dialogueLines.text: ...I think you could be my best friend! Have you... tried being my best friend?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hypnos_01
dialogueLines.text: How's my best friend doing, huh?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hypnos_01
dialogueLines.text: You'll get them next time, right?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hypnos_01
dialogueLines.text: Don't let me keep you, huh?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hypnos_01
dialogueLines.text: Signed you in, you're good to go!

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hypnos_01
dialogueLines.text: I like having a best friend!

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hypnos_01
dialogueLines.text: Glad you died so I could talk to you!

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hypnos_01
dialogueLines.text: Nice to have a heart-to-heart like this, you know?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hypnos_01
dialogueLines.text: See you next time you die, OK?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hypnos_01
dialogueLines.text: Hope you enjoy your stay!

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hypnos_01
dialogueLines.text: I've got a survey here if you'd like to fill it out?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hypnos_01
dialogueLines.text: Welcome to the House of Hades! Just come on in!

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hypnos_01
dialogueLines.text: Welcome to the House of... oh, you know!

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hypnos_01
dialogueLines.text: Welcome back to our most frequent guest!

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hypnos_01
dialogueLines.text: There's no escape, in case you hadn't heard!

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hypnos_01
dialogueLines.text: I want you to go out there, and die some other way!

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hypnos_01
dialogueLines.text: You can do this! Whatever it is you're doing!

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hypnos_01
dialogueLines.text: It's usually pretty quiet, keeping watch over the Pool of Styx back there. But it's sure been busy lately! Why, somebody else came through just earlier! You should have seen the look on my face when it wasn't you!

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hypnos_01
dialogueLines.text: Oh! Hi. I'd ask you how it went, but since you're here again, I guess I know already, huh! Oh well, just come on in!

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hypnos_01
dialogueLines.text: Ah, good, you're back already, huh? A few of us, we had a bet, about how long you'd last out there, and I just knew you'd be back in no time!

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hypnos_01
dialogueLines.text: Oh, hi! Um, welcome home! Again! Go right ahead! I think your dad's mad about something or other, though.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hypnos_01
dialogueLines.text: Wow, you've died at least five times so far, I think? Dying's good for the whole ecosystem down here, so, uh, thanks! You're helping make the Underworld a better place!

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: Great! Just keep dying over and over and everything will be fine.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hypnos_01
dialogueLines.text: It's fun watching the Pool of Styx, you know? The ripples in the blood, and everything, and sometimes, somebody pops out of there, like you! House staff and guests of honor, no one else. I sign them in, and help make sure they have a pleasant stay!

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hypnos_01
dialogueLines.text: Ooh, why you made it all the way to Asphodel that time, that's pretty neat! Still died and everything but not as quickly as you used to, so, that's progress, right?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hypnos_01
dialogueLines.text: Hey, did you see?! The Queen's come back! Though, did she ever take her sweet time!

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: Hey watch it, Hypnos, that's my mother you're talking about!

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hypnos_01
dialogueLines.text: What, really? Huh! So then your mom doesn't spend any time with you, either! We've got something in common!

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hypnos_01
dialogueLines.text: Wait, wait, wait, so what's all this about you working here? I thought they fired you, and that was why you left!

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: Hypnos... you think all these times I've died, it's because I was upset I was let go from a miserable job?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hypnos_01
dialogueLines.text: I heard the feast you had here, it was really something else! All the gods, together in one place, eating, drinking, not killing one another! That must have been a sight.

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: You should have been there, Hypnos! Not that I noticed your absence, though, what happened? Other pressing business?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hypnos_01
dialogueLines.text: Slept through the whole thing!

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: Hey, Hypnos? What would it take for you to let me through the locked door down the hall?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hypnos_01
dialogueLines.text: Oh, hahaha, that's a good one, you know I can't let you enter your dad's private chambers anymore, since you betrayed his trust and tried to run away from home! Sorry, Master's orders!

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hypnos_01
dialogueLines.text: Hey, let me be the first to say: Congratulations! You have died a hundred times since I've been keeping score. Since I've been keeping track. No prize for it or anything, but the journey is its own reward, or something, right?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hypnos_01
dialogueLines.text: Hey, wow, you did it! According to my list it says here you've died, what, fifty times at least! That's more than anybody's died around here, I think!

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hypnos_01
dialogueLines.text: You're not going to believe this, here, you ready? Three.. hundred... times!! That's right! Three hundred times, you've died! That's like... more than an entire village from an earthquake, all on your own!

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hypnos_01
dialogueLines.text: Oh, I just can't believe it, although I know my list here never lies! And it says that you have died all of five hundred times! Five hundred!! I can't even think of a number higher than that!

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hades_01
dialogueLines.text: ...You're late with your report, Hypnos. Again. Again! The dead of course can wait, but I'm less patient. Now, explain yourself to me.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hypnos_01
dialogueLines.text: Oh! Well, let's see, I woke up, I don't know what time it was, and I made sure that every shade had been accounted for, just like I always do, except I couldn't find my list at first, can you believe it? I thought maybe my brother played a little prank, and so...

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hades_01
dialogueLines.text: Get to the blasted point already, I don't have all day! Or night. Urgh! Your report, where is it?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hypnos_01
dialogueLines.text: Sure I was just getting to it, Master! My list, I found it over by the lounge, it was inside, but I am not the one who put it there, no sir! So I've been making positively sure it's not been tampered with, that it has everybody's name, cause of death, place of birth, former body weight, any previous allergies or medications...

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hades_01
dialogueLines.text: Enough!! Blood and darkness, that's enough from you, just... see to it the ledger of the dead is accurate, and get back to your post. Ugh, would that you could be more like your brother.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hades_01
dialogueLines.text: ...Your reports have been much more punctual of late, Hypnos. That is to be expected. However... the illustrated edges... a clever detail. Ensures certain reports are easily procured from a stack of parchment.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hypnos_01
dialogueLines.text: Oh, thanks, Master, glad you like those! Ever since Thanatos made pretty clear to me I was about to lose my job, I started keeping a list of my own tasks, which I sort through each day, deciding on priority, estimating time to completion, cost-benefit analysis...

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hades_01
dialogueLines.text: Yes, well. Certainly, then, you have a number of such tasks requiring your attention. Continue working diligently, then.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hypnos_01
dialogueLines.text: Master, I just can't thank you enough for putting my picture on the board there in the lounge! I heard it's a good picture! I haven't had time to see it yet, but I am going to, it's on the list!

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hades_01
dialogueLines.text: Hypnos... that shall be all. Lest I rescind your commendation and cast you out of here.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hypnos_01
dialogueLines.text: ....Um, could you say all that again with smaller words, Master, because I thought it maybe sounded kind of bad?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hypnos_01
dialogueLines.text: ...I just get sleepy, sometimes, Mom, I mean, I've been working so much, lately, just over there? You've probably seen me, greeting shades, making my list, creating a welcoming environment...

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Nyx_01
dialogueLines.text: Remember what we previously spoke of, Hypnos. Do not address me thus whilst we are here. Conduct yourself as befitting a servant of the House of Hades... and, continue practicing your independence, please.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Nyx_01
dialogueLines.text: You have demonstrated sustained competence in your duties, Hypnos. It gladdens me to see measurable progress. Your efforts are to be commended.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hypnos_01
dialogueLines.text: Aw, wow, thanks! Commended by whom, exactly? Megaera? Thanatos? Dusa, maybe! Not the Master himself?!

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Nyx_01
dialogueLines.text: Commended by me. I cannot always look after all of my children, as you know. It falls upon them to take care of themselves, as well as their obligations to our domain. As your mother... it is reassuring to see that you are capable.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hypnos_01
dialogueLines.text: Capable? Me? Aw, Mom... I mean, um, Mother Nyx! Nobody's ever called me that before!

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Nyx_01
dialogueLines.text: You are my son. It is to be expected. However... I know it is not effortless, to be of use. Continue to do well. I shall be watching.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hypnos_01
dialogueLines.text: ...And if you ever need anything at all, Your Royalness, you can always find me wide awake, right over there! Though I really shouldn't leave my post like this! Not even for you!

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_FurySister_01
dialogueLines.text: Can't you see I'm busy now with more important things than you?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_FurySister_01
dialogueLines.text: May I help you somehow, Zagreus? Because if not, why don't you leave me be.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_FurySister_01
dialogueLines.text: May I help you somehow, Zagreus, because if not, why don't you leave me be?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_FurySister_01
dialogueLines.text: Do I look like I'm in the mood to chat?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_FurySister_01
dialogueLines.text: Do I look like I'm in the mood to chat?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_FurySister_01
dialogueLines.text: You've come to gloat, I guess? Too bad I can't just kill you where you stand, within this blasted House.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_FurySister_01
dialogueLines.text: Have I somehow created the impression that I wish to speak with you? Because if so, there's been a terrible mistake.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_FurySister_01
dialogueLines.text: There must be someone else whose time you could be wasting, Zagreus.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_FurySister_01
dialogueLines.text: It's embarrassing enough having returned here, much less to be seen talking to you.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_FurySister_01
dialogueLines.text: Sooner or later when we meet out there again, I'll kill you, Zagreus.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_FurySister_01
dialogueLines.text: If I have something I decide I wish to tell you, Zagreus, I'll make it clear. Leave me alone.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_FurySister_01
dialogueLines.text: Your father's absolutely right about you, Zag. You are and always will be an insufferable brat.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_FurySister_01
dialogueLines.text: Don't test me any further, Zagreus. You know the rules, no violence in the House.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_FurySister_01
dialogueLines.text: Blood and darkness, leave me be, damn you. I've better things to do than occupy your time.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_FurySister_01
dialogueLines.text: I have to hand it to you, Zag. You've gotten strong. Sending me all the way back here like this.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_FurySister_01
dialogueLines.text: Nyx must be worried, seeing us like this. Lately always at each other's throats.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_FurySister_01
dialogueLines.text: Oh don't worry, I'll be back in fighting shape in no time, Zag.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_FurySister_01
dialogueLines.text: Your father's not been very pleased with my performance lately as you might have guessed.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_FurySister_01
dialogueLines.text: Sometimes, Zagreus, I'm almost not detested by the sight and smell of you.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_FurySister_01
dialogueLines.text: I shouldn't be back here right now. No thanks to you, of course.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_FurySister_01
dialogueLines.text: I'm keeping careful track of all the times you've sent me home like this.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_FurySister_01
dialogueLines.text: At least the House seems well enough in order since I saw it last.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_FurySister_01
dialogueLines.text: We keep on running into one another lately, don't we Zag.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_FurySister_01
dialogueLines.text: You got me fair and square last time. Though I'm a little bitter anyway.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_FurySister_01
dialogueLines.text: You know we can't be seen together like this, Zagreus.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_FurySister_01
dialogueLines.text: I should be whipping Sisyphus' lazy hide right now instead of standing here.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_FurySister_01
dialogueLines.text: Don't like having to come back here quite this often, Zagreus.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_FurySister_01
dialogueLines.text: I have to speak with you in private, Zagreus. Urgently.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_FurySister_01
dialogueLines.text: It's fortunate that matter is resolved. I'll see you out there, then.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_FurySister_01
dialogueLines.text: Let's go someplace a little quieter than this. Where we can concentrate.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_FurySister_01
dialogueLines.text: A little peace and quiet's good from time to time, don't you agree?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_FurySister_01
dialogueLines.text: Don't even say a word, and follow me.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_FurySister_01
dialogueLines.text: Well, Zagreus? Take care until we meet again, I guess.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_FurySister_01
dialogueLines.text: Zagreus, did you forget about our private tutoring, again? Come on.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_FurySister_01
dialogueLines.text: There. I take it you'll remember what I've taught you, hm?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_FurySister_01
dialogueLines.text: Come with me, Zagreus. Your chambers are past due for a routine inspection.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_FurySister_01
dialogueLines.text: Well! Seems everything's in order. Carry on.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_FurySister_01
dialogueLines.text: If you're available right now, I have some parchmentwork that needs review.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_FurySister_01
dialogueLines.text: Hmm. Less parchmentwork than I expected. Well, thank you for your time.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_FurySister_01
dialogueLines.text: I can't believe you got me like that, Zag. I'm going to get you back.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_FurySister_01
dialogueLines.text: Just here licking my wounds until we meet again out there.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_FurySister_01
dialogueLines.text: Heard you made it pretty far last time.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_FurySister_01
dialogueLines.text: You almost got there last time, didn't you.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_FurySister_01
dialogueLines.text: Heard you made it all the way last time.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_FurySister_01
dialogueLines.text: You're back. Got all the way to the surface back there, huh?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_FurySister_01
dialogueLines.text: Keep letting down my guard against you, Zag.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_FurySister_01
dialogueLines.text: Went all the way to the top last time, huh? Not bad at all.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_FurySister_01
dialogueLines.text: Ugh, I don't remember the last time I beat you, Zag...

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_FurySister_01
dialogueLines.text: Break's just about over. See you out there?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_FurySister_01
dialogueLines.text: Hypnos give you any good pointers lately?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_FurySister_01
dialogueLines.text: Taking a break from putting up with Alecto out there...

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_FurySister_01
dialogueLines.text: Got to say, I like what you and Dusa did with this place.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_FurySister_01
dialogueLines.text: Didn't rough you up too bad last time, did I?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_FurySister_01
dialogueLines.text: You showed Alecto again. She must be so mad.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_FurySister_01
dialogueLines.text: About ready to go another round with me out there?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_FurySister_01
dialogueLines.text: I'm not even remotely interested in chatting with you, Zagreus.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_FurySister_01
dialogueLines.text: Why don't you go get out of here if you despise this place so much?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_FurySister_01
dialogueLines.text: Don't you so much as dare talk to me.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_FurySister_01
dialogueLines.text: Get out of my sight, Zagreus. And be thankful violence is prohibited in here.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_FurySister_01
dialogueLines.text: Right now I wouldn't talk to me if I were you.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_FurySister_01
dialogueLines.text: I think you'd best be on your way right now, you understand me, Zagreus?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_FurySister_01
dialogueLines.text: I think you've wasted quite enough of both our time, don't you?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_FurySister_01
dialogueLines.text: Whatever's on your mind, believe me, it's the last thing that I want to hear right now.

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: Alecto's really something else. She must have been a real handful, huh?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_FurySister_01
dialogueLines.text: My youngest sister takes a lot of pleasure from her work. I've learned something of that from her. But that's as far as we relate, I think.

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: She could learn a thing or two from you, I'm sure. About having a little common decency, perhaps.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_FurySister_01
dialogueLines.text: She won't be learning anything from me. The contempt you get from her is unconditional. I learned not to take it personally.

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: Meg, you know what's going on with Than and me, don't you? I just... want things to be out in the open, between us.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_FurySister_01
dialogueLines.text: Oh I know everything about you, Zag. And Thanatos, maybe even better. Though I appreciate you telling me. It's fine.

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: It's fine? I mean... upon Olympus, jealousy runs rampant, as I'm told. But here it's not even an issue, then?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_FurySister_01
dialogueLines.text: If you prefer the way they do things on Olympus, then I think you know the way. As for me... dealing with jealousy is what I do. Tell me, should I feel threatened by Thanatos?

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: No... he thinks so highly of you, he... he'd never wish to cause you any harm.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_FurySister_01
dialogueLines.text: And I feel much the same. He and I, we're not the same to you. I think we both see that, and understand. Now quit your second-guessing me, all right?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_FurySister_01
dialogueLines.text: I did not appreciate you sending Dusa after me out there, Zag. You leave her out of this! Though... it was good to see her out of this damned House for a change.

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: See, I, too, thought she could use the fresh air. Or... however you'd describe the quality of the air on the edge of Tartarus. You know she'd never mean you any harm, right? In her mind... I think she's trying to keep it friendly between us.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_FurySister_01
dialogueLines.text: Eugh, she said the same thing to me. Look, you know what, fine. If that's the only way to get her to take a break from this place, I guess it's for the best.

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: I'm so proud of Dusa lately, Meg! Chief Custodian of the House, was it? That's quite an honor coming from Nyx.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_FurySister_01
dialogueLines.text: I thought all the added responsibility would stress Dusa out worse than usual. But she's taking it in stride.

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: You're always looking out for her. Thanks for that.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_FurySister_01
dialogueLines.text: Not doing it for you. Though... thanks for looking out for her, yourself.

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: Meg, what's going on with Nyx and Dusa, do you know? I think they've been butting heads a bit lately.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_FurySister_01
dialogueLines.text: I can't imagine why. Nyx has her under strict orders, yet you keep on stopping by for your little chats. You think she doesn't notice?

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: What does she care? I talk to everybody around here at least a bit.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_FurySister_01
dialogueLines.text: It's not my business, Zag. Not even for Dusa. But you watch yourself.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_FurySister_01
dialogueLines.text: Damn you, Zagreus. She was my friend. And now she's gone.

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: Who, you mean Dusa? Like I had anything to do with that. It's Nyx you should be cross with, not me!

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_FurySister_01
dialogueLines.text: I don't think so. Nyx warned you. Ungh, you know I can't go to her myself about this, so fix it!

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: You saw Dusa's back, right, Meg? She's getting another chance. Nyx reconsidered!

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_FurySister_01
dialogueLines.text: I saw. Nyx doesn't often change her mind when it comes to this House. But I won't second guess what happened or why.

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: You don't sound all that pleased...

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: You're not too broken up about how things ended between you and Hypnos, are you, Meg?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_FurySister_01
dialogueLines.text: I'm still in a state of shock. I'll have to pick up the pieces somehow, and figure out how to get on with my existence.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_FurySister_01
dialogueLines.text: How's my little Battie doing, Zag? The one I gave you. Was just a little pup. I figured she'd be better off with you. Please tell me I was right.

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: Battie's doing great, Meg. Reminds me of you every time I think of her. Helps keep me company throughout my little jaunts outside. So you've a thing for bats, then?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_FurySister_01
dialogueLines.text: That's good. And yes, the bats around here, they and I have always shared an understanding, you could say. I usually care for the pups myself. But Battie? She seemed more your style.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_FurySister_01
dialogueLines.text: You got that court musician to pipe up again. I know you can't shut up, but didn't know it was contagious.

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: Hey, it was all Orpheus, not me. Can't sing like that without a lot of motivation, inner turmoil, who knows what else. Why, don't you like it? This lounge seems better with a little song.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_FurySister_01
dialogueLines.text: This lounge is fine. And the singing... not used to it yet.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_FurySister_01
dialogueLines.text: Zagreus. I heard Lord Hades served you with a Pact of Punishment. You must be very proud.

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: What, you mean the gigantic infernal contract reserved only for extraordinary circumstances? Yeah, I suppose I'm pleased that Father's gone to all that trouble just for me.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_FurySister_01
dialogueLines.text: Well, when you're finished being smug about it? Know that all of us are bound by its terms. You can force a lot of things to change if you're not careful.

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: A lot of things, such as how you refuse to reason with your positively charming sisters, Meg?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_FurySister_01
dialogueLines.text: The Extreme Measures condition... you wouldn't dare. Do you have any idea what you're talking about?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_FurySister_01
dialogueLines.text: What are you doing here? I heard you finally made it out. What, did you break out of here, only to die in some pathetic way on the surface?

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: Long story, but yeah. Boils down to what you said, more or less.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_FurySister_01
dialogueLines.text: So you're going back out there, again. Well, it's your business, not mine. For your sake... I hope it's worth it.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_FurySister_01
dialogueLines.text: What are you doing here? I heard you finally made it out. What, did you break out of here, only to die in some pathetic way on the surface?

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: Long story, but yeah. Boils down to what you said, more or less.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_FurySister_01
dialogueLines.text: So you're going back out there, again. Well, it's your business, not mine. For your sake... I hope it's worth it.

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: Meg, did you... did you ever meet the Queen...? Remember anything about her, at all?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_FurySister_01
dialogueLines.text: It was not my place to address her. I barely ever saw her. Then, at some point... I never saw her again. And, unlike you, I know better than to get involved in things that are none of my business.

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: How is it that this place has this secret Queen for I don't know how long, then suddenly she just vanishes, and no one speaks a word of it?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_FurySister_01
dialogueLines.text: How is she doing, Zagreus? The Queen, I mean. She was... I had admired her.

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: Mother... she's doing well, I think. She's in good spirits every time we meet. Happy to let her know you're thinking of her, by the way!

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_FurySister_01
dialogueLines.text: Don't, it's... it isn't necessary. She doesn't need you telling her about this place. Sounds to me like she is better off without.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_FurySister_01
dialogueLines.text: Well, Zag? You like things nice and complicated, don't you. Apparently so do I.

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: If you're asking if I have any regrets about what happened between us, the answer is no. Being with you... any complications there are worth it.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_FurySister_01
dialogueLines.text: You don't need to tell me. Two rules, if you want to keep this up. First... we set the personal aside when we're out there. I better not hear a soul say I'm going soft on you. And second... keep up this slightly more mature thing you've had going for you recently.

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: Wasn't planning on sliding back into the insensitive nonsense of youth, so I don't think the second thing will be a problem. But the first... can we really just keep killing each other out there like nothing happened?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_FurySister_01
dialogueLines.text: Tsk... Come off it, Zag. Are you really going to act all uncomfortable every time you see me now? More than usual I mean.

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: No, it's just... I felt terrible, turning you away. It's just... I've been so confused lately, and... I trust you. You're someone I can talk to. I guess I'm... afraid to lose that.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_FurySister_01
dialogueLines.text: Don't be an idiot, and you won't. We've been through a lot together. Grown, maybe. This particular relationship... it can't die so easily, I think.

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: I feel like everybody knows about us, Meg. Even Aphrodite, herself, seemed to know we were involved. It's not like I've been going around boasting. You wanted to keep things quiet, didn't you?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_FurySister_01
dialogueLines.text: Zagreus. Are you happy with how things are between us now? Or do you have a problem with it?

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: Well, I could do without the kill-or-be-killed stuff if we're being honest, that part is still a little weird, but otherwise... yes, I mean... I'm happy. You're you.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_FurySister_01
dialogueLines.text: Then, that's enough. Let them say what they want. What does it matter? We're gods. The things they say about us... even other gods... you pay too much attention to it, it'll drive you out of your mind.

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: But... I know how important your career is to you. This won't create a problem?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_FurySister_01
dialogueLines.text: We've already crossed that bridge, I think. Stop worrying.

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: Megaera... you ever been in love with somebody?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_FurySister_01
dialogueLines.text: ...Hm. Yes.

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: Who?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_FurySister_01
dialogueLines.text: ...Shut up.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_FurySister_01
dialogueLines.text: Got to tell you something, Zagreus. I'm not getting any more used to losing to you like this. I mean... you don't even stop to rub it in. You know how humiliating that is?

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: Wait, you're saying you would feel less ashamed about the outcomes of our fights if I were to gloat more about beating you? How does that make any sense?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_FurySister_01
dialogueLines.text: You know what doesn't make any sense? That you keep beating me. I must be getting sloppy if I'm that predictable... too bad none of the other wretches I have to deal with put up much of a fight.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_FurySister_01
dialogueLines.text: Not seen the court musician sulking over there quite as much lately. Your doing, I hear?

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: You'll just have to sulk for the both of you, I guess, then, Meg. But if you must know, on my authority I have released Orpheus from the terms of his old, rather unfair agreement with Father, so he can see his wife at least from time to time.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_FurySister_01
dialogueLines.text: And Lord Hades is OK with you just picking out old binding contracts and nullifying them? You're exploiting your status.

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: I'm doing no such thing. Father wanted me involved in all this business with his pacts, and presiding over the dead, then fine. But I'll do it my way.

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: You and your sisters really don't get along well, do you.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_FurySister_01
dialogueLines.text: Alecto and Tisiphone and I each have a different outlook, I would say. We've learned mostly to stay out of one another's way.

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: That doesn't sound like a healthy basis for a relationship between siblings.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_FurySister_01
dialogueLines.text: Are you lecturing me about healthy relationships with family, Zagreus? Your family, your father's family, is the most broken and corrupted in the history of the entire concept.

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: ...Fair point. I... didn't mean to sound like I had any meaningful advice, there, Meg. We're stuck with our bloodlines and our relatives, aren't we.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_FurySister_01
dialogueLines.text: Your family cut their Titan parents into pieces and then scattered them in Tartarus. Remember that when next you tell me about family.

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: You know, while it was brutal squaring off against the three of you, I have to say, Meg... I was happy you were with your sisters. Nothing like a common cause for bringing bitter rivals together, right?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_FurySister_01
dialogueLines.text: You have your Pact of Punishment to thank for our reunion, there. We have to answer to its terms. But my sisters and I, we got on better than I had expected, I will give you that.

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: So you've patched things up, then? Going to start spending more quality time together and all that?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_FurySister_01
dialogueLines.text: Hm, let's not get carried away. A work assignment forcing our cooperation is not exactly a big step in the quality of our relationship.

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: Hey, Meg, I understand you're under orders to kill me on sight out there, but... do you really have to go so hard on Sisyphus?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_FurySister_01
dialogueLines.text: That wretched shade isn't who you think he is. Do you have any idea of the magnitude of his crimes from when he led a mortal life?

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: He cheated death. Tricked Thanatos and maybe Father, too.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_FurySister_01
dialogueLines.text: Before that, he did worse. He was an awful man. I think that is the real reason that your father punished him.

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: What if he's changed? Or... is that impossible. No coming back from certain crimes.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_FurySister_01
dialogueLines.text: No coming back from certain crimes, indeed. Though you may have more mercy in your heart to spare than I in mine.

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: I have to ask you for a favor, Meg. And I'm willing to make it worth your while, if you'll hear me out.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_FurySister_01
dialogueLines.text: A proposition, Zagreus? Let's hear it, then. While I'm in a charitable mood.

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: It's about Sisyphus. If I can get my father to alleviate his sentence, would you just... ease off of him, a little bit? You and your sisters. I think he's suffered quite enough under your whips.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_FurySister_01
dialogueLines.text: I don't know what you see in him, I swear. But you don't need to ask such things of me. If you can overturn his sentence with Lord Hades, I'd like nothing better than to never see that miserable shade again.

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: You and Thanatos don't work together much anymore, do you, Meg?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_FurySister_01
dialogueLines.text: No. Though he still brings me my assignments every now and then.

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: Oathbreakers, traitors, and the like? Sending them straight to Tartarus, huh. Must be quite an experience going from living and breathing, to... being dead, to being whisked off by Thanatos, straight to you and your whip.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_FurySister_01
dialogueLines.text: If that's not the experience mortals are looking for, they need only keep their oaths and not betray the ones they're closest to. It's not that hard.

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: Erm, so, about Tisiphone, is... uh... everything all right with her? Talk of murder, all the time, and all.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_FurySister_01
dialogueLines.text: Tisiphone is charged with punishing the worst that mortals have to offer. More than charged. Her work is everything to her.

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: No time for family or recreation, then, I take it? Pity what happens to those overly obsessed with work.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_FurySister_01
dialogueLines.text: I get what you're insinuating, Zagreus. I'm nothing like her. Not even close. And don't you dare compare the two of us again.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_FurySister_01
dialogueLines.text: So you've been trying to give Tisiphone speech lessons between fights to the death. Are you bored, or just naive?

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: Come on, if her vocabulary consists of two different although admittedly almost identical words, there's got to be room for growth, there. Besides, I feel like... maybe she's lonely, or something. Has anyone ever tried to teach her anything?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_FurySister_01
dialogueLines.text: Maybe, just maybe, you're projecting your own insecurities onto her a little bit? Tis can take care of herself. Always has. Talking's not her strong suit like it's yours. But if you, in all your wisdom, think you've got something to teach her, you go right ahead.

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: You've tried already, haven't you? She's pretty dead-set on the murder thing, then?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_FurySister_01
dialogueLines.text: Never in my wildest fantasies did I imagine you'd ever end up on the Featured Houseservant Board, Zagreus. I still can't entirely believe it.

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: Whoa, back up! Can you elaborate a bit about these wildest fantasies, Meg?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_FurySister_01
dialogueLines.text: I'm serious. You should be proud. Not arrogantly proud to a fatal fault or anything, but a little bit is all right in a case like this.

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: Megaera, I have to ask you something, please. We really need to talk.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_FurySister_01
dialogueLines.text: ...For once, I think you're right. Although, I'm curious, what is it that you think we have to talk about?

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: I still have feelings for you. At least... I think I do. Isn't there... isn't there someway for us to, you know... work through all of this?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_FurySister_01
dialogueLines.text: In spite of everything that's happened, and that's happening right now? What are the outcomes, Zagreus? Either you eventually succeed, and leave this place, or else, what, exactly?

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: ...I haven't thought that far ahead. But I don't think it has to be this way. I don't hate you, Meg. I don't think I can ever hate you.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_FurySister_01
dialogueLines.text: ...Despite my best intentions. Unfortunately, I don't know that I can say the same. Look, I'm at a very difficult point in my career. I need to focus on that now. Do we understand each other?

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: Megaera, I have to ask you something, please. We really need to talk.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_FurySister_01
dialogueLines.text: ...For once, I think you're right. Although, I'm curious, what is it that you think we have to talk about?

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: I still have feelings for you. At least... I think I do. Isn't there... isn't there someway for us to, you know... work through all of this?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_FurySister_01
dialogueLines.text: In spite of everything that's happened, and that's happening right now? What are the outcomes, Zagreus? What, exactly?

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: ...I haven't thought that far ahead. But I don't think it has to be this way. I don't hate you, Meg. I don't think I can ever hate you.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_FurySister_01
dialogueLines.text: ...Despite my best intentions. Unfortunately, I don't know that I can say the same. Look, I'm at a very difficult point in my career. I need to focus on that now. Do we understand each other?

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: What do you think of all the lounge decor, there, Meg? Looking a little better these days, wouldn't you say?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_FurySister_01
dialogueLines.text: You know, it isn't bad. Though I have heard the strangest thing, that you're responsible, for all of this? I couldn't understand why you would even bother, honestly.

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: Well, it's a popular locale for off-duty Underworld officers to take a load off between shifts, and so I figure... least I can do is pitch in and make the place a little better than I left it, right?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_FurySister_01
dialogueLines.text: How very selfless of you, Zagreus. Soon doubtless it'll be your portrait hanging on that wall back there.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_FurySister_01
dialogueLines.text: The prism-sphere in the corner is maybe a little much. But the fireplace... the bat-cage... the aquarium... this lounge is practically comfortable. Lord Hades would strongly disapprove.

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: Good thing he never sets foot in here, then, huh? Wait, you don't miss when things were all sad and dreary in here, do you?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_FurySister_01
dialogueLines.text: I'm still getting used to it. But, no.

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: Hey, uh, Meg? Look, I know you're just doing your job out there, and, anyway, found this, thought of you and all that, so... here.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_FurySister_01
dialogueLines.text: I... I'll see to it this contraband is put away at once. Now, take your finder's fee and go about your business.

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: Hey, Meg! I, uh, thought I'd better hand this over.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_FurySister_01
dialogueLines.text: I'll confiscate that if you really want. Although, I'm warning you, I'm not susceptible to bribes. Many have tried.

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: Hey I'm just handing over contraband to the appropriate authorities, think nothing of it, Meg. Just doing our jobs, right?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_FurySister_01
dialogueLines.text: Tsch. You're serious. You're really giving that to me. I mean, I'll take it off your hands if that is really what you want.

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: It's really what I want. And, I sincerely hope we can move past our differences. Not that turning in this contraband has anything to do with that.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_FurySister_01
dialogueLines.text: Such gifts won't stay my whip when next we meet out there, you realize that, right?

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: I realize it. And I hope you never change, Meg.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_FurySister_01
dialogueLines.text: Flattery won't get you anywhere. We can't go back to how things used to be.

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: Flattery never got me anywhere with you to begin with, Meg. Doesn't mean I won't keep trying. I am ever stubborn, aren't I.

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: Got you some more of that contraband you like. Don't suppose I could help you put it away or anything...?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_FurySister_01
dialogueLines.text: Look at me, Zag.... You think that all these gifts will make things go back to the way they were? They won't. It isn't possible. We may be immortals but we're older now.

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: I'd hope we're wiser, too.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_FurySister_01
dialogueLines.text: Ambrosia... you know the penalty for attempting to sway an Overseer of the House of Hades with foreign contraband, don't you?

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: I can't entirely say I do, though if it involves lashings and whatnot, I already get more than my share of that, so... figured I would take the risk. So what do you say?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_FurySister_01
dialogueLines.text: I say hand over the damn bottle. I'll see that it's disposed of properly. Now as for you... you wouldn't know who misplaced something like this, would you? On my authority as Overseer... hang onto it. Though if you tell so much as anyone...!

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: I had this awful contraband I wanted to turn in as soon as possible, if you don't mind taking it from me, Meg.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_FurySister_01
dialogueLines.text: Tsch... you still have no idea how to be up front with me, do you, Zag. Why don't you tell me why you're here, and what you want.

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: Erm... so the whole contraband thing, you aren't buying it, I guess? Although I see you took the bottle there, now, anyway... I wanted just to talk to you. And patch things up, maybe.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_FurySister_01
dialogueLines.text: Patch things up. What is it, by your estimation, that could use repairs, between the two of us, hm?

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: I... I was naive. I tried not to get too close, but then I did, and said a lot of stupid things, and then... well, we've since tried to kill each other lots of times, there's that. Look, I am sorry, Meg.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_FurySister_01
dialogueLines.text: You said a lot of stupid things, all right. And we Furies, we're not much for forgiveness or apologies. But... I can see you're making an attempt. Now, thank you for the contraband. Good-bye.

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: Here, Meg. Contraband. I wish I could do over how we started.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_FurySister_01
dialogueLines.text: I, too, wish for a lot of things. Unfortunately there's no unraveling the Fates' patterns. We can learn from our mistakes or keep repeating them. That's it.

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: What if I've learned from my mistakes? Have I ever done anything to hurt you since we've been like this? Discounting the unavoidable and frequent fights to the death.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_FurySister_01
dialogueLines.text: There's nothing you can do to hurt me, Zag. It's just a question of whether you're worth my time.

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: Well... am I worth your time, then, Meg?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_FurySister_01
dialogueLines.text: You know... let me get back to you on that. I need a little time to think it through.

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: Hey Meg, you're off duty, aren't you? Look, I was just... hoping you might want to split this with me, best stuff I could find out there, and I would give it all to you, it's just... maybe you wouldn't mind some company. From me.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_FurySister_01
dialogueLines.text: You sound a little tongue-tied, Zagreus. Just like you always used to around me.

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: You're not exactly easy to approach, you know. So... how about it, then? Care for a drink with me?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_FurySister_01
dialogueLines.text: Open it up, and we can go from there.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_FurySister_01
dialogueLines.text: ...You should have seen the look on your face. You act a lot more confidently now. Though, I think, deep down, you are still that inexperienced little godling that you used to be.

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: And I think, deep down, you are not the heartless harbinger of retribution that you want for everyone to think you are. But I can keep your secret if you're willing to keep mine, what do you say?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_FurySister_01
dialogueLines.text: Tsch... that sounds an awful lot like blackmail, Zagreus. You'd stoop to that, with me?

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: No, I... I was just, that's not what I meant at all...

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_FurySister_01
dialogueLines.text: Hahaha, I just want to see you squirm. Thanks for the company and for the drink. Guess I'll be seeing you around.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_FurySister_01
dialogueLines.text: Tsch. The prodigal son returns.... Everyone's saying I went easy on you, Zag.

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: Don't worry, Meg. I eventually managed to get home the painful way after all.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_FurySister_01
dialogueLines.text: Look, I've a reputation to uphold. You get past me again like that, you best go all the way. Now, leave me be, and don't think you're going to be so lucky next we meet out there.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_FurySister_01
dialogueLines.text: Tsch. The prodigal son returns.... Everyone's saying I went easy on you, Zag.

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: Don't worry, Meg. I eventually managed to get home the painful way after all.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_FurySister_01
dialogueLines.text: Look, I've a reputation to uphold. You get past me again like that, you best go all the way. Now, leave me be, and don't think you're going to be so lucky next we meet out there.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_FurySister_01
dialogueLines.text: Tsch. The prodigal son returns.... Everyone's saying I went easy on you, Zag.

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: Don't worry, Meg. I eventually managed to get home the painful way after all. Though... I managed to get much farther than you'd probably expect.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_FurySister_01
dialogueLines.text: I expected you to not get past me, yes. Look, I've a reputation to uphold. Don't think you're going to be so lucky next we meet out there.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_FurySister_01
dialogueLines.text: Tsch. The prodigal son returns.... Everyone's saying I went easy on you, Zag.

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: Don't worry, Meg. I eventually managed to get home the painful way after all. Though... I managed to get much farther than you'd probably expect.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_FurySister_01
dialogueLines.text: I expected you to not get past me, yes. Look, I've a reputation to uphold. Don't think you're going to be so lucky next we meet out there.

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: Hey, uh, what's that you're drinking, Meg? Anything good?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_FurySister_01
dialogueLines.text: Whatever it is, it's weak. Enough to be mistaken for your blood.

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: Nice! Well, I'll just leave you to it, then.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_FurySister_01
dialogueLines.text: I'll tear you limb from limb out there next time, you mark my words.

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: What are your sisters up to these days, Meg? I've not seen them around.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_FurySister_01
dialogueLines.text: We're stretched a little thin, as you can see. Some kind of war up there meant lots of business here. I'm left to hold the line. And deal with you.

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: What are your sisters up to these days, Meg? I've not seen them around.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_FurySister_01
dialogueLines.text: We're stretched a little thin, as you can see. Some kind of war up there meant lots of business here. I'm left to hold the line. And deal with you.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_FurySister_01
dialogueLines.text: What do you want from me, leave me alone. We've nothing to discuss here anymore.

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: I was just checking up on you, just... let me know if you wanted to talk, for any reason.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_FurySister_01
dialogueLines.text: I am not interested in having company, especially from you. You stay the hell away while I'm off duty.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_FurySister_01
dialogueLines.text: I told you to back off.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_FurySister_01
dialogueLines.text: I told you to back off.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_FurySister_01
dialogueLines.text: What do you want from me, leave me alone. We've nothing to discuss here anymore.

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: I was just checking up on you, just... let me know if you wanted to talk, for any reason.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_FurySister_01
dialogueLines.text: I am not interested in having company, especially from you. You stay the hell away while I'm off duty.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_FurySister_01
dialogueLines.text: Excuse me, is there something I can do for you, Prince Zagreus?

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: It's nothing, I just thought I'd say, that was well fought back there.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_FurySister_01
dialogueLines.text: There's no returning to the way things used to be, Zag. Now can I offer you some words of advice? Get over yourself. And get ready to die out there next time we meet.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_FurySister_01
dialogueLines.text: Zagreus. Good, I wanted to tell you something. Your victories against me? They don't count for anything.

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: Sounds like you've been giving this a lot of thought, there, Meg. How do you figure?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_FurySister_01
dialogueLines.text: Nyx's Mirror. You couldn't possibly beat me without its help, or hers.

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: I'm not competing with you, Meg. I'm trying to get past you, to the surface.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_FurySister_01
dialogueLines.text: Call it what you want. You're doing what you have to do. I understand. But you are not stronger than me. You just have more help.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_FurySister_01
dialogueLines.text: Beaten. By you. Without the help of Nyx's mirror. You humiliated me. And now you've come to rub it in, I guess? Well, go on, then. Not like you can shame me any more.

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: Meg, what is it going to take for you to quit treating me like I'm incompetent? Have I not grown even a little since we met? You were supposed to teach me something; what does that make you?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_FurySister_01
dialogueLines.text: Tsch... why don't you raise your voice a little louder so that all the House can hear. Is that what you want? For everyone to know that you're superior to me?

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: You're not hearing me, Meg. I know it sounds a little disingenuous considering we keep fighting to the death and all, but... I never meant to hurt you.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_FurySister_01
dialogueLines.text: You give yourself far too much credit, Zagreus. Leave me alone.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_FurySister_01
dialogueLines.text: Beaten. By you. Without the help of Nyx's mirror. You humiliated me. And now you've come to rub it in, I guess? Well, go on, then. Not like you can shame me any more.

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: Meg, that isn't why I'm here, you know that isn't how I am. Why can't you see it? All I've ever wanted was to prove myself to you.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_FurySister_01
dialogueLines.text: Tsch... you think that you can prove yourself to me by winning in a fight?

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: Sometimes I think that I can never really win with you, Meg. If I held back, what would you have thought?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_FurySister_01
dialogueLines.text: If you think you know me so well, Zagreus, then you ought to know better than to keep this conversation going with me, now.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_FurySister_01
dialogueLines.text: I can't believe it, Zag. I thought for sure that Nyx's mirror was the only reason you could beat me. I knew you'd changed to some extent, but... not this much.

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: I had to see if I could do it, Meg. I've learned so much from you, I... thought maybe this was a way that I could prove myself to you. And to myself.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_FurySister_01
dialogueLines.text: Prove yourself, to me...? You must not be as comfortable in your own skin as you let on. I can't decide if I like that about you. Now leave me alone while I give it some thought.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_FurySister_01
dialogueLines.text: So, Zagreus. How's it going with that Pact of Punishment you signed? You've made some meaningful improvements to the Underworld.

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: I'll fight my way out of this place, or earn it. The Pact is nothing to me other than a way to keep things lively.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_FurySister_01
