dialogueLines.text: You are delusional. I'll never understand you or your father. Anyway, if you're looking for more punishment, you know where to find me.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_FurySister_01
dialogueLines.text: So, Zagreus. How's it going with that Pact of Punishment you signed? You've made some meaningful improvements to the Underworld.

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: I'll fight my way out of this place, or earn it. The Pact is nothing to me other than a way to keep things lively.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_FurySister_01
dialogueLines.text: You are delusional. I'll never understand you or your father. Anyway, if you're looking for more punishment, you know where to find me.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_FurySister_01
dialogueLines.text: Just so I understand. Breaking through everything in your path, taking resources from your father's realm, trying to get through me... all officially part of the job description?

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: I guess that's right... but, before you ask, I'm not doing it for him.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_FurySister_01
dialogueLines.text: I didn't think you'd have it in you. To leave this place for good. But I didn't think you'd take responsibility for anything, either. Anyway... we'll just have to kill each other more officially, I guess.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_FurySister_01
dialogueLines.text: I never thought I'd see the Queen again. I still can't quite believe she's back... and staying? Is that really your doing?

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: It's her doing, not mine. So you know her? Because... you could have told me. About her. Who she was to me.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_FurySister_01
dialogueLines.text: Don't know her all that well. But I do know Nyx, and I've been bound to secrecy as part of my oath to this House. I flay oathbreakers and liars, Zagreus. Wouldn't do for me to be no better than them, now, would it?

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: No, I suppose not. So what does this mean for Nyx, now that she's back? Or for you, for that matter?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_FurySister_01
dialogueLines.text: The Queen always gave Nyx her space. Your father, though... I don't know what it'll be like for him, if she decides to stick around this time.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_FurySister_01
dialogueLines.text: So the Olympians are backing you officially now? Doing their part to help the family business, finally. Lord Hades probably never thought he'd see the day. Night? You know what I mean.

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: Seems only fair they should help in some capacity. They all work together on the surface, and, let's face it, they're responsible for a good number of shades pouring into this realm. Least they can do is help shore up the whole 'there is no escape' reputation we've got.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_FurySister_01
dialogueLines.text: By making you unfairly stronger by the time you try to get through me?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_FurySister_01
dialogueLines.text: Zagreus, please tell me that the rumors are untrue. You really vanquished him? Have you completely lost your mind? You're going to destroy this blasted House!

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: Come on, Meg, Father had it coming for some time. What's your concern, besides? The House is safe.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_FurySister_01
dialogueLines.text: He's not going to take this lying down. He'll find some way to take it out on you. On all of us. You watch yourself, or all of us will pay.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_FurySister_01
dialogueLines.text: Zagreus, please tell me that the rumors are untrue. You really vanquished him? Have you completely lost your mind? You're going to destroy this blasted House!

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: Come on, Meg, Father had it coming for some time. What's your concern, besides? The House is safe.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_FurySister_01
dialogueLines.text: He's not going to take this lying down. He'll find some way to take it out on you. On all of us. You watch yourself, or all of us will pay.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_FurySister_01
dialogueLines.text: Well, how was it up there, Zagreus? Within Elysium. You'll have to tell us all about it every time the greatest strike you down.

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: You've never been there, have you? It's... lush? Is that a word? It feels closer to the surface. Closer to the sky.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_FurySister_01
dialogueLines.text: How poetic. You could have visited as part of your responsibilities. Instead, you trespass. Incurring wrath of fallen champions in service of your father.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_FurySister_01
dialogueLines.text: Well, how was it up there, Zagreus? Within Elysium. You'll have to tell us all about it every time the greatest strike you down.

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: You've never been there, have you? It's... lush? Is that a word? It feels closer to the surface. Closer to the sky.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_FurySister_01
dialogueLines.text: How poetic. You could have visited as part of your responsibilities. Instead, you trespass. Incurring wrath of fallen champions in service of your father.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_FurySister_01
dialogueLines.text: So you cleared out some of the Satyr infestation near the surface, then. How come the change of heart? Not like you to assist Lord Hades in such things.

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: I wasn't trying to do my father any favors, Meg. Those Satyrs seem to hate me just as much as him. My one way out involves a detour straight into their dens.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_FurySister_01
dialogueLines.text: An interesting coincidence. At any rate, that's one less problem for the rest of us to deal with. Until the Satyrs all come back, at least, which won't be long.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_FurySister_01
dialogueLines.text: You've been through hell more times than you can count by now, haven't you, Zag. You getting used to it? No wonder that you're friends with Sisyphus.

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: I'm getting used to it, all right. I'll keep going, for as many chances as it takes. I'll look forward to our next appointment out there.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_FurySister_01
dialogueLines.text: Appointment. Indeed. Don't keep me waiting. For all your toil, I'm sure you're not even close.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hades_01
dialogueLines.text: ...Make certain of it, Megaera. I am finding, lately, that the help within this House is much more lacking than it used to be. I trust that you shall set it back on course.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_FurySister_01
dialogueLines.text: ...If that is your will, Lord Hades, then it will be done. To the fullest limits of my capability.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_FurySister_01
dialogueLines.text: ...Give me another chance, Lord Hades. I can do better. Don't... bring them here.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hades_01
dialogueLines.text: It is already too late for that, Megaera. They have been notified and are en route to Tartarus even as we speak.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_FurySister_01
dialogueLines.text: You know what they're like. They are best left to their specific tasks. Not this.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hades_01
dialogueLines.text: Megaera, trust me when I say I know the feeling all too well. However, surely, in your ingenuity, you may yet find a way to get your sisters to see eye to eye.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_FurySister_01
dialogueLines.text: ...I cannot work with them, Lord Hades. Please. They don't respect my words, or even listen.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hades_01
dialogueLines.text: Enough! Besides, Megaera, this is not your failing. It is mine. I must attend to these affairs more actively.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_FurySister_01
dialogueLines.text: ...I've often tried, but never to a satisfactory result. You're asking the impossible of me.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hades_01
dialogueLines.text: I am not asking, Megaera.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_FurySister_01
dialogueLines.text: ...But, I don't understand, Lord Hades. I have fallen to him countless times. How can this be...?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hades_01
dialogueLines.text: You and your sisters have protected Tartarus unerringly. The Prince has proven uncontainable. The fault is mine, not yours. Your duty to this realm must be commended.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_FurySister_01
dialogueLines.text: You honor me... I have done nothing to deserve this. There must be something more that I can do?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hades_01
dialogueLines.text: There is, and isn't, Megaera. I ask only that you remain committed to this realm. Bar passage in and out of Tartarus. And, if the Prince continues to show up... you give him hell.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_FurySister_01
dialogueLines.text: Lord Hades, it will be my pleasure, sir.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_FurySister_01
dialogueLines.text: ...Would you look at that, Hypnos. Care to repeat to me the unsolicited advice you just now offered, now that Zagreus is here?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hypnos_01
dialogueLines.text: Oh, hahaha, well, it is my custom to give pointers to our visitors, who come involuntarily, because, I do pick up a lot of bits and pieces, talking here to everyone! About, you know... dying?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_FurySister_01
dialogueLines.text: Zagreus. He told me that I ought to evade more of your attacks next time. So you just watch your back out there, because I know your secrets thanks to Hypnos here.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_FurySister_01
dialogueLines.text: ...I think that I can find the lounge well on my own, Hypnos. Or... wait... what is this, did you just ask me out?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hypnos_01
dialogueLines.text: Me? No, nonononono, no! I'd never even think to do that, Miss, I mean, you have your station, and I have mine. We're from two different worlds, you could say. Fated never to be seen together much.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_FurySister_01
dialogueLines.text: Every time I talk to you, you somehow take it as a compliment, is that correct? Then, let me give you some advice, for once. Stop being an idiot.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_FurySister_01
dialogueLines.text: ...You haven't greeted me as often lately, Hypnos. I wouldn't have noticed if it weren't half your job. If there's something you wish to say to me, why not just come right out.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hypnos_01
dialogueLines.text: Oh, um, well, I, see, the thing is, Megaera, Miss Megaera, mind if I call you that? As I was saying, I just, thought maybe, you know, we had a thing, a real connection, lately, since you keep on dying more and more, and I keep standing here? But then... I always thought you were invincible...

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_FurySister_01
dialogueLines.text: I see. And so you're realizing now that your entire image of me came from your imagination, is that it?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hypnos_01
dialogueLines.text: Well, it's just! I really thought with all my advice that you'd go back to killing Zagreus, rather than the other way around! I just don't think things are working out the way I hoped, you know?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_FurySister_01
dialogueLines.text: It almost sounds as though you've broken up with me, except... well, are you sure that you'll be all right?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hypnos_01
dialogueLines.text: Oh, yeah! Look, I'm just glad it's one of you two, sure beats having to greet shades nonstop! They never talk to me.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_FurySister_01
dialogueLines.text: All right, then... you greet me next I'm here.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Nyx_01
dialogueLines.text: ...We each have our commitments to this House, Megaera. There is no shame in yours. And whilst your sisters are away, you bear their burden of responsibility, as well.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_FurySister_01
dialogueLines.text: I understand, it's just... am I supposed to keep pretending nothing's changed? This House is cracking at the seams, and no one's going to admit it?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Nyx_01
dialogueLines.text: The House shall stand eternally, although in what condition, it is ours to decide. Our obligation is to keep the House in order.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_FurySister_01
dialogueLines.text: I know. You're right of course, you're right... I'll do my best. I'll do my best for both our sakes, Nyx.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Nyx_01
dialogueLines.text: ...and so, who else is there to be depended on...? You are a trusted overseer, Megaera. Your station is no accident and there is no one better fit for what you do. I urge you to look inwardly. How you approach your work is vital to the end result.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_FurySister_01
dialogueLines.text: ...Thank you for the advice, Nyx. I don't know what I'd do without you. Or what this House would be if not for you.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_FurySister_01
dialogueLines.text: ...But, how can you do this to me? Blast your Mirror. By taking sides with him, you're standing against me...!

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Nyx_01
dialogueLines.text: Neutrality ceases to be an option when a crisis rises to a certain point, Megaera. You, yourself, shall have to choose allegiances. I have steadfastly told you this throughout our shared ordeal.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_FurySister_01
dialogueLines.text: I thought that both of our allegiances were to this House, Nyx. It seems I was mistaken. Do you have any idea what sort of pressure I am under here...?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Nyx_01
dialogueLines.text: Use caution with the tone you take with me, Overseer. I am the mother of the Fates, themselves. I do not act by whim or by mistake. Mind your own station, and perform your role. All shall unfold eventually as it must. I like the intervening time no more than you.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_FurySister_01
dialogueLines.text: ...Nyx, you'd tell me if I made a terrible mistake, or even just a bad one, wouldn't you? I need your guidance on this one, or maybe your approval.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Nyx_01
dialogueLines.text: You shall always be the best determiner of what is right about such matters, Megaera. Though, if you seek my blessings or encouragement in this particular pursuit, please rest assured... you have them both. You know the way, from here.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_FurySister_01
dialogueLines.text: I hope that you are right. You always have been, it's just that... I wish I had your confidence in this.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_FurySister_01
dialogueLines.text: ...I think I understand, now, Nyx. You've been here longer than the rest of us combined. You've seen this House go through a lot. Even before Lord Hades took his place. I never should have doubted you.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Nyx_01
dialogueLines.text: To doubt is an important instinct, Megaera. Without it, we could not conceive of better circumstances than the ones we know. Besides... I am the Night. Others shall always doubt me. You may doubt me again. I ask only that you heed well your intuitions, from now on.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_FurySister_01
dialogueLines.text: I will. I promise, Nyx. And if there's anything you ever need that I can do, well... please, just ask of me.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_FurySister_01
dialogueLines.text: ...They can't be reasoned with, Nyx. You know that. What use are they to us, trying to manage this? They'll make things worse.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Nyx_01
dialogueLines.text: They are your sisters. They are part of your life, whether you like it or not. Your relationship with them is yours to mold.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_FurySister_01
dialogueLines.text: I'm afraid there isn't much for me to work with. You have a lot more faith in them than I do.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Nyx_01
dialogueLines.text: I have a lot more faith in you, Megaera. There are many obstinate personalities with whom we regularly interact. Thus, we do what is possible.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_FurySister_01
dialogueLines.text: ...It was my duty to prevent him from getting farther. If I succeeded... he never would have found you. So this once, I'm glad I failed. I hope you know that, my Queen.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Persephone_Home_01
dialogueLines.text: I hoped, but did not know for certain till I heard from you just now, Overseer Megaera! And, if I may? I understand you have achieved an awful lot since I took my, erm, leave of absence from this House. I see why Nyx and Hades put their trust in you!

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_FurySister_01
dialogueLines.text: Thank you, my Queen. Then, if there's anything else that I can do to help you reacclimate yourself, I'm always at your service. You are most welcome, here.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Persephone_Home_01
dialogueLines.text: Likewise, you be sure to let me know if my son there happens to make more trouble for you than you welcome in your life. All right?

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: She's gone... You still in one piece, Sisyphus? Not hurt or anything?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Sisyphus_01
dialogueLines.text: Oh I'm just fine, thanks, Prince. That Fury Sister, she is quite a lass, I have to say, and very, very strong. Bet she could push old Bouldy here better than I!

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Sisyphus_01
dialogueLines.text: Sisyphus furtively offers a choice of benefits.

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: You've really never gotten Bouldy to the top before?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Sisyphus_01
dialogueLines.text: Never got the boulder to the top, not even once. Always tumbles down right at the very end. Lost count after the nineteen thousandth time or so, but you know what? One of these days, I bet it's going to stick.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Sisyphus_01
dialogueLines.text: Sisyphus furtively offers a choice of benefits.

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: Sisyphus, did I just hear correctly that Megaera has vowed to leave you be for now? Both you and Bouldy there?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Sisyphus_01
dialogueLines.text: Why, I believe you heard just that, Your Highness! Seems the Fates are giving me a break. Although, the Fury Sister did declare that she'll be back to haunting me after she's through with you...

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: Well, I bet I can keep her busy for a while yet. I'll take it as a bit of good news, sir.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Sisyphus_01
dialogueLines.text: Sisyphus furtively offers a choice of benefits.

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: Achilles! Your pact with Father is officially amended, sir. Specifically, it no longer has any stipulation barring you from entering Elysium during the indefinite term of your employment. On my authority in the name of Hades, thank you for your service.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Achilles_01
dialogueLines.text: What, are you... you're serious. I'm able to go. You're right, I feel it. I'm unbound. So I can... simply leave... right now.

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: You can simply leave, right now. And, might I add... it's about damn time.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Achilles_01
dialogueLines.text: I... should get going then, although... I am unprepared, I... didn't think it would come to this. Not that I had no faith in you, it's just...

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: Incidentally, there's this one glade out in Elysium I highly recommend... go to him, sir. You've waited long enough. Fear is for the weak.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Achilles_01
dialogueLines.text: ...Fear is for the weak. Farewell for now, lad. I can't thank you enough.

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: Erm, Nyx, at first I thought I maybe should ignore this, but I wanted to give you greetings from Lord Ares, who seems rather interested in you.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Nyx_01
dialogueLines.text: Ah, yes, the god of war. Among all the Olympians, he often struck me as much more accepting of our kind, born of the Underworld.

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: You're not... wait, you're not interested in him, I mean, he's... murderous... polite, though, I don't know.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Nyx_01
dialogueLines.text: I take an interest in whomever I take interest in, my child. I thank you for the message.

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: Hey, Nyx, erm, Lord Ares wanted you to know, and I believe I quote, that 'I am often thinking of her here'. Take that for what it's worth?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Nyx_01
dialogueLines.text: I shall, thank you, my child. If the god of war is thus preoccupied with me, well then, perhaps he shall be slower to send droves of mortals to our doorstep here.

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: Help me, Nyx. Or rather, help yourself. Lord Ares seeks for me to put the two of you in contact. I mean... I don't know why I'm getting so defensive, here, now that I think on it.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Nyx_01
dialogueLines.text: Do not concern yourself with my ability to navigate my interpersonal relationships, my child. It pleases me Lord Ares told you this. Those of Olympus rarely say such things, to any of us here.

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: Nyx, did you really start a correspondence with Lord Ares, as he put it to me recently? Is everything all right with you and him?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Nyx_01
dialogueLines.text: His interest in the Underworld is genuine, and he does not regard me as beneath him, even if the technicality exists. The Olympians and the chthonic gods... we have much left to learn of one another's kind.

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: What of Father? He's forbidden any contact whatsoever!

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Nyx_01
dialogueLines.text: You have repeatedly received such contact, all despite your father's wishes, child.

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: Nyx, did you really start a correspondence with Lord Ares, as he put it to me recently? Is everything all right with you and him?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Nyx_01
dialogueLines.text: His interest in the Underworld is genuine, and he does not regard me as beneath him, even if the technicality exists. The Olympians and the chthonic gods... we have much left to learn of one another's kind.

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: You're going to meet Ares, sometime, Nyx? Or maybe you already have?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Nyx_01
dialogueLines.text: What is your concern, exactly, child? Speak plain with me, or else take not my time.

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: Well, I... it's just, Ares, he's... I don't know, he just doesn't seem your type! And isn't he a little young for you?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Nyx_01
dialogueLines.text: You do not understand the basis of our friendship, child. Lord Ares may not meet the moral standard of the mortals who hate him, or Olympians who shun him, but to me? He is cordial and eager to learn. I am quite capable of forming my own views on him.

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: Erm, Nyx, I... Ares sends you his regards again. Wanted to let you know.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Nyx_01
dialogueLines.text: Thank you, my child. We may seldom have occasion to encounter one another, he and I, but it is good to have another friend upon Olympus. Even if goddess Athena and he are not on the best of terms.

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: I heard lately from the goddess Athena, Nyx. She seems to admire you. I thought Olympians didn't like our kind?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Nyx_01
dialogueLines.text: They typically do not. But, you are family, child. And I, why... I believed Athena wise enough to understand why someone such as I would seek her, in particular. She has, in turn, made others in her family aware.

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: Athena sends you her kindest regards, Nyx. Sounds like you have maintained some correspondence since all this began?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Nyx_01
dialogueLines.text: We have, thank you, my child. Goddess Athena is reputed to be wise, and so she was the one I felt was the most likely to listen to reason, understand my view, and ultimately have a willingness to help. It is most fortunate that I was not mistaken about her.

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: But... you told her you're my mother. If she's supposedly so wise... isn't that quite a risk? If she finds out you've been misleading her...

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Nyx_01
dialogueLines.text: She is wise, child. It is precisely for that reason I expected she would understand.

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: Athena sends you her kindest regards, Nyx. Sounds like you have maintained some correspondence since all this began?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Nyx_01
dialogueLines.text: We have, thank you, my child. Goddess Athena is reputed to be wise, and so she was the one I felt was the most likely to listen to reason, understand my view, and ultimately have a willingness to help. It is most fortunate that I was not mistaken about her.

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: The Olympians can't really see me, can they, Nyx? I mean, not very well. I get that they can sense details here and there, but... they don't know the full extent of what's going on. Are we really so far beyond their influence?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Nyx_01
dialogueLines.text: You are enshrouded from them, child. For your own benefit, and theirs. They know as much as necessary to assist. And I trust they shall, for as long as you require. They are bound to one another as family. And they know that you require aid. Do not be too prideful to accept.

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: Nyx, do you know anything about a softly-spoken gentlebeing under the guise of Chaos? They approached me recently, and offered me a Boon, of sorts.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Nyx_01
dialogueLines.text: Primordial Chaos... indeed, I know them well. They must have taken interest in your quest. I would advise that you beware their offerings, though know that Chaos, whilst a trickster, is not a deceiver.

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: Can you tell me something more of Chaos, Nyx? I... have a lot of questions.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Nyx_01
dialogueLines.text: My place is not to speak too openly of Chaos, child. It is their own prerogative to tell you of themselves. I can say they are the eldest force within this world. Before them, there was nothing.

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: Chaos seems to have taken somewhat of a liking to me, Nyx. They mentioned you.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Nyx_01
dialogueLines.text: They mentioned me, did they... I thought, perhaps, that they had long forgotten. You should know they are my mother and my father, child. We have not spoken since the dawn of time.

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: Nyx, I hate to be a bother, but I wanted to ask you about Chaos once again. Won't you at least try to reach out to them?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Nyx_01
dialogueLines.text: It is for Chaos to reach out to me if they wish to reacquaint themselves with me. Child, it would please me if we spoke no more of this for now.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Nyx_01
dialogueLines.text: Child, I... know not exactly what you did, but... I detected faintly an attempt by Chaos to reach out to me. A message, merely at the edges of my consciousness, yet I felt certain it was there. But I could not discern it, otherwise. What does Chaos want from me?

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: I... think Chaos wishes to reestablish contact with you, Nyx. The two of you drifted apart from one another, so, I think I can imagine. I lost touch with Thanatos awhile! But that was nothing in comparison to this. Isn't there someway you can reconnect?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Nyx_01
dialogueLines.text: Now that the House is strong, and this realm orderly, I do not know. But I shall think upon it, child. Leave me, please.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Nyx_01
dialogueLines.text: I know what must be done, regarding Chaos and myself, my child. It is not often that I ask for your assistance, but I need for you to do something for me, if you would hear me out.

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: Of course, Nyx. Anything. What can I do?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Nyx_01
dialogueLines.text: In the administrative chamber, you perhaps recall the Eldest Sigil there. Through it we have access directly to the darkest reaches of the realm. But not to Chaos, anymore. Not currently. Though, this is solvable, I think.

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: Wait, the Sigil? But I'm not authorized to use it. It's never worked for me, and I have even less authority around here now.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Nyx_01
dialogueLines.text: That you were stripped of your authority is what makes this possible at all. It shall require a significant expenditure of Darkness. Then... the bridge to Chaos may be restored. So that I might travel again to visit them. Will you bring Darkness to the Sigil for me, please? When possible.

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: I'll do everything I can. Hey maybe you can put in a request with the House Contractor for me? Make it official, right?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Nyx_01
dialogueLines.text: At last. The bridge to Chaos is restored. Thank you so very much, my child. I shall be going, then. And I shall presently return. Please, have no fear. And now, farewell. I shall bring Chaos your kindest regards.

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: Nyx, have you seen Charon lately? Everything all right with him? I can never tell how he's doing.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Nyx_01
dialogueLines.text: Charon and I have not lately taken opportunities to correspond. But he has always been among the least dependent of my children, other than perhaps only the Fates. This much I know and can assure to you: He always is content to steer his boat.

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: I suppose you are the one to thank for his assistance, when it comes to selling me his wares and such.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Nyx_01
dialogueLines.text: No. He is the one to thank. Do not mistake his lack of speech for lack of cleverness. He serves this House as I do. But he does not answer to me.

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: Erm, Nyx? You don't suppose Charon would hold it against me if... we had a little fight? To the death, I mean.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Nyx_01
dialogueLines.text: None of Charon's brothers ever dared fight with him whilst growing up. Why would you fathom such a ruinous idea, child?

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: It... kind of already happened. And, I can confirm he's rather strong. Something I can do to make it up to him?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Nyx_01
dialogueLines.text: Probably not. For it is unnecessary. Likely he appreciates having an adversary, in his own way. Few would so much as dare.

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: Someday I'd like to meet more of your children, Nyx. Eris, Nemesis... Thanatos told me of several others. Have they ever visited the House?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Nyx_01
dialogueLines.text: No. They are required elsewhere. It is for the best. Some of my children are not on speaking terms with one another, or with me. As a mother... one has her victories, and her defeats.

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: Nyx... I encountered Demeter. She mentioned her daughter... Persephone. You never said she was my grandmother. Didn't you think... under the circumstances... that was something I should know?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Nyx_01
dialogueLines.text: ...Listen to me, child. What you know, or do not know, is for your protection. Your protection, and your birthmother's, as well. Your suspicion and your curiosity are warranted. But I can do no more about them, now, except to caution you. Are we understood?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Nyx_01
dialogueLines.text: What is it, child? I sense that your concern is of a different nature than it usually is. Yet, it is veiled to me, somehow.

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: Oh, it's just.... Please don't take this the wrong way, Nyx, but I was worried you were being rather harsh to Dusa. You asked her not to speak to me, at all?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Nyx_01
dialogueLines.text: I asked she mind her own responsibilities. I fear she forgets herself, in this House. Though you have an informal air... you are the Prince! The only son of the Lord Hades, Fate-appointed master of this realm. You shall have the company of gods. Not severed gorgon heads.

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: Pff! If I'm so important, shouldn't I be the one to choose whom I get to interact with around here?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Nyx_01
dialogueLines.text: You do not fully know your place, my child. The gorgon-head most certainly does not know hers. Please focus on your task at hand, and leave her be.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Nyx_01
dialogueLines.text: You are consorting with the severed gorgon head, my child. Despite my wishes that you maintain respectful distances between yourselves.

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: She's my friend, Nyx. Look... I love you very much, you know I do, except... you cannot choose my friends for me! I can't allow that. I know you don't trust her. Don't you trust me?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Nyx_01
dialogueLines.text: ...I do. Then, very well. But understand that your relationship with her does not preclude the need for her continual improvement with respect to her responsibilities.

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: What happened with Dusa, Nyx? You really fired her because she talked to me? She was my friend!

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Nyx_01
dialogueLines.text: She was removed for insubordination, and repeated overextension and lack of proper judgment, child. Your affinity for her is the reason her removal was as amicable as it was.

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: Insubordination, and repeated lack of what? She worked harder than anyone here to keep the House neat and orderly! I'd thought you would have valued that!

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Nyx_01
dialogueLines.text: Her eagerness to please was to a fault. Perhaps your Olympian relatives require sacrifices, child, but this House requires efficiency, and nothing more.

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: Efficiency... you know who you sound like right now, don't you, Nyx? I can't believe this.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Nyx_01
dialogueLines.text: If you are bent on undermining my authority, go do so someplace else.

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: Nyx... I'm sorry that I raised my voice with you before, it's just... I cared about Dusa, and I know just how much she cared about working here, with you, and all of us... isn't there some way you would reconsider letting her go?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Nyx_01
dialogueLines.text: I appreciate that you have calmed yourself, my child. As for the gorgon head, this House needs steady-tempered vigilance, not unthinking devotion. Despite repeated feedback, she refused to approach any aspect of her duties with restraint.

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: Wait, you're saying you fired Dusa for working too hard? Did you give her any warning?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Nyx_01
dialogueLines.text: She did not understand how to work properly. Despite all that she did, she ultimately was a burden to this House. There was no warning necessary on my part.

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: Please give her another chance, Nyx. She can learn, she just needs to know what she did wrong. She gave this work everything. She just pushed too hard. Shouldn't something like that be forgivable?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Nyx_01
dialogueLines.text: ...It seems you have more patience than I, child. And, I did not realize you cared. But do not ask again for me to rescind my decision. Are we understood?

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: You really asked Dusa to come back. Thank you, Nyx! I'm very grateful. And Megaera. Probably everybody here! I'm sure she won't let you down.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Nyx_01
dialogueLines.text: I know you care for her, child. And I admit I retain several concerns. Not only about the appropriateness of her conduct, but of her reckless inexperience. I do, however, see the possibility that she can work through these issues. Perhaps with your support.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Nyx_01
dialogueLines.text: At first, I was deeply concerned that you were spending so much time with Dusa, child. It seemed unbecoming for the both of you, and threatened to disrupt our custodian's routine. Except... it pleases me to say that, in this case, I was mistaken about you.

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: Thank you for saying so, Nyx. Though, you mean you were mistaken about me, or about Dusa?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Nyx_01
dialogueLines.text: I was mistaken about both of you. That you would influence her in a harmful way, yet here, in point of fact, she has performed her duties with increasing skill. That she would fail to perform her role, or to learn well from her mistakes. Please do continue to look out for her.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Nyx_01
dialogueLines.text: Persephone, the Queen... she has returned! I scarce believe it, child. You have brought her back, to all of us.

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: I scarce believe it, too. What happens now? Pick up the pieces and begin again?

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: How has it been for you of late, Nyx? Having Mother here again, for old time's sake?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Nyx_01
dialogueLines.text: Though reminiscent of an older time, it is now very different, child. For one thing... you are present, now. Unlike before.

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: Oh, right. So, are you getting on OK, is everything all right?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Nyx_01
dialogueLines.text: Everything is all right. Your birthmother... Persephone... her presence has been welcome here, I think.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Nyx_01
dialogueLines.text: Congratulations on your new responsibilities, my child. So, you have reason to rise from here to the surface, still.

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: It's more of a formality, I think. My responsibilities, such as they are, remain quite similar to those that got me to this point. But thank you, Nyx. It's strange... to think that I can serve the House, by trying to break from it, time after time.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Nyx_01
dialogueLines.text: There can be no escape. Your father wishes that this reputation spread. So that the mortals and the shades never attempt what you were able to achieve.

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: Well, it does beat standing around in my chambers aimlessly. And... I daresay it feels rather good not to feel utterly incompetent, for a change.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Nyx_01
dialogueLines.text: It is an honor to be working with you, child.

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: Nyx, meant to ask... Charon was there to take Mother and me home the one time. So is he... has he been working for you? All part of some elaborate plan, or something?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Nyx_01
dialogueLines.text: Charon serves only this realm, child. And, he is very trustworthy. I suppose that it is also possible he has some affection for the Queen, if not for you.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Nyx_01
dialogueLines.text: I thank you for the lovely flowers, child. They make my corner of the House hospitable. Such simple comforts never were for me, and yet... now that I am surrounded by them, I have found that I enjoy their presence, more than I would have admitted in the past.

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: I'm glad you like them, Nyx. Though if you don't, just give the word, and I will have them removed. Probably incinerated for good measure! Now that you mention it, though, how come you are always in this corner in particular?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Nyx_01
dialogueLines.text: This point is closest to the center of the House. To its foundation. And, therefore, to the center of the entire realm.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Nyx_01
dialogueLines.text: What is it, child? Is something amiss?

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: No, it's just... the latest renovations from the Contractor... Father told me you've been supervising all of that. Look, just between us, and I'll not speak of it again, but... thanks. So much.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Nyx_01
dialogueLines.text: Ah. Yes, no need to speak of it again. Though, I am pleased to oblige.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Nyx_01
dialogueLines.text: This House is not a place of cheer, child. A certain severity always struck me as appropriate, if not necessary, given our role.

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: Is this about all the House Contractor decorations? Look, it's one thing if Father objects, but if you do as well... just tell me, and I can have it taken down.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Nyx_01
dialogueLines.text: You misunderstand. I am grateful. These changes... I would not have commissioned them, myself. But, seeing them now... I appreciate their presence. You have a discerning eye.

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: I have to ask you something, Nyx. What is this debt you owe my father, anyway? Forgive my eavesdropping, it's just, I heard the two of you discussing things, and... it came up.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Nyx_01
dialogueLines.text: ...I was here long before your father. The responsibility to govern fell to me. Yet, I could not manage on my own. I shape the darkness; ensure the integrity of the domain. I am less prone to census-taking and administering rules. I receded from such responsibilities.

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: It was too much, even for you. So... when father arrived... you welcomed him? He helped?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Nyx_01
dialogueLines.text: I was quite skeptical of him, at first. I thought his presence merely a cruel trick of Fate. But... he worked diligently, from the start. Soon there was order where once there was none. I fashioned this House for him, and I maintain it still. We remain in partnership. In interdependency.

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: He has never taken advantage of you? Of your generosity?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Nyx_01
dialogueLines.text: No, child. He has not. He can be short with me, as you have seen. But that is the extent of it. We have found ways to reconcile disagreements. Though, your relationship with him... it is more complicated than my own, I fear...

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: Nyx, all you've done to help me, with the Olympians, the Mirror, the House Contractor, I... I worry that if Father were to know, that he would turn his wrath upon you. I don't want anything to happen.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Nyx_01
dialogueLines.text: You need not fear for me, my child. For I am not some underling your father has authority to punish if it pleases him. I am the Night; and it is by my grace that this House stands. Your father knows this. Thus, he and I have certain common goals. We are obligated to cooperate.

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: Then... if there's really nothing he can do, why go along with his deception? About my birthmother, I mean?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Nyx_01
dialogueLines.text: Please... that is something I am not at liberty to say. When you meet your birthmother eventually... you shall understand.

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: Nyx, all you've done to help me, with the Olympians, the Mirror, the House Contractor, I... I worry that if Father were to know, that he would turn his wrath upon you. I don't want anything to happen.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Nyx_01
dialogueLines.text: You need not fear for me, my child. For I am not some underling your father has authority to punish if it pleases him. I am the Night; and it is by my grace that this House stands. Your father knows this. Thus, he and I have certain common goals. We are obligated to cooperate.

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: Obligated to cooperate... no wonder I thought you two truly were husband and wife.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Nyx_01
dialogueLines.text: Your father and I... sometimes convince ourselves.

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: Nyx, I've come to know the god of swiftness, Hermes, though... it seems to me he doesn't work directly with the others on Olympus. Do you know him well?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Nyx_01
dialogueLines.text: Lord Hermes is a point of contact, yes. Among Olympians he works the closest with our operations here, and aids with shepherding the souls of the departed to our gates. Accept his help; for he is faithful to our cause.

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: Don't you ever get lonely down here, Nyx...? For companionship, I mean, because... I mean, just look at you, you're very beautiful, the night herself! There's nothing you can't do. When I was younger, I thought you and father, you know, but... is there not someone else?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Nyx_01
dialogueLines.text: How very forward of you, child. I accept your compliment. Although, I am the Night, as you observe. The night conceals all. I know nothing of the loneliness which you describe. This entire realm... it is a part of me. And many of its subjects sprang from me, alone.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Nyx_01
dialogueLines.text: Alas, you have seen much of Hypnos lately, child. I hope you find his disposition somewhat tolerable? Please tell me plain if this is not the case.

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: Oh, he's not so bad, Nyx. Be proud of your sleepy, cheerful son! Who else among us is able to muster that kind of enthusiasm?

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: I don't often see you interact with Hypnos, Nyx, so I just wanted to make sure... is everything all right?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Nyx_01
dialogueLines.text: Hypnos still is learning independence, child. Thus, I have decided it is best to keep my distance from him temporarily, else he shall continue to rely upon me, even for completing the most rudimentary requests. We have agreed he shall not call upon me for the time being.

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: For the time being? How long are we talking? Can't remember the last time I saw you speaking with him, now that you mention it.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Nyx_01
dialogueLines.text: Based on his rate of progress, I suspect his education shall have been complete well before the dawning of the next age.

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: Hey Nyx, I would have you know, I'm now proud owner of my very own Chthonic Companion! You really built them to last. Just... not very many of them, apparently. I'd all but given up on ever finding one.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Nyx_01
dialogueLines.text: I sensed this outcome, and it pleases me. Though, you never would have simply discovered such a thing. They each are unique artifacts that must be given willingly if they are to retain their shape, and power.

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: I have to say, and I do mean this as a compliment: It's hard for me to imagine Night Incarnate herself fashioning these little keepsakes to be given between friends.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Nyx_01
dialogueLines.text: Do not mistake them for their unassuming look. Within them is the strength of an unbreaking bond between two souls conjoined.

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: Nyx, did you really give Dusa quite possibly the sole surviving epsilon edition Chthonic Companion in the entire Underworld? She's very grateful for it, so much so that she entrusted Fidi to my care. They grow in power that way, right?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Nyx_01
dialogueLines.text: The severed gorgon head is not without redeeming attributes, my child. Though, I expected this companion would soon find its way to you, the way the two of you have grown attached. Thus, in one action, both of you have found encouragement.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Nyx_01
dialogueLines.text: The Fury Megaera is under orders to prevent your passage out of Tartarus, my child. Conflict between the two of you is unavoidable. It brings me pain to have to see you fight.

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: Don't say that, Nyx. Besides! Conflict between Megaera and me has always put the spark in our relationship. We're going to be fine.

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: Nyx, you've known Megaera practically forever. Well, before my time at least, so could you... tell me more about what she was like, back then?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Nyx_01
dialogueLines.text: Ah-hahah. I am pleased to see the two of you on better terms of late. Megaera always was as she appears. Dutiful and quite particular. Thoughtful and dependable. Respectful.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Nyx_01
dialogueLines.text: It pleases me that you and Megaera have come to deeply understand each other, child. I must admit to you, it was my hope that you and she were capable of this result.

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: You're pleased we're together, Nyx? I thought you'd be concerned that such relationships distract from work responsibilities and such. Although, I'm grateful to have your approval.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Nyx_01
dialogueLines.text: I have no fear that Megaera shall be distracted, child. Besides; such matters are more likely to inspire, and to spur greater accomplishment. Although, the greater sense of contentment tends to be a sufficient end unto itself.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Nyx_01
dialogueLines.text: It pleases me that you and Megaera have come to deeply understand each other, child. I must admit to you, it was my hope that you and she were capable of this result.

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: You're happy Meg and I are friends now, Nyx? I appreciate it, certainly, though... I worry it might get in the way of Meg's ability to do her job.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Nyx_01
dialogueLines.text: I have no such fear for either one of you. You both are dedicated to your tasks. I trust your friendship can withstand your confrontations every now and then. For that is why the friendship blossomed, is it not?

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: I, ah, happened to be looking into our security situation here, just running background checks, that sort of thing, and... I was wondering if you could help me locate an old pact signed by... signed by Achilles, Nyx. It would mean a lot... not just to him, but me.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Nyx_01
dialogueLines.text: Achilles... We would struggle to put someone comparable in his role. Though, be that as it may... epsilon-iota-theta. Rho folder, middle, I believe. You are certain about this, child?

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: I saw Achilles with Patroclus in Elysium, Nyx. They're free to be together, now, thanks to you. If only you could have seen them.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Nyx_01
dialogueLines.text: Oh, I can see them, my child, and I have. And it pleases me Achilles has earned some degree of respite from his servitude, for all his loyalty to our House. For what you have done for him, thank you.

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: Father isn't going to be thrilled I've let him out of a binding pact.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Nyx_01
dialogueLines.text: Your father is committed to his rules, and you released Achilles from his pact according to those rules. There is not much that even he can do about it save to have Achilles sign again.

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: Nyx, why is it the mortals come here knowing all about Olympus, while knowing so little about what goes on in our realm? Is there something more we could be doing to help spread the word?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Nyx_01
dialogueLines.text: This realm remains enshrouded and obscure by my design and by your father's will, my child. Tell me, what would be the benefit of the attention you would seek?

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: Oh, I... I don't know, it's just, for all the famous gods and monsters and the like, it seems to me that someone such as you, who does so much, why... you should be at least as known as any one of them!

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Nyx_01
dialogueLines.text: Obscurity is not a weakness, child. Are we understood? Seek to be valuable, not to be known. If you are valuable, then fame or notoriety can be a consequence. Should they become a burden... I can help.

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: Nyx, if, hypothetically, I felt that some injustice had been dealt to someone who deserved a better lot... say, that they were subjected to too harsh a punishment for all eternity... don't I have some of my father's authority to do something about it?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Nyx_01
dialogueLines.text: Now that you have regained your access privileges to the administrative chamber, child, yes; that is a level of authority you hypothetically possess, again.

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: But how can I possibly find anything in there? The place is wall-to-wall pacts and parchments. I've no idea where to look.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Nyx_01
dialogueLines.text: You can be made aware. Though, use discretion, if and when you need to exercise this power. And make certain that the time is right. Now, are we understood?

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: Never thought that the administrative chamber would have been so invaluable, Nyx. Though, I have you to thank for all of it.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Nyx_01
dialogueLines.text: Nonsense, child. Our records must be kept up-to-date. You merely observed that several were long past due. Not even your father can argue with that.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Nyx_01
dialogueLines.text: You are still in a difficult predicament, my child. And I, with you, in turn. Those on Olympus think you seek them, even now. That you are my own son by birth. Our deception, well intentioned as it was, has multiplied in size.

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: You're right. We have to do something. We can't just keep pretending with them.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Nyx_01
dialogueLines.text: But if you reveal to them the truth of everything... I fear the wrath they shall unleash. Perhaps not all of them, but some, at least, shall not take such deceptions well.

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: I couldn't agree more. Fortunately, Mother has a plan! Asked me to get in touch with everybody on Olympus, one by one.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Nyx_01
dialogueLines.text: Your birthmother is resourceful, certainly. Though can you say as yet, which sort of plan?

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: Would you believe it if I told you she wants to have all of them over for Ambrosia?

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: Nyx, this feast Mother has planned... would it be all right with you if I were to invite Chaos?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Nyx_01
dialogueLines.text: I... hardly think that Chaos would attend any festivity of ours. I am uncertain that they even could. Even more doubtful that they would want this. However... if you truly wish to ask, proceed.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Nyx_01
dialogueLines.text: You have met most of your Olympian relatives by now, child. Although the power of their blessings is fleeting here, they shall continue to support you. As long as necessary.

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: They all still think I'm headed to join them on Olympus. That's why they're helping me.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Nyx_01
dialogueLines.text: They are helping you because you are family. Family with whom they do not yet have a complicated past.

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: Nyx... the Olympians have been immensely helpful, but... did they really have to get mixed up in all this? I could have gone off on my own.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Nyx_01
dialogueLines.text: And you would not have gone far, child. Your father is too proud to accept assistance in personal matters. Do not cede the advantage that you have.

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: But I'm misleading them. Taking advantage of their good graces trying to find Mother. What if they find out?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Nyx_01
dialogueLines.text: I shall do everything within my power to prevent this, as shall you.

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: I was pleased to see an order pending approval for the release of Orpheus with the House Contractor, Nyx. If Father wants him to start singing again, locking him in Tartarus doesn't seem like the most efficient way to go about it. Besides, I think he's suffered enough.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Nyx_01
dialogueLines.text: Your father found a certain peace from song, my child. Perhaps with this reprieve, our Orpheus shall reconsider and resume his craft.

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: You're worried Father doesn't have enough live music to enjoy while brooding over his gloomy court?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Nyx_01
dialogueLines.text: Your father's temperament is easier to navigate when he is in relatively better spirits. And my opinion is that punishing the court musician does not further your father's goal with him.

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: Do you enjoy Orpheus' singing, Nyx? He seems a bit concerned that you might not.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Nyx_01
dialogueLines.text: I am amenable to the consonance of his harmonies, child.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Nyx_01
dialogueLines.text: You have been served a Pact of Punishment, now, child. I thought to talk your father out of it, at first, but... it may serve to let you grow your power, even more.

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: Something about setbacks building character, I guess? I didn't take you for someone to look on the bright side of things, Nyx.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Nyx_01
dialogueLines.text: It is a point of fact. If you can overcome the conditions of the Pact, your influence throughout this realm shall surely grow. Though, do not overextend, of course.

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: Nyx, can you... tell me more about my birthmother? Anything at all. What was she like, when you first met? What did you think of her?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Nyx_01
dialogueLines.text: Your birthmother left a strong impression on us, child. It was apparent she was from a far-off place, and yet... she brought with her a lightness I had not experienced before. My first impression soon subsided, and I grew to care for her. You possess many of her finer qualities.

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: Finer qualities? Such as abandoning her family members? Why did she leave? Why won't you tell me?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Nyx_01
dialogueLines.text: Please... you must not ask such things of me. I swore a binding oath never to say. I have already circumvented it as much as can be done. Go find her, child. Then, you shall understand.

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: Nyx, can you... tell me more about my birthmother? Anything at all. What was she like, when you first met? What did you think of her?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Nyx_01
dialogueLines.text: Your birthmother left a strong impression on us, child. It was apparent she was from a far-off place, and yet... she brought with her a lightness I had not experienced before. My first impression soon subsided, and I grew to care for her. You possess many of her finer qualities.

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: Nyx, if I can ever make my way from here, out to the surface... how do you know that I can find her? The instructions you gave me... how can you know for sure that's where she is, that she hasn't left, or...?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Nyx_01
dialogueLines.text: When your birthmother departed, I bent the light for her, shrouding her path, though I myself could see it. I trust she remains there, and that I am not betraying her confidence by telling you.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Nyx_01
dialogueLines.text: Your father fell to you, as once his father fell to him. Although, you were substantially more merciful. I know it is a bitter victory, at best. Though, tell me... what transpired subsequently, that you have returned? Did you locate your birthmother?

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: I did, I... she was there. She was... where you said she'd be. And, more than I imagined, but... I had so little time with her. I can't survive up there, for very long. Why didn't you tell me I'd just die, no matter what?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Nyx_01
dialogueLines.text: Oh, my child... I say this seldomly, but... I did not know. Or... I did not wish to believe... that the surface would have such an effect on you. But it seems you share your father's fate, after all.

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: My father's fate? But he is smugly waiting for me every single time I make it there, and seems quite healthy by then, all in all! Though... maybe the farther we get from this realm... the worse we get. Because he cast his lot... inheriting the Underworld?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Nyx_01
dialogueLines.text: So I now presume. You are bound to this realm by powers greater even than mine. Tell me something... if you knew that you could only see Persephone for but a moment's time... would you still make the journey to her, there?

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: ...Yes. Yes, I would. I had so many questions left for her!

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Nyx_01
dialogueLines.text: Then, go and ask them, child. Steady your resolve, and find her there, again. And use well what brief time you have with one another, whilst you can. As mortals do.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Nyx_01
dialogueLines.text: Your father fell to you, as once his father fell to him. Although, you were substantially more merciful. I know it is a bitter victory, at best. Though, tell me... what transpired subsequently, that you have returned? Did you locate your birthmother?

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: I did, I... she was there. She was... where you said she'd be. And, more than I imagined, but... I had so little time with her. I can't survive up there, for very long. Why didn't you tell me I'd just die, no matter what?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Nyx_01
dialogueLines.text: Oh, my child... I say this seldomly, but... I did not know. Or... I did not wish to believe... that the surface would have such an effect on you. But it seems you share your father's fate, after all.

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: My father's fate? But he was smugly waiting for me up there, and seemed quite healthy, all in all! Though... maybe the farther we get from this realm... the worse we get. Because he cast his lot... inheriting the Underworld?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Nyx_01
dialogueLines.text: So I now presume. You are bound to this realm by powers greater even than mine. Tell me something... if you knew that you could only see Persephone for but a moment's time... would you still make the journey to her, there?

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: ...Yes. Yes, I would. I had so many questions left for her!

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Nyx_01
dialogueLines.text: Then, go and ask them, child. Steady your resolve, and find her there, again. And use well what brief time you have with one another, whilst you can. As mortals do.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Nyx_01
dialogueLines.text: How is she, child? Queen Persephone, I mean. Has she been well up there, in her abode? What is it like?

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: She's doing well, I think. It's nice to visit her. Her residence is beautiful. A little home nestled amid the cold. All of these plants, in bloom! It's difficult even to describe. And very bright, as well.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Nyx_01
dialogueLines.text: That sounds quite pleasant for someone who values brightness, I suppose. Well, then... I trust your visits are worthwhile for the both of you.

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: Nyx, my mother was quite concerned to learn Olympus has been aiding me. Did you... haven't you... put her in danger by involving them? Was there no other way?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Nyx_01
dialogueLines.text: No. There was not. She has been in danger, all along. I concealed her, yes. Even from me. But, it is not enough. I do not think that you could have found her without the Olympians. And, if I did not reveal your existence to them... if they discovered who you are... it would be worse.

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: Ugh, what a mess. How are we going to fix this?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Nyx_01
dialogueLines.text: The Olympians maintain belief that you are trying to reach them. I trust we can sustain this understanding for a long, long time. Perhaps not forever, but... long enough to discover a solution. Perhaps with your birthmother's help.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Nyx_01
dialogueLines.text: Child, quickly now, before you go. I know how all of this must look. Know that I was true to her. Persephone. And, that I think fondly on the time she spent with us.

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: Of course, Nyx, though... why did she really leave the Underworld, do you think? Besides because of me. So what if she thought I died at birth?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Nyx_01
dialogueLines.text: She did not feel that she belonged. It was a very difficult adjustment she was forced to make. And... she feared that she would inevitably end up caught between Olympus and this realm. That she might start a war.

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: A war... wars have been fought for less, I understand. And, Father must have been a real burden, too.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Nyx_01
dialogueLines.text: You do not know. Ask her yourself, about her time with him. But... I have already said too much. And I cannot say more.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Nyx_01
dialogueLines.text: What is the matter, child? I sense your distress, not the specific cause.

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: Mother doesn't want to see me again. I mean... she asked me not to return. Thinks the risk is too great.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Nyx_01
dialogueLines.text: Oh. That is a fair-enough assessment. What shall you do?

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: ...I'm going back.

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: Father loved her, didn't he? Mother, I mean. Faith like that... he's just stubborn enough to hold onto the idea of her, isn't he? Though, what she saw in him, I'm not sure.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Nyx_01
dialogueLines.text: Your father was different with Persephone, to some extent. He has always been as you know him, yet... her lightness... it affected even him, I think. And he knows... that he was better with her, than without.

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: Sounds like a lot of pressure on Mother to fix all his failings... but, it sounds as though she cared for him, as well. And you, and Cerberus. She said she misses you.

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: Nyx... why did you never send word to Mother that I lived? You knew at least approximately where she went. You and Father... you could have spared her so much pain!

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Nyx_01
dialogueLines.text: I swore a binding oath never to say, child. I have already circumvented it as much as can be done. Know that we... that I acted with only her wellbeing in mind. As well as yours.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Nyx_01
dialogueLines.text: I saw you took possession of the List of Minor Prophecies, of the Three Fates. As ever, I should never have doubted my daughters' expectations. I was uncertain you had necessary guidance to select it from among the House Contractor's many opportunities.

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: You offer something called the Fated List of Minor Prophecies to me, chances are I'm going to pick it up. Though, you make it sound like there's more I should know about it. Should I be concerned?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Nyx_01
dialogueLines.text: You know exactly what is necessary, and no more. I know better than anyone never to tamper with the Fates' design. Though, if they had cruelty in store for you through their List... I think that I would warn you, anyway.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Nyx_01
dialogueLines.text: You have fulfilled already many prophecies envisioned by my daughters, child. Although perhaps inevitable, I find it impressive, nonetheless.

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: Thank you, Nyx. Though, they're just minor prophecies, right? The kind that won't necessarily come to pass unless spelled out and coupled with a decent reward to make it worth my while. Not even the Fates are above greasing the wheels of progress, then?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Nyx_01
dialogueLines.text: The Fates have perfect knowledge of exactly what is necessary for the future they have woven to someday come to pass. We each are instrumental to their ultimate design. Some more than others, it would seem, of course.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Nyx_01
dialogueLines.text: I sense all of the minor prophecies described within the Fated List have been fulfilled...? I do not understand how that is possible.

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: Well, it certainly took some doing, and several of those really were rather difficult, and took some time, but... what's so special about getting through them all? It's a list, right?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Nyx_01
dialogueLines.text: The Fates are always at their weaving. To have caught up with their designs... well, I should think that they shall have to learn to weave a little faster, perhaps.

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: Any new prophecies need fulfilling, they know where to find me.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Nyx_01
dialogueLines.text: I sense all of the minor prophecies described within the Fated List have been fulfilled...? I do not understand how that is possible.

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: Well, it certainly took some doing, and several of those really were rather difficult, and took some time, but... what's so special about getting through them all? It's a list, right?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Nyx_01
dialogueLines.text: The Fates are always at their weaving. To have caught up with their designs... well, I should think that they shall have to learn to weave a little faster, perhaps.

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: Any new prophecies need fulfilling, they know where to find me.

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: Erm, Nyx, you ever have a burning urge to find the case files for the circumstances under which our court-musician bound himself to eternal servitude here in this House, and forever lost his opportunity to be with his beloved muse? I was wondering if you had a remedy for that.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Nyx_01
dialogueLines.text: Our court musician brings a certain peace to this abode, my child. But, you may discover, once you find his case files in section zeta-pi-omega, upper beta folder, that the consequences that he suffers may be quite in excess of his trespasses...

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: You don't know how pleased I was to see Orpheus visiting with his muse Eurydice over in Asphodel. Best of all... I don't think he's left for good. He'll just have some time to visit her now and again! Thank you, Nyx, on their behalf, and mine.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Nyx_01
dialogueLines.text: You need not thank me, child. Your actions made this outcome possible. I merely did what anyone in my position would have done.

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: Nyx, I was just busy doing some administrative chamber record-keeping recently, and, wouldn't you know, it somehow slipped my mind where I could find the sentencing parchmentwork for the knave-king Sisyphus, forced to toil out in Tartarus? I ran it by Megaera...

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Nyx_01
dialogueLines.text: He has offered you aid, repeatedly. At first I saw it for a brazen act of bribery, but... try as I might, there was no deeper underpinning to his actions that I could detect. That is to say... the records you seek may be found in section sigma-phi-alpha, lower gamma folder.

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: I waived the punishment-sentence for Sisyphus the knave-king, Nyx, except... he chose to stay! He chose to stay, in Tartarus, I think in part because he likes getting to speak with me, from time to time! Also his boulder friend.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Nyx_01
dialogueLines.text: That sounds to be an agreeable outcome for him. Though, his liberty is not the end of his rehabilitation. As his friend... please make certain he does not run afoul again of your father, child.

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: I don't suppose the name Skelly happens to mean anything to you, does it, Nyx?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Nyx_01
dialogueLines.text: It means nothing to me at all, my child. Though, why do you invoke such names within this House? Who is this Skelly, and what do you presume is the connection that exists between us?

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: Oh, I... well, essentially, he is an animated skeleton who shows up just outside my bedchambers, and lets me practice with my weapons on him. I sort of figured that you put him up to it. So it wasn't you?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Nyx_01
dialogueLines.text: Achilles moves throughout these chambers under my own shroud, if you must know. And thus you have in your possession the Infernal Arms. But, I have not exerted further influence than that. Is this skeleton to be trusted, then?

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: Nyx... I ran into Thanatos out there. He aided me, although I think he's rather upset. That I took off like this.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Nyx_01
dialogueLines.text: Thanatos is worried for you, child. I hope you understand whatever anger you may sense from him is not the feeling at the source. Please forgive his rudeness.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Nyx_01
dialogueLines.text: Thanatos has finally come home. Have you not seen him, child? He has been very much preoccupied of late. It must have gladdened him to see you once again.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Nyx_01
dialogueLines.text: You and my son... it pleases me to see the two of you have grown so close of late. Thanatos has always led a solitary life. He never would complain of it, and yet... I know he yearned for closeness, in his way.

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: Thank you, Nyx. I think we each have harbored feelings for each other for some time. We've finally been true about them.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Nyx_01
dialogueLines.text: Then, in this you each achieved what you desired. I ask that you take care of my dear son. I often fear that he shall work himself too hard.

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: Oh, he's all right. Though, I'll be sure to keep an eye on him.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Nyx_01
dialogueLines.text: You and my son... it pleases me to see the two of you have grown so close of late. Thanatos has always led a solitary life. He never would complain of it, and yet... I know he yearned for closeness, in his way.

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: It's difficult to find a friend down here. Though, I have always had a trusted one in Thanatos, and he in me. I think we have a better understanding of that now.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Nyx_01
dialogueLines.text: Then, may the bonds of your friendship last for as long as this House shall reign beneath the earth, here in the dark. Take care of my dear son.

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: Nyx... you saved my life. Gave me life. When I was born. Father told me. I don't know what to say. Why didn't you tell me?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Nyx_01
dialogueLines.text: There are many things that I am not at liberty to speak of, child. Through my own binding choice. These circumstances are among them. Suffice it to say that your revival... required the extent of my ability. And considerable time.

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: I never should have been born... is that what the Fates wanted? Did you defy your own daughters?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Nyx_01
dialogueLines.text: Silence. Tempt not my daughters thus.

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: Somehow, I always knew. You know... that you were not my birthmother. I mean, look at Hypnos and Thanatos. There's no way that I could have been related to them, everybody knew, and yet... when I finally learned for certain, it was still a shock.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Nyx_01
dialogueLines.text: I always knew, in turn, that you would learn the truth of it, eventually. And I was fearful of what would occur after that time arrived. I'm grateful it has come and gone. Though, tell me. I still find I call you child. Is your preference that I cease to use such terms, from this point on?

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: No, Nyx, please, it's... quite all right. You may not have given birth to me, but you still cared for me. I never doubt that. Likewise, are you sure it's all right that I continue to address you by name?

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: Nyx, you must know something about why Father broke off ties with the Olympians, don't you?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Nyx_01
dialogueLines.text: That is a question only your father can answer, child. I cannot tell you more than you already know.

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: Nyx, you're back! So, how did things with Chaos go? Please tell me it went well?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Nyx_01
