dialogueLines.text: It did. It did go well, my child, I... appreciated having had a chance to reconnect with my great ancestor. Thanks to your generosity, to both of us. We had to make up for a great deal of lost time.

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: So what happens now? You're back on speaking terms, then... does it mean Chaos will stop by for a visit soon, something like that?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Nyx_01
dialogueLines.text: Chaos is comfortable in their realm. I may, however, visit every now and then. Keep them apprised of what unfolds up here. Beyond that... Chaos knows better than to exert too much influence upon this realm. Beyond having already formulated it, and everything.

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: How are you and Chaos getting on, lately, Nyx? Sounds like they expected an awful lot from you before. That you should shine as bright as day, something like that? I can't imagine.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Nyx_01
dialogueLines.text: To shine was never quite my strongest suit. During the dawn of creation, there was much to do. This place... all that you see around you... it was more my leaning. Chaos understands that better, now. As for me, the work helped me to know which limits I have.

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: What limits are those? You seem capable of anything. Probably even shining bright.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Nyx_01
dialogueLines.text: Come, child. We each have our limits.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Nyx_01
dialogueLines.text: Darkness guide you, child.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Nyx_01
dialogueLines.text: I am preoccupied, so please excuse me, child.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Nyx_01
dialogueLines.text: Take care of yourself, child.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Nyx_01
dialogueLines.text: I shall be watching over you, and our House alike.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Nyx_01
dialogueLines.text: Do not concern yourself about your father. I shall be watching him.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Nyx_01
dialogueLines.text: Fear not for Cerberus. He is well taken care of in your absence, and well-fed.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Nyx_01
dialogueLines.text: Go, fulfill your purpose, child.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Nyx_01
dialogueLines.text: Your fate lies somewhere past these walls, child.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Nyx_01
dialogueLines.text: You are always welcome in my company, child.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Nyx_01
dialogueLines.text: Do not despair, and hold to your resolve, child.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Nyx_01
dialogueLines.text: Remember there are those who love you, child.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Nyx_01
dialogueLines.text: Do not let circumstances rattle your resolve, child.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Nyx_01
dialogueLines.text: Thank you for visiting me, child.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Nyx_01
dialogueLines.text: Such brief exchanges gladden me, my child.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Nyx_01
dialogueLines.text: Your father is the same as ever, child.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Nyx_01
dialogueLines.text: Someday, you shall achieve your heart's desire, child.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Nyx_01
dialogueLines.text: The life the Fates apportioned to you lies ahead, my child.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Nyx_01
dialogueLines.text: May you discover what you seek, my child.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Nyx_01
dialogueLines.text: I shall keep you safe during your stay here, child.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Nyx_01
dialogueLines.text: I am well, though thank you for your remembering me, child.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Nyx_01
dialogueLines.text: I am filled with joy to see you whole, my child.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Nyx_01
dialogueLines.text: Always am I grateful for your company, my child.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Nyx_01
dialogueLines.text: You are most generous to visit with me, child.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Nyx_01
dialogueLines.text: You look rather well on this occasion, child.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Nyx_01
dialogueLines.text: It pleases me having these brief exchanges, child.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Nyx_01
dialogueLines.text: My faith in you has never faltered, child.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Nyx_01
dialogueLines.text: I sense your worry for me, child, yet would seek to disabuse you of the sentiment.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Nyx_01
dialogueLines.text: Though your journeys take you elsewhere, still it pleases me to see you, child.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Nyx_01
dialogueLines.text: My focus is on other matters at this time.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Nyx_01
dialogueLines.text: Please leave me to my current task at hand.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Nyx_01
dialogueLines.text: Let us communicate again some other time.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Nyx_01
dialogueLines.text: I shall support you however I can, my child.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Nyx_01
dialogueLines.text: Through any setback, you are always welcome here with me, dear child.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Nyx_01
dialogueLines.text: The nature of this realm, my child, is not fully known or understood, not by anyone within it or above.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Nyx_01
dialogueLines.text: There is no setback which you cannot overcome.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Nyx_01
dialogueLines.text: All here is well at present, child.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Nyx_01
dialogueLines.text: The House stands strong from all your labors, child.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Nyx_01
dialogueLines.text: You bring me pride through your accomplishments.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Nyx_01
dialogueLines.text: Go, and serve this House.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Nyx_01
dialogueLines.text: May Thanatos assist you should you cross his path.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Nyx_01
dialogueLines.text: Remain unseen, my child.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Nyx_01
dialogueLines.text: The Queen Persephone must be so proud of you.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Nyx_01
dialogueLines.text: Go, in honor of your birthmother, the Queen.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Nyx_01
dialogueLines.text: This House is stronger with the Queen returned.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Nyx_01
dialogueLines.text: Honor the Queen, my child.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Nyx_01
dialogueLines.text: Your father's spirits have been better as of late.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Nyx_01
dialogueLines.text: Your birthmother awaits. Go find her, child.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Nyx_01
dialogueLines.text: Seek out your birthmother again, my child.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Nyx_01
dialogueLines.text: Persephone awaits above.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Nyx_01
dialogueLines.text: I know for certain your birthmother waits for you.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Nyx_01
dialogueLines.text: Go. You know precisely where to find her now.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Nyx_01
dialogueLines.text: I am afraid I cannot help you now.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Nyx_01
dialogueLines.text: You shall discover what you seek when it is time.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Nyx_01
dialogueLines.text: You appear to have grown stronger since when last we interacted, child. Your bedroom mirror may appear to be the cause... but then of course the real cause is you.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Nyx_01
dialogueLines.text: I sense the growing darkness in you, child. I am so very proud of you. You always have been capable, yet now, you seem to radiate with newfound power.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Nyx_01
dialogueLines.text: The unfathomable power of the Underworld thrives within you, child. Your godliness has grown since last we spoke; you have learned much of harnessing the dark.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Nyx_01
dialogueLines.text: Even among those born longest ago within the deepest reaches of the Underworld... I have seldom encountered anyone so united with the Darkness of this domain as you are, child.

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: United with the Darkness? I've just... been picking it up here and there. Although, for quite a while, since you mention it! I'm not sure that I feel any different, though.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Nyx_01
dialogueLines.text: Surely that is not so. Think back on when you started all of this. You now know so much more. Are capable of so much more.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Nyx_01
dialogueLines.text: Do not despair, child. Such setbacks are inevitable, and may be overcome with effort, and with time. You made contact with the goddess Athena. She shall be true to her word.

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: I believe it, Nyx. I'm grateful that you put us into contact. I know you took a considerable risk in reaching out.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Nyx_01
dialogueLines.text: The risk is not to me. I expected the Olympians would involve themselves in this, eventually. Reveal to them no more than they already know. Are we understood?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Nyx_01
dialogueLines.text: It pleases me to speak with you at last, child. So the goddess Athena was true to her word. You and she were wise to use discretion in your task.

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: I believe it, Nyx. I'm grateful that you put us into contact. I know you took a considerable risk in reaching out.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Nyx_01
dialogueLines.text: The risk was not to me. It was inevitable that the Olympians would involve themselves in this. The only question was how.

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: You... you're... Nyx, you're not my mother. All this time, you and my father, you... you lied to me?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Nyx_01
dialogueLines.text: Oh, my dear child! So now you know...? So now you know. But, only half the truth. I can explain the rest, so, please.... It never was my wish to hurt you.

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: Erm, Nyx? You've done so much for me, I.. thought you might like this.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Nyx_01
dialogueLines.text: You know your father does not like it when Nectar is doled out on the premises, dear child. However, I am not he... and I wished, regardless, to provide to you a token of my affection.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Nyx_01
dialogueLines.text: How can I possibly accept such generosity, my child? Surely others whom you know are more deserving of such offerings.

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: Nonsense, Nyx. I want you to have it. You've always cared for me. I can't ever repay you for that.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Nyx_01
dialogueLines.text: Your kindness toward me is unnecessary, child. You know I have no expectation of such gifts, from you or anyone.

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: I think of you always, Nyx. You raised me as your own, and I am grateful for it.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Nyx_01
dialogueLines.text: You honor me, my child; although I dread that I am not deserving of such generosity. There is no need to flatter me like this.

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: Be that as it may, Nyx! I would still like for you to have it, with my compliments.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Nyx_01
dialogueLines.text: No gift which you could bring me can exceed the value of our kinship, child. Though, it moves me to receive this offering.

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: It's nothing, Nyx, really. I know you've always believed in me, and it's not something I ever take for granted.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Nyx_01
dialogueLines.text: Among my many children, both those born to me and surrogate to me, you are one of the most thoughtful and most generous of all.

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: You have always cared for me and protected me. And given me someone to look up to. This is nothing, Nyx.

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: Nyx, if you'll forgive me this transgression for a moment, I would like for you to have this vintage bottle of Ambrosia, please. You can hand it off to Megaera as contraband, but... just in case, I wanted you to have it, first.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Nyx_01
dialogueLines.text: Ah. A crystal bottle come all the way from the highest peaks of Mount Olympus to these depths within the earth. Your father always said he hated it.

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: Though, Father does have questionable taste.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Nyx_01
dialogueLines.text: Ah, hahahaha. I accept this gift, with gratitude. This is a delicacy I have not experienced myself. Perhaps I may yet save it for appropriate occasions still to come. Thank you, my child.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Nyx_01
dialogueLines.text: By the way, I meant to tell you, child. The Ambrosia that you gave me? Rather than hold onto it indefinitely, I decided recently to sample it. Your father's claims about its flavor are quite false. I have never tasted anything like it.

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: So glad you liked it, Nyx! And, good timing, too, because... I have for you another bottle, here. They say each one is different in its own respect! And besides, the pleasures of Olympus should be shared. Please, take it, won't you?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Nyx_01
dialogueLines.text: You are persuasive like your mother, and determined like your father, child. But if you so insist, then I shall not deny myself the simple pleasures of this drink. Though rare is the occasion for it here.

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: Nyx, along with giving you this bottle of Ambrosia here, there's something I have meant to say to you. The Three Fates have been very kind to me. Because, in denying me my birthmother... they gave me you. Without your steady presence here, I... can't imagine what it would be like.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Nyx_01
dialogueLines.text: Oh. Then, the Three Fates are whimsical, indeed. When you were born, and I was filled with fear for what it meant for you to be alive, to be responsible for you... I must admit I did not guess that, one evening, you would provide for me both such a gift, and such a sentiment.

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: Well, I mean every word. I love you, Nyx.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Nyx_01
dialogueLines.text: I trust that the Mirror in your bedchamber has proved satisfactory, dear child...?

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: Better than I could have possibly imagined, Nyx. You mentioned there was something more that it could do, once I had grown accustomed. I think I'm ready for that now.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Nyx_01
dialogueLines.text: I think you are, as well. Then, it shall be as you wish.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Nyx_Story_01
dialogueLines.text: My greetings to you, child. How unusual that we should come in contact here. Chaos and I had quite a bit of catching up to do. Please go about your business, and I shall see you back inside the House in time. Provided you return there, of course. And, thank you for this, once again.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Nyx_Story_01
dialogueLines.text: Chaos and I have much catching up to do.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Nyx_Story_01
dialogueLines.text: How interesting to see you here, my child.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Nyx_Story_01
dialogueLines.text: Please go ahead and do not mind me, child.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Nyx_Story_01
dialogueLines.text: Thank you for keeping Chaos company.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Nyx_Story_01
dialogueLines.text: Go forth, bearing the mark of Chaos.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Nyx_Story_01
dialogueLines.text: Chaos and I have so much left to discuss.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Nyx_Story_01
dialogueLines.text: It is comforting to be here again, in such darkness.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Nyx_Story_01
dialogueLines.text: I shall see you back at the House soon, child.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Nyx_Story_01
dialogueLines.text: It is odd to have returned to where I was born.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Nyx_Story_01
dialogueLines.text: We are pleased to see you here, however briefly.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Nyx_Story_01
dialogueLines.text: As Chaos cannot visit us as easily, now I can visit them.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Nyx_01
dialogueLines.text: You must have vanquished Megaera, my child, for she has been sulking in the lounge for quite some time. You are safe here from her whip, but not her words.

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: I'd better talk to her. Thank you for the heads-up, Nyx. See you later, I'm sure.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Nyx_01
dialogueLines.text: You must have vanquished Megaera, my child. For she has been sulking in the corner there for quite some time. You are safe here from her whip, but not her words.

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: I'd better talk to her. Thank you for the heads-up, Nyx. See you later, I'm sure.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Nyx_01
dialogueLines.text: You have revealed every facet of the Mirror of Night, child. The full extent of its power. I was aware this was possible, yet... you achieved it sooner than expected.

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: I don't know where I'd be without it. Probably still in Tartarus trying to get past Meg. I only wish I could get even stronger.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Nyx_01
dialogueLines.text: The Mirror has little to do with your power, let alone your capability. You may deactivate its effects and see for yourself. It merely gave you greater opportunity to learn. And you shall continue to do so.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Nyx_01
dialogueLines.text: Before you go, my child... your father's mood is rather difficult right now. Perhaps just leave him be.

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: Nyx, this may be an odd question, but... do you know what the Fates may have in store for me? They are your daughters, right?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Nyx_01
dialogueLines.text: The Three Fates are my daughters, yes, my child. However, I am no more privy to their workings than the naive mortals living up above. Not even the Olympians can know the weavings of the Fates.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Nyx_01
dialogueLines.text: Darkness guide you, child. You have outgrown this House, of that I am now certain. Should you return again here, I shall keep you safe.

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: I won't forget you, Nyx. I swear it.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Nyx_01
dialogueLines.text: It was a privilege to meet your relatives at last, child. Athena, Ares, and the rest. Confronted face-to-face, they were much more agreeable than I expected. A night we shall remember, was it not?

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: A memorable night it was, indeed, Nyx! For most of us, at least. Dionysus, he won't remember any of it, I don't think. But, it was really good to see everyone having a good time. Getting along as though they didn't hold a grudge! So, then, we're clear, I think?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Nyx_01
dialogueLines.text: Persephone told them everything they needed to hear. Sometimes that is the only way, with family. Though, the Olympians... they are prone to their misunderstandings, are they not? This state of kinship may not last eternally.

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: Well, let's enjoy it while it does, I guess.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Nyx_01
dialogueLines.text: Pride has been the failing of so many mortals, child, and has brought low members of your family, as well. And yet... I am so very proud of you, for what you have achieved. I wanted you to be aware of that.

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: That means a lot to me, Nyx. Though I never could have done any of this without your help. Even after I lashed out at you after I learned the truth about Mother, you never stopped caring for me... and you put yourself directly in harm's way, defying Father's will. Why do all that, for me?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Nyx_01
dialogueLines.text: I am eternally committed to this House. Long before your father came to rule, I was already here. When he arrived... I needed to do everything I could to keep order, and establish with Lord Hades an agreeable relationship. But, there is one more reason, besides that...

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: To get on better terms with the Olympians. They always used to frown on the Underworld, but... now that they know us, they seem much more willing to get involved and help with things.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Nyx_01
dialogueLines.text: A reasonable guess, and not without some truth to it, but no, my child. My reason for helping you is simpler than that. It is because I love you. You may not be born of me, but I raised you like one of my own. And I have cared for you as I am capable.

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: Nyx... I love you, too. Thank you for everything.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Nyx_01
dialogueLines.text: She has departed once again. Your birthmother the Queen. Although, it was not as difficult to see her off, this time. Knowing she shall return.

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: It must be difficult for her... returning to Olympus. She had no love for it at all. I hope she'll be all right.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Nyx_01
dialogueLines.text: Do not despair. We have ways of watching over her. Not that I expect anything untoward to happen, but... well, with your family, one learns to take precautions, as you know.

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: ...I'll miss her.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Nyx_01
dialogueLines.text: So shall I. But it shall not be long, I think.

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: I was pleased to see even Chaos was able to attend our little feast there with the Olympians, in spirit, anyway! Were you able to chat at all?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Nyx_01
dialogueLines.text: Only a little bit. The first communication we have had in quite some time. It went better than expected. Perhaps they and I can continue the conversation at some point. Thank you for your initiative, child.

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: Well, let's enjoy it while it does, I guess.

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: Nyx... I have to ask you something. Are you certain that my search isn't in vain? My mother, what if... what if she's gone?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Nyx_01
dialogueLines.text: Your mother yet lives. There can be no other explanation. For, if she did not, she would be with us, here.... She was a good woman. Stately, kind. I wished to raise you as I thought she would have.

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: Nyx, sometimes I get the impression the Olympians look down on those of us born of the Underworld. Is that my imagination, or, what do you make of it?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Nyx_01
dialogueLines.text: The gods of Mount Olympus tend to think themselves above us, child. Or so your father often likes to say. He alone was relegated to this realm, and grew resentful of his relatives on high.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Nyx_01
dialogueLines.text: Hold, child. I have a message for you from my daughters, themselves. Concerning the Blade of the Underworld in your possession.

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: Wait, you mean the Fates? And Stygius? I'm honored that they would have taken any notice of me. What is it that they want for me to know?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Nyx_01
dialogueLines.text: They wish for me to impart to you these words, so that your blade, in turn, may hear them uttered from your lips: I see your kingly pardon from a prison of stone. Repeat them, and remember.

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: I see your kingly pardon from a prison of stone... this must be for one of the weapon's hidden forms. Please tell your daughters I am very grateful, Nyx.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Nyx_01
dialogueLines.text: I shall, child. Although, I'm certain they already know. Though, whether they are aiding you or this is simply what must come to pass, I cannot say.

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: Nyx, the words you told me... when I spoke them to Stygius, the blade revealed to me the form of a great leader among men, taking counsel with his loyal retinue about a circular table. But if his reign is inevitable, why would the Fates need me?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Nyx_01
dialogueLines.text: That I cannot say, child. It is peculiar having daughters who know every outcome that shall come to pass. It puts a strain on our relationship, and... I have learned never to ask they tell me more than they are willing to impart.

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: I finally made it to the surface, Nyx! Confronted Father, there, out in the cold. I couldn't make it past him, but... I was so close!

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Nyx_01
dialogueLines.text: Do not despair, as I told you before. With effort and with time, most any setback can be overcome. Even your father. You have come closer to your goal. Achieve it!

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Nyx_01
dialogueLines.text: Your father is not here, and you are back. Then, it can only mean one thing. Are you all right, my child?

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: Yes, I think so, Nyx. Returning here by Father's hand does put a special sting into it, though. So, any invaluable tips on how to vanquish him? Some hidden weaknesses only you know about, something like that?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Nyx_01
dialogueLines.text: You know his weaknesses better than anyone. Better even than he. It pains me to know that the conflict between you could not be settled in some other way. But if this is how it has to be... destroy him for me, next you fight up there. However long it takes. Would you do that?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Nyx_01
dialogueLines.text: You have encountered setbacks within Asphodel, have you not, child? The Bloodless shades residing there are eager to bring death to any form of life. Beware of their infernal power.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Nyx_01
dialogueLines.text: You have confronted the Exalted Shades within Elysium, my child. Your father offers them a handsome bounty should they bring your journey to an end. Although, I think the pride of vanquishing you would more than suffice in getting them to take up arms again.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Nyx_01
dialogueLines.text: You nearly gained the surface, child. Considerable progress. Though, the setbacks that you faced within the Temple... if it is of any consolation, the foes you faced there have been quite a burden to your father, too.

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: What, Father can't control the pest problem? Never thought I'd be of any help rampaging through the upper levels of his domain.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Achilles_01
dialogueLines.text: ...I've done the best I could, preparing him. Though, I am fearful it was not enough. No one ever has escaped this place.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Nyx_01
dialogueLines.text: Your expertise has readied him, Achilles. Yet I sense it is your confidence in him which spurs him on. Do not lose faith in yourself now, lest soon he do the same.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Achilles_01
dialogueLines.text: ...You raised him well, O Night. You know that, I hope? Though, I can't imagine even you could have expected this outcome. Thank you... for making all this possible. For all of us.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Nyx_01
dialogueLines.text: You are to thank for this outcome as much as I. Perhaps more so. For I have little choice but to serve this House. I was created in this realm, and it is everything to me. But you? Your loyalty could not have been assumed.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Achilles_01
dialogueLines.text: Oh, I don't know. I felt I had no choice, either, except to serve this House. At least at first. But in training the Prince... preparing him... I felt like I was living for something again. As strange as that may sound.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Nyx_01
dialogueLines.text: Nevertheless, given the circumstances of your life and servitude, you need not have given so much of yourself. The greatness of your mortal days followed you into death.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Achilles_01
dialogueLines.text: ...I cannot ever repay you for what you have done, O Night. Know only that it is my honor to continue to serve at your pleasure, here.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Nyx_01
dialogueLines.text: I am certain I know nothing of what you mean, Achilles. Though, your continued service here is more than welcome. The honor is mine.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hades_01
dialogueLines.text: ...You are not to aid him in these matters, Nyx; his foolishness is to be scorned and reprimanded, not indulged. Do we have an understanding?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Nyx_01
dialogueLines.text: We have an understanding, Hades, yes. He is your own son, after all, and therefore your responsibility. I, in turn, have my commitments to this realm, and to this House. You have aided me in them, so I remain, as ever, in your debt.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hades_01
dialogueLines.text: It is not necessary to remind me. Surely all those who once defied my brothers and myself would much prefer a post within my House over eternity within the lowest depths of Tartarus. Not that the darkness there would trouble you.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hades_01
dialogueLines.text: ...It is plain to me that you are helping him; in spite of my insistence you do not. What do you expect that I should do about this, Nyx? What would you do, in my position? Deception, in my very house and home...! Answer me.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Nyx_01
dialogueLines.text: Hades, my relationship with him remains unchanged. It is the very same relationship from when you first requested I take care of him. He has my full support, and always shall. You must know this. He wishes now to leave. I shall make no attempt to stop him.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hades_01
dialogueLines.text: Tsch. What good is any one of you? He's not even your son; and yet you coddle him. You know as well as I that he cannot succeed. You fill his mind with harmful delusions. If that is what you want for him, then, please! Proceed at pace.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hades_01
dialogueLines.text: Olympus knows of him?! Bestowing on him all their worthless blessings, here, within my realm! Our realm! Explain to me how, exactly, this is possible, Nyx. Either your limitless power has considerably waned, or you are up to something.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Nyx_01
dialogueLines.text: Do not question my power, Hades. Your son was bound to be discovered the closer he came to the surface. The Olympians are pleased to know they have a distant relative, and bid him welcome to their mountain. That is all. Their influence is very limited.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hades_01
dialogueLines.text: Blood and darkness! Do not speak to me about their influence! I've seen their influence firsthand. You've made a foolish mistake.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Nyx_01
dialogueLines.text: You would speak to me of foolish mistakes? You cannot change the course that has been set. Try all you like.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hades_01
dialogueLines.text: What now, Nyx? Do you realize the danger you have put her in? You claim to care for her, but now you threaten to expose her, to them!

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Nyx_01
dialogueLines.text: Hades. When have I even once been unaware of precisely what I mean to do? I shall bring no harm to the Queen, and neither shall your son.

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: Harm to the Queen, wait... what are you talking about?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Nyx_01
dialogueLines.text: Child! Zagreus. Do you intend to betray the presence of your birthmother to the Olympians?

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: No! Of course not. She... seems content to be all on her own.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hades_01
dialogueLines.text: You take his word for it? How can you trust him not to ruin everything?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Nyx_01
dialogueLines.text: Precisely how I trusted him to get us to this point.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hades_01
dialogueLines.text: ...What I attempt to tell you, Nyx, is I am grateful. For our long-standing collaboration, here. My rule was thrust upon you and your kin. By all rights, you ought have undermined my every move. I often thought you would. Yet once Olympus and I severed ties... you stood by me still.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Nyx_01
dialogueLines.text: I am the Night Itself, as you well know, Hades. As I declared to you when first we met: I shall stand by your side, so long as you remain the fate-appointed ruler of this realm.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hades_01
dialogueLines.text: I shall require no additional reminders. Know, in turn, that I shall always oversee this realm to the full extent of my ability.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Nyx_01
dialogueLines.text: ...Persephone, my Queen... it has been far too long. Although, the passing of the time was very kind. It is an honor to see you again.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Persephone_Home_01
dialogueLines.text: Oh, please, Nyx! No formalities between us, right? It's wonderful to see you, too, my friend. The days there on the surface, they were very different, but the nights... I was reminded of our times together, here.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Nyx_01
dialogueLines.text: Good times, I trust.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Persephone_Home_01
dialogueLines.text: Good times, most certainly! I try not to remember the others!

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Nyx_01
dialogueLines.text: We are pleased to have you back. Although, it shall be a difficult adjustment for you once again, I fear. For all of us.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Persephone_Home_01
dialogueLines.text: Well, having built a little house all by myself up there, I think I'm up for anything! You know what it's like.

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: It's done, Mother! Father. The invitations. Personally sent to everyone with whom I've been in contact. So, what do we do now?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Persephone_Home_01
dialogueLines.text: Excellent, Zagreus! Congratulate him, Hades! On a job well done.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hades_01
dialogueLines.text: Grr. Persephone, how can you be so certain your request was executed to your satisfaction?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Persephone_Home_01
dialogueLines.text: It's known as trust, Hades. Or faith? They're similar. Try one, sometime! It'll be good for you. As for what we do next... why, we've a great, big feast we must prepare!

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hades_01
dialogueLines.text: This House is not a suitable location for such guests! Dionysus, alone, shall bring ruin to this place! Or... urgh... the faith thing that you said.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Persephone_Home_01
dialogueLines.text: Yes, good! Now then... we have a lot of work to go around, so why don't we call everyone on staff, and let them in on this, and dole out suitable responsibilities, all right?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hades_01
dialogueLines.text: All right....

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: All right.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Persephone_Home_01
dialogueLines.text: All right!

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Persephone_Home_01
dialogueLines.text: ...Whew! I'd say that went about as well as could have been expected! And the cleanup wasn't all that bad. For the most part.

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: We're fortunate Uncle Zeus seemed to catch on right away, and went along with it. But, this whole elaborate tale you spun... I remember how hurt I felt when I found out Father lied to me about you. Now we're lying to Olympus. What if they find out? Even if they don't... I don't like it.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Persephone_Home_01
dialogueLines.text: Zagreus... I may not be an expert... but here's what I've come to understand about how families work. At least our own. The thing is... how we speak to one another is even more important than the words we use. I think, deep down, everyone in our family knows this.

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: You're saying they all knew you weren't being perfectly honest, and went along with it anyway? I guess the pomegranate thing in particular was a bit of a stretch.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Persephone_Home_01
dialogueLines.text: The common ground in our family is that we want peace. Besides Ares, though even he would rather the conflict occur elsewhere. That elaborate tale... it's less a deception, and more a concession. A way of saying let's move on from the past, and start anew. No hard feelings.

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: You don't have hard feelings? What about Father? What about Demeter?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Persephone_Home_01
dialogueLines.text: Well, let's see. My hard feelings are nothing next to the joy of having my son in my life. Your father never imagined his relationship with me could be out in the open. And my mother... she's just glad I'm alive.

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: And Uncle Zeus is glad things didn't turn out worse for him. I get the sense he acts a bit impulsively at times, and deals with the consequences later.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Persephone_Home_01
dialogueLines.text: What can I tell you, Zagreus? We're family. You said it yourself to me before: We're stuck with one another. That any of us like each other in the slightest... it's a wonder and a blessing, I think.

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: Yeah. I know what you mean. Thanks, Mother. Think I'll be heading out again soon. See you when I get back?

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: Hey, Orpheus. The keepsake that you gave me... it's come in useful, thanks. I can't quite see what's in the container, though. I shouldn't open it, should I?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Orpheus_01
dialogueLines.text: No, Zagreus. Unless of course you wanted to set loose a cherished living memory of mine, and take from me that fleeting bit of joy?

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: Orpheus, the Distant Memory you gave me... it's a bit of your past with Eurydice in there, isn't it. I'm grateful, but... why give me such a precious gift as that?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Orpheus_01
dialogueLines.text: I'm glad you like it, Zagreus. Some memories are cherished, yes, indeed. But there can come a point where they provide more benefit to others than ourselves. Don't you agree?

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: Say, Orpheus. You wouldn't happen to know the nymph Eurydice, would you? Tall, woodsy type? I get the impression maybe you know each other.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Orpheus_01
dialogueLines.text: What? Why... yes. I know Eurydice. Or, I once knew her, I should say. Whyever would you ask me such a thing, my friend?

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: I saw her, mate. In Asphodel. She's doing fine up there! All on her own. The two of you, you used to know each other, then?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Orpheus_01
dialogueLines.text: We more than knew each other, Zagreus. We were husband and wife. She was my muse. To think that we are doomed to be apart during our afterlives. Ah, well!

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: Ah, well? Don't you have a message I could bring to her, if we should meet again? Something like that?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Orpheus_01
dialogueLines.text: I do not wish to trouble her again, my friend. Speak not to her of me, all right? I have my memory of her; it is enough.

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: Say, Orpheus. You're not going to believe this. I happened on a certain nymph during my journeys recently. It was Eurydice. Tall, woodsy type?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Orpheus_01
dialogueLines.text: What? Why... truly. I knew Eurydice resided in your father's realm, but... I had not anticipated you would cross her path. You're certain of this, then?

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: I saw her, mate. In Asphodel. She's doing fine up there! All on her own. You said the two of you were very close, isn't that right?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Orpheus_01
dialogueLines.text: We were much more than that, Zagreus. We were husband and wife. She was my muse. To think that we are doomed to be apart during our afterlives. Ah, well!

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: Ah, well? Don't you have a message I could bring to her, if we should meet again? Something like that?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Orpheus_01
dialogueLines.text: I do not wish to trouble her again, my friend. Speak not to her of me, all right? I have my memory of her; it is enough.

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: Orpheus, I saw Eurydice again. I... couldn't bring myself to do exactly as you asked, I... sort of mentioned you.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Orpheus_01
dialogueLines.text: You what?! Or, what I meant to say was: Oh. Did you perchance get the impression she yet harbors toward me feelings of resentment, and the like?

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: You seemed to be a bit of a sore subject, yes. What happened to the two of you, if I may ask?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Orpheus_01
dialogueLines.text: Oh, it's just... I almost saved her soul from this domain, back when I lived and breathed. But in the final moments of my rescue, my faith faltered. She is lost to me forever, now. Though, I'm pleased to hear she isn't lost to you!

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: Orpheus, I saw Eurydice again. I... couldn't bring myself to do exactly as you asked, I... sort of mentioned you.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Orpheus_01
dialogueLines.text: You what?! Or, what I meant to say was: Oh. Did you perchance get the impression she yet harbors toward me feelings of resentment, and the like?

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: You seemed to be a bit of a sore subject, yes. You really almost freed her from the Underworld, huh?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Orpheus_01
dialogueLines.text: Almost, my friend, almost. But in the final moments of my rescue, my faith faltered. She is lost to me forever, now. Though, I'm pleased to hear she isn't lost to you!

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Orpheus_01
dialogueLines.text: Pray tell me something, Zagreus. How is she lately, hm? Eurydice, I mean. Her hair, fall colors, or full bloom? Her poise, as proud and supple as the oak?

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: She's all right, Orpheus. Seems comfortable within her little place in Asphodel. The only thing that seems to bother her at all is... when I mention you. Is there nothing you can do to patch things up?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Orpheus_01
dialogueLines.text: Alas, I cannot change the past, my friend. So, no. As you can plainly see by now, I'm dead to her. Rightfully so, at that.

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: But that's just a technicality! I mean, you're here, she's there! So what if you're not breathing anymore, you have all eternity ahead of you. Don't you want to see her again?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Orpheus_01
dialogueLines.text: Don't I want to see my Eurydice again...? Why, yes. That, more than anything, my friend. Provided she wanted to see me. I tried once to disturb her everlasting rest, as you well know. And that did not pan out as I had hoped....

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Orpheus_01
dialogueLines.text: What news of late about Eurydice, my friend? She is as delicate as I described, no doubt? Ah, but... I am dwelling on her once again, it seems.

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: I knew she was a talented musician, mate, but you never let on that she is an amazing chef, as well. She's been hospitable to me each time we've met. Though you still seem to be a bit of a sore subject.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Orpheus_01
dialogueLines.text: How very understandable, indeed. Were I to have been failed to be rescued from the depths of hell, mere moments from escape, I too would be quite cross with my attempted rescuer. And for quite some time.

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: Orpheus, I don't suppose you know how come I keep running into Eurydice in particular out there? For all the shades resting eternally in Asphodel, it seems beyond coincidence I always find the one that's dear to you.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Orpheus_01
dialogueLines.text: I have no knowledge of the weavings of the Fates, my friend. Perhaps they trample on what used to be my heart for leisure or for sport, do you suppose?

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: Say, Orpheus, Eurydice told me your songs, some of them came from her? She seems quite brilliant. You choose your muses well!

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Orpheus_01
dialogueLines.text: I owe my everything to my Eurydice. She authored many of my songs, indeed; and she inspired many, many more. Whilst living, we collaborated frequently, you see.

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: But weren't you the famous one? I don't understand why she doesn't share your renown.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Orpheus_01
dialogueLines.text: It is but one of life's injustices, I fear. I always sang her praises every chance I had. And she asserted herself splendidly. Yet even still, our listeners believed our songs, they came from me... what else could I have done?

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: I don't exactly know the ways of mortals, mate. But I'm pleased to hear you always giving credit where it's due.

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: Orpheus, you said that it was one of life's injustices that Eurydice doesn't get her fair share of the credit for the songs she wrote. Isn't there something we can do?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Orpheus_01
dialogueLines.text: Why, I don't know, my friend. Once people set their minds to certain things... it can be difficult to show them other possibilities exist. What's to be done?

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: I don't know... tell them they're wrong? Politely, perhaps not? Or... tell them what's right. Get the word out about what's right? Can't we do that?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Orpheus_01
dialogueLines.text: Get the word out...? But I am court musician of this House. My songs no longer reach too many ears. Although... I do suppose all of these shades about... they are my audience, as well. I shall make certain that they know whose songs I sing.

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: It sounds like Father's very cross with you, Orpheus. Won't you consider singing once again? You know his threat of further punishment is not a bluff.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Orpheus_01
dialogueLines.text: Your father can be.... most persuasive, yes, my friend. However, this time, I am... putting my foot down! I have no plans to sing for him again.

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: Well then how about for me? Or for your other friends among the court?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Orpheus_01
dialogueLines.text: I... no. Please. My muse is gone to me. I can do no such thing.

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: Oh, Orpheus? I heard from Hermes, messenger and god of travel, and all that? It's odd I'm giving you his message in this case, but he says hey! Admires your musical ability, as well.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Orpheus_01
dialogueLines.text: Oh, that was kind of him, my friend! When I first ventured deep into this realm, whilst searching for the soul of my lost muse... after I failed utterly, it was Lord Hermes who whisked her away. It happened so fast. He did an excellent job!

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Orpheus_01
dialogueLines.text: Have you considered doing music-making as a recreational pursuit, my friend? You have such slender hands, why, I believe you'd do quite well at it.

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: Not really been at the top of my list of late, mate, but who knows. It's something I've been meaning to try at some point, I suppose. Thanks for the words of encouragement!

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Orpheus_01
dialogueLines.text: Are you the one whom I should thank for this most wondrous music stand, my friend? Why, it is positively splendid, isn't it?

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: Our renowned court musician ought to have the finest means available, I think. I'm pleased you like it, Orpheus. Though, tell me something, how can it play your music when you're not even here?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Orpheus_01
dialogueLines.text: Who can explain the secret art of Daedalus, my friend? Know only that, whenever it produces music for your listening enjoyment, that the sound you hear is coming from my soul!

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: Orpheus? I know Father appreciates your songs, to the extent he's capable of appreciating anything. Though, has Nyx ever responded to them? She's always right nearby yet you two don't seem to talk...

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Orpheus_01
dialogueLines.text: Almighty Nyx appears to tolerate my presence, Zagreus. For she could cast me into everlasting darkness if my song failed to be pleasing to her. She is a most-discerning listener!

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Orpheus_01
dialogueLines.text: You seem quite agitated, as of late, my friend. Is anything amiss with you, perhaps?

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: Oh! No, I'm all right, Orpheus. Just a lot on my mind is all, sorting out what's next. Stuff I need to work through on my own.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Orpheus_01
dialogueLines.text: Ah, well, I truly understand. I was reluctant to discuss my long-lost muse, really for quite some time. Well, if you do require some emotional support, know that I likely shall be standing over here...

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Orpheus_01
dialogueLines.text: Zagreus! The most amazing circumstances have occurred. The terms of my employment, they've been altered such that... I may visit Asphodel sometimes. May visit her.

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: That's great! Orpheus, what are you waiting for, mate? Go and find your muse out there! You two have lots to catch up on, I think.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Orpheus_01
dialogueLines.text: I think you're right. I am so very anxious, I must say. But I must also thank you, for this opportunity, and... for speaking with me all this time. So, how might I repay you?

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: Why is it always talk of payment around here? You're my mate. And your songs have lifted many spirits here, including mine. Now go, and make the most of this, will you?

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: Hey Orpheus, you're back! How was your trip? I hope it was all right.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Orpheus_01
dialogueLines.text: It was more than all right, my friend. Why, it was positively inspirational. But I've returned as promised to this House. My love is there, whilst my responsibilities are here.

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: You sound despondent again, mate. You must wish you were there right now. I'm sorry you had to come all the way back.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Orpheus_01
dialogueLines.text: Oh, on the contrary, Zagreus. It dawned upon me that having some distance between my responsibilities and my domestic life, why... it is not so bad. Besides, Charon makes travel to and fro quite simple for me, really.

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: I have to ask you something, Orpheus. It seems like everything has been all right for you, except... you don't look very happy to me, mate. Is everything all right? Between you and Eurydice, I mean?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Orpheus_01
dialogueLines.text: Oh, haha, but your concern is much appreciated, friend. I feel I am in everlasting bliss. It simply does not show upon my countenance. Perhaps through song is how I best express myself.

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: Hey, Orpheus. You know I'm very happy that things turned out well between you and Eurydice, but... looking back, I think I might have overstepped my bounds with you a bit, and wanted to say I'm sorry.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Orpheus_01
dialogueLines.text: You're sorry, Zagreus? Whatever for? If not for you, I fear I never would have seen my muse again.

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: Well, it's just... when I met her, and I found out the two of you were having problems, I... well, I just wanted to see you patch things up. But, it wasn't any of my business, and you never asked my help. Neither did she. I could have made things worse.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Orpheus_01
dialogueLines.text: But, you didn't make things worse, my friend. And... truth be told, I do not think I ever would have asked for help, at any point, because... I don't entirely know how. So I am grateful to you, and your generous instinct.

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: I always meant to ask you, Orpheus. Where did you learn to sing like that? Was it really Apollo himself who taught you?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Orpheus_01
dialogueLines.text: Oh, why, I learned from many different sources, Zagreus. That is before all of my inspiration started coming from a single source. I credit my accomplishments to her, and to Apollo, and the gods. And to considerable time spent practicing!

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Orpheus_01
dialogueLines.text: I'm sorry I've no will for singing, Zagreus.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Orpheus_01
dialogueLines.text: I feel a bit self-conscious seeing as I must refuse to sing...!

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Orpheus_01
dialogueLines.text: I remain your humble court musician, Zagreus.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Orpheus_01
dialogueLines.text: I'm truly pleased to see you, Zagreus.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Orpheus_01
dialogueLines.text: Would that I could aid you in some way, my friend.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Orpheus_01
dialogueLines.text: It's awfully quiet whilst you're not around. Still rather quiet now!

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Orpheus_01
dialogueLines.text: Nice speaking with you, Zagreus.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Orpheus_01
dialogueLines.text: I'm afraid I haven't much to say on this occasion, Zagreus.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Orpheus_01
dialogueLines.text: I hope your day or night, I guess, is going nicely, Zagreus.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Orpheus_01
dialogueLines.text: Please do be careful out there, Zagreus.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Orpheus_01
dialogueLines.text: I wish I could direct you towards a much more lively court musician.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Orpheus_01
dialogueLines.text: Normally I'd offer up a song for such occasions, Zagreus.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Orpheus_01
dialogueLines.text: Be strong out there, my friend, OK?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Orpheus_01
dialogueLines.text: I'm cheering for you all the way, my friend.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Orpheus_01
dialogueLines.text: Good luck to you out in that deadly hellscape, Zagreus.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Orpheus_01
dialogueLines.text: Thank you for checking up on me, my friend, although I'm quite all right.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Orpheus_01
dialogueLines.text: I'm still fresh out of songs or will to sing them, Zagreus.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Orpheus_01
dialogueLines.text: I'd ask if you remember what it's like to feel inspired, my friend, but I don't want to know.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Orpheus_01
dialogueLines.text: Oh, hello, Zagreus....

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Orpheus_01
dialogueLines.text: I'm in the middle of a song my friend...!

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Orpheus_01
dialogueLines.text: I'd best get back to singing, Zagreus.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Orpheus_01
dialogueLines.text: Let's talk when I'm not singing, Zagreus?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Orpheus_01
dialogueLines.text: Hello, my friend, let's get back to my song?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Orpheus_01
dialogueLines.text: I'm kind of singing at the moment, Zagreus.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Orpheus_01
dialogueLines.text: Terribly sorry I can't speak with you right now.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Orpheus_01
dialogueLines.text: I thought I'd lost even the gift of music, Zagreus. So, thanks again for helping find that one for me!

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Orpheus_01
dialogueLines.text: I must say, it's nice having some music in my life again, and hopefully in yours!

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Orpheus_01
dialogueLines.text: We all could use a little song from time to time, isn't that so?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Orpheus_01
dialogueLines.text: It's nice that I no longer have to live and breathe, and can be focused purely on my song...!

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Orpheus_01
dialogueLines.text: You rescued me from an eternity of not getting to sing, my friend.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Orpheus_01
dialogueLines.text: My muse writes absolutely wondrous songs, don't you agree?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Orpheus_01
dialogueLines.text: Ah, this reminds me so of my Eurydice...

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Orpheus_01
dialogueLines.text: Would that I were together with her now...

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Orpheus_01
dialogueLines.text: The songs of my Eurydice are without peer...

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Orpheus_01
dialogueLines.text: This is a favorite song of mine, and perhaps yours?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Orpheus_01
dialogueLines.text: Oh, hello, Zagreus. I understand you liberated me from my eternal punishment. And so I have returned to serve your father here. Thanks.

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: Orpheus, you're back! Look, don't get on Father's bad side like that and you're going to be fine. Besides... right now I think he's angrier with me.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Orpheus_01
dialogueLines.text: I grieve for you, my friend. But if you've come to ask me for a song, why, I'm afraid I must stand firm about my answer.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Orpheus_01
dialogueLines.text: Oh, hello, Zagreus. I understand you liberated me from my eternal punishment. And so I have returned to serve your father here. Thanks.

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: Orpheus, you're back! Look, don't get on Father's bad side like that, and you're going to be fine. I've fought with him enough for all our sakes.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Orpheus_01
dialogueLines.text: I grieve for you, my friend. But if you've come to ask me for a song, why, I'm afraid I must stand firm about my answer.

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: Hey, mate, here. I got this for my favorite court musician.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Orpheus_01
dialogueLines.text: Oh, my, but you can't simply be this kind to me without retaliation on my part, my friend! And so, in turn, I have a gift for you!

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Orpheus_01
dialogueLines.text: Why, this is absolutely splendid, Zagreus. Is this really for me? It's so very kind of you to think of me like this.

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: I want you to have it, Orpheus. You've brightened up our lives down here more than you know. I only wish that you could see that.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Orpheus_01
dialogueLines.text: Oh, why! I'm very much obliged that you have thought of me again, for such a wonderfully thoughtful gift!

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: It's nothing, Orpheus. Thank you for all the inspiration through your song. I love your work, mate.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Orpheus_01
dialogueLines.text: You're always much too kind, you know, my friend? Now typically I would express my gratitude through song, except, you understand, don't you?

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: I understand completely, Orpheus. I think we'll both get through this rough patch in due time.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Orpheus_01
dialogueLines.text: You always offer means to quench my thirst, my friend. A wonder that I have such feelings, even now.

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: Now that you've started singing once again, I figured it was thirst-inducing work! Your music stirs so many souls around here, mate.

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: Hey, mate! What do you say we share this over in the lounge?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Orpheus_01
dialogueLines.text: Oh, I, certainly, why, I don't see why not. I mean, it's not as though I've got something to do that isn't that.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Orpheus_01
dialogueLines.text: ...And, anyways, I oughtn't have looked back, I mean, the moral of the story is to not look back when you are told not to look back, you understand, don't you, my friend?

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: Don't look back, huh. I'll try not to. Though, you can't be blamed for doing it yourself, just the one time. What happened to you... it wasn't fair. If you ask me.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Orpheus_01
dialogueLines.text: ...I quite enjoyed our outing, Zagreus. So, I suppose I shall be seeing you around?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Orpheus_01
dialogueLines.text: I am indebted to you, Zagreus, both for your friendship and embarrassment of generosity.

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: And I, in turn, am indebted to you, Orpheus, for your calm demeanor and ability to accept life's greatest challenges. You inspire me, and I wish you the best.

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: Hey, Orpheus, mate! I have something good for you right here... Ambrosia! All the way from the surface. I want you to have it.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Orpheus_01
dialogueLines.text: Ah, but this is splendid, Zagreus. And I must say, apart from my Eurydice, I've never felt as strong a bond of kinship as with you.

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: Hey Orpheus, you know I once wrested this bottle of Ambrosia from the Champions of Elysium, defeating them two against one, just so I could bring it here to you personally?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Orpheus_01
dialogueLines.text: Please, my friend! I feel I am undeserving of such a relentless show of kindness on your part. I shall accept, but you'll relent, won't you? As well as forgive me?

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: Mate, if that is your wish, then it will be my pleasure to oblige. Though, may this gift symbolize the strength of our friendship for an eternity to come. I've learned so much from you.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Orpheus_01
dialogueLines.text: Thank you, my friend. Would that I had capacity to take some joy from this.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Orpheus_01
dialogueLines.text: I hope your day or night is just as splendid as my own, my friend.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Orpheus_01
dialogueLines.text: I pray the Fates fulfill your dreams as they did mine!

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Orpheus_01
dialogueLines.text: Oh, Zagreus, how wonderful to see you once again!

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Orpheus_01
dialogueLines.text: I was just thinking of you, Zagreus.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Orpheus_01
dialogueLines.text: My greetings to my very dearest friend.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Orpheus_01
dialogueLines.text: Oh, hello Zagreus. I was just wondering if you had ever heard of any other court musicians who had lost the will to sing? Your father's asked me to explain myself and honestly it has been rather difficult.

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: If it's any consolation, mate, I'm certain every single undead court musician serving in the throne room of the god of the dead has been through everything you're going through.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Orpheus_01
dialogueLines.text: It's awfully quiet here, you know, when you're out and about? Makes me a bit self-conscious, truth be told, seeing as ostensibly I'm the musician of this court.

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: Look, if you decide to start strumming on that lyre again, I'm sure nobody here is going to mind. Though you take care, OK?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Orpheus_01
dialogueLines.text: Oh, hello Zagreus. You look a little down. And so I was just wondering, would you perchance fancy a song right now?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Orpheus_01
dialogueLines.text: Orpheus wishes to know your choice of music.

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: How about The Lament? You used to play that one a lot!

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Orpheus_01
dialogueLines.text: Oh, fancy that one do you? Alas, I've not the heart to sing it anymore. I'm glad you like it, though.

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: How about In the Blood? That's one of your old hits, right?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Orpheus_01
dialogueLines.text: Oh, fancy that one do you? Alas, I've not the heart to sing it anymore. I'm glad you like it, though.

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: How about Argonauts, didn't you sail with them?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Orpheus_01
dialogueLines.text: Oh, fancy that one do you? Alas, I've not the heart to sing it anymore. I'm glad you like it, though.

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: Orpheus, forgive my bluntness here, but I have to ask. Why won't you sing? You were a legend, mate! Our court musician!

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Orpheus_01
dialogueLines.text: Why, it's that I have lost my muse, my friend. It isn't harder to explain than that. Have you not had such moments in your life, where you had lost the will to chase your passions?

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: I, I suppose I have, yeah. But recently I found my drive again, and that's why I have to get out of here.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Orpheus_01
dialogueLines.text: And I am truly happy for you, friend. Alas, I've had no luck in my own search, not that I'm searching any longer, so, I'll just be over here, OK?

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: Anything I can do to help you find your muse, mate?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Orpheus_01
dialogueLines.text: I fear my muse is long since lost to me, my friend, though truly thanks for asking. I suppose she must be out there somewhere still, since I once traveled 'cross this land in search of her, and found her then. But that was quite a while back.

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: Wait, you found her? What happened?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Orpheus_01
dialogueLines.text: Well, this was back when I was still alive, you understand. Your father, why, he doesn't care for anyone to leave this realm, and, basically now I'm stuck here as well! Not seen her since, and, I'm not much for travel anymore.

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: That's... I'm sorry to hear that. Thank you for letting me know. I hope you get to see her again someday.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Orpheus_01
dialogueLines.text: I appreciate the sentiment, my friend, though please don't worry about her or me. I mean, we're dead! We're fine.

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: Mate, if you don't mind, I'd love it if you told me more about your muse.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Orpheus_01
dialogueLines.text: My muse... never has somebody been as splendorous as my Eurydice...! She was so gentle, yet so stout of heart that it would make yours ache, just thinking on it. She inspired innumerable songs of mine.

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: That's quite a few. She sounds like she was a real catch all right. I'm sorry for your loss. Thank you for sharing your memory of her with me.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Orpheus_01
dialogueLines.text: Thank you for listening, my friend. Eurydice, she likewise used to listen to me all the time....

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: Mate, if you don't mind, I'd love it if you told me more about your muse.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Orpheus_01
dialogueLines.text: My muse... never has somebody been as splendorous as my Eurydice...! She was so gentle, yet so stout of heart that it would make yours ache, just thinking on it. She inspired innumerable songs of mine.

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: Eurydice...? I know a... anyway, she must have been a real catch! I'm sorry for your loss. I have to go look into something here.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Orpheus_01
dialogueLines.text: Thank you for listening, my friend. Eurydice, she likewise used to listen to me all the time....

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Orpheus_01
dialogueLines.text: I understand you've journeyed well beyond the pits of Tartarus, my friend? Another most heroic feat of yours, and truth be told I find it quite inspiring.

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: Inspiring enough to... make you want to sing, again?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Orpheus_01
dialogueLines.text: Oh, that's a rather bold insinuation, Zagreus. I don't know that I've the will for that, I... just, I need some time to think, is that all right?

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: Hey Orpheus! I got myself a lyre much like yours, except I'm absolutely miserable when I try to play! Got any advice?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Orpheus_01
dialogueLines.text: Oh, why, that's splendid, Zagreus. And I can certainly show you a thing or two. First, the proper posture is important, here...

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Orpheus_01
dialogueLines.text: ...now remember to practice the plucking technique which I taught you, and mind your off-hand constantly. And, above all, continue practicing! The joyfulness is in the practicing itself.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Orpheus_01
dialogueLines.text: Zagreus, I heard your recent lyre-playing from your bedchambers. Let no one lead you to believe it's not a reassuring start.

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: Oh, come now, mate, I'm terrible and you know it. You pulling one over on me as payback?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Orpheus_01
dialogueLines.text: I'm being serious, my friend. It is a challenging pursuit requiring a dedicated state of mind. The progress shall come. You shall discover what I mean.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Orpheus_01
dialogueLines.text: Your music-playing has improved substantially. Perhaps in time you shall surpass my own abilities!

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: I highly doubt that, mate, though thanks for noticing. Creating music's quite rewarding once you get the hang of it!

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Orpheus_01
dialogueLines.text: I must commend you on the most splendid lyre-playing emanating from your bedchambers, my friend. It seems we have a second court musician, now.

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: Thank you for encouraging me to give it a shot, mate. And for the kind words.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Orpheus_01
dialogueLines.text: May you emerge from all of this unscathed, my friend.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Orpheus_01
dialogueLines.text: I trust your neverending toil in the Underworld is proceeding well, my friend?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Orpheus_01
dialogueLines.text: I have an optimistic feeling about your next attempt to run away, my friend!

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Orpheus_01
dialogueLines.text: May you yet find your muse somewhere out there, my friend.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Orpheus_01
dialogueLines.text: Each failure brings new opportunities for self-reflection, right, my friend?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Orpheus_01
dialogueLines.text: Please keep me well apprised of all of your incredible achievements, Zagreus.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Orpheus_01
dialogueLines.text: It lifts my spirits when we briefly interact, my friend.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Orpheus_01
dialogueLines.text: I pray the Fates not ruin all your dreams as they did mine!

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: Say, Orpheus! I heard what must have been a song of yours, it went something, something, in the blood, you know that one? It was amazing, mate.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Orpheus_01
dialogueLines.text: Oh, that one, yes, I know! I mean, it was a favorite, I was told, by those who listened to the music I created with my muse. It was a most productive time, back when we were alive!

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: Would you ever sing it again?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Orpheus_01
dialogueLines.text: So, I am quite confused, I must admit, my friend. The Queen Persephone, she is your mother, then? How is it that she came to reside, here?

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: Well, erm... it all started when my father saw her on Olympus, and... they fell madly in love. He swept her off her feet, and brought her here. The Olympians, they got pretty worried when they saw she was gone. But now it's OK! Everything's out in the open.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Orpheus_01
dialogueLines.text: But, surely the Queen's mother on Olympus, the great goddess Demeter... surely she would quite like it if she saw her daughter still, from time to time?

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: Oh, that! Thing is, Mother ate some... pomegranate seeds when she was here. And we have rules about that stuff, you know! It means she cannot leave this place for very long at all. I'm sure Demeter will understand. And unfreeze everything when her daughter's around.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Orpheus_01
dialogueLines.text: Ah, yes, pomegranate seeds, of course, of course! Though, there is one more subject I don't understand, which is... how you fit into all of this. Were you not trying to escape from here?

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: Forget about me, Orpheus! This isn't about me, it's about Mother. What's important is she's here, where she belongs.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Orpheus_01
dialogueLines.text: I see! I understand. Well, thank you, then, for clearing all that up. It is a splendid tale, I must say, and every part of it makes sense to me, at last.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Orpheus_01
dialogueLines.text: I must confess something to you, my friend. My mind still wanders to Eurydice quite often, and my memories of her. Despite my best attempts to move along with my existence here. I would call it a fatal flaw, except, I am already dead.

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: Don't torture yourself, mate. You've been through enough. As has she! You still care for one another just don't have a way to get the point across. But I have an idea.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Orpheus_01
dialogueLines.text: I, too, once had ideas, Zagreus. Ideas, and inspirations. It was so very wonderful, to have a muse...

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: I got you something, Orpheus. It's a new song. From someone who still cares for you.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Orpheus_01
dialogueLines.text: My friend, you wrote a song, for me...? Oh, but this is not from you, it's from... Eurydice... I, no, I don't know what to say, I... I...

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Orpheus_01
dialogueLines.text: I am in concert as perhaps you see, my friend!

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Orpheus_01
dialogueLines.text: Begging your pardon, Zagreus, but I'm amid performance at this time!

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Orpheus_01
dialogueLines.text: I'm quite preoccupied with singing all your praises, Zagreus!

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Orpheus_01
dialogueLines.text: This hymn is dedicated to your deeds, my friend!

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Orpheus_01
dialogueLines.text: I cannot talk right now, my friend, for I must sing!

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Orpheus_01
dialogueLines.text: I trust my music brings some lightness to our gloom-filled times, my friend?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Orpheus_01
dialogueLines.text: The tale of your birth is so complex, my friend!

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Orpheus_01
dialogueLines.text: I ask you please hold your applause until the end!

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Orpheus_01
dialogueLines.text: May my performances spread wide your fame, my friend!

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Orpheus_01
dialogueLines.text: Your parentage need not be secret any longer, Zagreus!

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Orpheus_01
dialogueLines.text: I shall keep singing of your praises, far and wide!

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Orpheus_01
dialogueLines.text: You've helped inspire me to song, my friend!

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Orpheus_01
dialogueLines.text: I must stay focused on my singing, Zagreus.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Orpheus_01
dialogueLines.text: I trust my hymn provides some solace to your days or nights?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Orpheus_01
dialogueLines.text: I've decided I should give this singing thing another shot for now, my friend. I hope you like it.

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: This is amazing, mate. So does this mean you found your muse?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Orpheus_01
dialogueLines.text: Oh no, I never found my muse, my friend. It's just... seeing you carrying on like this time after time, I was reminded I ought to get on with my life, too, such as it is right now.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Orpheus_01
dialogueLines.text: I've decided I should give this singing thing another shot for now, my friend. I hope you like it.

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: This is amazing, mate. You sing quite beautifully, with or without your muse.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Orpheus_01
dialogueLines.text: How kind of you. It's just... seeing you carry on like this, time after time, I was reminded that I might as well get on with my existence, too. Such as it is right now.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Orpheus_01
dialogueLines.text: I've decided I should give this singing thing another shot for now, my friend. I hope you like it.

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: It's beautiful, mate. If only Eurydice could hear you right now.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Orpheus_01
dialogueLines.text: How kind of you, my friend. It's just... seeing you carry on like this, time after time, I was reminded I ought to get on with my existence, too. Such as it is right now.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Orpheus_01
dialogueLines.text: I became so enthralled with song once more, my friend, that I have been inspired to compose another one. For old time's sake, perhaps.

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: This is amazing, mate. You sing quite beautifully, with or without your muse.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Orpheus_01
dialogueLines.text: How kind of you, although... my voice is nothing but the crunch of gravel underfoot compared to hers, which soars as though on wings.

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: Orpheus, sorry to interrupt, it's just... I couldn't help but notice that new ballad that you're playing, there.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Orpheus_01
dialogueLines.text: Technically, one ought consider it a hymn, my friend! In honor of your numerous travails, prior to your current set of circumstances, which must pale in comparison to all the rigors you've endured to date!

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
