dialogueLines.text: Erm, the thing is... none of the information in your hymn is accurate. It's nonsense. I was just kidding with you, mate! I tried to make that clear. You get so lively when I tell you my tall tales, you know? You're not angry with me, are you...?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Orpheus_01
dialogueLines.text: Heavens, no, my friend! Your humility is matched only by your perseverance in the face of adversity! May your great deeds live on forever, much like you!

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: You sing beautifully, mate. If only Eurydice could hear you singing this right now.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Orpheus_01
dialogueLines.text: Oh. Thanks for the sentiment, my friend, it's just... when I sing this song of hers, why, I've this feeling where my heart once was that, well... perhaps she can.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Orpheus_01
dialogueLines.text: I've decided I should give this singing thing another shot for now, my friend. I hope you like it.

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: You sing beautifully, mate. If only Eurydice could hear you singing this right now.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Orpheus_01
dialogueLines.text: Oh. Thanks for the sentiment, my friend, it's just... when I sing this song of hers, why, I've this feeling where my heart once was that, well... perhaps she can.

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: Say, Orpheus? I ever tell you I once single-handedly slew a multi-headed bone hydra within the magma fields of Asphodel without so much as breaking a sweat?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Orpheus_01
dialogueLines.text: My, that is wonderful to hear, my friend! Another in a litany of such heroic deeds, more noble than the heroes of our verse, and even great Achilles over there!

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: I'm glad you think so, mate! These tales of my valorous accomplishments do seem to cheer you up, so, I will keep reporting back once I rack up some more.

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: Hey Orpheus? You know about the time I vanquished seven legions of Bloodless warriors with both hands tied behind my back while hopping on one foot? Lord Poseidon dared me to do it, you know.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Orpheus_01
dialogueLines.text: Why, that is a spectacularly vivid tale, Zagreus, and most deserving to be chronicled in song! Would that there were a poet here still willing to commit such deeds to verse.

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: Hey, Orpheus? I take it you know all about the time I burned my father's hands to a crisp the moment I was born? He grasped me by the feet, and they caught fire! And that's why he still wears those fancy rings: to cover up the hideous scar tissue.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Orpheus_01
dialogueLines.text: But that is such a splendid tale, Zagreus, deserving to be sung both far and wide, until it turns into the stuff of myth, as deathless as the gods themselves! I'll gladly tell that one about!

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: Hey Orpheus, you've noticed the resemblance between me and Dionysus, haven't you, mate? I'll tell you a little secret but you have to promise not to tell... We're really the same god. I love a good feast, don't I?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Orpheus_01
dialogueLines.text: But, Zagreus, that is a striking revelation, the connection you describe. I see it! I can see it now! To think the god of wine and you are intricately linked, it isn't obvious, is it?

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: Oh, there's more to it! A bit grim, really, as I'm a little older than I look. You see, the Titans, they once tore me limb from limb, but then my heart was saved, and that's where Dionysus comes from! That is the connection between us, you understand me, mate?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Orpheus_01
dialogueLines.text: Your tale stirs my soul, it truly does, my friend. It is a marvel to me that such heroism isn't widely known, and I'm committed to the spread of all such truths.

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: Hey Orpheus, you know I've just been messing with you, with those Dionysus tales, and all that? Just trying to liven things up for us, mate.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Orpheus_01
dialogueLines.text: Oh, Zagreus! There isn't need for such humility, not when one's deeds of heroism are as great and numerous as yours! It is an honor to have heard such deeds first-hand!

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: You know, mate, sometimes I worry that you're taking my tall tales rather seriously. You're not really going about and telling everyone this stuff, are you?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Orpheus_01
dialogueLines.text: Come now, my friend! I know this is a test of faith! Of course I am committed to the spread of such heroic deeds as yours. And I do so enjoy getting to hear them first!

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: Hey, Orpheus? I figure that you've heard by now about how I bested the Minotaur Asterius, alongside Theseus, the Champion of Elysium, himself? I don't think I even broke a sweat.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Orpheus_01
dialogueLines.text: I scarce can possibly imagine such a spectacle, my friend! But certainly I shall attempt to try!

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: Orpheus, you have to hear this straight from me. I vanquished my own father, on the outskirts of the Temple of Styx. At the surface! I know how this looks, since I'm back here, and so is he. You believe me, don't you?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Orpheus_01
dialogueLines.text: Of course I do, my friend! Though, you must know, your father has requested his relationship to you be kept quite clandestine. You'll understand if I not sing your praises in this case, won't you?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Orpheus_01
dialogueLines.text: ...You're much too modest, Lord Achilles, for someone with such a number of heroic deeds and glorious decapitations to his name! You ought to be more like ever-boastful Zagreus, and talk it up some more!

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Achilles_01
dialogueLines.text: Oh, fear not, Orpheus, I was quite boastful in my day. And would you look at where it got me? Besides, I've not the imagination to come up with tales as tall as those of our Prince Zagreus.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Orpheus_01
dialogueLines.text: ...Do you miss it, yourself, Lord Achilles, sir? The freshness of the air. The need to have meals several times a day, and such.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Achilles_01
dialogueLines.text: I scarce remember it, my friend. Although your songs do stir up memories, sometimes. I'm used to things the way they are, I think. But you, not so much?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Orpheus_01
dialogueLines.text: Oh, on the contrary, sir, I am profoundly satisfied. We are most fortunate to have employment even after death. Imagine, merely resting for eternity, when you could work!

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Orpheus_Story_01
dialogueLines.text: Why, Zagreus, how wonderful to see you here. Eurydice and I, we have been catching up for all the time we've spent apart.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Eurydice_01
dialogueLines.text: Yeah. So, if you would excuse us for a little while, hon? I'm not finished catching up with him just yet. But first, before you go!

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Orpheus_Story_01
dialogueLines.text: The singers offer several delectable treats.

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: I've never heard anything like this. As good as you two sound each on your own, you're positively wonderful together.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Orpheus_Story_01
dialogueLines.text: A splendid compliment, my friend. Doubtless the Fates themselves conspired to ensure Eurydice and I would meet, and sing together. Fall in love. And then be cruelly separated for some time!

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Eurydice_01
dialogueLines.text: Now don't you start with that again, you get me, Orphy? Got our whole entire afterlives ahead of us! And lots of songs to catch up on.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Orpheus_Story_01
dialogueLines.text: Why, you're correct of course, my dear. If you'll excuse us, Zagreus? Our calling beckons.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Orpheus_Story_01
dialogueLines.text: The singers offer several delectable treats.

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: Hey, you two, how's everything lately? On speaking terms again? Or singing terms?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Orpheus_Story_01
dialogueLines.text: Oh, things are going very wonderfully, my friend. I never thought I'd see Eurydice again, after I...

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Eurydice_01
dialogueLines.text: Orphy still just can't shut up about that time, hon. But we're making progress. Thanks for having him stop by. I'm sure he needed plenty of encouragement.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Orpheus_Story_01
dialogueLines.text: The singers offer several delectable treats.

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: Hey, you're singing In the Blood after all, Orpheus! Thank you for taking my request!

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Orpheus_Story_01
dialogueLines.text: Oh, it is certainly my pleasure, Zagreus. This song of Eurydice's and mine, I've a connection to it, I suppose. Reminded me of some fond memories!

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Orpheus_Story_01
dialogueLines.text: The singers offer several delectable treats.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Eurydice_01
dialogueLines.text: You know this tune, hon? It's one of our old hits!

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Eurydice_01
dialogueLines.text: The singers offer several delectable treats.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Eurydice_01
dialogueLines.text: We were just thinking about old times, Orphy and I!

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Eurydice_01
dialogueLines.text: The singers offer several delectable treats.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Eurydice_01
dialogueLines.text: Thought we'd mix up our singing just a bit here, hon!

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Eurydice_01
dialogueLines.text: The singers offer several delectable treats.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Eurydice_01
dialogueLines.text: Got to tell you I've warmed up to this old song over the years!

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Eurydice_01
dialogueLines.text: The singers offer several delectable treats.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Orpheus_Story_01
dialogueLines.text: I know that you enjoy this one, my friend!

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Eurydice_01
dialogueLines.text: The singers offer several delectable treats.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Orpheus_Story_01
dialogueLines.text: This one reminds me of my days alive with my Eurydice...!

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Eurydice_01
dialogueLines.text: The singers offer several delectable treats.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Orpheus_Story_01
dialogueLines.text: This song had quite a bit of popularity, one time.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Eurydice_01
dialogueLines.text: The singers offer several delectable treats.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Eurydice_01
dialogueLines.text: Hey, nice of you to visit us, Your Royal Majesty! For you!

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Eurydice_01
dialogueLines.text: The singers offer several delectable treats.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Eurydice_01
dialogueLines.text: Orphy and I, we're doing great here thanks to you. You take care, hon!

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Eurydice_01
dialogueLines.text: The singers offer several delectable treats.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Eurydice_01
dialogueLines.text: Hope you find yourself someone to sing with, too, hon.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Eurydice_01
dialogueLines.text: The singers offer several delectable treats.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Eurydice_01
dialogueLines.text: Don't fret, Your Majesty, I'll have him back to work on time, you hear that, Orphy?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Eurydice_01
dialogueLines.text: The singers offer several delectable treats.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Eurydice_01
dialogueLines.text: Orphy and me, we're like your typical old married couple, hon.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Eurydice_01
dialogueLines.text: The singers offer several delectable treats.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Eurydice_01
dialogueLines.text: If you find happiness out there, hon, don't look back.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Eurydice_01
dialogueLines.text: The singers offer several delectable treats.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Eurydice_01
dialogueLines.text: Things aren't so bad here, hon. Hope you can say the same.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Eurydice_01
dialogueLines.text: The singers offer several delectable treats.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Eurydice_01
dialogueLines.text: What can I say, hon? We like practicing our favorite tune!

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Eurydice_01
dialogueLines.text: The singers offer several delectable treats.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Eurydice_01
dialogueLines.text: Well if it isn't the Hades kid come to pay the both of us a visit!

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Eurydice_01
dialogueLines.text: The singers offer several delectable treats.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Orpheus_Story_01
dialogueLines.text: ...You must know, you sound as wonderful as ever, now, my dear!

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Eurydice_01
dialogueLines.text: The singers offer several delectable treats.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Orpheus_Story_01
dialogueLines.text: Is she not truly wonderful, my friend?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Eurydice_01
dialogueLines.text: The singers offer several delectable treats.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Orpheus_Story_01
dialogueLines.text: We sing, and sing, and never once grow weary, Zagreus!

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Eurydice_01
dialogueLines.text: The singers offer several delectable treats.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Orpheus_Story_01
dialogueLines.text: I never thought I would see her again. You are to thank for this, my friend.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Eurydice_01
dialogueLines.text: The singers offer several delectable treats.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Orpheus_Story_01
dialogueLines.text: When we thus sing together, Zagreus, I lose all track of time.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Eurydice_01
dialogueLines.text: The singers offer several delectable treats.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Orpheus_Story_01
dialogueLines.text: Is the singing of Eurydice not the most splendid thing that you have ever heard?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Eurydice_01
dialogueLines.text: The singers offer several delectable treats.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Orpheus_Story_01
dialogueLines.text: Shall there ever be a voice as lovely as Eurydice's, my friend?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Eurydice_01
dialogueLines.text: The singers offer several delectable treats.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Orpheus_Story_01
dialogueLines.text: It gladdens me that you could join us here, my friend.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Eurydice_01
dialogueLines.text: The singers offer several delectable treats.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Eurydice_01
dialogueLines.text: Guess what we've been up to lately, hon? Now if you'll excuse us?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Eurydice_01
dialogueLines.text: The singers offer several delectable treats.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Eurydice_01
dialogueLines.text: We just finished eating, here! Got plenty left over.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Eurydice_01
dialogueLines.text: The singers offer several delectable treats.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Eurydice_01
dialogueLines.text: The one thing better than singing, hon, is singing with somebody you love.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Eurydice_01
dialogueLines.text: The singers offer several delectable treats.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Eurydice_01
dialogueLines.text: Orphy here's just practicing for when he's got to sing next for Hades Senior!

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Eurydice_01
dialogueLines.text: The singers offer several delectable treats.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Eurydice_01
dialogueLines.text: What do you know, we were just in the middle of a song here, hon!

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Eurydice_01
dialogueLines.text: The singers offer several delectable treats.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Eurydice_01
dialogueLines.text: Not much luck trying to teach Orphy to cook, but he can sing, all right!

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Eurydice_01
dialogueLines.text: The singers offer several delectable treats.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Eurydice_01
dialogueLines.text: Look, Orphy, we've got an audience! So sing your heart out!

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Eurydice_01
dialogueLines.text: The singers offer several delectable treats.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Eurydice_01
dialogueLines.text: Don't mind Orphy, here, hon. Can't snap him out of it once he gets singing!

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Eurydice_01
dialogueLines.text: The singers offer several delectable treats.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Orpheus_01
dialogueLines.text: ...You called upon your humble court musician, as I understand, Lord Hades sir? Referring to myself of course?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hades_01
dialogueLines.text: Well, Orpheus... if you're quite finished with your little stint within the depths of Tartarus, I'd like to ask again: Play us a song, already. Or else next time I'll not be quite so charitable with your punishment.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Orpheus_01
dialogueLines.text: Oh, that. I must apologize, again, Lord Hades sir. For I'm afraid that I've no wish to sing as yet. In spite of your persuasiveness even. And, that, um, is my final word upon the subject. At this time.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hades_01
dialogueLines.text: Is that so...? Why, then, I'll go ahead and wait. I've all the patience in the world.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hades_01
dialogueLines.text: Help me to understand something here, Orpheus. Why do I bother keeping such a worthless shadow of a court musician in my House? You prattle ceaselessly, yet I can't get even a single note out of you?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Orpheus_01
dialogueLines.text: If I may be so bold, my lord? Perhaps the merest sight of me still stirs in you a memory of my performances of yore. It gladdens me you used to like my songs.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hades_01
dialogueLines.text: I used to tolerate your insolence. We're finished here. You find your voice. Immediately! You have one hundred years, and not a moment more.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hades_01
dialogueLines.text: Finally returned to your senses, haven't you, Orpheus. A court musician who refused to sing! Such nonsense. I trust there shall be no more insubordination from you, moving forward?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Orpheus_01
dialogueLines.text: I'm gladdened that my songs yet stir your soul, my lord. But, I have to confess my choice to sing again was motivated largely by the Prince. And by my muse, of course.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hades_01
dialogueLines.text: Still going on about your so-called muse, I see. You had your chance with Eurydice. You squandered it. And now you're here; a deal is a deal. I expect you to comply with your end of our agreement.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Orpheus_01
dialogueLines.text: ...I'm pleased to know my new, more flexible arrangement remains to your satisfaction, still, my lord. I must admit I feared that my departure would result once more in a protracted stay within the pits of Tartarus.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hades_01
dialogueLines.text: Don't give me such ideas, Orpheus! Instead, you go about your business, there, and play your blasted songs when circumstances call for it.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Orpheus_01
dialogueLines.text: I shall be happy to oblige, my lord. Eurydice and I are grateful for your magnanimity.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Persephone_Home_01
dialogueLines.text: ...Ah, but to think that our esteemed musician of the court is none other than you, Orpheus! In matters of music, at least, Hades and I have similar tastes. Anyway, I don't mean to keep you!

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Orpheus_01
dialogueLines.text: I shall be honored to perform my songs, Your Grace. And, might I add, Your Grace bears many of the striking features of your son? I understand now where he gets his charm.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Patroclus_01
dialogueLines.text: You mentioned something of your parentage, stranger. I have a question on that subject, if you please.

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: I please, sir. What's your question? Ask away.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Patroclus_01
dialogueLines.text: Your father must have records of the dead. So I was wondering if you might do for me a favor, and look up the whereabouts of someone I once knew.

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: You've done a lot for me. The least I can do is ask around. Who is it that you're looking for?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Patroclus_01
dialogueLines.text: I seek one called Achilles. Was rather famous once among the living, but among the dead, who knows. I thought he would be here, but he is not.

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: ...Achilles... I've heard the name before. And, erm, can I ask why you wish to know of him? In case it happens to come up.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Patroclus_01
dialogueLines.text: It's just... I simply miss him, stranger. That is all.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Patroclus_01
dialogueLines.text: Patroclus offers one of several premium goods.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Patroclus_01
dialogueLines.text: Something the matter, there, stranger? Or have you come to torment me some more with idle chat?

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: You know Achilles, don't you, sir. You're Patroclus. Achilles... serves us at my home. He trained me. He sends you his regards and love. He says to tell you he is thinking of you always.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Patroclus_01
dialogueLines.text: ...That name. So then you've seen him, have you. In your father's house. And want me to believe he said all that? Tsk. You'll forgive me if I am a little skeptical. In life, I left Achilles. And in death, he left me. We're even. And we've nothing left to say to one another, now.

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: But, that can't possibly be true. There must be some misunderstanding here. You haven't spoken since you died?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Patroclus_01
dialogueLines.text: We haven't, no. And I don't think we shall. But, you can humor him for me, I guess. Tell him I'm doing perfectly all right, won't you?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Patroclus_01
dialogueLines.text: Patroclus offers one of several premium goods.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Patroclus_01
dialogueLines.text: Something the matter, there, stranger? Or have you come to torment me some more with idle chat?

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: Patroclus. I know who you are, sir. And, you know Achilles. He serves us at my home. He trained me. He sends you his regards and love. He says to tell you he is thinking of you always.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Patroclus_01
dialogueLines.text: ...Ah. So then you've seen him, have you. In your father's house. And want me to believe he said all that? Tsk. You'll forgive me if I am a little skeptical. In life, I left Achilles. And in death, he left me. We're even. And we've nothing left to say to one another, now.

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: But, that can't possibly be true. There must be some misunderstanding here. You haven't spoken since you died?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Patroclus_01
dialogueLines.text: We haven't, no. And I don't think we shall. But, you can humor him for me, I guess. Tell him I'm doing perfectly all right, won't you?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Patroclus_01
dialogueLines.text: Patroclus offers one of several premium goods.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Patroclus_01
dialogueLines.text: Something the matter, there, stranger? Or have you come to torment me some more with idle chat?

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: Patroclus. I know who you are, sir. And, you know Achilles. He serves us at my home. He trained me. He sends you his regards and love. He says to tell you he is thinking of you always.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Patroclus_01
dialogueLines.text: ...Ah. So then you've seen him, have you. In your father's house. And want me to believe he said all that? Tsk. You'll forgive me if I am a little skeptical. In life, I left Achilles. And in death, he left me. We're even. And we've nothing left to say to one another, now.

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: But, that can't possibly be true. There must be some misunderstanding here. You haven't spoken since you died?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Patroclus_01
dialogueLines.text: We haven't, no. And I don't think we shall. But, you can humor him for me, I guess. Tell him I'm doing perfectly all right, won't you?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Patroclus_01
dialogueLines.text: Patroclus offers one of several premium goods.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Patroclus_01
dialogueLines.text: So, how's Achilles doing lately, stranger? I figured you could tell me since you've all this access to him, and I don't.

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: He's forced to do my father's bidding. And sworn to secrecy, apparently. I don't pretend to fully understand. But, all in all, I think he's doing well. I hope... you'll see him for yourself.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Patroclus_01
dialogueLines.text: You don't fully understand, but hope. I see. Well, if you do get to the bottom of this little mystery at some point, you'll indulge me, won't you?

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: I'll... see what I can do about it, sir.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Patroclus_01
dialogueLines.text: Patroclus offers one of several premium goods.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Patroclus_01
dialogueLines.text: He's never going to return to me, is he. He's in your father's service now. He's never getting out. Neither are you.

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: No. That's not acceptable to me. None of it.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Patroclus_01
dialogueLines.text: How bold. So what do you intend to do? You're leaving, anyway.

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: I'll figure something out. But you do something for me, in the mean time: Don't give in to what you're feeling now, all right? Achilles loves you still, like you don't know.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Patroclus_01
dialogueLines.text: Does he? I'm past the point of making promises, but... I pray the Fates help you in your endeavors, there.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Patroclus_01
dialogueLines.text: Patroclus offers one of several premium goods.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Patroclus_01
dialogueLines.text: He's never going to return to me, is he. He's in your father's service now. He's never getting out. Neither are you.

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: No. That's not acceptable to me. None of it.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Patroclus_01
dialogueLines.text: How bold. So what do you intend to do? You're leaving, anyway.

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: I'll figure something out. But you do something for me, in the meantime: Don't give in to what you're feeling now, all right? Achilles loves you still, like you don't know.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Patroclus_01
dialogueLines.text: I'm past the point of making promises, but... thank you for your words. I love him still, in turn. I pray the Fates help you in your endeavors, there.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Patroclus_01
dialogueLines.text: Patroclus offers one of several premium goods.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Patroclus_01
dialogueLines.text: He's just as obstinate as ever, isn't he? Always refused to budge. He ever tell you of the war in which we fought? I fought, I should say. He could have ended it. But he refused to take up arms.

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: Achilles hasn't been particularly forthcoming about his mortal days, no. So he wouldn't fight? I've heard that mortals sometimes frown on that, but... why should he have fought if he didn't believe in the cause?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Patroclus_01
dialogueLines.text: He shared our cause! So what if it was not completely his? If not for his stubbornness, I... well... I would have come here just a bit later, I think.

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: He blames himself for what happened to you, you know. I figured he failed to defend you or something, not that... he didn't fight at all. Although, he said something about how, once you died, he shortly followed in a rage...

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Patroclus_01
dialogueLines.text: I wouldn't know a thing about that, stranger, for you see... I was already dead. Getting my place here in the afterlife all sorted out. Took quite some time, getting accustomed to all this.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Patroclus_01
dialogueLines.text: Patroclus offers one of several premium goods.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Patroclus_01
dialogueLines.text: A question, if you don't mind, stranger? Because, I've had one nagging at me since we last discussed the subject. What, exactly, happened to Achilles, can you say? After I died, that is. I've heard the rumors, but... I don't trust any of this boastful lot.

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: Well, from what I gathered, sir... after you fell, he was overcome with grief. And anger. He joined the war, just like you urged. And, single-handedly, he almost ended it.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Patroclus_01
dialogueLines.text: So then, he threw away his life. Soon enough after I lost mine. Yet, despite vanquishing an army on his own, he wound up in the lowest pits of Tartarus? Instead of here, among the great?

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: You should know, sir. You were buried together. Your ashes, that is. It was his final request. As for how he came to be at my father's house, that's something I think he can best explain. And I will do everything I can to make sure that he has the opportunity.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Patroclus_01
dialogueLines.text: ...Our ashes, you say. Together? Tsk. The fool. We could have lived! Not been reduced to meal for the worms. But... I appreciate the information, stranger. Now... please, go.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Patroclus_01
dialogueLines.text: Patroclus offers one of several premium goods.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Patroclus_01
dialogueLines.text: ...Forgive me, stranger. For, in retrospect, I was too terse after you told me what became of my Achilles. Tales of his climactic battle, I had heard. The stuff about... his ashes, and my own... that was all new.

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: No need to even ask forgiveness, sir. Achilles is one of my closest friends, but.. he doesn't care to talk about himself. About those times. It must be very painful. For the both of you.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Patroclus_01
dialogueLines.text: Oh, we've all seen our share of pain, by now. But... knowing that he cared for me, in the end... after the end... it dulls the feeling of it, for a bit. As do these conversations, I guess.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Patroclus_01
dialogueLines.text: Patroclus offers one of several premium goods.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Patroclus_01
dialogueLines.text: Must say that's an impressive spear you've got, there, stranger. Rather like the one Achilles held. He gave it to you, then? Followed him beyond the grave, only to be handed off.

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: It's... a bit more complicated than that, sir, though yes. This is the very same as he once wielded. I am honored to carry it now.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Patroclus_01
dialogueLines.text: I'll bet. Perhaps you'll even come to surpass the body count Achilles piled up with it back in his day, seeing as you seem to have limitless opportunity to use it.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Patroclus_01
dialogueLines.text: Patroclus offers one of several premium goods.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Patroclus_01
dialogueLines.text: You know, stranger, there was a time Achilles would have killed someone for taking up his spear. But there you are, like you own it.

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: I don't think anybody owns this spear, sir. I'm hanging onto it for the time being. Though I don't think I'll be the last to do so.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Patroclus_01
dialogueLines.text: Oh, there's no need to get all philosophical about it. It's a killing weapon. Nothing more.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Patroclus_01
dialogueLines.text: Patroclus offers one of several premium goods.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Patroclus_01
dialogueLines.text: What is that on your wrist, there, stranger? Let me see, is that... a bracer of the myrmidons. But, then...

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: Wait, this? How did you know? It's just a keepsake from a friend.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Patroclus_01
dialogueLines.text: A keepsake? From a friend...? You tell your friend, then, that Patroclus waits for him, and that he holds no grudge, should he return to him.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Patroclus_01
dialogueLines.text: Patroclus offers one of several premium goods.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Patroclus_01
dialogueLines.text: What is that on your wrist, there, stranger? Let me see, is that... a bracer of the myrmidons. But, then...

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: You recognize it, then. Of course. It's from Achilles, sir. He gifted it to me. He trained me.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Patroclus_01
dialogueLines.text: I see... a gift. Well, then, you let Achilles know Patroclus waits for him, and holds no grudge, should he return to him.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Patroclus_01
dialogueLines.text: Patroclus offers one of several premium goods.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Patroclus_Unnamed_01
dialogueLines.text: What is that on your wrist, there, stranger? Let me see, is that... a bracer of the myrmidons. But, then...

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: Wait, this? How did you know? It's just a keepsake from a friend.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Patroclus_01
dialogueLines.text: A keepsake? From a friend...? You tell your friend, then, that Patroclus waits for him, and that he holds no grudge, should he return to him.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Patroclus_01
dialogueLines.text: Patroclus offers one of several premium goods.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Patroclus_Unnamed_01
dialogueLines.text: What is that on your wrist, there, stranger? Let me see, is that... a bracer of the myrmidons. But, then...

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: You recognize it, then. Of course. It's from Achilles, sir. He gifted it to me. He trained me.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Patroclus_01
dialogueLines.text: I see... a gift. Well, then, you let Achilles know Patroclus waits for him, and holds no grudge, should he return to him.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Patroclus_01
dialogueLines.text: Patroclus offers one of several premium goods.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Patroclus_01
dialogueLines.text: What is that on your wrist, there, stranger? Let me see, is that... a bracer of the myrmidons. Achilles...

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: This is his bracer, yes. You recognize it, then. Were you a myrmidon, yourself? I heard they were unstoppable.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Patroclus_01
dialogueLines.text: Well, now... if we were so unstoppable, we'd not be here, would we? May that bracer protect you more than its like protected our phalanxes.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Patroclus_01
dialogueLines.text: Patroclus offers one of several premium goods.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Patroclus_01
dialogueLines.text: You've ample time for fishing, have you, stranger? And here I thought you'd more important business to attend to, though it's not for me to judge, I guess.

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: I'll have you know some of us take matters of fishing very seriously, sir! Besides... helps take my mind off having to fight your Elysian brethren nonstop.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Patroclus_01
dialogueLines.text: Oh I'd not take your mind off this fight if it's one that you hope to win, stranger. I once took my mind off a fight, and, well... I wound up here.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Patroclus_01
dialogueLines.text: Patroclus offers one of several premium goods.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Patroclus_01
dialogueLines.text: Never quite considered... you're related to the gods upon Olympus, aren't you, stranger...? I should be far more careful around you.

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: What do you mean by that, sir? Though, yes, my father being who he is, it means I have a lot of uncles, cousins, and the like, up on the surface, there.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Patroclus_01
dialogueLines.text: Well... you would be wise to watch yourself, with some of them, I think. Don't get on anyone's bad side, if you can help it. Which, when I yet lived, none of us could.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Patroclus_01
dialogueLines.text: Patroclus offers one of several premium goods.

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: You must have met so many great heroes in your day, having fought alongside Achilles... do you still keep in touch with any of them, here?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Patroclus_01
dialogueLines.text: Great heroes... haven't you seen, by now, what they are like, stranger? Not all entirely what they're cracked up to be. But, there were good ones, yes. Suppose they're mostly keeping to themselves. Like I am here. Perhaps we'll run into each other, down the line.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Patroclus_01
dialogueLines.text: Patroclus offers one of several premium goods.

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: This spearpoint that you gave me, sir. It's been quite useful, though I have to ask you... is there any particular significance to it? Most spearpoints aren't imbued with power such as this.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Patroclus_01
dialogueLines.text: Oh, that. Just something I'd been meaning to get rid of, but... it can be difficult, discarding trifles from the past. Knowing it's provided you some benefit is welcome news.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Patroclus_01
dialogueLines.text: Patroclus offers one of several premium goods.

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: Did you once wield a spear yourself, sir? In your fighting days?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Patroclus_01
dialogueLines.text: Did I once wield a spear? You could say that I did. I had my moments with it, anyway. Even once slew a demigod! He's probably somewhere about. Quite awkward, running into those you killed. But in Elysium it's all taken in stride.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Patroclus_01
dialogueLines.text: Patroclus offers one of several premium goods.

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: I have to tell you, sir, it was a real sight to see you fighting alongside Achilles. You two were amazing!

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Patroclus_01
dialogueLines.text: Oh, I'm a little rusty. But he's not. Still very much in tiptop shape, except more able to control his temper, I should think.

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: Don't sell yourself so short! Though, I thought you laid down your spear forever. Why choose to fight again?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Patroclus_01
dialogueLines.text: Achilles swayed me into realizing some activity may be quite good for me. And besides... if we can aid you in a pinch, that's worth my while.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Patroclus_01
dialogueLines.text: Patroclus offers one of several premium goods.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Patroclus_01
dialogueLines.text: You ever been in love with someone, stranger? Might be a mortals-only thing, for all I know. You gods have strange relationships, I hear.

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: I have been, sir. Or, I should say, I am.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Patroclus_01
dialogueLines.text: Ah, that's good. Cling to the feeling, then. And if you lose it... reach for it. Search for it, no matter what, all right? Because, without it... well, there isn't much of a comparison, I think.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Patroclus_01
dialogueLines.text: Patroclus offers one of several premium goods.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Patroclus_01
dialogueLines.text: You ever been in love with someone, stranger? Might be a mortals-only thing, for all I know. You gods have strange relationships, I hear.

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: Not sure that I have been, sir. I understand it's one of those things where, you'll know it when it happens.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Patroclus_01
dialogueLines.text: Perhaps you shall, someday or night down here. The feeling comes with pain, and plenty of it, but... the pain is worth it, in my limited experience. And once you find that feeling, cling to it. As tightly as you can. If you don't mind my saying.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Patroclus_01
dialogueLines.text: Patroclus offers one of several premium goods.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Patroclus_01
dialogueLines.text: Even the Bull of Minos couldn't stop you, huh. That's one whose way I would stay out of if I could, but... well, you'd not be here if you couldn't handle yourself, I guess.

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: You heard I battled with Asterius, here, sir? I thought you kept more to your own devices. Weren't much for conversation with the other shades.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Patroclus_01
dialogueLines.text: Oh, you're correct in that assessment, stranger. But, as you've seen, I've no ability to stop others from coming and going as they please. So I do tend to hear a thing or two from passersby.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Patroclus_01
dialogueLines.text: Patroclus offers one of several premium goods.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Patroclus_01
dialogueLines.text: I've meant to ask you something, stranger. Would you mind doing me a favor, please? If it happens to come up along your path. Suppose I ought to tell it to you first, prior to asking a response.

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: No, of course, sir, what is it? What's on your mind?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Patroclus_01
dialogueLines.text: If I know my Achilles, then he's being rather guarded, when it comes to this predicament we're in. How can somebody be so brash yet hate to take unnecessary risks? Just let him know I said to risk it all.

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: Risk it all? Risk what all? He wants what's best for you. He wouldn't want anything to happen.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Patroclus_01
dialogueLines.text: It's already long since happened. I daresay that, right now, provided what you've said is true, then... it appears we've more to gain than lose. Isn't fear for the weak?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Patroclus_01
dialogueLines.text: Patroclus offers one of several premium goods.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Patroclus_01
dialogueLines.text: I heard you've vanquished Theseus however many times of late. Or... someone matching your description has, stranger. Almost had to laugh when I heard that. Though, I suspect he hasn't learnt a thing.

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: What, you know him? Though, yes, he does seem rather set in his ways. Convinced himself that I'm some sort of demon from the lowest depths. Which... well, it's partly true, I guess.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Patroclus_01
dialogueLines.text: You're quite notorious, around these parts. The tales of your father's monstrous son, kept under lock and key within his House. Fear and ignorance make for great stories, no?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Patroclus_01
dialogueLines.text: Patroclus offers one of several premium goods.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Patroclus_01
dialogueLines.text: Couldn't help but hear the rattling of battle chariots of late. Raucous laughter and the like. You've really riled up the locals this time, stranger.

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: Must be Theseus and his fancy Macedonian. So he's been joyriding about in that thing? Showing it off?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Patroclus_01
dialogueLines.text: Your arrivals are becoming a bit of an event, I'd say. And, yes, the Champion and his bull friend are likely at the center of it. You'll have to forgive me that I've no intent on attending your contests, but... well, here.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Patroclus_01
dialogueLines.text: Patroclus offers one of several premium goods.

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: You know, for such a loudmouth obnoxious braggart, Theseus does seem to have formed a strong kinship with the Bull of Minos, hasn't he. Are they truly as inseparable as they seem? I feel bad for the Bull.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Patroclus_01
dialogueLines.text: Sometimes it's our differences that make us drawn to one another, no? And, at other times, there's no accounting for taste. I often don't know which is which, and they can have each other, far as I'm concerned.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Patroclus_01
dialogueLines.text: Patroclus offers one of several premium goods.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Patroclus_01
dialogueLines.text: Well, what's it going to be this time, stranger? The HydraLite? Maybe the Kiss of Styx?

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: You sure you don't need any of this stuff, sir? I really appreciate it, but... I don't want to take it for granted.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Patroclus_01
dialogueLines.text: Oh, come, this is Elysium. Taking such things for granted is the point. We gloried shades receive our stipend of the finest goods, so we can battle till we've had our fill. But I've already had my fill, I think.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Patroclus_01
dialogueLines.text: Patroclus offers one of several premium goods.

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: Whatever happened with that war you mentioned, sir? If you don't mind my asking.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Patroclus_01
dialogueLines.text: Well, truth be told, here, stranger... I do mind. You speak as one who's not experienced a war. Those of us who survived... or, you know what I mean... we do bear some responsibility to history. But, all the same, it's not a time and place to which I would return. I trust you'll understand.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Patroclus_01
dialogueLines.text: Patroclus offers one of several premium goods.

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: I always wondered, sir. What was Achilles like...? During his youth, I mean. If you can say.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Patroclus_01
dialogueLines.text: Hah. How to describe what somebody was like? Everyone who saw him on the battlefield agreed he was magnificent, stranger. His allies and his enemies alike. But, there was more to him, I think. A certain outlook. A sense of justice. I knew him before he was a warrior. Only reason I suppose I liked him.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Patroclus_01
dialogueLines.text: Patroclus offers one of several premium goods.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Patroclus_01
dialogueLines.text: Achilles is returned to work for now... but, waiting for him, knowing he'll return... rather than waiting for the sake of it, for nothing... such a different feeling, stranger. I'd forgotten it entirely, I think.

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: I'm happy for the both of you. So, is everything all right, then? The circumstances under which you were separated in life... it's unimaginable, really. How does one move on from a thing like that?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Patroclus_01
dialogueLines.text: Anger fades, I guess, stranger. Even the famous anger of Achilles. Anger burns hot, then burns out. What's left is a dull ache, but... underneath it, there was something else. It felt as though... well, I was still alive. That feeling... it's worth holding onto.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Patroclus_01
dialogueLines.text: Patroclus offers one of several premium goods.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Patroclus_01
dialogueLines.text: It's good to see you again, stranger. You don't mind if I keep calling you that, no? Couldn't find a better nickname for you, after all. Besides... I don't want to cause you any trouble.

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: Don't mind at all, Patroclus, sir. Coming from you I consider it a term of endearment.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Patroclus_01
dialogueLines.text: You have my thanks for that...

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Patroclus_01
dialogueLines.text: Patroclus offers one of several premium goods.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Patroclus_01
dialogueLines.text: You again? Then, here. Fates know I'll not be needing this.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Patroclus_01
dialogueLines.text: Patroclus offers one of several premium goods.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Patroclus_01
dialogueLines.text: Move right along, stranger. Let's skip the pleasantries.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Patroclus_01
dialogueLines.text: Patroclus offers one of several premium goods.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Patroclus_01
dialogueLines.text: And here I thought you must have given up already.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Patroclus_01
dialogueLines.text: Patroclus offers one of several premium goods.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Patroclus_01
dialogueLines.text: Enjoying the splendors of Elysium, yet, stranger?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Patroclus_01
dialogueLines.text: Patroclus offers one of several premium goods.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Patroclus_01
dialogueLines.text: Don't let me interrupt your stay in paradise, stranger.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Patroclus_01
dialogueLines.text: Patroclus offers one of several premium goods.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Patroclus_01
dialogueLines.text: Returned to take more of these trinkets off my hands, have you?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Patroclus_01
dialogueLines.text: Patroclus offers one of several premium goods.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Patroclus_01
dialogueLines.text: Oh, it's only you again, stranger. It's only me, as well.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Patroclus_01
dialogueLines.text: Patroclus offers one of several premium goods.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Patroclus_01
dialogueLines.text: Take one of these and then just leave me be, stranger.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Patroclus_01
dialogueLines.text: Patroclus offers one of several premium goods.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Patroclus_01
dialogueLines.text: I'd like to be alone again, stranger, so you go on ahead.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Patroclus_01
dialogueLines.text: Patroclus offers one of several premium goods.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Patroclus_01
dialogueLines.text: No need to linger with me, stranger. Go.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Patroclus_01
dialogueLines.text: Patroclus offers one of several premium goods.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Patroclus_01
dialogueLines.text: Why your path keeps on crossing mine, I've not the slightest clue.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Patroclus_01
dialogueLines.text: Patroclus offers one of several premium goods.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Patroclus_01
dialogueLines.text: Some other time, stranger. Right now I'm preoccupied with sitting here.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Patroclus_01
dialogueLines.text: Patroclus offers one of several premium goods.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Patroclus_01
dialogueLines.text: There's nothing you can do to help me, stranger.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Patroclus_01
dialogueLines.text: Patroclus offers one of several premium goods.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Patroclus_01
dialogueLines.text: Can't you see I'm busy whiling away for all eternity?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Patroclus_01
dialogueLines.text: Patroclus offers one of several premium goods.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Patroclus_01
dialogueLines.text: A fine whatever time it is to you, stranger.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Patroclus_01
dialogueLines.text: Patroclus offers one of several premium goods.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Patroclus_01
dialogueLines.text: You haven't lost your will just yet, I see.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Patroclus_01
dialogueLines.text: Patroclus offers one of several premium goods.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Patroclus_01
dialogueLines.text: Expecting I'll still be right here next time, stranger.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Patroclus_01
dialogueLines.text: Patroclus offers one of several premium goods.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Patroclus_01
dialogueLines.text: Just go, already, towards your next death or whatever else awaits.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Patroclus_01
dialogueLines.text: Patroclus offers one of several premium goods.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Patroclus_01
dialogueLines.text: I'll wait for him, however long it takes. You tell him that.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Patroclus_01
dialogueLines.text: Patroclus offers one of several premium goods.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Patroclus_01
dialogueLines.text: I pray Achilles is surviving well. And same for you.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Patroclus_01
dialogueLines.text: Patroclus offers one of several premium goods.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Patroclus_01
dialogueLines.text: It's good of you to visit, though there's little I can offer in return.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Patroclus_01
dialogueLines.text: Patroclus offers one of several premium goods.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Patroclus_01
dialogueLines.text: Please make yourself at home here in this corner of eternal paradise.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Patroclus_01
dialogueLines.text: Patroclus offers one of several premium goods.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Patroclus_01
dialogueLines.text: Do let Achilles know I'm doing well, and I'll be ever patient, here.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Patroclus_01
dialogueLines.text: Patroclus offers one of several premium goods.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Patroclus_01
dialogueLines.text: I doubt there's much that I can do for you.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Patroclus_01
dialogueLines.text: Patroclus offers one of several premium goods.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Patroclus_01
dialogueLines.text: You're interfering with my quiet time, you know.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Patroclus_01
dialogueLines.text: Patroclus offers one of several premium goods.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Patroclus_01
dialogueLines.text: Yes, what is it, this time?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Patroclus_01
dialogueLines.text: Patroclus offers one of several premium goods.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Patroclus_01
dialogueLines.text: Might I somehow assist you, then?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Patroclus_01
dialogueLines.text: Patroclus offers one of several premium goods.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Patroclus_01
dialogueLines.text: Oh good, somebody's here to save me from myself.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Patroclus_01
dialogueLines.text: Patroclus offers one of several premium goods.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Patroclus_01
dialogueLines.text: How can I be of use to you, stranger?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Patroclus_01
dialogueLines.text: Patroclus offers one of several premium goods.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Patroclus_01
dialogueLines.text: I've no shortage of time to spare, I guess.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Patroclus_01
dialogueLines.text: Patroclus offers one of several premium goods.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Patroclus_01
dialogueLines.text: Ah, just a stranger passing through, I see.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Patroclus_01
dialogueLines.text: Patroclus offers one of several premium goods.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Patroclus_01
dialogueLines.text: Not in the mood for conversation now, stranger.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Patroclus_01
dialogueLines.text: Patroclus offers one of several premium goods.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Patroclus_01
dialogueLines.text: Go on about your business, there, stranger.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Patroclus_01
dialogueLines.text: Patroclus offers one of several premium goods.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Patroclus_01
dialogueLines.text: Good fortune getting out of here, stranger.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Patroclus_01
dialogueLines.text: Patroclus offers one of several premium goods.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Patroclus_01
dialogueLines.text: Here's something for the journey still to come.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Patroclus_01
dialogueLines.text: Patroclus offers one of several premium goods.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Patroclus_01
dialogueLines.text: Prepared to face the champion, soon, stranger?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Patroclus_01
dialogueLines.text: Patroclus offers one of several premium goods.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Patroclus_01
dialogueLines.text: May this aid you against the champion, out there.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Patroclus_01
dialogueLines.text: Patroclus offers one of several premium goods.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Patroclus_01
dialogueLines.text: Be careful with my brethren out there, stranger.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Patroclus_01
dialogueLines.text: Patroclus offers one of several premium goods.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Patroclus_01
dialogueLines.text: Battle with glory and all that, all right, stranger?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Patroclus_01
dialogueLines.text: Patroclus offers one of several premium goods.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Patroclus_01
dialogueLines.text: I would prefer to be alone for now, stranger.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Patroclus_01
dialogueLines.text: Patroclus offers one of several premium goods.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Patroclus_01
dialogueLines.text: Just don't go starting any wars you don't intend to finish, stranger.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Patroclus_01
dialogueLines.text: Patroclus offers one of several premium goods.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Patroclus_Unnamed_01
dialogueLines.text: ...Go on, stranger. Plenty more shades looking to fight with you out there.

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: I'm just passing through. You seem less warlike than the rest. May I ask your name, sir? I'm Zagreus.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Patroclus_Unnamed_01
dialogueLines.text: Names are there to be forgotten, stranger. You shouldn't be here. May the Fates favor your journey. But just in case they don't, here. Why don't you take this?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Patroclus_01
dialogueLines.text: The shade offers one of several premium goods.

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: Hey, look, I can tell you're struggling right now, and... maybe this isn't the way to help you through it all, but... I want you to have this.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Patroclus_01
dialogueLines.text: Ahh, so you are taking pity on me, then? Well, I've no pride like many others here, so I shall take it. Though, let's make it a fair exchange, shall we?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Patroclus_01
dialogueLines.text: Why would you waste a proper vintage on the likes of me? Questions that are better left unanswered, I would guess.

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: If this eases your burden even for a little while, then it's in good hands, as far as I'm concerned.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Patroclus_01
dialogueLines.text: Ah, a means to drown my sorrows temporarily. That's good. And rather kind of you, I have to say.

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: It's nothing. Thank you for not trying to slay me where I stand and boast about it, like seemingly everyone else around here.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Patroclus_01
dialogueLines.text: Oh. Here in Elysium you have to win this stuff, so I have rarely had occasion to enjoy it. Thanks.

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: I'd say you earned it more than anybody else I've seen around here, lately. Though I don't make the rules.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Patroclus_01
dialogueLines.text: You owe me neither time nor generosity, and certainly not this. But if you truly have no better use for it...

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: It's not a matter of owe, sir. Please open your mind to the possibility, nay the fact, that there are those who care about your wellbeing.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Patroclus_01
dialogueLines.text: Just the relief from misery that I was looking for. A fleeting feeling, really, but I'll take a fleeting feeling over none.

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: That's both very dejected and very astute of you to say. Take care of yourself, will you?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Patroclus_01
dialogueLines.text: You're much too kind to such a wretched shade, you know. But I'll not turn away this type of thing.

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: You don't attack me indiscriminately, I give you Nectar. I don't think I'm being over-generous!

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: Here, sir. The drink of the gods, themselves. Thought maybe you would like to try it! Just a small token of my appreciation for your selflessness with me, time after time.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Patroclus_01
dialogueLines.text: You'd give the precious gift of Ambrosia to someone like me, stranger? A gloried thing like that. Why bother...? I should be just another shade to you.

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: Well, you're not. You're someone I look forward to seeing here in Elysium, which is more than I can say for just about anybody else around here, so... please, enjoy it. Perhaps with Achilles.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Patroclus_01
dialogueLines.text: Well... perhaps I'll try. You're not so bad, you know that, stranger? Careful with that, or you'll undermine the ruthless reputation that you have.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Patroclus_01
dialogueLines.text: Forgive my bluntness, but, why go out of your way for me like this...?

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: I'm not going out of my way, sir. Look, I don't pretend to know the depths of your despair. But when I look at you, I'm reminded of how I've often felt. When I was in that dark place, someone said to me: Don't give up. If nothing else... it gives you something to do.

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: I don't see that last bottle of Ambrosia anywhere, which I can only take to mean... you need another. Here, sir. Thanks for seeing me through all of this, time after time.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Patroclus_01
dialogueLines.text: You're thanking me, stranger? I've been here for some time, I think, and yet... some of your customs I'll admit remain quite strange to me. You've done more for me than I've any right to expect, from anyone.

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: It's just a bottle of Ambrosia, sir, it's really no big deal. OK, fine, it's rather difficult to come by, but still!

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Patroclus_01
dialogueLines.text: You jest, but I do not. I'd offer you my lifelong gratitude, but... well, you know.

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: You've got the rest of your life ahead of you, as far as I'm concerned. Here's to starting anew, right? You can call me Zagreus, or stranger, whatever suits you.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Patroclus_01
dialogueLines.text: And... I'm Patroclus, by the way. I'm realizing now... we've not officially met. It's good to know you, Zagreus. Stranger.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Patroclus_01
dialogueLines.text: You look positively beaten, there, stranger. Let's see if we can't get you on your feet. For I can do the listless sitting for the both of us.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Patroclus_01
dialogueLines.text: Patroclus offers one of several premium goods.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Patroclus_01
dialogueLines.text: Oh, you look terrible, if I may say, stranger. Perhaps one of these trinkets can be of some small help.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Patroclus_01
dialogueLines.text: Patroclus offers one of several premium goods.

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: Is something wrong, there, sir? I mean, shouldn't you be up and about, competing for eternal pride and glory and all that?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Patroclus_01
dialogueLines.text: What's the use? Let's say we fought, if anybody asks. Now, go. I've no quarrel with you, besides. I'll just remain here, comfortably at rest, for some untold millennia, I guess. Have a nice... whatever time it is.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Patroclus_01
dialogueLines.text: Patroclus offers one of several premium goods.

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: Is something wrong, there, sir? I mean, shouldn't you be up and about, competing for eternal pride and glory and all that?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Patroclus_01
dialogueLines.text: What's the use? Let's say we fought, if anybody asks, now go. I've no quarrel with you, besides. I'll just remain here, comfortably at rest, for some untold millennia, I guess. Have a nice... whatever time it is.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Patroclus_01
dialogueLines.text: Patroclus offers one of several premium goods.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Patroclus_01
dialogueLines.text: Still toiling around within Elysium, I see. Battling my brethren, in the name of glory, is that it?

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: I'm heading for the surface. Though your brethren are slowing me down. Whether for glory or at my father's behest.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Patroclus_01
dialogueLines.text: Your father...? Ah, that's right, but you're the Prince, aren't you. You must see plainly, then, what your birthright amounts to: You're no better off than any of us here. Now, go.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Patroclus_01
dialogueLines.text: Patroclus offers one of several premium goods.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Patroclus_01
dialogueLines.text: You again. I told you to stay clear of me. Your father's quite the big shot around here, but that means nothing to me, understand? Go, and tell him he can cast me in the pits of Tartarus, for all I care. It'd be a welcome change of pace from being put to pasture here.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Patroclus_01
dialogueLines.text: Patroclus still offers one of his premium goods.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Patroclus_01
dialogueLines.text: I was unkind last time, stranger. Forgive my indiscretions there. Or don't. But I wished to apologize.

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: Think nothing of it, sir. Though if there's something I could do to help, you'd tell me, wouldn't you?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Patroclus_01
dialogueLines.text: You have no power to release me from this coil, I'm afraid. So just go on about your business, and leave me to mine.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Patroclus_01
dialogueLines.text: Patroclus offers one of several premium goods.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Patroclus_01
dialogueLines.text: Stranger, I am warning you: If you keep showing up like this, I'm going to have to find a new nickname for you.

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: No need to go out of your way, there, sir. We're just acquaintances. I don't know anything about you, either.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Patroclus_01
dialogueLines.text: And you are better off. This place is for the greatest of the Greeks. Do I seem like the sort to fit in, here? Go on and chat with Theseus, or someone of that sort.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Patroclus_01
dialogueLines.text: Patroclus offers one of several premium goods.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Patroclus_01
dialogueLines.text: Stranger, I am warning you: If you keep showing up like this, I'm going to have to find a new nickname for you.

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: No need to go out of your way, there, sir. I rather feel like a stranger around here. I have no plans to extend my stay.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Patroclus_01
dialogueLines.text: And you are better off. This place is for the greatest of the Greeks. Do I seem like the sort to fit in, here? Go on and chat with Theseus, or someone of that sort.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Patroclus_01
dialogueLines.text: Patroclus offers one of several premium goods.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Patroclus_01
dialogueLines.text: Tell me, stranger, if it pleases you. How is it you keep running into me...? I thought this the remotest corner of Elysium.

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: The ever-shifting chambers of the Underworld leave quite a bit to chance. I took our meetings merely for coincidence.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Patroclus_01
dialogueLines.text: When chance is too far-fetched to be believed, then it's the Fates at play. Or something else. I don't know whether to believe you, but I know that I want you to leave me, now.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Patroclus_01
dialogueLines.text: Patroclus offers one of several premium goods.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Patroclus_01
dialogueLines.text: Tell me, stranger, if it pleases you. How is it you keep running into me...? I thought this the remotest corner of Elysium.

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: The ever-shifting chambers of the Underworld leave quite a bit to chance. I took our meetings merely for coincidence.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Patroclus_01
dialogueLines.text: When chance is too far-fetched to be believed, then it's the Fates at play. Or something else.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Patroclus_01
dialogueLines.text: Patroclus offers one of several premium goods.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Patroclus_01
dialogueLines.text: What is it this time, stranger? You're quite effective at locating me, but not so good at leaving me in peace.

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: Just on one of my little jaunts through paradise is all, sir. Happened to be on the way toward my destination.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Patroclus_01
dialogueLines.text: You think this place a paradise? At least down in the depths, there are so many other souls with whom to share one's misery. No offense... I know that you're just passing through.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Patroclus_01
dialogueLines.text: Patroclus offers one of several premium goods.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Patroclus_01
dialogueLines.text: So, stranger. How goes willful disobedience of late? Would that I, too, had it within me to defy the gods.

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: Oh, you know, it's going. That I've been able to make it this far means I am getting somewhere, I suppose. No thanks to all your brethren in Elysium.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Patroclus_01
dialogueLines.text: No use trying to reason with my brethren here. Many of the shades whom you've encountered, why, I watched them throw away their lives when they were men. Yet still they seek to prove to one another and themselves that they are great warriors.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Patroclus_01
dialogueLines.text: Patroclus offers one of several premium goods.

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: Say, isn't that the Lethe right nearby? You've been through so much, don't you ever... you could just drink deeply of the River of Forgetfulness, and be done with, no?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Patroclus_01
dialogueLines.text: It has been rather tempting, yes. And I have taken sips, from time to time. But I've some memories I'm not quite ready to give up on, yet.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Patroclus_01
dialogueLines.text: Patroclus offers one of several premium goods.

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: You ever want to travel past this glade, sir? Elysium in all its splendors... there's a lot to see. Sure you're all right?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Patroclus_01
dialogueLines.text: I'm sure enough, stranger. I traveled far and wide whilst I drew breath. That got me rid of any wanderlust. This little corner suits me well, I think. I have the statue there, the river... it's quite nice.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Patroclus_01
dialogueLines.text: Patroclus offers one of several premium goods.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Patroclus_01
dialogueLines.text: Achilles tells me that you're here to stay, at least for now, stranger. A welcome bit of news. Assuming, of course, that it's for the best, for you?

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: I think so, sir. I'll still be passing through, and visiting the surface every opportunity I get. But it seems my place really is here. Besides, it means getting to see you every now and then, like this.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Patroclus_01
dialogueLines.text: That sounds agreeable to me. Then, you take care.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Patroclus_01
dialogueLines.text: Patroclus offers one of several premium goods.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Patroclus_01
dialogueLines.text: There's been considerable talk of late about the Queen Persephone, stranger. Your mother, it would seem? Sounds to me your tale's almost as complicated as my own.

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: It's a long story, at this point, indeed. But yes. The Queen... my mother... she's returned. And, it's been really good, having her back.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Patroclus_01
dialogueLines.text: I can only begin to imagine. May she help govern all of this with wisdom and with grace.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Patroclus_01
dialogueLines.text: Patroclus offers one of several premium goods.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Patroclus_01
dialogueLines.text: It's good to see you once again, stranger. Now, here.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Patroclus_01
dialogueLines.text: Patroclus offers one of several premium goods.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Patroclus_01
dialogueLines.text: We're faring well enough, and may you fare the same.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Patroclus_01
dialogueLines.text: Patroclus offers one of several premium goods.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Patroclus_01
dialogueLines.text: You've come to be a welcome sight, stranger.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Patroclus_01
dialogueLines.text: Patroclus offers one of several premium goods.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Patroclus_01
dialogueLines.text: May you prevail in your battles still to come, stranger.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Patroclus_01
dialogueLines.text: Patroclus offers one of several premium goods.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Patroclus_01
dialogueLines.text: Achilles and I were just passing the time I guess.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Patroclus_01
dialogueLines.text: Patroclus offers one of several premium goods.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Patroclus_01
dialogueLines.text: We were just wondering when you'd arrive, stranger.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Patroclus_01
dialogueLines.text: Patroclus offers one of several premium goods.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Patroclus_01
dialogueLines.text: I and Achilles here still have a lot of catching up to do.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Patroclus_01
dialogueLines.text: Patroclus offers one of several premium goods.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Patroclus_01
dialogueLines.text: Fight like my friend here used to fight, stranger.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Patroclus_01
dialogueLines.text: Patroclus offers one of several premium goods.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Patroclus_01
dialogueLines.text: May all your fears leave you behind once you walk past that door.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Patroclus_01
dialogueLines.text: Patroclus offers one of several premium goods.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Patroclus_01
dialogueLines.text: You'll show that champion of ours, won't you, stranger?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Patroclus_01
dialogueLines.text: Patroclus offers one of several premium goods.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Patroclus_01
dialogueLines.text: I'm pleased to be of any help at all.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Patroclus_01
dialogueLines.text: Patroclus offers one of several premium goods.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Patroclus_01
dialogueLines.text: First a visit from Achilles, and now you.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Patroclus_01
dialogueLines.text: Patroclus offers one of several premium goods.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Patroclus_01
dialogueLines.text: Please stay as long as you would like here in our little glade.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Patroclus_01
dialogueLines.text: Patroclus offers one of several premium goods.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Patroclus_01
dialogueLines.text: Take caution with our battle-loving brethren around here.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Patroclus_01
dialogueLines.text: Patroclus offers one of several premium goods.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Patroclus_01
dialogueLines.text: If you can make yourself at home in such a place, please do, stranger.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Patroclus_01
dialogueLines.text: Patroclus offers one of several premium goods.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Patroclus_01
dialogueLines.text: Our so-called champion isn't as popular around here as he used to be.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Patroclus_01
dialogueLines.text: Patroclus offers one of several premium goods.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Patroclus_01
dialogueLines.text: Just restocked on these battle-trinkets, if you'd like one, stranger.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Patroclus_01
dialogueLines.text: Patroclus offers one of several premium goods.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Patroclus_01
dialogueLines.text: You've become quite the talk of all Elysium of late.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Patroclus_01
dialogueLines.text: Patroclus offers one of several premium goods.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Patroclus_01
dialogueLines.text: I'm grateful for everything you've done.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Patroclus_01
dialogueLines.text: Patroclus offers one of several premium goods.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Patroclus_01
dialogueLines.text: I want you to have this.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Patroclus_01
dialogueLines.text: Patroclus offers one of several premium goods.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Patroclus_01
dialogueLines.text: For all your patience and your kindness.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Patroclus_01
dialogueLines.text: Patroclus offers one of several premium goods.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Patroclus_Unnamed_01
dialogueLines.text: Leave me be to my eternal paradise, damn you.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Patroclus_01
dialogueLines.text: The shade offers one of several premium goods.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Patroclus_Unnamed_01
dialogueLines.text: I shall not take up arms again, stranger, not even against you.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Patroclus_01
dialogueLines.text: The shade offers one of several premium goods.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Patroclus_Unnamed_01
dialogueLines.text: Begone from here, we've nothing to discuss.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Patroclus_01
dialogueLines.text: The shade offers one of several premium goods.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Patroclus_Unnamed_01
dialogueLines.text: Go haunt some other corner of this realm; this one is mine.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Patroclus_01
dialogueLines.text: The shade offers one of several premium goods.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Patroclus_Unnamed_01
dialogueLines.text: I thought I told you to leave me alone?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Patroclus_01
dialogueLines.text: The shade still offers one of his premium goods.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Patroclus_Unnamed_01
dialogueLines.text: Do I look like I want to talk to you?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Patroclus_01
dialogueLines.text: The shade still offers one of his premium goods.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Patroclus_Unnamed_01
dialogueLines.text: What is it that you want from me this time?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Patroclus_01
dialogueLines.text: The shade still offers one of his premium goods.
