dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Patroclus_Unnamed_01
dialogueLines.text: What would it take for you to leave me be?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Patroclus_01
dialogueLines.text: The shade still offers one of his premium goods.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Patroclus_Unnamed_01
dialogueLines.text: Yes, what is it this time?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Patroclus_01
dialogueLines.text: The shade still offers one of his premium goods.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Patroclus_Unnamed_01
dialogueLines.text: What would you have me do?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Patroclus_01
dialogueLines.text: The shade still offers one of his premium goods.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Achilles_Story_01
dialogueLines.text: I thought we might run into you, but... how strange to see you face-to-face out here, lad. To see this place myself. I still can't quite believe any of this...

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Patroclus_01
dialogueLines.text: He really can't. Though I keep telling him, it's true. Nothing's accomplished without risk, isn't that so? But, we of course have you to thank, stranger. Our troubles needn't have been your concern.

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: Nonsense, sir. You're my friend. And Achilles... I don't know where I would even be, if not for you.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Patroclus_01
dialogueLines.text: Well, no need to get emotional, is there? We myrmidons, we're not the sentimental type. Achilles is simply on break, is all. From a demanding job. You've a demanding job as well, so here! My customary gift.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Patroclus_01
dialogueLines.text: Patroclus offers one of several premium goods.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Patroclus_01
dialogueLines.text: Well if it isn't the ward of Achilles, come to visit us. I don't know how I can ever repay you, stranger. I'm quite sure that it isn't possible.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Achilles_Story_01
dialogueLines.text: I reached more or less the same conclusion, Pat.

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: How about you just keep offering me those premium goods you have no interest in when I pass through, and we can call it even, sir?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Patroclus_01
dialogueLines.text: That I had plans to do, regardless, though... if you should think of something else at any point, please do let either of us know.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Patroclus_01
dialogueLines.text: Patroclus offers one of several premium goods.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Patroclus_01
dialogueLines.text: Good, there you are, stranger. Settle something for us quickly, hm? Would Achilles not make for the perfect Champion of Elysium? For I think almost anybody would be better than the current one.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Achilles_Story_01
dialogueLines.text: I think I'd rather maintain watch over a nearly empty hall than battle all the shades around here, Pat.

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: I, too, would vastly prefer having to contend with Theseus rather than yourself, Achilles. Although, that's not to say I disagree that almost anybody would be better in the role than him.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Patroclus_01
dialogueLines.text: It seems, then, I am overruled. But I know Champion material when I see it, and I see it now.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Patroclus_01
dialogueLines.text: Patroclus offers one of several premium goods.

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: Mother, one thing I was wondering about was, how did you summon Charon to your surface residence? Especially without, you know, being dead.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Persephone_Home_01
dialogueLines.text: Oh, why... he gave me his calling card after he graciously helped get me out of here the first time. No doubt at Nyx's request, but still. Never thought I'd use it, but... I still kept it. Just in case.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Persephone_Home_01
dialogueLines.text: Zagreus, I know you're upset about what happened between Nyx and Dusa. Before you ask... I need to respect Nyx's space and authority on this one, all right?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Persephone_Home_01
dialogueLines.text: I saw that Dusa had returned. I trust Nyx's judgment, though I must admit I was pleased to see she decided to give her another chance.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Persephone_Home_01
dialogueLines.text: That Dusa sure has quite the uplifting presence around here, doesn't she, Zagreus? Sometimes I see you chatting and it warms my heart.

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: Mother, just how long has it been snowing on the surface, do you know? Because, it seems to me that Demeter... Grandmother, I should say, she... well, it's been very cold up there each time I've been.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Persephone_Home_01
dialogueLines.text: I was afraid of that... I wanted to believe the snow about my cottage, it was native to the climate there, but... seems that Mother took my absence harder than I thought. Oh, what have I done. All those poor people.

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: It's not your fault. You couldn't have known. And we can fix this, right?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Persephone_Home_01
dialogueLines.text: Tell me something, Zagreus... sometimes I fear... do you hate him? I mean your father. Regardless of his intentions... it sounds as though he made your life extraordinarily difficult. All his pent-up fear and anger... you bore the brunt of it.

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: Do I hate him...? Mother, I don't know that I hate anybody, really. The tales of him and the Olympians rending their immortal Titan parents to bits... I can't relate to how they must have felt. But, I resented him, for sure. And... I don't know if I'll ever get over that, entirely.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Persephone_Home_01
dialogueLines.text: I understand. I think that when Hades told you he asked no forgiveness, he understood as well, and meant it. Well, for all his failings, I'm thankful that he did not teach you how to hate. For your own sake, if not for his.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Persephone_Home_01
dialogueLines.text: So, how has it been between you and your father lately, Zagreus? I know the two of you have lots of work ahead of you. Especially him.

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: Well, it's been all right, I guess. I think he's set in some of his ways. But at least we have some sort of understanding now. Even if he acts like everything is still the same when I'm out there on the job.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Persephone_Home_01
dialogueLines.text: He says it's so you put forth your fullest effort every time. All this is a big adjustment for him as well. Not that it's any excuse for how he's treated you. Are you... all right, seeing us together?

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: Erm, so are things with you and Father... everything all right, so far?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Persephone_Home_01
dialogueLines.text: Things are good, Zagreus. I'm from Olympus... all the relationships I've ever known have been the complicated sort. But with your father... despite our different dispositions and interests... beneath it all, we share a simple understanding.

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: And... what understanding is that? If you can share.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Persephone_Home_01
dialogueLines.text: That we're all we've got. And that we love each other. Through thick and thin.

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: Wait, you're not... you're not thinking about having more children, are you...?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Persephone_Home_01
dialogueLines.text: It seems Achilles had a complicated arrangement with your father. I understand you simplified things for the better, there, and he was able to visit Elysium at last. Well done.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Persephone_Home_01
dialogueLines.text: Nyx has a very steady presence, doesn't she? I want to say she hasn't changed a bit since before you were born, but... I know that she's been through a lot.

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: She's always managed, and quite gracefully at that. Never seen her break a sweat over anything. Even when I confronted her about you. Though, you two seem closer than I realized, I think.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Persephone_Home_01
dialogueLines.text: Well, your father isn't always the conversationalist, and Nyx... she was the first chthonic god I ever met. I suppose she welcomed having another lady about the House.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Persephone_Home_01
dialogueLines.text: Zagreus... how am I doing? As a mother. Tell me honestly. Nyx has had so much to share about raising you. Foods you hated or enjoyed, your natural abilities, your friendships... she knows everything! Sometimes I wonder how I can ever compete?

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: Mother, I think you're doing great. And I'm sure you know it's not a competition between you and Nyx. I just get the benefit of having two great mothers rather than one. Or zero.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Persephone_Home_01
dialogueLines.text: Thank you, my son. I know I wasn't there for most of the hard part, but... I'm here, now. Though, when I inevitably, inadvertently trample all over your feelings at some point, please tell me, all right?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Persephone_Home_01
dialogueLines.text: Nyx told me that you helped her reconnect with her parent... after a long, long time. That was very kind of you. She never asks for anything. But we all owe her a lot, don't we?

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: I think so. If not for her... I can't even imagine, really. Did Nyx ever speak to you of Chaos, before I was born?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Persephone_Home_01
dialogueLines.text: Not really, no. Nyx is very private when it comes to personal matters. But she has feelings and desires same as anybody. Well, mostly, but the point is... I know this meant a lot to her.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Persephone_Home_01
dialogueLines.text: All right. We've matters with Olympus to resolve. They don't know I'm here, most of them, besides Zeus, maybe Athena. They think you're Nyx's son rather than mine, and that your goal is still to join them on their mountaintop.

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: What a mess. What can we do? The gods of Olympus have all been very good to me, for the most part. I don't feel great about deceiving them. They keep trying to help me escape!

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Persephone_Home_01
dialogueLines.text: What can we do? We can make peace with them. And let them know the truth. So much of it as they would tolerate. Diplomacy is not your father's strong suit. But I am going to need you two to do exactly as I say. Do you understand?

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: Yes, Mother. So what do you intend to do, and how do I fit in?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Persephone_Home_01
dialogueLines.text: We're going to invite all of them to a little family gathering! Here. And you shall personally deliver them their invitations, Zagreus.

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: Mother, are you serious? So, we're just going to have a bash with them, and they'll just see you after all this time, and be fine with everything?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Persephone_Home_01
dialogueLines.text: They certainly shall not be fine with everything. But, Hades and I, we'll be prepared to tell them what they need to hear. Don't worry, I shall do the talking.

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: What are you going to say?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Persephone_Home_01
dialogueLines.text: Oh, you know! That we met, fell in love, eloped. Had a child. A child who, once grown, sought desperately to reconnect with his extended family. To the chagrin of his grim lord father...

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: That's... that's all true. As individual statements, at least.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Persephone_Home_01
dialogueLines.text: ...Fortunately, our loving and dutiful son at last persuaded his mother and father to reveal their secretive relationship, and make an effort to rejoin their great family.

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: Your mother won't be happy that you left.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Persephone_Home_01
dialogueLines.text: I'll deal with her. Perhaps I'll have to visit her and the rest of them from time to time, if that's what it shall take. But I'll be back. I've no choice! Did you know that if you eat the seeds of an Underworld pomegranate, then you cannot leave for one month out of the year, per seed?

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: What? No, I never knew that.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Persephone_Home_01
dialogueLines.text: Neither do they.

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: How soon is this family gathering you have in mind going to take place?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Persephone_Home_01
dialogueLines.text: Soon after you invite them all, I hope. Now, go. And trust in me, all right? We have a lot to learn of trust, I think.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Persephone_Home_01
dialogueLines.text: How goes the doling out of invitations, Zagreus? Have the Olympians been amenable to the idea?

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: Not just amenable, Mother. Positively intrigued. I can't believe you scrambled the invitations in such a way that, only with the lot of them combined will they get all of the details!

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Persephone_Home_01
dialogueLines.text: A bit of collaboration seemed in the spirit of things. We're a great, big family, Zagreus. Past time we started acting like one, no? Now, keep it up. And thank you for your help.

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: Mother, this family gathering you're planning... are all our relatives welcome? Because I was wondering if I could extend the invitation to a distant ancestor... you know... Chaos.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Persephone_Home_01
dialogueLines.text: Nyx's parent... you've been in contact, then? Look, if you feel it's the right thing to do, I trust your judgment. The spirit of this event is to bring the family together.

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: Thanks. I doubt Chaos would be able to attend, though would still appreciate the thought.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Persephone_Home_01
dialogueLines.text: So Orpheus was finally able to reunite with Eurydice because of you, my son? Ah, I'm so glad! Your father's arrangement with him was quite severe. Though he did ask for a lot, for a mortal soul to return to the land of the living...

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: Is it even possible, for Father to return a mortal back to life if he so chose? Just by letting their shade escape, or something?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Persephone_Home_01
dialogueLines.text: Oh, I've no idea, probably not? But we were so moved by Orpheus' plea and song, that, well... I think your father took pity on him.

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: Wait, we were so moved? You were there when Orpheus came to try and rescue Eurydice?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Persephone_Home_01
dialogueLines.text: I was. Your father knew I loved their music. Imagine my surprise to see him as our court musician, here!

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Persephone_Home_01
dialogueLines.text: I always fancied Thanatos, you know. So put together and everything! I'm pleased to see you've grown so close. Things good between you two?

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: Mother, you'll forgive my asking, but... your mother used to call you by a different name, didn't she?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Persephone_Home_01
dialogueLines.text: Ah, don't remind me. Imagine if I called you Zaggie-poo from when you were born to this very moment, that's how that name felt to me. I much preferred the name my father gave to me.

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: Your mortal father, right...? So he called you Persephone. What was he like?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Persephone_Home_01
dialogueLines.text: He was kind to me. And funny! Little bringer of destruction, I was to him. I liked that. He was right, I was a terror. But then he passed. I was very little still. My mother never spoke of him again.

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: Demeter mentioned him a bit to me. She seems... I don't know, but I don't sense that she regrets the relationship or anything. Just sad about the outcome I think.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Persephone_Home_01
dialogueLines.text: I suppose I can see that. I thought I might end up like Father... growing old, wasting away. Then the time flew by, and I realized I was closer to Mother's side. Mortals certainly don't have it easy. But their limited days, it means... they live their lives to the fullest, I think.

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: This is embarrassing, but... I don't know the way to get back in. Except, you know, the customary way.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Persephone_01
dialogueLines.text: That's quite all right; I do. In the name of Persephone, the Queen!! Open the gates!

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Persephone_Home_01
dialogueLines.text: I'm sorry, Zagreus, I've pressing matters to attend to now.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Persephone_Home_01
dialogueLines.text: Do let me know if I can help with anything, all right?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Persephone_Home_01
dialogueLines.text: Your father's prouder of you than you think.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Persephone_Home_01
dialogueLines.text: Go make us proud, my son.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Persephone_Home_01
dialogueLines.text: Keeping the realm secure as ever, right?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Persephone_Home_01
dialogueLines.text: It's always nice to have a chat with you.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Persephone_Home_01
dialogueLines.text: I think the House is well enough in order as of late.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Persephone_Home_01
dialogueLines.text: I hope your last assignment turned out well.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Persephone_Home_01
dialogueLines.text: Do give yourself a break from time to time!

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Persephone_Home_01
dialogueLines.text: I ever tell you you're the best son that a mother's ever had?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Persephone_Home_01
dialogueLines.text: Hope my little cottage on the coast is holding up!

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Persephone_Home_01
dialogueLines.text: Please keep an eye on my garden on the surface when you get a chance.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Persephone_Home_01
dialogueLines.text: I'd say this garden's doing quite all right for now.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Persephone_Home_01
dialogueLines.text: Don't let your father get to you too much, all right?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Persephone_Home_01
dialogueLines.text: You're looking just as well as ever, Zagreus.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Persephone_Home_01
dialogueLines.text: You show your father who's in charge next chance you get, all right?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Persephone_Home_01
dialogueLines.text: The pomegranate trees are coming in nicely still, the tough old things.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Persephone_Home_01
dialogueLines.text: You'll let me know if there's something that I can do for you?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Persephone_Home_01
dialogueLines.text: Please excuse your father and me for the moment, Zagreus.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Persephone_Home_01
dialogueLines.text: Go on, Hades. Try saying something nice to him, for once.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Persephone_Home_01
dialogueLines.text: Your father and I still have a bit of work here to wrap up.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Persephone_Home_01
dialogueLines.text: The work certainly never ceases, Zagreus!

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Persephone_Home_01
dialogueLines.text: More than enough parchmentwork to go around!

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Persephone_Home_01
dialogueLines.text: Plenty of parchmentwork to contend with, I can assure you, Zagreus!

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Persephone_Home_01
dialogueLines.text: Don't go too hard on each other out there, all right?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Persephone_Home_01
dialogueLines.text: I've heard that you've been doing great out there lately, Zagreus.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Persephone_Home_01
dialogueLines.text: Oh we're busy just formulating more of our dark schemes together, Zagreus.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Persephone_Home_01
dialogueLines.text: Nyx here is a wellspring of parental advice, as it turns out!

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Persephone_Home_01
dialogueLines.text: Nyx was just telling me some more about your early days, my son.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Persephone_Home_01
dialogueLines.text: It seems I failed once again to persuade Nyx to take some personal time!

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Persephone_Unnamed_01
dialogueLines.text: Pray, who might you be, wandering all the way out here? You're trespassing on private property, you know. Speak up!

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: Erm, you must be... Persephone. My name is Zagreus, and... I think I might be your son.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Persephone_Unnamed_01
dialogueLines.text: What?! How dare...! Get out of here! Get out, or I shall make you!! Out with you!

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: No!! So then you are her. I, I don't care if you hate me, but I'm not leaving until I get some answers! I never even knew you existed. Why did you leave me?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Persephone_01
dialogueLines.text: You died! You, you died...! Those burning feet, they... sputtered out, when you were born. But... now you're standing here before me. How...?

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: I died? I died... what, you mean permanently died, but, I'm alive! Oh, gods... you never even knew I existed, either.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Persephone_01
dialogueLines.text: Alive! Zagreus, he... even kept your name! You live, how can this be, you live? You live...! Oh, the cruel Fates! My son!

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: ...In short, I beat him, finally, though he did everything he could to prevent our meeting here like this. But there's something he wanted me to tell you, in the end. He said to tell you Cerberus is doing very well.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Persephone_01
dialogueLines.text: Ah. He told you that, did he. That's good. That's good! Though, tell me something, Zagreus. How... is it that you found me? To have journeyed all this way, I scarce believe it.... Zagreus?

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: Ugh... Oh, it's... guess I'm just a little tired, that's all. Not accustomed to the weather, as I think you call it.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Persephone_01
dialogueLines.text: No... no, that isn't it, it's that... you cannot stay here. You can't stay here.

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: Really, you're... heh... kicking me out? Why?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Persephone_01
dialogueLines.text: The Fates are cruel, Zagreus. You're bound to that place... same as your father. So they would have us say good-bye, for now. My heart soars, knowing you live. Then it breaks, that our time together was so brief.

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: No... I can... come back... I can come back, now that I found you, he... maybe he'll just let me come back.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Persephone_01
dialogueLines.text: You tell him I demand it. And I demand he also tell me how come he, or for that matter Nyx, never sent notice that you lived! Olympus is all rife with schemers, but I was so naive to think that they were different.

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: I'll tell him... I'll tell him, I... feel awful. I... I have to go.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Persephone_01
dialogueLines.text: The Styx shall take you, then. Oh, Zagreus. Farewell, my son. Won't you come back to me? When you are able, please. Come back. I shall be waiting here. However long it takes.

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: Erm, Mother? Was it always a custom in Father's House to secretly give Nectar to those you care about? I guess it also could have been a thing on Olympus. Look, I wanted you to have this. I'm so glad I found you.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Persephone_Home_01
dialogueLines.text: Oh, Zagreus, you're so thoughtful, truly! Your father never permitted Nectar out in the open so I'd practically forgotten its savor. Though, just a moment, I have something for you, too!

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Persephone_Home_01
dialogueLines.text: For me? Are you quite sure? You know, this vintage must have made it all the way up from the surface, to the mountains, then to here. We've both had distant journeys, haven't we?

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: Well, I, for one, am certain this vintage here has journeyed to the right place. Please enjoy it.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Persephone_Home_01
dialogueLines.text: I must confess I do enjoy having a good bottle of Nectar, every now and then. And you appear to have a careful eye for such bottles, from what I can tell!

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: My careful eye has got to be the green one, hasn't it. To having a discerning eye, Mother!

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Persephone_Home_01
dialogueLines.text: Thank you very much, my son! You know, gift-giving customs of the Underworld are just so much more straightforward than the complicated social graces of Olympus!

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: If there are other ways of showing one's appreciation than with Nectar and the like, well, they must pale in comparison I'm sure!

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Persephone_Home_01
dialogueLines.text: Oh, this is wonderful, except... I'm wracked with guilt for having nothing much to give you, in return. You never even got a mother's love from me.

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: Please don't say that, it's just a gift, all right? Besides... can't we start now? Mother and son type things. If you would like.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Persephone_Home_01
dialogueLines.text: I'd like that even more than this fine Nectar, Zagreus!

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Persephone_Home_01
dialogueLines.text: Somebody raised you very well indeed, my son. Nyx, I suppose. I'll never be half the mother she is. She's done it for aeons! While I'm only getting started.

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: For what it's worth, I happen to think you're off to an excellent start. Nyx did teach me a lot. Achilles, too, and Father, in a way. But there is no replacing your presence. I felt that before we ever met. And now I know for sure.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Persephone_Home_01
dialogueLines.text: Thank you, Zagreus. Let's hope you're right. I nurtured very many plants, at least, of that I can assure you! Perhaps my gardening experience provided a bit of a head start on this whole mothering thing.

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: Mother, I know there may not be many things you miss from living on Olympus, though I wondered if perhaps Ambrosia may be one of them. And got you some, because... well, here.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Persephone_Home_01
dialogueLines.text: ...Why, this really is Ambrosia! How did you even acquire this, Zagreus? It brings back memories, for certain. And, never the bad ones, which... I think it must be why we prize this stuff so much. This is for me?

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: It is, it is, of course! And, it's my dearest hope that, at some point long from now, when you're relaxing with a bottle of Ambrosia, dare I say perhaps with Father... that these memories will be among the ones that spring to mind.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Persephone_Home_01
dialogueLines.text: Oh, my son... I shall certainly not need Ambrosia to think back upon getting to know you with the greatest fondness. Thank you very much.

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: Here, Mother. More bottled pleasant memories for you. I know you're glad you've long since left Olympus, but... I'm glad you were there, because the experience of it must have played no small part in making you who you are.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Persephone_Home_01
dialogueLines.text: You know, the last bottle you gave me brought more pleasant memories than I expected. No telling just how many more of them I've tucked away. Say, would it be all right if I shared this with your father?

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: It is yours to do with as you please.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Persephone_Home_01
dialogueLines.text: Haha, almost sounded like a no to me!

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: No! I mean, yes, please share it with Father, if he would be willing... I imagine what few pleasant memories he has are all of you.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Persephone_Home_01
dialogueLines.text: Nonsense, there's also Cerberus! Present company excluded. Come on, Son. Your father has a lot of work to do, but I'm absolutely certain that he has fond memories of you. Though, I know that isn't reassuring, coming from me.

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: Everything is somehow reassuring, coming from you, Mother.

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: I thought about it more, Mother, and I would like very much if you and Father were to share this. Though, maybe don't let on where you got it, just in case?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Persephone_Home_01
dialogueLines.text: This is splendid, Zagreus, but please! If there's one thing I've learned since we met, it's that the trust we share is at the very foundation of our relationships. Why shouldn't he know who this is from? Besides, I think he could figure it out.

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: Well, when it came to the Olympians, you said yourself that there was only so much truth that they could tolerate. Are you sure Father would be tolerant knowing I got this for the both of you?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Persephone_Home_01
dialogueLines.text: I'm sure enough! And if he isn't, I suppose it's more for me.

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: Haha, well! Just as long as someone puts it to good use.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Persephone_Home_01
dialogueLines.text: ...You know I always wanted to be a mother, Zagreus? But after you were born... I was certain I had lost my chance. Then one fine morning you just walked into my life. And here we are. I still cannot entirely believe it. What I mean is... I love you, Son. I'm doing my best.

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: I know. I love you, too, Mother.

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: How has it been for you so far, Mother? Reacclimating to all this. You said before it was a struggle. Must be very strange, being back. Being in darkness all the time.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Persephone_Home_01
dialogueLines.text: You know, I think it's gone all right so far! Thank you for asking, Son. Everyone has been quite welcoming. The darkness is a pleasant change of pace, and while this dress can be a little difficult to move in... I quite like it.

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: That's good to hear. I have to admit I was a little worried you'd be desperate to return to your cottage by now. This place can be a little much.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Persephone_Home_01
dialogueLines.text: I'm sure it can. And I do miss my little cottage here and there, but... I think I'm through running away. There may be times when it is necessary, of course. But no matter how far you run, it doesn't make your problems go away. You taught me that, you know.

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: I did? But... I tried desperately to run away, myself.... Oh.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Persephone_Home_01
dialogueLines.text: Turns out there's still an awful lot to do around here, isn't there, Zagreus? I'm still getting caught up on day-to-day affairs.

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: Day-to-day, how can you even tell it's day around here, anyway, Mother?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Persephone_Home_01
dialogueLines.text: Well that was just a guess, now that you mention it. In any case, forgive me if I don't get swooped up in every household detail, all right?

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: So, Mother, all the pomegranates around here... those are your doing?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Persephone_Home_01
dialogueLines.text: Not a lot of fruit trees take to this environment, my son. Though, there's something about pomegranates! Maybe it's that their seeds so resemble the gemstones your father likes so much. Or that they don't need much water.

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: ...Fruit trees, huh. I grew up around all these poms and never thought much of it. Are there many other types of fruit trees on the surface, then?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Persephone_Home_01
dialogueLines.text: Well... I suppose there are a few. But I like pomegranates most of all.

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: This garden isn't quite the sight yours was back on the surface, is it, Mother.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Persephone_Home_01
dialogueLines.text: On the contrary, Zagreus! It has its own unique charm. As each garden does. I was impressed it wasn't left entirely to rot after my absence.

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: It was totally off limits to me. Though I guess Father must have taken care to maintain it as best he could.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Persephone_Home_01
dialogueLines.text: He did, all right. Minus the part where you weren't allowed. Though, let's look after it from here, shall we?

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: You know, that painting in Father's... I mean your bedchambers, it turned out rather well, didn't it? Some very talented shades around here.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Persephone_Home_01
dialogueLines.text: Oh, it's beautiful! I tried to persuade your father to hang it out in the hall but he's too coy about such things. Though, I like it where it is... a reminder to us, every day and night.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Persephone_Home_01
dialogueLines.text: This garden may not have all the varieties available up on the surface... though it's coming along rather nicely, I should say!

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Persephone_Home_01
dialogueLines.text: It's lovely what you've done to this place, Zagreus! It's looking better than ever, thank you.

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: I have to say, Mother, that was a clever plan. Convincing Father to finally let somebody have some fun around here.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Persephone_Home_01
dialogueLines.text: Haha, well! I'm glad it worked out for the best. There's just one thing that happened that I ought to tell you about, Son. No secrets between us, and all. This... is quite difficult for me to say.

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: It's about Demeter, isn't it. You two were talking half the night. She wants you to come home with her, doesn't she? You'll always be her little one, something like that?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Persephone_Home_01
dialogueLines.text: Indeed... something like that. You're quite perceptive, have I ever told you, Zagreus? But yes, my mother, she was quite conflicted. Didn't know whether to smite me or to hold me, maybe both. And, yes. She asked that I come home... to my responsibilities.

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: So... you're leaving...? No, Mother, you can't be leaving, you just got here!

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Persephone_Home_01
dialogueLines.text: Oh, it's not as bad as that, it's just... if we are to keep the peace down here, then, I must spend some time up there. Every so often! But, I shall always return, in no time at all. I promised Hades, and I promise you.

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: Well then... when are you going to go?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Persephone_Home_01
dialogueLines.text: Soon, Zagreus. Perhaps by the time you're back.

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: I don't suppose... I can come with you, can I?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Persephone_Home_01
dialogueLines.text: Tsk, you know the surface isn't good for you! Besides, you're needed here. But, if you really want to see it for yourself, someday, who knows? Perhaps eventually we'll figure out a way.

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: I'm going to miss you, Mother.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Persephone_Home_01
dialogueLines.text: We have to say farewell again, for now, my son. Olympus calls, but I'll be back, all right?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Persephone_Home_01
dialogueLines.text: I shall be leaving again for Olympus soon. I'll miss you, Son.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Persephone_Home_01
dialogueLines.text: I must go visit Mother on Olympus, Zagreus. I'll tell her you said hi.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Persephone_Home_01
dialogueLines.text: I have to go again, soon, Zagreus, all right? I'll see you soon.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Persephone_Home_01
dialogueLines.text: I'm due to visit Mount Olympus once again, so please be good to one another while I'm out.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Persephone_Home_01
dialogueLines.text: Time that I headed up to Mount Olympus, though you keep an eye on things till I get back?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Persephone_Home_01
dialogueLines.text: I have to go now, Zagreus, but I shall see you just as soon as I return.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Persephone_01
dialogueLines.text: I begged the Fates that you'd return to me. Perhaps I ought to be more careful how I speak of them. Tell me, how was your journey? What happened?

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: Father didn't make it any easier for me. Nor would he tell me why he never sent for you, after you left. So, I don't know, what's going on, still, but... I wanted to see you, again.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Persephone_01
dialogueLines.text: I wanted to see you, too. Come, speak quickly with me, that our time together here may be as full as possible, all right?

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: I just... I need to know what happened, between you and Father. Why are you here, and not with us?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Persephone_01
dialogueLines.text: I couldn't live with myself anymore, down there. After I left Olympus, a long story in itself, I came to be with your father, in the Underworld. It was a shock, to say the least. The others in your father's house were welcoming enough, but... I never felt that I belonged.

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: I know the feeling, I think. But you stayed. Long enough to have me. Those whom I've spoken to about you, it's as you said, they have fond memories of you. And then, you even had a child. Did Father... did he mistreat you?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Persephone_01
dialogueLines.text: No, no. Your father could be very difficult. Though, he was gentle, with me. Soon enough, we got on fine, better than fine, at times, but... when I was with child... why, I struggled terribly. Because... a child born of surface-dwellers could not live, down there.

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: Could not live down there, according to whom, exactly? The Fates?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Persephone_01
dialogueLines.text: According to them, yes. According to your father, and to Nyx. Even having heard... I took my chances, anyway. But when you emerged stillborn... it was too much. I fled. I fled, and came to reside here. Above the notice of the Underworld. Beneath the notice of Olympus.

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: Urgh... but... why would Father lie to you? If he never wanted you... or never wanted me... surely he had the means.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Persephone_01
dialogueLines.text: I don't know. I'm wondering the same. But, oh, look at you, Zagreus! We're running out of time. It's happening again! Stay with me, please?

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: ...I'm trying... but I can't... stay that much longer... I don't think.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Persephone_01
dialogueLines.text: Oh, my dear son! Then please... ask Hades what you asked of me! Why he would lie? Tell him I must know, the same as you. And you farewell. Until we meet again!

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: Mother, I... discovered how come Father led you to believe that I could not survive past birth. He didn't lie to you, exactly. So he says.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Persephone_01
dialogueLines.text: Wait, slow down, Zagreus, what do you mean? He told me you would not survive your birth, yet here you are. How wasn't that a lie?

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: Because... he thought it was the truth. The Fates appointed him dominion over the entire Underworld. But he would never have an heir, so he was led to understand. He never expected me to be born, much less survive.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Persephone_01
dialogueLines.text: He never expected you to survive? Then, forgive my asking, but... why did you? How did you? The Fates are not known for making mistakes when it comes to such things.

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: Nyx saved my life. Or gave me life, I guess. Then raised me in your absence. The Fates are her daughters, right? Maybe she persuaded them to go easier on me. She cannot say more. In any case, apparently it took her quite a while to revive me, and... by then, you were gone.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Persephone_01
dialogueLines.text: Nyx... of course she was involved. I only managed to escape because of her. Then, she restored my stillborn son to life, and nurtured him? And, I never would have known, unless that child, fully grown and having learned the truth, defied his father, and then found me here?

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: Yes. Although, we still don't know how come Father and Nyx never sent for you. Though, ungh, let me ask you something, Mother, if you please. If you had found out... that I was born, and very much alive... would you have come back?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Persephone_01
dialogueLines.text: Would I have come back... to see my only child? Had I but known you lived... I would have gladly gone through hell again to see you, Zagreus. Of course I would have.

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: Well then... what if... urgh... what if Father... didn't want you... doing that?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Persephone_01
dialogueLines.text: What if he didn't want me coming back to him? Or seeing you? Oh no, you're fading again, Zagreus. You've pushed yourself too hard! But what is it you meant, by what you said?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Persephone_01
dialogueLines.text: So good to see you, again, Zagreus. Since last you left, I couldn't help but think the worst, about what happened after I had left... or soon before. You mentioned Nyx... you don't suppose that she and Hades... wanted me out of the way?

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: I just... don't think that can be. Though, let me ask you. How long did you know Father? And Nyx, for that matter? Did they seem trustworthy?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Persephone_01
dialogueLines.text: Yes. I knew them long enough to have decided yes, that they were trustworthy. As much as anyone I've ever met. But I also know never to be trusting to a fault. I have to consider the possibility I was deceived.

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: Especially since neither of them is being forthcoming with me even now, right? And yet, I have this feeling something else is going on, Mother. The way that Father speaks of you, the way Nyx speaks of you... I think... they care for you.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Persephone_01
dialogueLines.text: Ah, hahaha! Well, they have a funny way of showing it, I think. Your father, so... you have to get through him each time you visit me?

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: I do. Mother, what if... what if Father thinks that he's protecting you? Have you... considered that?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Persephone_01
dialogueLines.text: Protecting me? Why Zagreus, what do you mean by that? As you can see, I can fend for myself.

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: Well... urgh... you're from Olympus, aren't you? But... the gods, none of them seem to know... you're here... so...

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Persephone_01
dialogueLines.text: What, how would you know something like that? Zagreus? Ah, curse the Fates, you're leaving me again...

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Persephone_01
dialogueLines.text: Son, I have to ask you, how much do you know about your relatives upon Olympus?

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: I think I know quite a bit by now. I've been in steady contact with Athena, Zeus, and other members of the family ever since I first left Father's House... they've been assisting me in getting here.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Persephone_01
dialogueLines.text: No... Zagreus, what have you done? You've led them here?

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: No, no, they think... they think I'm trying to escape from Father so that I can join them on their mountaintop. At least I think that's what they think. Most of them, anyway.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Persephone_01
dialogueLines.text: But then... does Hades know of this? That Olympus is involved?

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: He does. Both he and Nyx. In fact, it's Nyx who put me and Olympus into contact. I could never have broken free of the Underworld if not for their help.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Persephone_01
dialogueLines.text: So Nyx and Hades are against each other, then. And Olympus is helping you... but they don't know I'm here, as far as you're aware?

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: That's right. More or less. Nyx and Father seem to put up with each other. Don't seem to have much of a choice. Though, ever since I learned of you, Nyx has been helping me to find you... while Father's tried to stop me.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Persephone_01
dialogueLines.text: That fool... what was he thinking? What is he thinking...? Do you suppose... he thinks that he's protecting me from them? Oh, no...

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: I... urgh... I think maybe he does think so, indeed... because, now having met my relatives upon Olympus, I... can definitely see that they can be a bit... vindictive about things.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Persephone_01
dialogueLines.text: Vindictiveness runs in the family, my son. Then, if Olympus is involved, that would explain a lot. I'm here, not there with them, as you can see. But, oh, it seems we'll have to speak some more, when next you return...

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Persephone_01
dialogueLines.text: Son, I have to ask you, how much do you know about your relatives upon Olympus?

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: A little. I've been in contact with Athena, Zeus, and other members of the family ever since I first left Father's House... they've been assisting me in getting here.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Persephone_01
dialogueLines.text: No... Zagreus, what have you done? You've led them here?

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: No, no, they think... they think I'm trying to escape from Father so that I can join them on their mountaintop. At least I think that's what they think. Most of them, anyway.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Persephone_01
dialogueLines.text: But then... does Hades know of this? That Olympus is involved?

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: He does. Both he and Nyx. In fact, it's Nyx who put me and Olympus into contact. I could never have broken free of the Underworld if not for their help.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Persephone_01
dialogueLines.text: So Nyx and Hades are against each other, then. And Olympus is helping you... but they don't know I'm here, as far as you're aware?

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: That's right. More or less. Nyx and Father seem to put up with each other. Don't seem to have much of a choice. Though, ever since I learned of you, Nyx has been helping me to find you... while Father's tried to stop me.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Persephone_01
dialogueLines.text: That fool... what was he thinking? What is he thinking...? Do you suppose... he thinks that he's protecting me from them? Oh, no...

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: I... urgh... I think maybe he does think so, indeed... because, now having met my relatives upon Olympus, I... can definitely see that they can be a bit... vindictive about things.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Persephone_01
dialogueLines.text: Vindictiveness runs in the family, my son. Then, if Olympus is involved, that would explain a lot. I'm here, not there with them, as you can see. But, oh, it seems we'll have to speak some more, when next you return...

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Persephone_01
dialogueLines.text: I could not stand it on Olympus, Zagreus. The blustering, the posturing... my overbearing mother... none of it. I would have left the mountain voluntarily, except...

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: ...Except Father absconded with you, didn't he? Dragged you down with him into the Underworld. So if Olympus were to find that out... I shudder to think of the consequences.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Persephone_01
dialogueLines.text: That fool is trying to protect me even now. It wasn't he who absconded with me, my son. And it's more complicated even than that. I was content to leave. Willing to make my life within the Underworld. With your father.

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: I dare not ask who did bring you to Father, then. So, essentially, you ran away from home, to live with him? I mean no disrespect, but... did you realize what you were getting yourself into?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Persephone_01
dialogueLines.text: We have a tendency to be impetuous when we are younger, Zagreus. I'd heard all about your father before we met. Admired paintings of him. It was very awkward, at first. But he had a bluntness I fancied. A sense of principle. I much preferred him to his brothers, I can tell you that.

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: So, urgh... then you secretly left Mount Olympus to be with the god of the dead in the Underworld. And Olympus never found out. What do you suppose would happen... if they did?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Persephone_01
dialogueLines.text: ...I think... there would be war between Olympus and your father, Zagreus, if they found out. Especially now. After all this. And after all this time. They slew their parents once. If the Titans could be wiped from this world, so can we all.

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: So it makes sense... if Father, he agrees with you, and thinks... that leaving you in hiding here, that it's the only way... he can prevent that war...

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Persephone_01
dialogueLines.text: I can see how your father, shrewd but also single-minded as he was, might have followed such a line of reasoning as that. Oh, Zagreus... what have I done?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Persephone_01
dialogueLines.text: Zagreus... it's wonderful to see you, but... I need to ask something of you. That you not visit me again, here in this place. Both for your safety, and for mine.

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: Wait, what? Because of what would happen if the Olympians found out? So, after everything, you're taking Father's side in all of this?!

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Persephone_01
dialogueLines.text: I am. Your father is no fool when it comes to matters such as this. Besides... all that has happened... it's my fault. I was selfish! And now both sides of our family threaten to be torn apart.

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: Your fault? So let's say you never left Olympus! Then it'd be just like you thought, until quite recently, I think. That I'd never existed! Besides, they haven't found out any of this. What makes you so sure they will, all the sudden?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Persephone_01
dialogueLines.text: Nyx may be veiling you from them as she veiled me when I fled here myself. But even her own powers aren't infallible. Each time you visit me, you are incurring risk. One small slip, and they shall find me. Discover your deception. Go home, Zagreus. Please.

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: Go home with me, Mother! You came here under false pretenses. I haven't fought all this way, over and over again, only to turn back now. I need you in my life! How can you just... turn me away like this?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Persephone_01
dialogueLines.text: Oh, Zagreus... I cannot return. After all that happened between Hades and me, and everyone, I just cannot fathom the idea. Besides... the risks we're taking would be even greater if Olympus were to find me there, with him. And you.

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: Then... ungh... you're just... going to stay here? Alone, in the middle of nowhere, in this little garden... forever? After we part ways, just like that?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Persephone_01
dialogueLines.text: My son... I know you've made hard choices and sacrifices to be here with me. And I'm forever grateful to have finally met you. But this is my home, now, yes. While your home is below. Please, if not for your sake, then for mine... do not return. I love you, Zagreus. Good-bye.

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: You can't do this to me, Mother. You said Father is no fool, and neither are you. There has to be a way to fix this. Not just with you and Father, but with everyone. There has to be a way!

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Persephone_01
dialogueLines.text: ...You have your father's stubbornness. Listen to me. You're endangering everything you care about by coming here. Don't you understand? Besides...

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: What? Besides what? Tell me, please.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Persephone_01
dialogueLines.text: ...The truth is that I like it here, Zagreus. I have done well for myself on my own. And your father, he has his domain to look after. The feelings he once had for me... feelings we shared... they fade, with time. You'll understand, someday.

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: No! I won't be understanding anything of the sort. Father still loves you. I'm sure of it! And, he owes you an apology, damn it! I cannot make amends with you on his behalf. It's something only you can do together.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Persephone_01
dialogueLines.text: What do you mean he still loves me, Zagreus...? How would you know?

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: Because... ungh... I'm his blasted son, that's how I know! But trust me isn't ever good enough, I know that, too. I'll prove it to you, Mother! Just you wait and see.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Persephone_01
dialogueLines.text: You're fading, Son... you don't need to prove anything to me. Please don't do anything rash. All of us made our choices. Now we have to live with them. Isn't it enough, to know that I am safe? That I'm content to be alone?

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: No... urgh... it's not... it's not enough, for me, because... this blasted family... it cannot just keep hiding from its problems! Running from them! We're stuck with one another, we... we have to do everything we can... for one another!

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Persephone_01
dialogueLines.text: I... oh, my dear boy...

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Persephone_01
dialogueLines.text: ...Of everything down in that gloomy House, I miss old Cerberus the most, I think. But, I miss other things as well. The lack of light was gentler on my skin. Not being as cold. And Nyx, and her young sons. It was all very odd, but...

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: Mother... in Father's bedchambers... mind you, there's almost nothing there, just neatly sorted rows of all his samey capes... but on his nightstand... is a portrait of you. Wearing a regal crown of red laurels. A Queen. His Queen.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Persephone_01
dialogueLines.text: I was fitted for those garments when I first arrived. They... were not my customary style. So Hades has a portrait of me, still. Why would he put it back on display, now?

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: No, no, he's never let anybody in his bedchambers! I broke in. Don't you see? He didn't put your portrait back on display. It's always been there! Ever since you left. So, Mother, why do you suppose he would do that?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Persephone_01
dialogueLines.text: Because he... because he loves me still. Is that what you want me to say?

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: Have you ever loved him, yourself?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Persephone_01
dialogueLines.text: All I really wanted was to leave Olympus. I didn't exactly abscond. I doubt I had much of a choice, but I went willingly. Perhaps Zeus knew how I felt, and thought he was doing me a favor. As for Hades... I grew to love him, yes. He always gave me my space.

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: Won't you... urgh... won't you please talk to him? Or visit Cerberus again, or Nyx? As for Olympus... surely all of you... can figure something out...?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Persephone_01
dialogueLines.text: ...I just don't know, my son. Olympus is behind me. And the Underworld... I cannot imagine I would be very welcome there after all this time.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Persephone_Home_01
dialogueLines.text: Things are well enough upon Olympus. Though, it's a relief to have come home again.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Persephone_Home_01
dialogueLines.text: Don't think I'll ever get fully accustomed to Olympus, Son.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Persephone_Home_01
dialogueLines.text: Zagreus, you're the most welcome sight I've seen in quite some time.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Persephone_Home_01
dialogueLines.text: Always such an exhausting journey, from Olympus down to here.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Persephone_Home_01
dialogueLines.text: I trust that you and Hades have been good to one another since I left?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Persephone_Home_01
dialogueLines.text: I'll tell you, it is so much colder up there on that mountaintop.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Persephone_Home_01
dialogueLines.text: This is a welcome sight from all the snow-capped mountaintops.

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: Welcome home, Mother! How was your trip, things go all right with you and Demeter? With... Grandmother. Still getting used to thinking of her that way.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Persephone_Home_01
dialogueLines.text: You know, it's funny... I think she was so overwhelmed to see me again that maybe something finally changed in her. I always thought she was utterly unyielding. She asked me if I truly came here by choice, and I put her at ease.

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: Ah, that's a relief. Grandmother can be rather intense, but... she's been kind to me, and very helpful, too. Do you suppose she'll ease up on the whole eternal winter thing there on the surface, now that she knows you're safe?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Persephone_Home_01
dialogueLines.text: I asked. She told me that she would. Warmth shall return to the surface. Some of the time, at least. Likely on a rigid schedule, knowing her. And perhaps not in the vicinity of our domain. She's not the sort to forgive and simply forget.

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: Mother, wait, what is all this?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Persephone_01
dialogueLines.text: Oh, Zagreus, I almost missed you. Truthfully, I hoped that I could make it a surprise...

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: Make what a surprise?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Persephone_01
dialogueLines.text: My arrival. I'm going. To your home. To Cerberus. Nyx. Your father.

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: You're... really?! Mother, you, you're serious?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Persephone_01
dialogueLines.text: I am. Now if you could just help me pack up my remaining things? Only a few loose odds I'm bringing, just in case. If we hurry, maybe you could join me, rather than return the customary way. Not something a mother ever wants to see.

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: Join you, but... wait, how are you going to get back? I... hadn't thought that far ahead.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Persephone_01
dialogueLines.text: Just so happens I know somebody who can give us a ride.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Persephone_01
dialogueLines.text: Doubtless he has pressing matters to attend to. So let's not keep him waiting any longer, Son. Come on, then!

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: <Sigh> ...I'm still alive... but, how?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Persephone_01
dialogueLines.text: You're on the River Styx. No longer in the mortal realm, entirely.

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: I can't believe that this is happening.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Persephone_01
dialogueLines.text: I know. My heart is racing. Nothing like a river journey to remind oneself that there's no turning back.

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: Seriously. Though, even after all of this... it's like we only just met. I have so many questions for you still.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Persephone_01
dialogueLines.text: We'll have plenty of time for them, I think! For now, best we keep quiet. Pretend we're just a couple of dead souls on their final voyage.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Persephone_Home_01
dialogueLines.text: Were you able to make it to my cottage on the surface, Zagreus? I hope my lavender patches are doing all right.

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: I, erm... I couldn't make it all the way there, Mother. It's like... without your presence on the surface, I'm unable to hang on for very long at all.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Persephone_Home_01
dialogueLines.text: Ah, I was afraid of that. Well, don't despair. I placed a charm upon my seedlings there that ought to keep them flourishing well on their own. Your duty now is simply to escape. You keep it up!

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Persephone_Home_01
dialogueLines.text: Oh, Cerberus! Doubtless you deserve the biggest thanks that this House still stands, don't you? Did you enjoy the treat I brought you from the surface?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Persephone_Home_01
dialogueLines.text: The surface-treat-devouring hound of hell provides the Queen a most decidedly affirmative response.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Persephone_Home_01
dialogueLines.text: I thought you might. You wouldn't know it wasn't made of meat, I'll bet! I'll see if we can't grow you plenty more.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Persephone_Home_01
dialogueLines.text: You're such a good boy, aren't you, Cerberus? Yes you are! Guarded the place while I was out. And even let my son come visit me!

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Persephone_Home_01
dialogueLines.text: The ever-vigilant and terrifying Cerberus merely accepts the praise.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Persephone_Home_01
dialogueLines.text: ...Oh, come on, Hades! Mortals are much more resilient than that! What, you think they'll show up at your gates here, all at once? They have our family looking out for them.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hades_01
dialogueLines.text: That is what I'm afraid of. Do you truly believe my brothers and the rest shall take the sort of care of them while they draw breath, as we do here after they have expired?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Persephone_Home_01
dialogueLines.text: It's true we have to look out for them indefinitely, while Olympus need only look out for them for a little while. But our relatives are always watching from their mountain! And if they do turn a blind eye once in a while... mortals aren't bad at fending for themselves.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hades_01
dialogueLines.text: But they are never satisfied! Always begging us to provide more, and more.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Persephone_Home_01
dialogueLines.text: They like us, Hades! They praise us, tell one another about us. What do you expect? Would you rather they forgot about us?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hades_01
dialogueLines.text: ...No. No, I suppose not. But they need us!

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hades_01
dialogueLines.text: ...And what of the complaints from Elysium? They wanted the lawns thoroughly manicured for their athletic contests.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Persephone_Home_01
dialogueLines.text: Already taken care of. Though, the flooding in Asphodel, hm, that doesn't look like something we can clear up for quite some time. Fortunately, the residents seem to be getting accustomed to it.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hades_01
dialogueLines.text: There have been fewer reports about it as of late. And what of the Satyr problem? Blasted things are rampant up there, still.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Persephone_Home_01
dialogueLines.text: Well, they're probably contained up there, at least. And Zagreus has been keeping them in check, hasn't he?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hades_01
dialogueLines.text: ...Well, be that as it may... if they pester you at all, do let me know, and I shall have them dealt with quickly.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Persephone_Home_01
dialogueLines.text: Oh, Hades... I think the shades around here have been through enough as it is! I don't mind them. After all the time I spent on my own, having some company has been a welcome change.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hades_01
dialogueLines.text: You are certain all your needs have been provided for? Because if there is anything that you require...

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Persephone_Home_01
dialogueLines.text: Require? Look at this place, Hades. Look at me! I have everything! And so do you. Try not to worry quite so much. And be patient with your son. That's all I ask.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hades_01
dialogueLines.text: Persephone, I cannot promise you such things, they're... not my strengths. Besides, I have a reputation to uphold! God of the dead! If the wretches do not fear me, or you for that matter... what, then?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Persephone_Home_01
dialogueLines.text: I'm not going to tell you how to balance your work, your marriage, and your parental responsibilities. I don't think anybody on Olympus or below knows quite how best to manage that. So long as we do our best.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hades_01
dialogueLines.text: ...Sometimes, I... still cannot believe that you are here. Returned to us. Your radiance, in all this darkness... you must miss seeing the coast.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Persephone_Home_01
dialogueLines.text: The coast was nice enough, Hades. But nothing is ever perfect, right? No matter how hard you try. Still... I think we get quite close around here, every once in a while.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hades_01
dialogueLines.text: You have taken much less time adjusting to this place than when first you joined us. Part of my surprise. I, myself, was forced to be here. I never thought anyone would come from the surface here by choice.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Persephone_Home_01
dialogueLines.text: The heart can make us do the strangest things, can't it?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Persephone_Home_01
dialogueLines.text: ...So if there's anything that I can do for you, Nyx, you need but ask, all right? I plan to pull my weight.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Nyx_01
dialogueLines.text: Your services were always welcome, here. Please acclimate yourself at your own rate. I am available to answer any questions you may have at any time.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Persephone_Home_01
dialogueLines.text: Thank you, my friend. I have to tell you... it's good to be back.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Persephone_Home_01
dialogueLines.text: ...I just don't know how I can ever repay you. For raising him. For everything. After I left... besides old Cerberus, I think maybe I missed you most of all.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Nyx_01
dialogueLines.text: I can remember feeling similar emotions at the time. However, it is quite unnecessary to give thanks to me this way. You are the Queen, safely returned where you belong. I did what anybody would have in my place.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Persephone_Home_01
dialogueLines.text: Anybody would have lovingly raised my estranged son, while keeping safe my secret, eventually orchestrating an elaborate plot to reunite him with me, so that I might someday return, to have a Nectar with them in the lounge?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Sisyphus_01
dialogueLines.text: Word is that the most beautiful music's been heard up in Asphodel, lately, Prince! From the sound of it, why... I think that has got to be Eurydice and Orpheus!

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: You heard correctly, Sisyphus! Orpheus is reunited with his muse, at last. Or at least, he gets to visit on the regular, when he isn't busy at my father's house. So you've heard of them, have you?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Sisyphus_01
dialogueLines.text: Oh, I've more than heard of them! I'm sure you know how Orpheus once ventured all the way down here, after Eurydice passed on. I'll never forget his song! Even the Fury Sisters gave me a break, they were so captivated by it, then. Ah, good times. For me, at least!

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Sisyphus_01
dialogueLines.text: Sisyphus furtively offers a choice of benefits.

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: You ever meet Alecto, Sisyphus? The Fury Megaera's nastier sister. We recently became acquainted, she and I.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Sisyphus_01
dialogueLines.text: Ah, fortunately I've not seen too much of her, myself, Prince Z. And based on everything that I have seen and heard, I'd like to keep it that way for a while! I hope she doesn't cause you too much trouble there.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Sisyphus_01
dialogueLines.text: Sisyphus furtively offers a choice of benefits.

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: You know I was just thinking, sir... sometimes I am a little envious of you and Bouldy there. You seem to have things pretty figured out. I still grow frustrated with myself quite often and don't always know whom to turn to.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Sisyphus_01
dialogueLines.text: Oh, hah! Well, I just think you're in a tough spot lately, Prince. But you'll get through! As for old Bouldy, why, by all means do not hesitate to lay your troubles on him if you like! Dumped all of mine on him a while back.

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: So I can just... walk up and talk to him, and chat? That's all it takes?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Sisyphus_01
dialogueLines.text: That's truly all it takes to make a friend sometimes, indeed, Highness. Now, here.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Sisyphus_01
dialogueLines.text: Sisyphus furtively offers a choice of benefits.

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: I have to thank you, Sisyphus, not just for all the help you've given me time after time, but for encouraging me to have a chat with Bouldy there. I daresay I feel a little more at peace of late, and maybe it's thanks to him.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Sisyphus_01
dialogueLines.text: He's a good one, all right, isn't he? Though if you've come to such an understanding with old Bouldy there, why, it must mean you've done some growing on your own as well, I think.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Sisyphus_01
dialogueLines.text: Sisyphus furtively offers a choice of benefits.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Sisyphus_01
dialogueLines.text: Hey, Your Highness, good, old Bouldy there had several things he wanted to tell you.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Sisyphus_01
dialogueLines.text: Thank you for listening to him! With all that said, I think I've got something for you right here.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Sisyphus_01
dialogueLines.text: Sisyphus furtively offers a choice of benefits.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Sisyphus_01
dialogueLines.text: Hey Prince, ah, so... you do know Bouldy's just a plain old rock with a few lines chiseled into him, resembling a face, don't you? Chiseled into it, I mean. Just so you don't get the wrong idea...

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: Come now, sir, we both know full well Bouldy's much more than that. You really think I doubt the power of Bouldy after everything we've been through?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Sisyphus_01
dialogueLines.text: Hahahaha, no. Was just a jest, there, at Bouldy's expense. Look at him! He gets so annoyed when I call him a plain old rock like that. But then he calls me an old lazy lump, and then we're even for a while.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Sisyphus_01
dialogueLines.text: Sisyphus furtively offers a choice of benefits.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Sisyphus_01
dialogueLines.text: Say, that's my Shattered Shackle on your person, right, Prince Z.? I'd recognize that clunky thing from anywhere. It serving you all right?

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: Hey sir, certainly it's been most welcome having something from you with me at all times. Though has it always held this power? And... how did you remove it to begin with?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Sisyphus_01
dialogueLines.text: Bit of an odd story, that. Involved a well-timed accident with the old boulder there. Though, I'd best not get into it so that you're not accomplice to that little crime. Surely you understand?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Sisyphus_01
dialogueLines.text: Sisyphus furtively offers a choice of benefits.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Sisyphus_01
dialogueLines.text: My Shattered Shackle's practically like new, there, Prince! Minus the part where it's still broken, and all that. Nice work!

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: Thanks, sir. It's been of help through my repeated treks across this realm, all thanks to you. You sure you don't want it back?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Sisyphus_01
dialogueLines.text: Hahahahah! Absolutely not, though truly I appreciate the sentiment. I'd rather have a look at the old boulder there than a symbol of the time I first got stuck here, when I wasn't doing quite as well.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Sisyphus_01
dialogueLines.text: Sisyphus furtively offers a choice of benefits.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Sisyphus_01
dialogueLines.text: Prince Z., it seems you've brought my little Shady back to visit me! How very kind. And I can tell he's in good care of late.

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: He is, sir, and I'm grateful once again to have adopted him. You sure you don't want him back, though? Always seems like you could use a friend.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Sisyphus_01
dialogueLines.text: All due respect, Highness, but I've the best friend anyone can ask for to my right just there. And as for little Shady, he was more inclined than I to see the farthest reaches of the Underworld. Safe travels to you both!

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Sisyphus_01
dialogueLines.text: Sisyphus furtively offers a choice of benefits.

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: Sisyphus, sir? Have you any idea how Shady works? I'm able to just whisk both you and Bouldy out of here, and to my aid? It must be awfully disorienting.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Sisyphus_01
dialogueLines.text: Well, Prince, I told you that we'd be with you in spirit, didn't I? It's not just Shady's power, I suppose, nor is it Bouldy's or my hidden strength. It's testament to our companionship, is all. You gods are capable of anything!

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Sisyphus_01
dialogueLines.text: Sisyphus furtively offers a choice of benefits.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Sisyphus_01
dialogueLines.text: How goes it with old Master Thanatos of late, Prince Z.? Is he at least less stern with you than with the poor mortals he's sent to take in here?

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: Oh, Thanatos is doing all right. Much like my father, I think he feels he has some sort of fearsome reputation to uphold. Or maybe he's perpetually stressed over his work. But he's been well lately.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Sisyphus_01
dialogueLines.text: That's good, I guess! Always appreciated that he ultimately took me in, rather than having to show up here through much more sudden, much more painful means. Didn't stop me from trying to avoid the trip, of course.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Sisyphus_01
dialogueLines.text: Sisyphus furtively offers a choice of benefits.

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: Sisyphus, have you had run-ins with Tisiphone the Fury here before? She isn't much for conversation, I don't think.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Sisyphus_01
dialogueLines.text: Tisiphone. Why, yes, I have, in fact, Prince Z. She's visited on multiple occasions, truth be told. So she is back, is she?

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: She is. Although, I think she's back to deal with me. I'll have to keep her plenty busy just in case.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Sisyphus_01
dialogueLines.text: Appreciate it, Prince. Of all the Fury Sisters, she's the one I would least care to see. But if she does stop by, you know, that's how it goes.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Sisyphus_01
dialogueLines.text: Sisyphus furtively offers a choice of benefits.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Sisyphus_01
dialogueLines.text: Say, tell me something, Prince, if you don't mind. How come you're so intent on getting out of here? I mean, what happens when you do?

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: Sisyphus, you ever lose somebody dear to you? There's someone out there whom I need to find. That's... all that I can say right now.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Sisyphus_01
dialogueLines.text: I get it, Highness, and I didn't mean to pry, it's just... I can't remember what it's like to want something so bad, you know? Whoever's out there, I hope you find them soon.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Sisyphus_01
dialogueLines.text: Sisyphus furtively offers a choice of benefits.

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: Hey sir, I've always wondered, what were you like back when you were alive? Were you always this wise?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Sisyphus_01
dialogueLines.text: Might surprise you, Highness, I've a bit of royalty in me, myself. Not on your level, mind, but I was very much a king. Though I'll be honest, I... I wasn't much of a good man.

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
