dialogueLines.text: Huh! I thought I sensed the royalty in you! Although, I can't imagine you as anything besides the gentleman I know you for, my friend.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Sisyphus_01
dialogueLines.text: Well that is awful kind of you to say. Though, I would like to think my punishment here wasn't without warrant. At least I've had a lot of time to think it through!

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Sisyphus_01
dialogueLines.text: Sisyphus furtively offers a choice of benefits.

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: Erm, Sisyphus, you mind my asking what you did to warrant this particular arrangement you've got here? Pushing a boulder till the end of time and all. Never heard it in your own words.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Sisyphus_01
dialogueLines.text: Well, it's a bit of a long story, Prince. My time had come, though I just wasn't ready, you might say. Your father thought he had me in his clutches, but then, shoom! I gave him the slip. Your father's henchman, Thanatos, he'll back me up on that.

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: Outsmarted Thanatos, that's amazing... what happened then?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Sisyphus_01
dialogueLines.text: Turns out your father doesn't like us mortals cheating death. Eventually they found me, so, now I'm stuck here with my boulder friend. I push old Bouldy up the hill to the beat of the Furies' whips, then he tumbles down, and then we go again.

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: So then, you're stuck forever, no reprieve. That's terrible... wish there was something I could do to help.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Sisyphus_01
dialogueLines.text: You save those sympathies for someone else, Your Highness! I could be Prometheus, spending an eternity having my liver gobbled up, but instead I get to stay in shape, and have these chats with you. Now I'd better get back to it!

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Sisyphus_01
dialogueLines.text: Sisyphus furtively offers a choice of benefits.

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: Sisyphus, I've meant to ask, where are you getting all this stuff you give to me? You must be pretty well connected around here.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Sisyphus_01
dialogueLines.text: Oh, well, you know! I have acquaintances amongst the locals here, try to be neighborly with everyone is all. It's not just Bouldy and myself as you can see. Sometimes I come across things that'd be better off in other hands than mine.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Sisyphus_01
dialogueLines.text: Sisyphus furtively offers a choice of benefits.

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: I have to tell you, Sisyphus. Sometimes I wish I knew more about your past. You must have been a great king.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Sisyphus_01
dialogueLines.text: Well, ah-hah, the thing is that I wasn't, Highness. And that's not modesty from me, all right? All behind me, now. And I'd appreciate it if we left it at that.

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: I hear you, sir. Though, whatever you did in your mortal days... you've since more than paid for it, I'm sure.... Wait, is that why you're still here, in Tartarus? Because you think you deserve this?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Sisyphus_01
dialogueLines.text: It's more like... this is where I belong. Where my path has taken me, you know? Here in the Underworld, you can't entirely escape your past... and yet, it's like you get another life to live. With all the wisdom that you picked up along the way.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Sisyphus_01
dialogueLines.text: Sisyphus furtively offers a choice of benefits.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Sisyphus_01
dialogueLines.text: You know, Prince Z., you really have a look about you that you know exactly where you're going, as of late. I used to think you weren't near as comfortable in your own skin.

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: Oh, you were right. I was never comfortable performing Father's routine Tartarus inspections. Though those responsibilities of mine didn't last long.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Sisyphus_01
dialogueLines.text: Nor did we have much opportunity to chat back then, but hey! You always struck me as a decent sort. Wasn't accustomed to your father's henchpeople greeting me with a smile.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Sisyphus_01
dialogueLines.text: Sisyphus furtively offers a choice of benefits.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Sisyphus_01
dialogueLines.text: Good to see you in one piece, Prince Z.!

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Sisyphus_01
dialogueLines.text: Sisyphus furtively offers a choice of benefits.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Sisyphus_01
dialogueLines.text: Life treating you all right, I take it, Prince?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Sisyphus_01
dialogueLines.text: Sisyphus furtively offers a choice of benefits.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Sisyphus_01
dialogueLines.text: Oh I'm hanging in there, thanks for asking, Prince!

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Sisyphus_01
dialogueLines.text: Sisyphus furtively offers a choice of benefits.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Sisyphus_01
dialogueLines.text: You'll make it out of here, I've no doubt about it!

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Sisyphus_01
dialogueLines.text: Sisyphus furtively offers a choice of benefits.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Sisyphus_01
dialogueLines.text: Nice of you to drop in for a bit!

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Sisyphus_01
dialogueLines.text: Sisyphus furtively offers a choice of benefits.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Sisyphus_01
dialogueLines.text: You are a sight for sore eyes, Prince!

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Sisyphus_01
dialogueLines.text: Sisyphus furtively offers a choice of benefits.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Sisyphus_01
dialogueLines.text: Now you be careful out there, won't you, Prince?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Sisyphus_01
dialogueLines.text: Sisyphus furtively offers a choice of benefits.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Sisyphus_01
dialogueLines.text: You give them hell for me, all right, Prince Z.?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Sisyphus_01
dialogueLines.text: Sisyphus furtively offers a choice of benefits.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Sisyphus_01
dialogueLines.text: Go get them for me, Prince!

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Sisyphus_01
dialogueLines.text: Sisyphus furtively offers a choice of benefits.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Sisyphus_01
dialogueLines.text: Got just the thing for you here, Prince!

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Sisyphus_01
dialogueLines.text: Sisyphus furtively offers a choice of benefits.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Sisyphus_01
dialogueLines.text: It's always good to see you, Prince!

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Sisyphus_01
dialogueLines.text: Sisyphus furtively offers a choice of benefits.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Sisyphus_01
dialogueLines.text: You keep your chin up, Prince!

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Sisyphus_01
dialogueLines.text: Sisyphus furtively offers a choice of benefits.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Sisyphus_01
dialogueLines.text: Keep fighting the good fight, there, Prince!

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Sisyphus_01
dialogueLines.text: Sisyphus furtively offers a choice of benefits.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Sisyphus_01
dialogueLines.text: Hey you take care, you hear me, Prince?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Sisyphus_01
dialogueLines.text: Sisyphus furtively offers a choice of benefits.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Sisyphus_01
dialogueLines.text: Let's both of us keep going, that a deal?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Sisyphus_01
dialogueLines.text: Sisyphus furtively offers a choice of benefits.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Sisyphus_01
dialogueLines.text: It's good of you to visit me like this!

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Sisyphus_01
dialogueLines.text: Sisyphus furtively offers a choice of benefits.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Sisyphus_01
dialogueLines.text: Hope everything's been well enough of late.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Sisyphus_01
dialogueLines.text: Sisyphus furtively offers a choice of benefits.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Sisyphus_01
dialogueLines.text: You watch your step out there, all right, Prince Z.?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Sisyphus_01
dialogueLines.text: Sisyphus furtively offers a choice of benefits.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Sisyphus_01
dialogueLines.text: Hullo Highness, you're looking well enough!

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Sisyphus_01
dialogueLines.text: Sisyphus furtively offers a choice of benefits.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Sisyphus_01
dialogueLines.text: Hey, Your Highness, how goes it, how is everything?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Sisyphus_01
dialogueLines.text: Sisyphus furtively offers a choice of benefits.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Sisyphus_01
dialogueLines.text: I have to say it's good to see a friendly face!

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Sisyphus_01
dialogueLines.text: Sisyphus furtively offers a choice of benefits.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Sisyphus_01
dialogueLines.text: Always a pleasure, Highness, you take care out there.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Sisyphus_01
dialogueLines.text: Sisyphus furtively offers a choice of benefits.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Sisyphus_01
dialogueLines.text: Here's my best friend in all the Underworld, how goes it, Prince?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Sisyphus_01
dialogueLines.text: Sisyphus furtively offers a choice of benefits.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Sisyphus_01
dialogueLines.text: Glad you made it all this way, Prince Z.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Sisyphus_01
dialogueLines.text: Sisyphus furtively offers a choice of benefits.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Sisyphus_01
dialogueLines.text: Think I've got just the thing for you right here, Highness!

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Sisyphus_01
dialogueLines.text: Sisyphus furtively offers a choice of benefits.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Sisyphus_01
dialogueLines.text: A brief hello from time to time does wonders, don't you think, Prince Z.?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Sisyphus_01
dialogueLines.text: Sisyphus furtively offers a choice of benefits.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Sisyphus_01
dialogueLines.text: Much as I like the company of Bouldy there, you're always welcome, Prince!

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Sisyphus_01
dialogueLines.text: Sisyphus furtively offers a choice of benefits.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Sisyphus_01
dialogueLines.text: Stop by whenever you're around these parts, all right, Prince Z.?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Sisyphus_01
dialogueLines.text: Sisyphus furtively offers a choice of benefits.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Sisyphus_01
dialogueLines.text: I know it isn't much, Highness, but I do hope this helps!

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Sisyphus_01
dialogueLines.text: Sisyphus furtively offers a choice of benefits.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Sisyphus_01
dialogueLines.text: Here's something courtesy of me and Bouldy, Prince!

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Sisyphus_01
dialogueLines.text: Sisyphus furtively offers a choice of benefits.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Sisyphus_01
dialogueLines.text: How about a little bit of help as you keep on your journey, Prince?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Sisyphus_01
dialogueLines.text: Sisyphus furtively offers a choice of benefits.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Sisyphus_01
dialogueLines.text: Let's see if we might make your going slightly easier, there, Prince!

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Sisyphus_01
dialogueLines.text: Sisyphus furtively offers a choice of benefits.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Sisyphus_01
dialogueLines.text: Should have something that ought to come in handy for you, Prince!

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Sisyphus_01
dialogueLines.text: Sisyphus furtively offers a choice of benefits.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Sisyphus_01
dialogueLines.text: No need for these, so take one off my hands, won't you, Prince Z.?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Sisyphus_01
dialogueLines.text: Sisyphus furtively offers a choice of benefits.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Sisyphus_01
dialogueLines.text: Bouldy and I have got the usual for you right here, Your Highness.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Sisyphus_01
dialogueLines.text: Sisyphus furtively offers a choice of benefits.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Sisyphus_01
dialogueLines.text: Highness, please take one of these and go!

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Sisyphus_01
dialogueLines.text: Sisyphus furtively offers a choice of benefits.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Sisyphus_01
dialogueLines.text: All's well out here in Tartarus, Prince Z.! What will it be?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Sisyphus_01
dialogueLines.text: Sisyphus furtively offers a choice of benefits.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Sisyphus_01
dialogueLines.text: Bouldy and I got our usual selection here, Highness!

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Sisyphus_01
dialogueLines.text: Sisyphus furtively offers a choice of benefits.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Sisyphus_01
dialogueLines.text: Don't let me keep you, Prince, I know you've plenty of important business here.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Sisyphus_01
dialogueLines.text: Sisyphus furtively offers a choice of benefits.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Sisyphus_01
dialogueLines.text: Before you go, Prince Z., a little souvenir from your fine stay in Tartarus.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Sisyphus_01
dialogueLines.text: Sisyphus furtively offers a choice of benefits.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Sisyphus_01
dialogueLines.text: Don't do anything I wouldn't do out there, you hear me, Prince?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Sisyphus_01
dialogueLines.text: Sisyphus furtively offers a choice of benefits.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Sisyphus_01
dialogueLines.text: You're looking good and ready for whatever lies ahead up there, Prince Z.!

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Sisyphus_01
dialogueLines.text: Sisyphus furtively offers a choice of benefits.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Sisyphus_01
dialogueLines.text: You know approximately where to find me if you happen to be in the area again, Prince Z.!

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Sisyphus_01
dialogueLines.text: Sisyphus furtively offers a choice of benefits.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Sisyphus_01
dialogueLines.text: Everything's fine as ever here, Prince Z., so you go on ahead, and don't look back!

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Sisyphus_01
dialogueLines.text: Sisyphus furtively offers a choice of benefits.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Sisyphus_01
dialogueLines.text: I have a feeling you are going to make it all the way topside this time, Highness!

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Sisyphus_01
dialogueLines.text: Sisyphus furtively offers a choice of benefits.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Sisyphus_01
dialogueLines.text: Need a little pick-me-up perhaps?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Sisyphus_01
dialogueLines.text: Sisyphus furtively offers a choice of benefits.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Sisyphus_01
dialogueLines.text: Hey, Prince Z. Must look like I'm slacking off with the old boulder, here, but... I was just getting back on my feet. The Fury Sisters don't often leave me be. Where'd they go, anyway? And... what are you doing here?

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: Oh, you know, just out on a little stroll is all. Do me a favor, though, you see the Fury Sisters, let them know I was headed down the way I came from, will you...?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Sisyphus_01
dialogueLines.text: Oh I'll let them know, all right, I hear you loud and clear, Your Highness. I'll just go about my business now, and you take care.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Sisyphus_01
dialogueLines.text: Sisyphus furtively offers a choice of benefits.

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: Hey, sir, thought this might ease your burden for a while.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Sisyphus_01
dialogueLines.text: Now hold a moment, Highness, I cannot accept something like this... not without giving back a little something in return, that is!

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Sisyphus_01
dialogueLines.text: Oh, my, you're really giving that to me, there, Prince? But, I'm just, why would you...?

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: You better believe it, sir. Now you take care, be seeing you around.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Sisyphus_01
dialogueLines.text: Why, Prince, you being serious right now, is that for me?

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: You deserve better, Sisyphus! This really isn't much, but maybe it'll help you while away the days. Nights. Whatever time it is.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Sisyphus_01
dialogueLines.text: Wow, Your Highness, you... you really shouldn't have. Thank you for not forgetting about me.

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: You're always a sight for sore eyes out here, Sisyphus. Thank you for always believing in me.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Sisyphus_01
dialogueLines.text: I'm really very flattered, Highness! You didn't have to get me anything, I mean, your company's more than enough for me!

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: I figured you could do with something more than that this time around, good sir.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Sisyphus_01
dialogueLines.text: Oh, but this is just too much for me, Your Highness, thank you, really. I don't know how I can ever repay you.

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: How about you keep on helping me not die as quickly as I might have otherwise, deal?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Sisyphus_01
dialogueLines.text: Wait, really, this, this is for me, you mean it, Prince? I mean, I won't say no, I can't say no to such a thing, so, thank you! Thank you kindly.

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: You're more than welcome, sir. For all you've done for me, it's the least I could do.

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: Sisyphus, sir? You've helped me selflessly time after time without so much as asking anything in return. Wanted to say I'm very grateful, and thought you might like to try this. Here, for a special occasion!

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Sisyphus_01
dialogueLines.text: Why, Prince, is this...? It is...! I've not so much as seen a bottle of it, much less sampled it, thank you so much! Although, with all respect, I cannot rightfully accept such a fine gift. Unless...

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: Unless...? Oh no, you're not making me take something in return, you know this was a gift not an exchange, sir, right? Wait, what is that?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Sisyphus_01
dialogueLines.text: This, Highness, is a little friend of mine kept tucked away in Bouldy's care. Wherever you'll be going next, he'll make a worthy traveling companion. And when he's with you, Bouldy and I... we'll be with you in spirit, too.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Sisyphus_01
dialogueLines.text: ...I'm really rather speechless, Your Highness. I'm just a shade doing my time. I don't deserve your generosity.

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: Come on, sir, what's a priceless bottle of the drink of the gods between friends?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Sisyphus_01
dialogueLines.text: Prince, that vintage Ambrosia bottle that you gave me, my! That was the greatest thing I ever tried, I think! Even Bouldy enjoyed the stuff!

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: I thought you'd like it, sir. And so, I'm pleased to offer you another! Almost a shame we seldom get such imports from Olympus around here. I hope I can appreciate it for myself sometime. Once all this is over with, I think.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Sisyphus_01
dialogueLines.text: What? You mean that you've never touched the stuff yourself? You've much more self control than I have, there. Perhaps someday we'll have a chance to share some between us!

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Sisyphus_01
dialogueLines.text: Prince, that vintage Ambrosia bottle that you gave me, my! That was the greatest thing I ever tried, I think! Even Bouldy enjoyed the stuff!

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: I thought you'd like it, sir. And so, I'm pleased to offer you another! Almost a shame we seldom get such imports from Olympus around here.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Sisyphus_01
dialogueLines.text: Ah, look at what the Prince has brought for us, Bouldy! Perhaps someday we'll have a chance to share some between us!

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Sisyphus_01
dialogueLines.text: All this, for someone such as me. You know I'm down here for a reason, don't you, Prince? Not just because your father and good Thanatos don't care for me. Yet, from the very first, you showed me decency I'd not experienced since I was a king. Perhaps not even then.

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: Whoever it was you used to be, sir, I believe you've changed. And I'm proud to say that you're my friend. And Bouldy there as well. It's odd for me to say, but I am glad you're here. I think I've learned a lot from you, about all this.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Sisyphus_01
dialogueLines.text: Well, Your Highness, I am honored very much indeed, and having you amongst my list of friends makes it perhaps the greatest such a list a fellow such as I has ever had. Isn't that right, Bouldy?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Sisyphus_01
dialogueLines.text: Take it from me, Prince Z., Bouldy concurs.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Sisyphus_01
dialogueLines.text: I will have you know, Prince, that your visits here have become a very important part of my routine!

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: And what routine is that, exactly, sir?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Sisyphus_01
dialogueLines.text: Why, the routine of looking forward, of course! I try to appreciate the moment as much as anybody, but you know? It's always nice having something to look forward to. So, see you next time, then?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Sisyphus_01
dialogueLines.text: Sisyphus furtively offers a choice of benefits.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Sisyphus_01
dialogueLines.text: Must say, Prince Z., the free life sure does have its perks down here! This place really isn't as bad as it's cracked up to be.

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: Glad to hear it, sir. But you're still here... the same as ever. Is it really all that different from the way it always was?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Sisyphus_01
dialogueLines.text: Well, it used to be I'd have to start heaving Bouldy there at any point, if the Fury Sisters decided to show up. But now? I just get to relax, chat a bit with the other shades, and give you one of these when you're in the area!

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Sisyphus_01
dialogueLines.text: Sisyphus furtively offers a choice of benefits.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Sisyphus_01
dialogueLines.text: Ah, there you are, Prince! I was just thinking about how grateful I am that you got me out of that contract with your father. It's been a real weight off my shoulders, truth be told.

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: You never seemed particularly concerned even before, sir. Must say you're very good at hiding your worries.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Sisyphus_01
dialogueLines.text: And I could say the same to you! Look, I know you were concerned for my wellbeing. Now you don't have to be. And I'll still be around to help you out. Good feeling, isn't it?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Sisyphus_01
dialogueLines.text: Sisyphus furtively offers a choice of benefits.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Sisyphus_01
dialogueLines.text: You must have seen everything there is to see here in your father's Underworld, haven't you, Prince? I figure that there isn't any place nicer than this!

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: Oh! Well, I suppose now that you're free, you could always see for yourself? It's pretty different as soon as you reach Asphodel. Don't you have someplace or somebody you'd like to see?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Sisyphus_01
dialogueLines.text: I'll take what's coming to me right here, thank you very much. You're the wayfaring sort, but my wayfaring days are over. It's hard to explain, but... this is a much-needed fresh start for me.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Sisyphus_01
dialogueLines.text: Sisyphus furtively offers a choice of benefits.

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: I have good news, sir! I located the contract binding you, and bought it out. It's null and void. You're free to go!

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Sisyphus_01
dialogueLines.text: Oh! Indeed, then, does this mean, Prince, then... the Fury Sisters, they won't come around as often anymore?

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: Not just that, Sisyphus! You can make your way someplace else, where other decent shades reside! You know, somewhere with fewer chains and torture devices and such?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Sisyphus_01
dialogueLines.text: You're much too kind to this old soul, Prince Z. The thing is, this is home. For me and Bouldy, it is what it is. Though, knowing that the Furies won't be visiting with quite their former regularity, that is a load off. Heavier than Bouldy, there.

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: You mean you're really going to stay? Even though you don't have to?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Sisyphus_01
dialogueLines.text: Is that so odd, Highness? Don't see myself lugging old Bouldy out of here, besides! And if I were to leave, why, we would not be having these exchanges now and then. I happen to enjoy them quite a bit! Now, here you go, and thank you, very much.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Sisyphus_01
dialogueLines.text: Sisyphus furtively offers a choice of benefits.

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: Sisyphus, I know you tend to be in remarkably good spirits, all things considered, but there must be something I can do to help with your predicament, isn't there?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Sisyphus_01
dialogueLines.text: Oh, come now, Prince, do Bouldy and I look like we could use your charity? We're planning to stay put within this chamber for a while. It's hardly a predicament, I'd say.

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: But, what of the Furies and their torments and all that? They might be back and make all this more difficult for you.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Sisyphus_01
dialogueLines.text: Ah, yes, the Fury Sisters, well. I wouldn't mind seeing them more infrequently now that you mention it, but either way! So don't you even worry about me. Now, here.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Sisyphus_01
dialogueLines.text: Sisyphus furtively offers a choice of benefits.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Sisyphus_01
dialogueLines.text: Bouldy and I will always be here in your time of need, Prince Z.!

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Sisyphus_01
dialogueLines.text: Sisyphus furtively offers a choice of benefits.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Sisyphus_01
dialogueLines.text: Bouldy says whatever we can do to help, we'll do it, Prince!

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Sisyphus_01
dialogueLines.text: Sisyphus furtively offers a choice of benefits.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Sisyphus_01
dialogueLines.text: In case words of encouragement are not enough, have one of these here, Prince!

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Sisyphus_01
dialogueLines.text: Sisyphus furtively offers a choice of benefits.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Sisyphus_01
dialogueLines.text: Let's get you on your way, Prince Z., and in good spirits, too!

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Sisyphus_01
dialogueLines.text: Sisyphus furtively offers a choice of benefits.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Sisyphus_01
dialogueLines.text: Don't you worry, Highness, we have got you covered here!

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Sisyphus_01
dialogueLines.text: Sisyphus furtively offers a choice of benefits.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Sisyphus_01
dialogueLines.text: Told old Bouldy here you'd probably be back if only to say hi!

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Sisyphus_01
dialogueLines.text: Sisyphus furtively offers a choice of benefits.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Sisyphus_01
dialogueLines.text: Hey Prince, Bouldy and I will not be needing these, but maybe they can help!

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Sisyphus_01
dialogueLines.text: Sisyphus furtively offers a choice of benefits.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Sisyphus_01
dialogueLines.text: Just do the best you can, if you don't mind me saying so, Prince Z.!

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Sisyphus_01
dialogueLines.text: Sisyphus furtively offers a choice of benefits.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Sisyphus_01
dialogueLines.text: Speaking for myself and Bouldy there, we're always at your service, Prince.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Sisyphus_01
dialogueLines.text: Sisyphus furtively offers a choice of benefits.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Sisyphus_01
dialogueLines.text: You get all the way up to the top and do whatever it is needs getting done, Highness!

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Sisyphus_01
dialogueLines.text: Sisyphus furtively offers a choice of benefits.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Sisyphus_01
dialogueLines.text: I know Bouldy believes in you every bit as much as I do, Prince!

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Sisyphus_01
dialogueLines.text: Sisyphus furtively offers a choice of benefits.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Sisyphus_01
dialogueLines.text: If I may be so bold, Your Highness, I was wondering... what brings you back around this way again? Same story as last time?

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: Look, I have to level with you, Sisyphus. I'm leaving the House. I've had quite enough. You understand, I'm sure. I'm going to the surface.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Sisyphus_01
dialogueLines.text: Whew, that is quite an undertaking, Prince. If it wasn't you yourself proposing it, I'd like to call it madness, truth be told. Though you're just mad enough to pull it off! Well hey, I hope you make it. Good fortune out there!

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Sisyphus_01
dialogueLines.text: Sisyphus furtively offers a choice of benefits.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Sisyphus_01
dialogueLines.text: You know, Prince Z., I'd not expected to run into you again, not after all this time. Something amiss down in the House, or what? Not had much company of late, is all.

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: Oh, something's just about always amiss back home, sir. Figured it couldn't hurt to check up on things in Tartarus a bit more frequently.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Sisyphus_01
dialogueLines.text: Well I am grateful for it, I and the old boulder there, in turn. I'm used to going lengthy stretches on my own, but having visitors from time to time would do me well!

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Sisyphus_01
dialogueLines.text: Sisyphus furtively offers a choice of benefits.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Sisyphus_01
dialogueLines.text: Hey Prince how goes the old escape plan, any luck?

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: You know it's been all right, all things considered. Dying now and then, and it's been hard, but hey, what can you do.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Sisyphus_01
dialogueLines.text: Ah, well, keep your chin up. Me, why I quit trying to get out of here a while back, but, if I was a fire-stepping youngster like yourself, perhaps I would have stuck to it.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Sisyphus_01
dialogueLines.text: Sisyphus furtively offers a choice of benefits.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Sisyphus_01
dialogueLines.text: Hey, Your Highness, anything the matter, or just passing through? Happy that we get to chat like this of late, of course.

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: Sisyphus, don't take this the wrong way, but... why are you helping me? My father's stuck you here for an eternity of torment and even still, you greet me with a smile, time after time.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Sisyphus_01
dialogueLines.text: Hah! Come now, Prince Z., that's just the way I am! Besides, you're not your father, thank the gods! You always seemed a decent lad to me, and if you're saying you want out of here, why, I suppose I can relate, is all.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Sisyphus_01
dialogueLines.text: Sisyphus furtively offers a choice of benefits.

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: Hey, quick question, Sisyphus, you seen the other Fury Sisters lately?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Sisyphus_01
dialogueLines.text: Not seen the other Fury Sisters in some time now that you mention it! Just Megaera, the kindly one, and she is quite a handful on her own. I'll keep an eye out for the other two!

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Sisyphus_01
dialogueLines.text: Sisyphus furtively offers a choice of benefits.

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: Sisyphus, how can you stand it, being here like this? You always seem in such good spirits, though.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Sisyphus_01
dialogueLines.text: You push old Bouldy here sufficient times, and you might get a different outlook on things, too. With the Furies all preoccupied and with my running into you from time to time, I'm positively doing great lately. I like to take what pleasures I can get!

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Sisyphus_01
dialogueLines.text: Sisyphus furtively offers a choice of benefits.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Sisyphus_01
dialogueLines.text: Look here, Bouldy, Prince Z. himself has come to visit us!

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: Does Bouldy really keep you company? Personally I would resent a giant boulder that keeps tumbling back down a hill after I push it all the way up there.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Sisyphus_01
dialogueLines.text: Oh, Bouldy's not so bad, I mean, he's a good listener, a good shoulder to lean on, doesn't wield a whip... I rightly can't complain! Good having company down here.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Sisyphus_01
dialogueLines.text: Sisyphus furtively offers a choice of benefits.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Sisyphus_01
dialogueLines.text: Look, Prince, I don't hold grudges, you know that. Push this boulder long as I have, you move past the petty stuff. So, just want you to know, in case you make it out this time, it's been a real pleasure.

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: Feeling's mutual, sir. Someday you'll have to tell me all about how you tricked Father. I might learn a thing or two.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Sisyphus_01
dialogueLines.text: Sisyphus furtively offers a choice of benefits.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Sisyphus_01
dialogueLines.text: You know, Prince, it's been good not having to see those Fury Sisters quite as much lately. The main one, what's her name, she's on your case now, isn't she.

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: You mean Megaera. She's on my case all right. Under strict orders not to let me out of Tartarus. I guess that means you've got yourself a break! About damn time they gave one to you, sir.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Sisyphus_01
dialogueLines.text: Sisyphus furtively offers a choice of benefits.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Sisyphus_01
dialogueLines.text: Hey, Prince Z., I heard the strangest rumor, here's the thing, I'll give it to you straight. I have heard you've been consorting both with Megaera the Fury and with that old stiff, Thanatos, can you believe it?

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: Sisyphus, I don't know where you heard that, but... it's true. They're... they're just doing their jobs, and I... I know how it looks, but... you're my friend, as well, and I don't want you to forget that.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Sisyphus_01
dialogueLines.text: I... look, I do appreciate the sentiment, Your Highness, I'm just surprised. I thought we shared a common foe in them, is all. What can I say, I hope that someday I can see them how you see them.

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: Thank you for understanding, sir. Besides... maybe I can get them to cut you some slack.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Sisyphus_01
dialogueLines.text: Sisyphus furtively offers a choice of benefits.

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: Sisyphus, I have to be up front with you. The Fury Megaera and Thanatos... I know you're not exactly on the best of terms with either one of them. But they're both close to me. You won't hold it against me, sir, will you?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Sisyphus_01
dialogueLines.text: Look, Prince, it's not for me to judge you, or your friends. The Fury Sisters, Master Thanatos... we got off on the wrong foot, you could say. My fault, entirely. I'd sooner make sure that our correspondence here doesn't make those relationships more difficult.

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: Don't even say that, sir. I think Meg and Than know me better than that by now. Well, thanks for understanding, as always.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Sisyphus_01
dialogueLines.text: Certainly, Highness. And who knows? Perhaps as you spend more time together, some of your finer qualities will rub off on those two!

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Sisyphus_01
dialogueLines.text: Sisyphus furtively offers a choice of benefits.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Sisyphus_01
dialogueLines.text: Hey Prince, I heard you showed that Fury Sister the what-for, is that the truth? Might explain why I've been left here to my own devices for a change.

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: I don't think she'll be out of commission for very long. She'd best find you minding that boulder, though at least you managed to catch a break for now. Be seeing you, Sisyphus.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Sisyphus_01
dialogueLines.text: Sisyphus furtively offers a choice of benefits.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Sisyphus_01
dialogueLines.text: Now you be careful out there, Highness. The kindly Megaera is out for blood. She kept on groaning 'bout the last exchange between the two of you. You must have showed her good!

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: I think I might have gotten lucky.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Sisyphus_01
dialogueLines.text: Sisyphus furtively offers a choice of benefits.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Sisyphus_01
dialogueLines.text: I heard straight from the source the Fury Megaera's been giving you a time, there, hasn't she. Trust me I know the feeling, so, I'm grateful that you're here and that she's not. You give her hell for me next time, won't you?

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: Oh I'll get her for the both of us one of these days. Nights? Whenever.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Sisyphus_01
dialogueLines.text: Sisyphus furtively offers a choice of benefits.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Sisyphus_01
dialogueLines.text: So, all this time, Prince, when you were battling up to the surface, and all that... you were searching for her, weren't you? The Queen?

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: I was. Although, between us, sir, it's important that you keep that one a secret, please. Did... you happen to meet her, ever?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Sisyphus_01
dialogueLines.text: Well, just the one time, briefly, when your father decided to put me here. And, you have my word, of course. About the... surface stuff.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Sisyphus_01
dialogueLines.text: Sisyphus furtively offers a choice of benefits.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Sisyphus_01
dialogueLines.text: Prince Z., my understanding is the Queen herself is back, is that correct? Moreover, I have heard a truly fascinating rumor about her...

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: That I'm her son...? It's true, sir. And she's back with us for now, indeed. I think we're all still getting used to it, but... it feels good, having her in my life.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Sisyphus_01
dialogueLines.text: That's wonderful to hear. I'd always heard good things about that one. No wonder such a quality runs in the family.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Sisyphus_01
dialogueLines.text: Sisyphus furtively offers a choice of benefits.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Sisyphus_01
dialogueLines.text: Just one thing I don't understand, Prince Z., if you'll indulge me for a moment, here. If everything is all right for you at the House, then... why is it you're fighting to the surface, still?

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: Oh, you know. Wouldn't want Father to get too comfortable thinking this place is foolproof. It has a terrifyingly ominous reputation to uphold! There is no escape.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Sisyphus_01
dialogueLines.text: Sure, that makes sense! Well, from a certain point of view. Escape over and over to prove it can't be done! I like that. Well, let's both of us keep at it, then!

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Sisyphus_01
dialogueLines.text: Sisyphus furtively offers a choice of benefits.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Sisyphus_01
dialogueLines.text: Not working yourself too hard of late, are you, Prince Z.? Remembering to take some breaks from time to time, and all?

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: I think I'm managing, thanks, sir. Though, back when you pushed Bouldy rather than talked to him... I suppose you didn't have much of a choice, did you?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Sisyphus_01
dialogueLines.text: Well, it was work, you know? Even your Father has to work. Of course, I've since retired! But it seems you and he like to keep yourselves occupied. Whatever makes you feel content.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Sisyphus_01
dialogueLines.text: Sisyphus furtively offers a choice of benefits.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Sisyphus_01
dialogueLines.text: Prince, you've been gone for quite a while, why... I heard-tell you made it all the way outside! And I believed it for a while there, yet here you are. What happened, might I ask?

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: I made it out of here, indeed, sir. I always said that I was leaving, but... turns out there was good reason for me to come back. Not the least of which is visiting my friends from time to time.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Sisyphus_01
dialogueLines.text: Well, I am certainly delighted to hear that! So long as you achieved what you set out to do. But, what brings you back again this way? More than a social visit I presume.

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: You're right. I'm back because... I'm leaving once again. Something I have to do if I get up there, though I should be back before long either way, I guess.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Sisyphus_01
dialogueLines.text: Going to try and make it out of here again and again, is it? Well they say repetition is the key to mastery, Prince Z. Now here, I'll send you on your way.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Sisyphus_01
dialogueLines.text: Sisyphus furtively offers a choice of benefits.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Sisyphus_01
dialogueLines.text: So, how do you like the surface, hey, Prince Z.? Now that you've finally had a chance to see it for yourself. Not too bad, I hope?

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: It takes some getting used to, that's for sure. I'm not sure I'll ever grow completely accustomed to it. Do you miss it, sir?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Sisyphus_01
dialogueLines.text: Oh, you know. I still have almost everything that matters, and the rest? It makes for precious memories I didn't have up there.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Sisyphus_01
dialogueLines.text: Sisyphus furtively offers a choice of benefits.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Sisyphus_01
dialogueLines.text: Word is you've caused quite the hubbub in Elysium, Prince Z.! Making the champion up there really work for it, I hear?

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: You could say that, I guess! Apparently word gets around. That Theseus, though... he doesn't care for me one bit, let me tell you!

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Sisyphus_01
dialogueLines.text: I bet he'd like you better if you were a regular pushover! Ah, well, he'll learn, I'm sure.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Sisyphus_01
dialogueLines.text: Sisyphus furtively offers a choice of benefits.

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: You and Achilles ever get to talk much, Than?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Thanatos_01
dialogueLines.text: I have my orders, he has his. What do you think? Even back when he was still alive, there was no real chance I was going to be the one to take him here, given his line of work.

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: Because he was a warrior. When mortals die a violent death, they're no longer your case, are they?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Thanatos_01
dialogueLines.text: No. I tend to those in less of a hurry to come here.

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: Than, what's your relationship with Ares on Olympus? I know he deals in death himself, but... his approach seems rather different from your own.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Thanatos_01
dialogueLines.text: I do not care for his approach at all. But he has been professionally courteous with me. More than I can say for most of your Olympian relatives, I suppose.

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: You mean the rest of the Olympians don't treat you well?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Thanatos_01
dialogueLines.text: Discounting Hermes, I should say, the rest of them have nothing much to do with me at all. I can imagine why, I know it's complicated with your father here. But as for Ares... I think he's all right.

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: Hey, what is it you're always staring at out there, anyway, Than? I don't see anything.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Thanatos_01
dialogueLines.text: The River Styx is right in front of you, and you don't see anything, Zag? I can watch it endlessly. Studying the competition, you might say. Most mortals expect to get here by river, with Charon. Not with me.

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: So you're locked in ruthless battle with a river, searching it for any signs of weakness, that sort of thing? That sounds stressful.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Thanatos_01
dialogueLines.text: I find it rather soothing as a matter of fact. A break from having to deal constantly with mortal souls. Speaking of which... I have to pick somebody up right now.

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: Than, you don't take issue with me being close with Dusa, do you? She brings such warmth to this place... I really appreciate having her here.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Thanatos_01
dialogueLines.text: You don't need to ask me such things, Zagreus. They're your feelings.

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: Than, there's something that I meant to tell you. Ask you, rather. You're close with Megaera, so, what I mean is... you know about us, right?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Thanatos_01
dialogueLines.text: Yes, of course. Why do you ask?

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: Well, it's just... I figured since you deal with mortals all the time, I know that some of them, they choose a single suitor. It's a valid custom, if it's yours...!

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Thanatos_01
dialogueLines.text: The mortals have their customs, yes, indeed. Quite a variety, in point of fact. However, none as yet have stuck with me, I think. So don't concern yourself with things like that.

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: You're saying you're not angry with me? Or Meg, for that matter?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Thanatos_01
dialogueLines.text: Not any more than usual. Tell her that I said hello when next you meet.

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: What's going on with Charon, by the way, if you can tell me, Than? He's... never been particularly talkative, but he really seems to know the inner workings of this place.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Thanatos_01
dialogueLines.text: I correspond with Charon all the time, but even I don't know the full extent of his responsibilities. He's an independent contractor of sorts, been here forever. Your father gives him plenty of space to work.

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: He must be compensated pretty well for having done this for so long. Tending the influx of new souls and all of that.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Thanatos_01
dialogueLines.text: I doubt he's in it for the compensation, Zagreus. There is no Underworld without Charon. I think he knows that, and your father knows it, too.

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: Than, you're not going to believe this, but... Charon's been helping me. All on his own, apparently! I was so sure that Nyx or someone put him up to it. That Skelly character I mentioned, Charon hired him!

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Thanatos_01
dialogueLines.text: What are you talking about, Zagreus? Just because Charon's selling you things doesn't mean he's helping you. Wait, are you sure?

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: I'm sure! His helper, Skelly, he's a lot more talkative, and finally let on. Although, he couldn't tell me how come Charon did a thing like this, I mean... we weren't ever close...

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Thanatos_01
dialogueLines.text: Hmm. All I can think of is... Charon's very dedicated to this House. Like some of us. Maybe he thought... he could help settle things, between you and your father. One way or another.

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: Hey, were you ever close with Charon, Than? Seems like no matter how much I try, I can't quite get through to him.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Thanatos_01
dialogueLines.text: Wouldn't be surprised if you were closer to him than I am. Not all of Nyx's sons and daughters keep in steady touch. Though at least we're not always at each others' throats, like the other side of your family.

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: Hey, Than, now hypothetically, if I were to have vanquished Charon in single combat after having, I don't know, say, helped myself to one of his wares at no charge, how long would you say he'd hold it over me?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Thanatos_01
dialogueLines.text: Everything is an exchange with Charon, Zag. If there was something you refused to pay for, then I'm going to assume that's why you're back here, now. The debt is paid in obols, or in blood. Or their closest counterparts, I guess.

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: Thanatos, I've meant to ask... is it really the case that many mortals have a crippling fear of death?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Thanatos_01
dialogueLines.text: It is, I'm quite feared, up there. Most of them cannot distinguish between me, and, say, Lord Ares, or your father, even. We fill them with despair. No helping it.

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: Why not? I mean, isn't it kind of a big problem that they have such negative associations, here? Can't we... I don't know, do something to educate them all?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Thanatos_01
dialogueLines.text: We've all the time to educate them, here. Their ignorance of death is solved almost as soon as they experience their first. Though I suppose they might be better off knowing before the fact.

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: Than? I had another question about death. So... why is it mortals grieve so much about the thing, I mean... it definitely hurts there for a little while, but... it's not that bad.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Thanatos_01
dialogueLines.text: They see it as their one and only chance. Imagine if, after you perished, that was it. Back to the infinite Chaos. Everything about you. Returned to how it was before you were even there.

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: But that's absurd. One life? Though... eugh. That's horrible, even to think about.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Thanatos_01
dialogueLines.text: I know, right? Some of them know that isn't how it is, and try to tell their kind about what it is we do here, but... it's slow going, getting the information out. Either way! I get to be the bearer of good news.

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: Hey Than? Something's been troubling me still, with what you said, about how mortals look at death. If most of them believe their life is all they have... how do they live? I mean, I can't even imagine what that's like. I'd be a lot more careful, at the very least!

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Thanatos_01
dialogueLines.text: They live with all their hearts, or at least try. I think there's something to it, truthfully. Though, some of them, they fall apart. They get here without me. On their own, gripped with despair. If only they knew what your father always says about all this.

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: What, you mean that 'there is no escape'? From what? The mortals are up there, not here. Not while they're technically alive in their sense, anyway.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Thanatos_01
dialogueLines.text: I mean, you can't escape your problems, Zagreus. You can run from your family, but you can't run from everything. You have no choice but to confront it, and work through it, sooner or later, one way or another. You, and all of us.

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: ...I'm starting to notice that, yes. Though, to a mortal who has yet to die... don't they think of death as some sort of release? You come to end their misery, something like that?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Thanatos_01
dialogueLines.text: I can release them from their decaying bodies. Sometimes, the Fates demand I bring them in sooner. That's about as much as I can do. Some of them get it early on, how to live. It's quite impressive, really, that they learn so quick.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Thanatos_01
dialogueLines.text: How's that Rail of Adamant been working out for you lately? Always figured the direct approach was more your style. Though, I happened to have a look at your permanent record lately, and turns out you're doing a lot of work with that thing.

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: Why, Thanatos, you're learning to pry in others' business! The Rail's been working out just fine, thanks. Though, now that you mention it, how well do you know Eris, one of Nyx's daughters, right? Seems she wielded the Rail before me.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Thanatos_01
dialogueLines.text: The mortals call her Strife. She and her children... it's thanks partly to their dedicated efforts I'm so busy all the time. Though, it is best we didn't speak of her, I think.

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: Than, you know, Hypnos, he adores you. Besides just driving you to madness, now and then.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Thanatos_01
dialogueLines.text: I'm rather used to the relationship, thanks, Zagreus. Seems like you got to know him pretty well.

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: Well, dying repeatedly can end up bringing people closer, I suppose.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Thanatos_01
dialogueLines.text: I don't know what's worse, dying repeatedly or Hypnos. Although, I guess I'm grateful for your patience with him, Zag.

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: Got to tell you something, Than. You can be pretty mean to Hypnos! And that's coming from someone who's mean to Hypnos a lot. Believe me, I completely understand, but... still.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Thanatos_01
dialogueLines.text: He's utterly incompetent, Zag. He wouldn't even be here if Mother Nyx wasn't trying to whip him into some sort of shape.

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: Yeah, but... pretty much none of this would be here, were it not for Nyx, right? Anyway my point is not that you should go easy on him, but... there must be some way not to get as frustrated. You'll both be better off.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Thanatos_01
dialogueLines.text: I know, it's just...! You're right. I catch myself feeling particularly horrid after talking to him. He can be very frustrating, and self-absorbed... though, I know he means well. Probably.

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: I can hardly believe it. Hypnos finally came out on top, huh? Even Father seemed pleased with him. That's more than I get.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Thanatos_01
dialogueLines.text: I can hardly believe it either, Zagreus. But then again, he's one of Mother Nyx's offspring, so we shouldn't be so surprised. Though, I have to thank you for talking to me about him a while back...

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: Oh, that? I noticed you two getting along better recently. So what's your secret?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Thanatos_01
dialogueLines.text: He loves lists. Doesn't do well with subtlety, so much, but spell things out for him, and he will get right on it. Long as he's awake, at least. He's still working on that part.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Thanatos_01
dialogueLines.text: You've seen a lot of Megaera lately, haven't you, Zag?

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: It's not like that, Than. She's there to slow me down. Get in my way.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Thanatos_01
dialogueLines.text: If you insist. Although I think it's much more complicated than you say.

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: Yeah... her sisters are back. They're... hard to deal with. I don't think Meg is on good terms with either one.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Thanatos_01
dialogueLines.text: That's not what I meant. But fine, you've told me all I need to know, for now.

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: You and Meg go back a long way, don't you? I forget sometimes you two are pretty close.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Thanatos_01
dialogueLines.text: Our responsibilities must have us cross paths often, yes. But I didn't really get to know her until after the whole humiliating affair with Sisyphus.

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: Oh, right. He got stuck in Tartarus after that, under Meg's personal supervision. So you bonded over your shared disdain for Sisyphus?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Thanatos_01
dialogueLines.text: We each had to blow off some steam, yes. That was some of the hardest I ever worked, back then, and her, too, I think. When you go through something like that with someone, it brings you closer, I suppose.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Thanatos_01
dialogueLines.text: Your trainer's been absent from his post, more so than usual. You voided his pact with Lord Hades, didn't you? I don't know how you did it.

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: You know he's more than my trainer, Than. As for how I did it, turns out being the first and only son of Hades has its privileges. Now Achilles gets to be with someone he loves. Who loves him back.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Thanatos_01
dialogueLines.text: Love is common among mortals. Is theirs really so special as to be worth the trouble? Not just on your part, but theirs?

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: Than, my blade, the one I'm borrowing from Father anyway, it... revealed to me an older aspect of itself, of Nemesis. Nyx never mentions her. Have you two ever met?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Thanatos_01
dialogueLines.text: In case you haven't noticed, most of us who sprang from Nyx, we're not exactly close. Nemesis is fully occupied meting out justice on the surface. Our paths are not designed to cross. And based on everything I know, that's fine with me.

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: Than? I thought you ought to know that Ares, he... I think he's taken quite an interest in Nyx. Keeps asking me to talk to her on his behalf.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Thanatos_01
dialogueLines.text: Finally. It's about time Olympus took notice of Mother and all her accomplishments.

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: Finally? You don't think Ares has untoward intentions or anything like that?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Thanatos_01
dialogueLines.text: He'd be a hollow fool if he did. Why do you think they fear us on Olympus, Zagreus? Because of Hypnos? I don't think Mother Nyx requires your concern. Though, by all means, talk to her about it, if you like.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Thanatos_01
dialogueLines.text: Mother Nyx told me you restored the Eldest Sigil, Zagreus. I couldn't believe that was your doing. What you did... it meant a lot to her.

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: It was my pleasure. Say, did Nyx ever let on to you of her relationship with Chaos, at any point?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Thanatos_01
dialogueLines.text: No, not everyone is quite as sharing as you are, Zag. Nor as inclined to pry. But still... in this case, sounds like it worked out. So, thank you. If she hasn't said as much.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Thanatos_01
dialogueLines.text: I can tell when Mother Nyx is upset, Zagreus. And I know better than to ask her, so I'm going to ask you. What happened?

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: Probably because Nyx took issue with me chatting with Dusa from time to time. Is that really so unbecoming of me? Not even Father seems to care.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Thanatos_01
dialogueLines.text: Well, Mother cares! And I, for one, know better than to question it. None of us would be here were it not for her. If she has a request for you, you honor it.

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: You're telling me I should just start ignoring Dusa from now on, like you do?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Thanatos_01
dialogueLines.text: You're a god. I'm telling you to learn to act like one.

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: I'm sorry I raised my voice with you last time, Than. I know Nyx puts so much into this place, and has nurtured so many of us here.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Thanatos_01
dialogueLines.text: It's not me you should be apologizing to, Zag. Although, with Mother Nyx, sometimes it's best to leave her be. As for Dusa... I didn't mean to suggest you should abandon your friend.

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: Nyx let her go, Than. Dusa, I mean, she's gone. What do I do?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Thanatos_01
dialogueLines.text: If I were you, I would start getting over it as soon as possible. But, I don't see you doing that. Just remember... maybe this isn't about you. Dusa's job may have been far too much for her. Mother Nyx is strict, but she is fair.

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: Then she should give Dusa another chance. Has Meg heard? She'll be furious.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Thanatos_01
dialogueLines.text: Megaera will be no such thing. Some of us don't default to questioning every decision from our superiors.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Thanatos_01
dialogueLines.text: I saw that Dusa's back. You must have worked your ways on Mother Nyx. Don't know how you do it. Can't remember the last time I saw her budge on anything.

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: Nyx genuinely cares about this House and all of us. She had to come around on something like this. Dusa's one of the hardest workers here. Even if she has room to improve.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Thanatos_01
dialogueLines.text: Incessant hard work can lead to trouble, Zagreus. I think maybe that's why Mother was concerned. We all know how your father can get. She doesn't need someone like Dusa taking after him.

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: Do you remember anything else about my mother, Than? Just, anything. You weren't close, I guess?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Thanatos_01
dialogueLines.text: We weren't. She kept to herself as I recall, and I would do the same. Unlike my present company, I do not ask too many questions. It was quite clear to me she wasn't from this place.

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: Father isn't from this place, either. How was she any different?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Thanatos_01
dialogueLines.text: Sometimes I have to be reminded that Lord Hades wasn't born here, like the rest of us. But Persephone, she... she had a radiance about her, I suppose. Reminded me of the outside, but... I don't mean that in a negative respect.

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: Have to tell you something, Than. My birthmother... she didn't abandon me. Or at least... she didn't think she did.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Thanatos_01
dialogueLines.text: She didn't think she did? What are you talking about, Zag? She left when you were born. Was never seen or spoken of again, until all this with you.

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: Well, it turns out... she never knew I was alive, at all. Complications when I was born. She had been struggling already here, and it was all too much, I guess. By the time Nyx brought me back... my mother was long gone.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Thanatos_01
dialogueLines.text: What? But, Mother Nyx, and your father, they... could have sent for her, let her know... they must have known where she went!

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: Nyx knew. But, Father must have convinced her that my mother... that it would be for the best if she never found out. About me. So she could live in peace. Away from all of this. And away from Olympus.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Thanatos_01
dialogueLines.text: That's... a lot to take in.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Thanatos_01
dialogueLines.text: I saw the Fated List of Minor Prophecies was requisitioned through the House Contractor, Zagreus. Didn't take you for an agent of the Fates.

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: Turns out I'm full of surprises, Thanatos. Though, the Three Fates are your sisters, aren't they? Surprised you didn't requisition the List yourself.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Thanatos_01
dialogueLines.text: Guess it just wasn't meant to be. Though, I don't know them at all. Keep only to themselves, somewhere. Sometimes I wonder whether everything really is preordained, or whether they're making all this up as they go.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Thanatos_01
dialogueLines.text: ...Answer me something, Zagreus. What am I to you, exactly, as of late? Because, sometimes, with you, I...

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: I'm sometimes at a loss myself, Thanatos. Though one thing I know is it's always really good running into you out there, it's... well, exciting. Sparring with you and all that! And then, I know it means that I might get to see you here.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Thanatos_01
dialogueLines.text: Well, here I am. But you didn't answer my question. Though, you know something? Forget I asked. I've got to go.

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: Hey, Than, tell me something, and forgive the awkward question here, but... are we good? I mean... between us. Any infuriating habits I should know about, that sort of thing?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Thanatos_01
dialogueLines.text: You know I don't have time to list all your infuriating habits, Zagreus. Though... if any of them were outside my usual tolerance threshold, I promise to let you know, all right?

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: So then, we're good. Because I think we're good, but I just wanted to make sure.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Thanatos_01
dialogueLines.text: We're good. See you, all right?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Thanatos_01
dialogueLines.text: ...It just struck me how much you've changed, Zag. I still remember when you kept trying to singe Cerberus' tail with your feet. Haven't seen you do that in a while.

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: Oh, I definitely learned my lesson there. But, thanks, I guess. You've opened up a lot, yourself, by the way. Talking to you lately, it's a world apart from the first time. You looked like you were going to slice my head off.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Thanatos_01
dialogueLines.text: It sure must have taken a lot of courage for you to come right up and tell me you thought I looked better before I cut my hair. I still ask myself how come I didn't keep ignoring you after that. Or slice your head off.

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: Well, the option is always available, I guess. Hey, you ever think of growing it out again?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Thanatos_01
dialogueLines.text: No. It kept getting in my way. Sliced it right off and never looked back. So... plenty of personal growth all around, then?

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: Hey, just how far are you able to shift, anyway, Than? I never could go more than a blade's length in front of me, while you're out there skipping across the entire world probably.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Thanatos_01
dialogueLines.text: Most of it, yes. Don't beat yourself up, though, Zagreus. It's a practiced skill but I've apparently been able to do it since birth. Mother Nyx says I was a real handful.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Thanatos_01
dialogueLines.text: I saw you voided the old pact between Orpheus and your father. So now he gets to see his wife from time to time?

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: He does, and I did, Than. I think we can stand to see a little less of him if it means he can be happier for the remainder of eternity.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Thanatos_01
dialogueLines.text: You really need to learn to stop meddling in others' affairs. Did either of them even ask your help? I can't imagine Orpheus did, and I'm guessing his wife's feelings were complicated, too, from what I know of the circumstances of their final meeting.

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: What? Orpheus is my mate, Than. And I came to know his muse, Eurydice, as well. They still had feelings for each other, but with no way to communicate.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Thanatos_01
dialogueLines.text: So you decided to step in. Tsch. I think my instincts on the subject are just not the same as yours. I know your intentions were in the right place, but... you know what, forget it.

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: Than, you don't hold a grudge against old Sisyphus, do you? He seems rather apprehensive about you. I understand you met not on the best of terms.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Thanatos_01
dialogueLines.text: He is a brazen one, I have to give him that. I hadn't realized that you were close. Despite some questionable choices on your part, I don't think you're the poorest judge of character, and so... maybe I have him wrong.

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: I only have a faint impression of the man he used to be, although it seems he's changed a lot, since then. Thank you for understanding, anyway.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Thanatos_01
dialogueLines.text: I heard you stayed the knave-king's sentence. I should be angry with you. Sisyphus must be the only mortal ever to have humiliated me. But Mother Nyx, she... must have backed you, didn't she...

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: We did what needed to be done. As much as Father likes making examples out of wretched shades, an eternal sentence felt a little excessive in this case. Besides... Sisyphus isn't leaving, anyway. Seems to have found his niche.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Thanatos_01
dialogueLines.text: Oh? Well, he's an odd one through and through, I guess. As long as I don't have to deal with him again.

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: Than, level with me on this. You didn't happen to hire a shifty character called Skelly to assist me here and there, did you?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Thanatos_01
dialogueLines.text: Who, what? No. Why would you even ask?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Thanatos_01
dialogueLines.text: ...You're really staying, Zagreus? I mean... between the generally ill-conceived attempts to reach the surface every so often.

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: I am. I figured it's the only way I could continue interrupting you while you try to do your job. You work too hard, Than, live a little, right?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Thanatos_01
dialogueLines.text: Heh. Well then, I'll see what I can do about that. You'll have to show me what you mean by 'live a little', though.

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: Thanatos, I ought have asked by now, but... what's it like? Up there I mean, outside. You never talk about it, I... almost forget that's where you go, for work.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Thanatos_01
dialogueLines.text: What can I say? It's... different. I never stay up there for very long, don't think I could, starts making me feel ill. Too many sensations all at once. And when the chariot of Helios is in the sky, it gets so bright that I can barely see.

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: That sounds awful. If it's that bad, why would all the Olympians chat it up so much?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Thanatos_01
dialogueLines.text: There's no accounting for their taste, I guess. But, I'm discounting certain aspects that are not so bad. You know what? I'll not spoil the surprise. I have a feeling you're going to see for yourself soon enough.

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: Thanatos, I ought have asked by now, but... what's it like? Up there I mean, outside. You never talk about it, I... almost forget that's where you go, for work.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Thanatos_01
dialogueLines.text: What can I say? It's... different. I never stay up there for very long, don't think I could, starts making me feel ill. Too many sensations all at once. And when the chariot of Helios is in the sky, it gets so bright that I can barely see.

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: That sounds awful. If it's that bad, why would all the Olympians chat it up so much?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Thanatos_01
dialogueLines.text: There's no accounting for their taste, I guess.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Thanatos_01
dialogueLines.text: How's everything been going with your mother, Zagreus? You've been pretty quiet about the whole thing, is everything all right? You can tell me.

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: Thank you for asking, Than. I don't know how much more I can say right now, given everything. I'm still figuring it out myself. I'm thankful you've been there to help me get to see her from time to time.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Thanatos_01
dialogueLines.text: Well, if there's something more that I can do to support you in this, let me know, all right? Sounds like you're making progress, at least.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Thanatos_01
dialogueLines.text: I met her, you know. Persephone, I mean. You look more like your father, but... you have her eyes. One of them, anyway.

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: You never spoke of her to me. You must have known... you kept the truth from me, the same as Nyx?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Thanatos_01
dialogueLines.text: It's not like that. I didn't know any more than you. Besides, Lord Hades ordered all of us to never speak of her again. I thought it just a bad relationship. I didn't realize she left her son.

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: Something tells me Father wouldn't let her take me even if she wanted.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Thanatos_01
dialogueLines.text: Maybe so. But even still, she did abandon you. Your family's here, if you ask me. But I can understand that... you need answers.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Thanatos_01
dialogueLines.text: I met her, you know. Persephone, I mean. You look more like your father, but, you have her eyes. One of them, anyway.

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: You never spoke of her to me. You must have known... you kept the truth from me, the same as Nyx?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Thanatos_01
dialogueLines.text: It's not like that. I didn't know any more than you. Besides, Lord Hades ordered all of us to never speak of her again. I thought it just a bad relationship. I didn't realize she left her son.

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: Something tells me Father wouldn't let her take me even if she wanted.

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: Hey, Than. Come join me in the lounge for old time's sake. The coast is clear. The work can wait.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Thanatos_01
dialogueLines.text: You're really asking me to join you for a drink...? I mean... you know what, sure. The work can wait.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Thanatos_01
dialogueLines.text: ...I somehow knew, you know. I always knew you weren't going to stay. You were... so restless here. You just needed a reason to leave! And I hoped you wouldn't find one this quickly. Eugh. What am I saying? You have no idea.

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: No, I know exactly what you're saying, Than. I'm sorry that I'm putting you through all of this, it's just... it's something that I have to do. But, I'm glad we know the truth about me, now. Maybe we can move on from there.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Thanatos_01
dialogueLines.text: Tsk... I've always lacked your optimism, Zag. Anyway... the work has waited long enough, I need to get back to it. Thanks for the drink.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Thanatos_01
dialogueLines.text: ...Watch yourself out there, Zagreus. I have to go.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Thanatos_01
dialogueLines.text: ...It shall be as you say, my lord. I will report back soon.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Thanatos_01
dialogueLines.text: ...Yes, my lord. I will begin, and not return until the deed is done.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Thanatos_01
dialogueLines.text: ...If that is all, my lord, then I will make way for the surface now.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Thanatos_01
dialogueLines.text: ...I understand, my lord. I know where they are and soon shall bring them here.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Thanatos_01
dialogueLines.text: ...I do appreciate your faith in me, my lord. Begging your pardon, please.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Thanatos_01
dialogueLines.text: ...This matter will be dealt with promptly, lord. I expect to return shortly.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Thanatos_01
dialogueLines.text: ...Looks like you have everything under control. Nice work, Hypnos.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Thanatos_01
dialogueLines.text: ...Plenty of time for sleeping once your shift is over, right, Brother?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Thanatos_01
dialogueLines.text: ...Heard your latest reports were looking good, Hypnos. You keep it up.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Thanatos_01
dialogueLines.text: ...Anyway, good catching up, Hypnos.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Thanatos_01
dialogueLines.text: ...That's the last of the new registrations, but I should have more soon.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Thanatos_01
dialogueLines.text: ...All right, I'd better get going. Take care, Brother.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Thanatos_01
dialogueLines.text: ...Looks like you have everything under control. Nice work, Hypnos.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Thanatos_01
dialogueLines.text: ...Looks like you have everything under control. Nice work, Hypnos.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_FurySister_01
dialogueLines.text: This is a private chat, if you'll excuse us, Zag.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Thanatos_01
dialogueLines.text: We'll see you later out there, Zagreus.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Thanatos_01
dialogueLines.text: Just catching up on things with Megaera here, Zag.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_FurySister_01
dialogueLines.text: We were just talking about you, Zagreus.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_FurySister_01
