dialogueLines.text: Well if it isn't our mutual friend.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Thanatos_01
dialogueLines.text: Enjoy the peace and quiet with us, Zag.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_FurySister_01
dialogueLines.text: Just catching up with Thanatos, if you don't mind.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Thanatos_01
dialogueLines.text: Discussing work-related matters, Zagreus.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_FurySister_01
dialogueLines.text: Look who it is, Than. Zagreus.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Thanatos_01
dialogueLines.text: We've been comparing notes about our latest tasks.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_FurySister_01
dialogueLines.text: We're in the middle of a conversation, Zag.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Thanatos_01
dialogueLines.text: Catch up with you some other time, all right?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Nyx_01
dialogueLines.text: ...We have another task for you.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Thanatos_01
dialogueLines.text: As you will, Mother. It will be done immediately, then.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Thanatos_01
dialogueLines.text: ...Understood, Mother. Commencing the assignment now.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Nyx_01
dialogueLines.text: ...Your expertise is needed once again.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Thanatos_01
dialogueLines.text: Then, very well, Mother. I'll begin right away, and be back soon.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Thanatos_01
dialogueLines.text: ...I hear you, Mother, and obey. I will return when the task is complete.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Thanatos_01
dialogueLines.text: Thank you for thinking of me for this task. I'll make it happen shortly.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Thanatos_01
dialogueLines.text: ...The task is clear to me, Mother. I go. Your will be done.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Thanatos_Field_01
dialogueLines.text: Barely caught up with you, here, Zag. You must have had a little help from Hermes, I suppose.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Thanatos_Field_01
dialogueLines.text: You have the butterfly I gave to you. Stay back, then, and it'll make you stronger once I'm finished here.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Thanatos_Field_01
dialogueLines.text: Good to see you taking little Mort there on one of your strolls. Means I can better keep an eye on you out there.

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: He's been great to have around, for sure. Still can't believe you found him after all this time! You used to always carry him around, I was so sad for you when he got lost.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Thanatos_Field_01
dialogueLines.text: And I cannot believe you still remember that. Regardless, he will not be getting lost again under your supervision, accurate?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Thanatos_Field_01
dialogueLines.text: Got little Mort with you, there, huh. Well, there's no need to summon me this time.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Thanatos_Field_01
dialogueLines.text: What are you doing back here, Zag? You made it out.

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: I did... but I had to come back. And, not exactly voluntarily. I'll be visiting up there from time to time, though... it looks like I'll be staying here.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Thanatos_Field_01
dialogueLines.text: Visiting from time to time...? I don't understand.

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: It's my mother. She's alive and well! It's just that I can't stay with her up there for very long! So we'll have to catch up... one visit at a time.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Thanatos_Field_01
dialogueLines.text: I see. Well then... that's good. That's good, because... it means that I won't have to track you down up top. Because, between us, I can only take so much of it up there.

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: About when we last spoke. I care about you, Thanatos. A lot. Though, you asked what you are to me... how am I supposed to even answer that?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Thanatos_Field_01
dialogueLines.text: Ugh, I don't know. Sometimes I miss not feeling anything. Can't always trust what feelings say. I get to thinking, are we only friends, or... look, this is embarrassing.

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: To think discussing feelings would be even harder than annihilating Father's forces. But... I've been asking myself the same thing, Than. If it's any reassurance.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Thanatos_Field_01
dialogueLines.text: Well, if you come up with any answers, I guess let me know. Good running into you.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Thanatos_Field_01
dialogueLines.text: Hey. I didn't mean to lay the guilt on you for what you did for Orpheus, Zag. I just worry when we get involved... we can make matters worse.

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: You haven't ever made anything worse, Than. But as for me, I realized what you meant. I was so eager to do something for Orpheus when I first met Eurydice, I... never asked them if it was OK.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Thanatos_Field_01
dialogueLines.text: At least things turned out well for them. But that was fortunate. Family troubles, personal relations, and the like... it's sensitive business. Though, who am I to talk. You didn't ask me to show up, like this.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Thanatos_Field_01
dialogueLines.text: Still running from yourself, I see. How's that been working out for you thus far?

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: I'm searching for my mother. You still live at home with yours. You don't know what it's like.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Thanatos_Field_01
dialogueLines.text: Mother Nyx was like a mother to you, too. And this is how you repay her. You should be ashamed of yourself, and learn your place.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Thanatos_Field_01
dialogueLines.text: Listen to me. I don't know how else to put this, but, I want you to come home. Voluntarily. It's not just you swept up in all this nonsense now. And, I know you didn't ask for me to get involved, but, what did you expect?

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: ...Than. Home is never going to be the same for me. There's no use trying to pretend. I have to go.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Thanatos_Field_01
dialogueLines.text: You don't have to do anything of the sort. You have no idea how good you've had it here. Maybe someday you'll come to understand.

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: Hey, Than, are you OK? I mean, with everything, lately?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Thanatos_Field_01
dialogueLines.text: I am. Though, thanks for asking, Zag. The risk of being close with anyone is sometimes things get complicated, fast. Admittedly it's not a risk I often take, but... in your case, it's been worth it, all in all. We're good, all right?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Thanatos_Field_01
dialogueLines.text: Outdid me once again, there, huh. Tsch. I have to admit, you're not as bad at all of this as I expected. First I found you, I was certain that you had no chance at all.

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: I seem to have this whole easy-to-underestimate thing about me, based on a decent sum of my relationships. In any case, I always welcome your appearances out here. I know it's not been easy for you, Than.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Thanatos_Field_01
dialogueLines.text: That's fair as an assessment, I would say. But then again, it's never been an easy time for me. I know I have to do my job. But I don't have to keep on helping you, like this.

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: ...What, you're telling me this is it? You're just going to leave me to my own devices, then, I've proved myself to you, something like that?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Thanatos_Field_01
dialogueLines.text: No, you deadbeat, I am telling you the opposite! Why do you think I keep on showing up? You may not really need me, Zagreus, but I will take these opportunities to help. I take it that's all right.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Thanatos_Field_01
dialogueLines.text: You left, without so much as telling me good-bye. I suppose you knew I'd catch up with you sooner or later, is that it? No escaping death, and all?

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: I left when it was necessary, Than. I thought of you and hoped you'd understand. I have to do this.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Thanatos_Field_01
dialogueLines.text: That's more motivation than I've ever heard from you. Well, if you won't say it, I'll say it. Good-bye, Zagreus.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Thanatos_Field_01
dialogueLines.text: I've got to hand it to you, Zag. You're easy to track down. The shades of Tartarus are beginning to fear you even more than your Lord Father.

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: Oh they won't fear me once they get to know me.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Thanatos_Field_01
dialogueLines.text: I'm not so sure about that. Even I'm beginning to fear you, I think. Seems I don't know you as well as I thought.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Thanatos_Field_01
dialogueLines.text: What's with the look? You had your hands full, now you don't thanks to me... Tsch, seems I'm left to thanking myself, since you're too proud to do it.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Thanatos_Field_01
dialogueLines.text: You seemed a little winded, there, no? Not even close to the surface, yet, either.

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: Thanks for the vote of confidence there, Than.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Thanatos_Field_01
dialogueLines.text: You're very welcome. Now, I'd better get back, before Lord Hades catches on to this.

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: I told you I don't need your help here, Thanatos.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Thanatos_Field_01
dialogueLines.text: It wasn't being offered. Just happened to be in the area is all. Carry on, Zagreus.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Thanatos_Field_01
dialogueLines.text: Good to be working with you in a more official capacity, Zag. Shall we get started?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Thanatos_Field_01
dialogueLines.text: You're back. I thought you made it out. No matter.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Thanatos_Field_01
dialogueLines.text: ...Thought you could just get away from me, did you?

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: Thanatos. I figured it was only a matter of time before Father sent you after me to do his dirty work.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Thanatos_Field_01
dialogueLines.text: Zagreus. Is that really why you think I'm here? Let's see how many of these exalted champions you can send back to their dooms, unless you'd rather I would send you back to yours.

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: It isn't much, Than, but I got you something.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Thanatos_01
dialogueLines.text: You want me to take that off your hands, fine. But you are taking this from me, and if anybody asks, we're even. Or, scratch that, don't tell anyone about this, understand?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Thanatos_01
dialogueLines.text: What, really...? Come on, what is this, Zagreus. You think handing this to me makes up for everything you've put us through?

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: It's just something I found out there that I thought you might like, Than. Don't think anything of it.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Thanatos_01
dialogueLines.text: Why bother with such empty gestures, Zag. You've said you're getting out of here; best focus on that and leave me be.

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: It's not an empty gesture, Than. Besides, if I wasn't trying to stay focused, I'd be drinking it myself.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Thanatos_01
dialogueLines.text: You shouldn't be doing this, Zag. You picked sides, and things are not the same. A momentary stab at thoughtfulness can't fix something like that.

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: We've been through a lot, Than, and I think we'll be going through a lot more yet. Consider it a token of our kinship.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Thanatos_01
dialogueLines.text: Don't know what you're expecting, giving this to me. If there's a catch here, I don't want to know.

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: We've been through a lot, Than, and I think we'll be going through a lot more yet. Consider it a token of our kinship.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Thanatos_01
dialogueLines.text: What is it with the generosity lately...? Look I'll just stash this with the rest if you really don't have a better thing to do with it than dump it off on me.

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: Hey, that's an idea. One of these days or nights, we'll have enough of this stuff to throw a feast to make all of Olympus jealous.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Thanatos_01
dialogueLines.text: You can't be serious. You're serious...? Eh, you know what, fine. I'll take this and we'll go about our business. Though I don't see why you bother.

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: Why I bother? Why, how else can I show you how I feel except by handing you these bottles from time to time?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Thanatos_01
dialogueLines.text: I have to hand it to you, Zag, you're really putting in the work to get me to forgive you after all you've done. I'm honestly surprised.

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: Don't be. That feast of ours is really going to be something, Than.

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: Hey Than. For all the times you've bailed me out, and just, all the times you've been around for me... I thought I'd get you this. The real thing.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Thanatos_01
dialogueLines.text: Ambrosia...! Zagreus, where did you even... don't you think this is a bit excessive?

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: You say that like it's a bad thing. Little bit of excess from time to time never hurt anyone I'm sure.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Thanatos_01
dialogueLines.text: Oh, you would be surprised. Though if you're going to embarrass me, you know what, I am going to reciprocate. Behold!

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: For you, Thanatos. The Olympians might be accustomed to this stuff but I think we should have our share of it down here. At least you should.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Thanatos_01
dialogueLines.text: You know... the Olympians do have good taste in certain things. The last bottle you gave me... it was... better than expected. Though, I almost decided not to try it at all.

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: But try it you did! And now, you'll get to try this one for comparison's sake. Each one's supposed to be different you know.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Thanatos_01
dialogueLines.text: Different, huh. Well, then, I guess I'll have to see what you mean by that. Thank you, Zag. You didn't have to do this.

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: I hope you're ready for some more of the real thing, there, Than, because I got you some, and I won't be taking 'Tsch, what is this, Zag' for an answer.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Thanatos_01
dialogueLines.text: Ugh, what is this? Come on, already, Zagreus, are you just messing with me, now? How did you even manage to get more of this, much less decide to hand it off to me?

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: In order: no, I'm not; by ransacking my father's realm repeatedly; and it's because I like you, Thanatos. In case you still have some misgivings about that.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Thanatos_01
dialogueLines.text: You like me, Zagreus...? I never thought, I... don't know why that sounds so strange, coming from you. Given everything that's happened as of late.

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: Yeah. Look, if you don't feel the same way about me at this point, I would rather know. Cease all these coy gift exchanges, and all that.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Thanatos_01
dialogueLines.text: I never said anything like that! You know what, there's... a bunch of mortals I have to go fetch. Good-bye.

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: Thanatos, I wanted to apologize for the last time I approached you with a bottle of the real thing, and give the whole exchange another shot. Would that be all right?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Thanatos_01
dialogueLines.text: Hmph! You're asking my consent to give me yet another lavish gift? Sure! Permission granted. Though, what makes you think that your approach this time is going to be any more successful than the last?

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: I don't know that it will. I never really know exactly where I stand with you. But I know how I feel. And I'd rather be up front with you, even if it means risking our relationship, such as it is.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Thanatos_01
dialogueLines.text: Our relationship? It wasn't long ago you were prepared to throw it all away while making for the surface, if you don't recall. But now you're saying that you care for me, and... what, exactly, Zagreus?

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: Yes, that's what I'm saying, Than. I should not have left the way I did, without letting you know. But when you found me, I... think that's when... that's when I knew. Or started to realize, you know?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Thanatos_01
dialogueLines.text: I... yes. I know, I think, because... when I heard you took off, the anger that I felt, it was... it wasn't something I expected. So... ungh. What do we do now?

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: Maybe we ought to take our time? Unlike the real thing there, that's a commodity we have in good supply. Just know that, if you feel the way I do... you know where to find me. And if not... I'm grateful, anyway.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Thanatos_01
dialogueLines.text: ...I see. Well, then. The best that I can say for now is that... I'm grateful, too, for this. Take care of yourself, Zag.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Thanatos_01
dialogueLines.text: Sorry, Zagreus, I have to go. Lots of assignments as of late. Too bad I can't be everywhere at once.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Thanatos_01
dialogueLines.text: No use in making idle chat, right, Zagreus?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Thanatos_01
dialogueLines.text: I have a job to do, and you've no interest in remaining here. See you around out there.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Thanatos_01
dialogueLines.text: Don't bother, Zagreus. I was just leaving.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Thanatos_01
dialogueLines.text: I hope you've thought through all of this. For both our sakes.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Thanatos_01
dialogueLines.text: I can't keep bailing you out each time you get in over your head.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Thanatos_01
dialogueLines.text: I have to say it's quite a mess you've gotten yourself into, Zagreus.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Thanatos_01
dialogueLines.text: You better know what you're doing.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Thanatos_01
dialogueLines.text: You're wasting your time. Just go.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Thanatos_01
dialogueLines.text: Shouldn't you be back out there by now, getting yourself killed?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Thanatos_01
dialogueLines.text: You know how much trouble I am getting myself into here?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Thanatos_01
dialogueLines.text: There's nothing left for us to talk about.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Thanatos_01
dialogueLines.text: Don't talk to me. Just go.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Thanatos_01
dialogueLines.text: Excuse me, I've got work to do.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Thanatos_01
dialogueLines.text: Not now, I have to go.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Thanatos_01
dialogueLines.text: I don't know what you want from me.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Thanatos_01
dialogueLines.text: I have to get back to my responsibilities.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Thanatos_01
dialogueLines.text: You are in so much trouble.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Thanatos_01
dialogueLines.text: You watch yourself out there.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Thanatos_01
dialogueLines.text: Take care of yourself.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Thanatos_01
dialogueLines.text: I'll see you out there, I guess.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Thanatos_01
dialogueLines.text: I hope all this is worth it.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Thanatos_01
dialogueLines.text: We'll meet again out there.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Thanatos_01
dialogueLines.text: I hope you know what you're doing.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Thanatos_01
dialogueLines.text: Can't talk right now, I have to get to work.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Thanatos_01
dialogueLines.text: I need to get to work. I'll see you later.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Thanatos_01
dialogueLines.text: Your father's going to have my hide.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Thanatos_01
dialogueLines.text: I am in so much trouble, you have no idea...

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Thanatos_01
dialogueLines.text: What have I gotten myself into, Zag...

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Thanatos_01
dialogueLines.text: Go about your business, Zag. What if Lord Hades saw us chatting like this, now...? I risked everything by helping you out there, and still you failed.

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: Turns out there's quite a lot of dead to fight down in the Underworld. Though I probably got farther than I would have, thanks to you.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Thanatos_01
dialogueLines.text: Always happy to oblige. Especially when it causes me to risk my station. To risk everything I've worked for.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Thanatos_01
dialogueLines.text: What are you doing back here, Zagreus? I risked everything by helping you out there. So that was all for nothing, then?

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: Far from it, Than. I got where I needed to go, and if not for your help, maybe I wouldn't have. As for why I'm back... as Father would say, I have some business to attend to, here.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Thanatos_01
dialogueLines.text: Well! Don't let me stop you, then. Always happy to risk my station for your sake. To risk everything I've worked for.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Thanatos_01
dialogueLines.text: You're not in too much of a hurry, are you, Zag?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Thanatos_01
dialogueLines.text: Well then... some mortal up there's lived a good, long life.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Thanatos_01
dialogueLines.text: Mind if I practice my ability to shift short distances with someone else?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Thanatos_01
dialogueLines.text: Well... I'd say that test was a success, wouldn't you?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Thanatos_01
dialogueLines.text: I have to shift away from here, though if you'd care to join me...

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Thanatos_01
dialogueLines.text: Too bad I can't just shift you to the top, though this wasn't so bad.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Thanatos_01
dialogueLines.text: Hey let's get out of here for just a bit, all right?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Thanatos_01
dialogueLines.text: Whew. Thanks for the break, now back to it, I guess.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Thanatos_01
dialogueLines.text: Was just about to go on break, if you would care to join me.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Thanatos_01
dialogueLines.text: Good catching up. Take care of yourself, Zag.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Thanatos_01
dialogueLines.text: I think you'd better head back to your quarters, Zagreus.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Thanatos_01
dialogueLines.text: Mm. I'd best get back to my responsibilities for now.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Thanatos_01
dialogueLines.text: Much as I would like to stay and chat, I'd better go.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Thanatos_01
dialogueLines.text: Zag, I've got so many mortals on my plate right now...

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Thanatos_01
dialogueLines.text: See you out there soon enough, all right?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Thanatos_01
dialogueLines.text: If you stay out too long, I'm going to track you down.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Thanatos_01
dialogueLines.text: Sounds like we've both been keeping plenty busy as of late.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Thanatos_01
dialogueLines.text: Now that you're here, I can finally head out.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Thanatos_01
dialogueLines.text: We'll have to catch up more some other time.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Thanatos_01
dialogueLines.text: Sorry Zag, I'm due to get a bunch of mortals now.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Thanatos_01
dialogueLines.text: Excuse me, Zag, just got word that I'm needed back up top.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Thanatos_01
dialogueLines.text: Hope your father's going to be all right with all of this.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Thanatos_01
dialogueLines.text: We showed those wretches last time, didn't we.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Thanatos_01
dialogueLines.text: Good running into you out there, and glad you made it out.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Thanatos_01
dialogueLines.text: Impressive that you went the distance once again.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Thanatos_01
dialogueLines.text: Was good having an opportunity to back you up out there.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Thanatos_01
dialogueLines.text: I heard you did good work out there last time.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Thanatos_01
dialogueLines.text: The shades are murmuring about your latest exploits, Zag.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Thanatos_01
dialogueLines.text: Don't have a lot of time for catching up right now, here, Zag.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Thanatos_01
dialogueLines.text: Be on your way, Zagreus. I have an audience with Lord Hades in a moment. An inquiry into my recent absences.

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: What are you worried about, Than? You've never given him a reason not to trust you.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Thanatos_01
dialogueLines.text: Recently, I'm not so sure. Whatever you're doing, it had better be worth it.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Thanatos_01
dialogueLines.text: I still cannot entirely believe you really think there's any way you're getting out of here.

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: Why? You've been out. It's thanks to you the mortals end up down here maybe half the time.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Thanatos_01
dialogueLines.text: That's different, that's my job. I can't stay there, and frankly I can barely stand it. Do you have any idea how bright it gets, up there?

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: No. But I look forward to finding out for myself.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Thanatos_01
dialogueLines.text: Tsch, well. We'll see what the Fates have to say about that. Maybe the shade of Daedalus can make you some protective eyewear or something.

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: Thanatos, the world outside... didn't you say that it would make you sick, being out there for very long at all? I understand now what you meant by that. Beautiful place and all, but... it's good to be back.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Thanatos_01
dialogueLines.text: See what I mean, now, Zag? Nice place to visit on occasion, sure. But I definitely wouldn't want to live there.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Thanatos_Field_01
dialogueLines.text: Thought I might find you all the way out here. Although, quite frankly, I'm surprised you're still alive.

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: I can hold my own in case you haven't noticed, Than.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Thanatos_Field_01
dialogueLines.text: I haven't, Zag! Now, stand back, and let me show you how it's done.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Thanatos_Field_01
dialogueLines.text: Still at it, I see. You're stubborn. And you are going to get me in a heap of trouble before all is said and done.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Thanatos_Field_01
dialogueLines.text: Good, I'm not too late. Got here as quickly as I could.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Thanatos_Field_01
dialogueLines.text: I got the message that you need assistance, Zagreus?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Thanatos_Field_01
dialogueLines.text: How about another of our little contests, Zagreus?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Thanatos_Field_01
dialogueLines.text: We'll have to catch up at some other time. Though good to see you anyway.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Thanatos_Field_01
dialogueLines.text: I have a feeling this is not going to take much time at all.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Thanatos_Field_01
dialogueLines.text: I only have a little time until my next assignment, but this shouldn't take too long.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Thanatos_Field_01
dialogueLines.text: Thought I might stop by and check up on this corner of the Underworld, Zag.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Thanatos_Field_01
dialogueLines.text: You've come this far, now let's make sure you go the distance, Zag.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Thanatos_Field_01
dialogueLines.text: I heard there was a matter that required my attention here.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Thanatos_Field_01
dialogueLines.text: Had a feeling I would find you all alone out here.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Thanatos_Field_01
dialogueLines.text: Want to stand back and watch me work, here, Zag?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Thanatos_Field_01
dialogueLines.text: This one is off the record, Zagreus. Come on!

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Thanatos_Field_01
dialogueLines.text: Looks like we've got an urgent work-related matter to attend to.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Thanatos_Field_01
dialogueLines.text: Your father's forces making trouble for you, Zag?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Thanatos_Field_01
dialogueLines.text: Not a word of this to anyone back home, you hear me, Zagreus?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Thanatos_Field_01
dialogueLines.text: I heard you got yourself into another mess that needed cleaning up.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Thanatos_Field_01
dialogueLines.text: We'll catch up later, now let's deal with these wretches and be done.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Thanatos_Field_01
dialogueLines.text: Hope you don't mind if I cut in, here, Zagreus.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Thanatos_Field_01
dialogueLines.text: What say we go take care of business here just like we used to, Zag?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Thanatos_Field_01
dialogueLines.text: I can't stay long, so how about we get this business over with.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Thanatos_Field_01
dialogueLines.text: Figured you'd be up to have another of our little contests. Now let's go.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Thanatos_Field_01
dialogueLines.text: What do you say we deal some death together, Zagreus?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Thanatos_Field_01
dialogueLines.text: Don't have much time, but let me see if I can pitch in.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Thanatos_Field_01
dialogueLines.text: Remember, not a word of this, to anyone. Let's go.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Thanatos_Field_01
dialogueLines.text: You look like you could use a hand.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Thanatos_Field_01
dialogueLines.text: Let's see who's better at this, Zagreus.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Thanatos_Field_01
dialogueLines.text: I got the feeling you could use some backup here.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Thanatos_Field_01
dialogueLines.text: Let's get this done.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Thanatos_01
dialogueLines.text: So, between us... all your attempting to break out of here, over and over... you're now authorized to keep that up? Exposing weaknesses in Underworld security, huh.

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: Something like that. I guess I've gotten pretty decent about escaping from a place that's known for being inescapable. Lets me keep in touch with the other side of the family. Work with you from time to time.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Thanatos_01
dialogueLines.text: I have to say I never thought I'd hear you talking about looking forward to working, Zag. You feeling all right? Spending too much time on the surface, maybe?

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: Oh I'm feeling fine. I never thought I was much good at anything, but... if my fated purpose is to be a master of frustrating Father's efforts to make everything strictly perfect, I think I'm OK with that.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Thanatos_01
dialogueLines.text: Well, then, I'll be looking forward to working with you in a more official capacity. Even if we're still supposed to keep it all under wraps, for sake of appearances. See you out there.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Thanatos_01
dialogueLines.text: Have to tell you, Zag. It's different, with your mother around here. Don't mean to be so obvious, but... it's something about your father, I suppose.

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: I think they have a lot of work ahead of them. As long as they're trying... he's trying... I think I'm good with that, for now. Though, I am glad she's here.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Thanatos_01
dialogueLines.text: I'm beginning to think we all are. And, please don't take this the wrong way, but... I think you and your father have a lot of work ahead of you, as well.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Thanatos_01
dialogueLines.text: Your mother's very clever, Zagreus. I always thought you got that from your father, now I'm not so sure. So all is well with the Olympians, again?

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: For now, I think it is. But, you know how they are. How we all are, I guess.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Thanatos_01
dialogueLines.text: Hah! Yes, indeed. Well, then! I guess enjoy it while it lasts, as mortals sometimes say. I'll see you out there.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Thanatos_01
dialogueLines.text: You took Lord Hades down... you really did. I've never seen him quite like this before. I could scarce believe it when he came up through the Pool of Styx, himself...

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: Seemed only fair in the grand scheme of things. He slew his own parents at some point, right? I think the Fates enjoy this sort of thing.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Thanatos_01
dialogueLines.text: Come on, Zag, take some credit where it's due. Though... you'll forgive me if I keep my distance for a little while, as I think you have some issues to resolve. You watch yourself.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hades_01
dialogueLines.text: Such tardiness is oft to be expected from your brother, but from you, Thanatos? You're practically the god of punctuality.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Thanatos_01
dialogueLines.text: My lord, I apologize sincerely for the lapse in my performance as of late. I think my duties following the recent war above took more of a toll from me than I expected.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hades_01
dialogueLines.text: Thanatos, even we gods require rest from time to time. Someday we might get it around here, once this accursed House is well in order, finally. Until then, I appreciate your diligence. Do not overextend yourself.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Thanatos_01
dialogueLines.text: Thank you, my lord. I am at your service. I'll be getting back to my assignment now.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hades_01
dialogueLines.text: ...You have been rather slow about your duties, lately, Thanatos. And I'm receiving incident reports from Tartarus involving you. Remember your responsibilities. And, remember, I am watching you. Now, get out of my sight.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hades_01
dialogueLines.text: You've been abetting him, Thanatos. Behind my back! Betrayal. I would ask you why. Well?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Thanatos_01
dialogueLines.text: My lord, he and I... please. Go lock me up in Tartarus, or deal whichever justice you see fit, but... my loyalty is not subject to change. And I cannot stand by and watch you fight. The matter between you and Zagreus... there has to be some resolution, here.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hades_01
dialogueLines.text: You dare to lecture me? Tsch. My loyal subjects are too few of late, I think. This is your realm, not mine; I'll not cast you into that wretched pit. It's not like I have someone to replace you here, besides. Now then, begone.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Thanatos_01
dialogueLines.text: I'm deeply sorry, lord...

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Thanatos_01
dialogueLines.text: You wished to see me once again, my lord? It's been... a while since I had an audience with you.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hades_01
dialogueLines.text: It has. The last time, what was it you said? That there must be some resolution to the matter, as you called it, with my son? Well it is settled, now. Isn't it, boy?

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: I mean, you're still trying just as hard as ever to prevent me from escaping past the threshold of your realm, so, I don't know if I'd exactly call the matter settled. But... it does feel different, now.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hades_01
dialogueLines.text: And so you see... I would continue in our mutual good graces, if you please. This House can only function if the upper management is in accord. What do you say?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Thanatos_01
dialogueLines.text: I say, you honor me, my lord. I appreciate you telling me, yourself. And I concur that, for the good of all the Underworld, it is imperative that each of us continue to perform at our full strength.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hades_01
dialogueLines.text: Then, as you were. Dismissed.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Thanatos_01
dialogueLines.text: Hypnos, really, that is the worst idea I think I've ever heard. Do you understand how little sense that makes?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hypnos_01
dialogueLines.text: I'm just saying, maybe if you just, I don't know, had all the mortals die at the same time, it'd save you a bunch of trips, and then we'd have more time to spend together, huh?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Thanatos_01
dialogueLines.text: I don't know why I bother with you, honestly. Get back to work, and let me do the same.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Thanatos_01
dialogueLines.text: You cannot keep falling asleep on the job like this, Hypnos. This is completely unacceptable.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hypnos_01
dialogueLines.text: Oh I just nodded off a little while, Thanatos! I rest easy knowing that you're here! But if I goofed, why, I guess you could kill me dead, or something, huh?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Thanatos_01
dialogueLines.text: Don't give me any ideas. Pull yourself together, and quit slacking off. The House needs help, and you've got a job to do. Now get to it!

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Thanatos_01
dialogueLines.text: ...Hypnos, this list has a bunch of tasks from I don't know how long ago, I mean... I took several of these last year! And you've already submitted this week's registrations, why isn't that marked complete?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hypnos_01
dialogueLines.text: Don't you know it, I was just about to get to that! Then you showed up, and I got so distracted, it just kind of slipped my mind! It happens, right?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Thanatos_01
dialogueLines.text: You need to focus, Brother. Or it'll be Lord Hades reprimanding you again, and that is not something you want.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hypnos_01
dialogueLines.text: Oh, I don't know about that, last time I talked to Master he made pretty clear he never wanted to speak to me again!

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Thanatos_01
dialogueLines.text: Hypnos, you're not irreplaceable, you understand? If you don't shape up, quickly, someone else is going to have your job.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hypnos_01
dialogueLines.text: Oh I hear you loud and clear, Thanatos! And, let me tell you, they will have the greatest job in all the Underworld!

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Thanatos_01
dialogueLines.text: I feel like I'm not getting through to you, Brother. Well, after the inevitable happens, don't come telling me I didn't try.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Thanatos_01
dialogueLines.text: ...Well, how often do you consult these lists you make? If you're spending too much time just making lists, you're not leaving time to get anything done.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hypnos_01
dialogueLines.text: Oh, I love making my lists... I always illustrate the edges, each one is unique! That way anyone who gets to review the work, it's a whole experience, you know? But... I guess it does take up a lot of time.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Thanatos_01
dialogueLines.text: You'll have to work around the constraints of your responsibilities. You do take breaks, don't you? Before and after each break, maybe that's a time to make sure your own list of priorities is up-to-date.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hypnos_01
dialogueLines.text: Wait, my own list of priorities...? But, you're saying I can just go and make a list... for me? Huh. Whoa. Whoa....

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Thanatos_01
dialogueLines.text: I've heard that your reports have been immaculate of late, Hypnos. I have to know, who's helping you with them? It can't be Zagreus.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hypnos_01
dialogueLines.text: Oh, haha, no, he doesn't help. It's just I took to listing only the important stuff up top, and spending just a bit more time on the priority reports, and... guess it's working out!

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Thanatos_01
dialogueLines.text: Look, whatever the case... the work's improved. It's been observed. You ought to keep it up.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Thanatos_01
dialogueLines.text: ...Though, what I mean to say is, that... I'm sorry, Hypnos. You're my brother, but I haven't always treated you that way. Would you be willing to forgive me?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hypnos_01
dialogueLines.text: Aww, come on, Thanatos, are you kidding me, right here? We're like two skeletons in a crypt! You're the best, and you're always looking out for me. Even when you're not around for what can seem like aeons at a time!

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Thanatos_01
dialogueLines.text: Well let's not get ahead of ourselves, here. We each have got important jobs to do. Isn't that right, Brother?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Thanatos_01
dialogueLines.text: ...Congratulations, Brother! I saw you finally made the board. You made us proud.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Hypnos_01
dialogueLines.text: Sure did! That's something, huh? Though, sorry Thanatos, can't really chat right now, the shades are stacking up, there's greetings to be done, and sign-ins... lots of work to do, you know?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Thanatos_01
dialogueLines.text: I definitely know the feeling. Well then, let's keep it up, the both of us, all right?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_FurySister_01
dialogueLines.text: ...But that was then and this is now, and we have work to do.... Uh, anyway, Than, I think we'd best continue this discussion at some other time.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Thanatos_01
dialogueLines.text: You're interrupting private conversation, Zagreus. Leave us.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_FurySister_01
dialogueLines.text: No, let him stay, if that's what he wants. What does it matter? He's technically allowed within these halls, but he must know he isn't welcome anymore.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Thanatos_01
dialogueLines.text: Mother, is there something I can do to help, with all of this? I spend too much time away from the House. Please, let me know.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Nyx_01
dialogueLines.text: You are already doing what you can to help, my son. Continue doing so. Matters are at stake here concerning you and all of us. Your support and choices in this shape the outcome that shall come to pass.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Thanatos_01
dialogueLines.text: I understand, and I will do my best. Thank you for taking care of everything. I've every faith in you, Mother. And in this House. Farewell.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Thanatos_01
dialogueLines.text: Am I imagining it, Mother, or has this House been running relatively smoothly as of late? Although I know I must be tempting the Three Fates by putting it like this.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Nyx_01
dialogueLines.text: It is through a concerted effort that the House thus thrives, for now. Though, it shall do so only through continued diligence on your behalf, and that of many others here. It is a good occasion to take brief reprieve. But we need you working to the best of your ability, here, Thanatos.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Thanatos_01
dialogueLines.text: Well... know that I am ever-grateful, Mother. And I love you very much. Where would we be right now if not for you? I don't take what you've done for us for granted, nor does anybody here. You rest assured.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Thanatos_01
dialogueLines.text: Queen Persephone. All of us, we are most pleased you're back. If there is anything that I can do to ease your acclimation to this House, you need but ask.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Persephone_Home_01
dialogueLines.text: Why, thank you, Thanatos! I'll take you up on that if I can think of anything anytime soon. But my, you've grown! I understand you've been looking after my son, in the meantime?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Thanatos_01
dialogueLines.text: Your son, he... is very capable of looking after himself, it turns out. Although I try to be there, just in case. Though, I must beg your pardon, Queen. Someone awaits me on the surface now.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Thanatos_01
dialogueLines.text: ...I thank you for your blessings, Queen Persephone. Your son and I... we always were quite close, but through all this ordeal, we've become rather inseparable, I think.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Persephone_Home_01
dialogueLines.text: Well, I can see that he has better taste than I do, Thanatos! I have a sense of how industrious you are. You just take care of yourself, please. And take care of each other, too, all right?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Thanatos_01
dialogueLines.text: As you will, Queen. I mean, all right.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Sisyphus_01
dialogueLines.text: Oh, I'm doing very well, thanks, Prince. I'm not afraid of him, I mean, what have I got to lose? Now here, I wanted you to have this.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Sisyphus_01
dialogueLines.text: Sisyphus furtively offers a choice of benefits.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Sisyphus_01
dialogueLines.text: Can you believe the nerve of that one, Prince? He's nothing but a blowhard, so, I simply tell him what he wants to hear! He can't stop me from helping you, besides. I'm dead already, what's he going to do?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Sisyphus_01
dialogueLines.text: Sisyphus furtively offers a choice of benefits.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Sisyphus_01
dialogueLines.text: Prince Z.! Just missed your stern-faced colleague, feel that stillness hanging in the air? Sounds like he won't be checking up on me again anytime soon.

dialogueLines.speaker: CharProtag
dialogueLines.text: Thanatos really isn't such a bad sort once you get to know him, sir. Though, I suppose you'll have to take my word for that.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Sisyphus_01
dialogueLines.text: Oh, I believe it, Highness. I should think that bringing death must be a lonely job. Perhaps he ought to try pushing a boulder sometime!

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Sisyphus_01
dialogueLines.text: Sisyphus furtively offers a choice of benefits.
